`README.md`:

```md
# FastLogs

Open-source debug engine for game builds where the engine console is out of reach.

![CI](https://github.com/AitiX/Fastlogs/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)
![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT-green.svg)
![Release](https://img.shields.io/github/v/release/AitiX/Fastlogs?sort=semver)

Русская версия: [docs/ru/README.md](docs/ru/README.md).

**The goal of FastLogs is to get as many bugs fixed as possible, as fast as
possible.** At its core it is a tool for the
fastest possible loop between QA and a developer: the tester ships a bug in one
gesture, the developer opens a link and sees the cause right away, and nobody
waits on retellings, follow-up questions or blind reproduction attempts. Every
feature is judged by one question: does it shorten the path from a bug happening
in a build to that bug being fixed and the fix confirmed? That is also how the
roadmap is chosen: whatever removes the most waiting between QA and dev goes first.

## Why

In a WebGL build, on a portal, on a phone or on a console you have no access to the engine console. A tester hits a bug, but the log stays on their device and you cannot dictate it over the phone. FastLogs fixes that: from a running build a single gesture (or an agent call) ships the recent log lines, a snapshot of the device (OS, GPU, memory, build) and optional screenshots to a server, and the tester gets a short link. They paste it to you and you open a filterable console, the device info, the context and the screenshot in a browser. One server serves every game and both engines (Unity and GameMaker) on a single JSON contract.

## How it works

```
   Game (client)                  FastLogs server                Recipients
   -------------                  ---------------                ----------
  Unity  C#  ----\                                          /--> short link (viewer)
                  >-- POST /api/logs --> ingest --> SQLite --+--> catalog /browse
  GameMaker GML -/        (JSON)         + blobs (log/PNG)   \--> sinks (Slack/Discord/
                                                                  webhook/Confluence/Sheet)
```

Engines: Unity (C#, UPM package) and GameMaker (GML; complete but less battle-tested - see [the guide's caveat](docs/GAMEMAKER.md)). Platforms: WebGL and web portals, iOS, Android, Standalone (Windows / macOS / Linux). On console retail the tool is stripped out entirely.

This is what a developer opens from the link: a filterable console with the device info, context, scene snapshot, and each recorded run folded under its own session divider.

![FastLogs viewer](docs/viewer.png)

## Features

- Recent log lines + device info + context/breadcrumbs + screenshots + scene snapshot in one report.
- Crash auto-capture with an offline outbox: an unhandled exception is written to disk before FastLogs even attempts the upload, so a report whose process dies mid-send still arrives on the next launch.
- Browsable catalog with full-text search (SQLite FTS5), sessions, crash grouping and triage status/tags. A report can also open a Redmine issue in one click, if you wire the tracker up.
- Agent channel: headless send plus a remote command channel (agent -> game), and config-driven component capture.
- Unity and GameMaker clients speak one JSON contract: a new engine needs one HTTP request plus the log-line format the server parses (see [CONTRACT.md](CONTRACT.md) section 1a). Skip the format and the report still returns 201 while crash grouping and session dividers go quietly dead.
- Privacy by default: PII scrubbing on log text, context and breadcrumbs; sensitive device fields off unless you opt in.

## For QA

- Nothing to install: FastLogs is already baked into the build, and there is no code to write.
- Catch a bug, open the overlay (`F8`, or three quick taps in the top-right corner). Put your name in once on the **Settings** tab (Send stays greyed out until it is there), type a short title, tick `Shot` / `Scene` if useful, and hit Send.
- The short link lands in your clipboard (Unity also draws a QR of it). Paste it to a developer; it opens for anyone, no token.
- Detailed guide: [docs/UNITY.md](docs/UNITY.md).

## Quick start

Five minutes to your first report.

1. Run the server:

```bash
cd server
npm install
node scripts/add-app.js mygame "My Game" 30 # register a game (30-day retention); prints the ingest token once
npm start                                   # node src/server.js on http://localhost:8787 (blocks; leave it running)
```

Copy the token `add-app` prints - it is shown once and stored only as a hash. Ingest rejects an untokenized send with 401. Just poking around locally? `node scripts/add-app.js mygame "My Game" 30 --no-token` opens ingest for that app and you can skip the `Token` field below. On a real deploy, keep the token.

2. Unity: add the package by Git URL in `Packages/manifest.json`, create the config asset, then init and send.

```jsonc
{
  "dependencies": {
    "com.playjoy.fastlogs": "https://github.com/AitiX/Fastlogs.git?path=/unity#v0.5.3"
  }
}
```

Create the config via `Tools > PlayJoy > FastLogs > Create Config Asset`, set `EndpointUrl`, `AppId` and `Token` (the one `add-app` printed) in its Server section, then:

```csharp
using PlayJoy.FastLogs;

FastLogs.Init();                       // loads Resources/FastLogsConfig; idempotent
FastLogs.SetContext("level", "3-2");   // capture already runs: Init starts it
var r = await FastLogs.SendAsync(includeScreenshot: true, title: "Crash on load");
if (r.Success) GUIUtility.systemCopyBuffer = r.Url;
```

3. Open the returned link in a browser. That is the whole loop.

Supported Unity versions: 6000.1 (Unity 6) and 2022.3 LTS. Server needs Node.js >= 18.

## For developers

- Add the client, point it at your server, call `FastLogs.Init()` and `Send()`. Logs are captured from `Init` onward, so a report sent a second later already carries them. Recording (the separate cross-session store on disk) is off by default: set `Recording > Enabled = true` first, then either tick `AutoStartRecording` or call `StartRecording()`.
- Enrich reports with `SetContext(...)`, `Breadcrumb(...)`, scene snapshots, file/save uploads and multi-screenshot capture.
- Gate for release: the whole client is stripped from retail via `#if UNITY_EDITOR || DEVELOPMENT_BUILD`; opt back in on non-console targets with `LOGSHARE_FORCE_ENABLED`. Consoles stay stripped regardless.
- Guides: Unity [docs/UNITY.md](docs/UNITY.md), GameMaker [docs/GAMEMAKER.md](docs/GAMEMAKER.md).

## Automation and agents

Agents get a two-way inspect + drive channel into a running build, no debugger attached.

- Headless send with no overlay, toast, screenshot or loop guard. On WebGL it posts through `fetch`, so a report leaves even a backgrounded tab with a stalled player loop. From JS after `Init`: `await window.FastLogs.send({ title, comment, sceneContext })` resolves with `{ok, id, url, status, error}` (the same object also lands on `window.FastLogs.lastResult`).
- Command channel (agent -> game): an agent enqueues a named command with `POST /api/commands/<appId>`, the game polls, runs its whitelisted handler and acks the result, and the agent reads it back. On WebGL the poll lives in JS and works even in a hidden tab. A result past the server's 64 KB inline cap is uploaded as a file instead of failing: the ack then carries `{"__fastlogsResultRef": "<url>", "bytes": N}`, so wide dumps survive their own size. See [CONTRACT.md](CONTRACT.md) section 8 and [docs/UNITY.md](docs/UNITY.md).
- Pointed inspection (`fastlogs.inspect`): ask one component for its live state instead of hauling the whole scene tree. Args `{type, go?, props?, max?}` return serialized fields plus `Renderer` / `MaterialPropertyBlock` values. Read-only by construction, and it works whether or not component capture is enabled.
- Component capture: rule-driven snapshots of live component state (materials, `MaterialPropertyBlock`, sorting) straight from the build.
- Command-to-report correlation: while a command runs, any report it produces is tagged with that command id, so `GET /api/await/<appId>?code=<commandId>` returns exactly what the command caused. Enqueue, then await, no timestamp matching.
- Liveness beacon: `GET /api/commands/<appId>/health` answers "is a build actually polling right now", so a command that never gets acked reads as "nothing is running" rather than "the handler hung".
- MCP server ([server/mcp/](server/mcp/)): the whole loop as native agent tools (run a command and wait for its result, check channel health, await a report by correlation code, search and read logs) instead of shelling out to CLI helpers. It dereferences an offloaded result for you.

`SECURITY.md`:

```md
# Security Policy

## Supported versions

| Version | Supported |
|---------|-----------|
| 0.5.x   | Yes       |
| < 0.5   | No        |

Security fixes land on the latest release. Please upgrade before reporting an issue against an older build.

## Reporting a vulnerability

Please report security issues privately, not through a public issue or pull request.

Use GitHub private vulnerability reporting: open the repository's **Security** tab and choose **Report a vulnerability** (this opens a private security advisory visible only to the maintainers). If that is unavailable to you, open a private draft security advisory instead.

Include enough to reproduce: affected version, a description of the impact, and step-by-step reproduction or a proof of concept.

## Responsible disclosure

Please give us reasonable time to investigate and ship a fix before disclosing the issue publicly. Do not open a public issue describing the vulnerability, and do not exploit it beyond what is needed to demonstrate the problem. We will acknowledge your report, keep you posted on progress, and credit you in the release notes if you would like.

```

`TODO.md`:

```md
# FastLogs TODO

## Vision: the canonical two-way game <-> agent communication channel

FastLogs is the tool an AI agent (or a human) uses to both **inspect** a running build
(logs, device slice, scene context, component state) and **drive** it (remote commands),
with no human in the loop. The 2026-06-24 LookingForAliens WebGL debugging session
produced the original four items - the command channel, the Unity command client,
headless send and component capture. All four shipped in 0.4.0 and are on `main`; the
root [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md) has the details. The automation path was verified
end-to-end in a real batch-built WebGL player (headless Edge + local server:
window.FastLogs.send created a report row, fastlogs.ping and fastlogs.send
round-tripped through /api/commands). What is left:

## Remaining

### GameMaker parity for the command channel
The server (`/api/commands`, CONTRACT.md section 8) and the Unity client exist; GML does not.

Worth weighing before starting: the GM client has never been proven in production. Prod
holds 18 logs (16 Unity, 2 unlabelled) and not one GameMaker report, so the GM report path
is itself untested against a real server, and command-channel parity would be built on top
of it. Proving a plain GM report round-trip first is the cheaper first step (background:
`server/test/fixtures/README.md`, "Status").

Implementation notes from the recon pass:
- GM http state is single-flight: `hs.is_sending`/`request_id` are shared by log/file/snapshot
  sends (scr_fastlogs_http.gml). A poll loop needs its **own** request slot, or a separate object
  with its own Async HTTP event, so polling and report sends do not block each other.
- Poll cadence: reuse the Alarm pattern (`__fastlogs_retry_arm_alarm` / `fastlogs_retry_tick`).
- Response parsing: add a `request_kind == "poll"` branch in the controller's Async HTTP event
  (the `"file"` branch is the template).
- New scr_fastlogs_commands: handler registry (`fastlogs_register_command`), poll + ack,
  `FASTLOGS_COMMAND_POLL_SEC` config macro. Update PUBLIC-API.md and CONTRACT clients note.

### Smaller follow-ups
- File uploads (`SendFileAsync` path) still dispatch on the next player-loop tick; apply the
  same synchronous-first-dispatch treatment as the log path if file sends from background
  tabs are ever needed.
- `FlogTask` has a single continuation slot; add multicast if a task ever needs two consumers.
- Headless send is single-shot by design (`Net.MaxRetries` does not apply; documented). Add a
  light fetch-side retry if automation runs need it.
- Interactive `Send()` in a hidden tab can still park on the screenshot coroutine
  (`WaitForEndOfFrame` never fires); the headless path avoids screenshots entirely, but a
  screenshot timeout would make the interactive path robust too.
- Editor asmdef helper rewrites .asmdef via a fixed schema (EditorJsonUtility); unknown/future
  fields would be dropped. Switch to a non-destructive insert if that ever matters.

```

`docs/ADMIN-GUIDE.md`:

```md
# FastLogs - admin / devops guide

How to deploy the server, register a game, hand out the right token, wire up sinks, set retention, back the thing up, and fix it when it misbehaves. The user-facing side lives in the root [`README.md`](../README.md) and in the [`UNITY.md`](./UNITY.md) / [`GAMEMAKER.md`](./GAMEMAKER.md) guides.

Sources of truth:

- Install - [`server/deploy/INSTALL.md`](../server/deploy/INSTALL.md) (generic, any VPS or Docker host)
- Server and every env variable - [`server/README.md`](../server/README.md)
- API contract - [`CONTRACT.md`](../CONTRACT.md)

The server is Node.js (>= 18) plus SQLite (`better-sqlite3`) behind nginx. One dependency, neutral defaults (`BASE_URL=http://localhost:8787`), MIT license. Point it at your own domain and it runs, no code changes.

---

## 1. Deploy the server

### Option A. Docker (fastest)

```bash
cd server
cp .env.example .env          # set BASE_URL, ADMIN_TOKEN, VIEWER_TOKEN, IP_SALT, ...
# In Docker also set HOST=0.0.0.0 in .env. The shipped HOST=127.0.0.1 binds the
# container's own loopback, so the published port never reaches it (and the
# healthcheck still passes, because it probes inside the container). Compose maps
# only the host loopback, so 0.0.0.0 keeps the service private behind nginx.
docker compose up -d          # builds the image, mounts ./data and ./blobs, listens on 8787
# Register a game inside the container:
docker compose exec fastlogs node scripts/add-app.js mygame "My Game" 30
```

### Option B. systemd / VPS

Prerequisites: Linux with systemd, Node >= 18, nginx, certbot. The full walkthrough is [`server/deploy/INSTALL.md`](../server/deploy/INSTALL.md); the short version:

1. System user `fastlogs` (`/var/lib/fastlogs`, `chmod 700`).
2. Deploy user: SSH-only, no root. Its sudo is scoped to running node/npm as `fastlogs` and to `systemctl restart fastlogsd`.
3. Directories `app` / `data` / `blobs` under `/var/lib/fastlogs`, owned by `fastlogs`.
4. Sync `server/` into `/var/lib/fastlogs/app/server`, then `npm ci --omit=dev`.
5. Config `/etc/fastlogs/fastlogs.env` (`chmod 640`, `root:fastlogs`): fill in `ADMIN_TOKEN`, `VIEWER_TOKEN`, `IP_SALT`, `BASE_URL=https://<your-domain>`; point `DATA_DIR` / `BLOB_DIR` at `/var/lib/fastlogs/{data,blobs}`.
6. systemd units: `fastlogsd.service`, `fastlogs-sweeper.service`, `fastlogs-sweeper.timer` -> `daemon-reload`, `enable --now`.
7. nginx reverse proxy (`nginx-fastlogs.conf`, `client_max_body_size 35m` - big enough for `MAX_FILE_BODY_BYTES`, the base64 envelope around a 25 MB file upload), then `nginx -t && reload`.
8. TLS: `certbot --nginx -d <your-domain>`.
9. DNS: an A record for `<your-domain>` pointing at the server IP.

Later deploys run `./deploy/deploy.sh user@host`: rsync the sources, `npm ci --omit=dev`, migrate, restart `fastlogsd.service`.

### Environment variables

Every variable with its default and description lives in [`server/README.md`](../server/README.md) (*Environment variables* + *Advanced tuning*); the annotated file is `server/.env.example`. Not duplicated here.

What you must set in production: `BASE_URL` (the real public URL), `ADMIN_TOKEN`, `VIEWER_TOKEN`, and a random `IP_SALT`. Optional, depending on how you run: `TEAM_INGEST_TOKEN` (one shared ingest secret instead of N per-app ones, see section 3), `COMMAND_TOKEN` (scoped token for agents / CI), `DEFAULT_RETENTION_DAYS` / `MAX_RETENTION_DAYS`.

Two tokens fail in opposite directions when left empty, so read this twice:

- empty `ADMIN_TOKEN` disables the admin tier (nobody can unpin under `UNPIN_REQUIRES_ADMIN`, nobody drives the command channel unless `COMMAND_TOKEN` is set). Fine for dev, wrong for prod.
- empty `VIEWER_TOKEN` **closes** the catalog: `/browse` answers 401 to everyone except the admin token. Fail-closed on purpose.

### Verify the deploy

`GET /api/health` returns `{"ok":true}` (unauthenticated, that is your uptime probe). Then push one real report from a test app end to end. Server tests: `npm test` (see the caveat in `server/README.md` - run `npm test`, not `node --test test/`).

---

## 2. Register a game

Every game is an `appId` row on the server. `add-app.js` creates it and mints its ingest token.

```bash
# With a token (printed once, save it), 30-day retention:
npm run add-app -- mygame "My Game Title" 30
# Open ingest, no token:
npm run add-app -- mygame "My Game Title" --no-token
# Own retention ceiling:
npm run add-app -- mygame "My Game Title" 14 --max-retention 90
# Straight on a systemd deploy (load the env first, or the script writes to the wrong DB):
sudo -u fastlogs bash -c 'set -a; . /etc/fastlogs/fastlogs.env; set +a; exec node /var/lib/fastlogs/app/server/scripts/add-app.js mygame "My Game" 30'
```

Only systemd reads `EnvironmentFile` automatically. A hand-run script without that `. fastlogs.env` line falls back to `DATA_DIR=./data` and silently creates a *second, empty* database next to the code, minting the token into it, so the token you hand the game never authenticates against the real server. Load the env, or use the Docker form above.

A bare call mints a token. You opt out of it with `--no-token`; the bare number is retention days and has nothing to do with tokens.

| Argument / flag | Meaning |
|-----------------|---------|
| `<appId>` | required, `[a-z0-9_-]{2,32}` - this is Project in the catalog |
| `"<name>"` | required, display name |
| `[retentionDays]` | optional, defaults to `DEFAULT_RETENTION_DAYS`, clamped to the app ceiling |
| `--max-retention N` | retention ceiling for this game |
| `--no-token` | open ingest (no token required) |
| `--keep-token` | on update, keep the current token |
| `--disabled` | register it disabled (`enabled=0`) |
| `--token <value>` | use a specific token (still stored as a hash only) |

The token is printed once on stdout. The DB keeps only its sha256 hash, so it is not recoverable - ship it to the client over a secure channel (CI secret, 1Password), never through the repo. The client sends it as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`: in Unity that is `Config > Server > Token`, in GameMaker the `FASTLOGS_TOKEN` macro.

Re-running `add-app` for an existing `appId` updates the row and mints a **new** token by default (the old one dies). Keep the old one with `--keep-token`.

List what you have: `node scripts/list-apps.js`.

---

## 3. Tokens and tiers

Five tiers. They do not nest except where the table says so, and picking the wrong one is how an automation ends up with the keys to the whole instance.

| Tier | Source | What it opens | Who holds it |
|------|--------|---------------|--------------|
| ingest (per app) | minted by `add-app.js`, hash in the DB | `POST /api/logs`, `POST /api/files`, command poll/ack for its own app | the game build |
| team ingest | `TEAM_INGEST_TOKEN` | the same, but valid for **any** app; with `ALLOW_AUTO_REGISTER=1` an unknown `appId` self-registers on first ingest | all builds, when one shared secret beats N per-app ones |
| viewer | `VIEWER_TOKEN` | catalog `/browse`, `/api/search`, `/api/folders`, `/api/await/:appId`, the Redmine button | the team |
| command | `COMMAND_TOKEN` | only `POST /api/commands/:appId`, `GET /api/commands/:appId/result/:cmdId`, `GET /api/commands/:appId/health` | AI agents, CI automation |
| admin | `ADMIN_TOKEN` | superset of viewer and command; plus unpin when `UNPIN_REQUIRES_ADMIN=1` and triage when `TRIAGE_REQUIRES_ADMIN=1` | the service owner |

Short links need no token at all: the id is unguessable, and an invalid one gets a uniform `404`. Pin, and by default unpin and triage, are open by link - that is deliberate, so a tester can pin their own report without asking anyone.

**Give an agent the `COMMAND_TOKEN`, not the `ADMIN_TOKEN`.** The scoped tier drives the command channel and nothing else: no unpin, no triage, no catalog. Admin still works on that channel (it is a superset), which is exactly why you should not hand it out. An empty `COMMAND_TOKEN` means the tier is off and the channel is admin-only.

There is no HTTP delete anywhere in the API, and app management is CLI-only (`scripts/add-app.js` and friends) - neither is reachable with any token.

---

## 4. Sinks (link forwarding)

On every successful ingest the server fires a compact payload at your destinations, asynchronously, without blocking the response. Payload shape: `project, projectName, version, platform, url, title, counts, time` (see `CONTRACT.md` section 5).

Config: global `config/sinks.json` (copy it from `config/sinks.example.json`) plus a per-game override in `apps.sinks_json`. Per-app sinks are added on top of the global list.

```bash
cp server/config/sinks.example.json server/config/sinks.json
# Fill in real webhook URLs / tokens. No real secrets in the repo.
```

Each sink is `{ type, name?, enabled?, filter?, ...type fields }`.

| Type | Key fields | Destination |
|------|------------|-------------|
| `slack` | `url` (webhook), `username`, `iconEmoji`, `mentionOnError` | a Slack channel |
| `discord` | `url` (webhook), `username`, `mentionOnError` | a Discord channel |
| `webhook` | `url`, `method`, `headers`, `bodyTemplate` (`{{project}}`, `{{url}}`, `{{counts.error}}`, `{{json}}`, ...), `retries`, `timeoutMs` | a generic endpoint with a templated body |
| `confluence` | `mode: "webhook"`, `url` (Atlassian automation hook) | a Confluence page via automation |
| `googlesheet` | `url` (Apps Script `/exec`), `secret` | a row in a Google Sheet |

`filter` keeps the noise down: `errorOnly`, `minError`, `minWarn`, `platforms` / `excludePlatforms`, `apps`. Errors only, into the QA channel:

```json
{ "type": "slack", "name": "qa-channel", "enabled": true,
  "filter": { "errorOnly": true },
  "url": "https://hooks.slack.com/services/REPLACE/WITH/REAL",
  "username": "FastLogs", "iconEmoji": ":rotating_light:", "mentionOnError": "<!here>" }
```

A sink failure **never** fails the ingest (it retries, then logs). The file may be a bare array or a `{ "sinks": [...] }` wrapper.

---

## 5. Retention and cleanup

- Default lifetime is `DEFAULT_RETENTION_DAYS`; a per-request `retentionDays` is clamped with `clamp(1, <the app's own ceiling>)`. `MAX_RETENTION_DAYS` is the ceiling a **new** app inherits, not a hard cap on the instance: `add-app --max-retention N` writes a per-app ceiling verbatim and may set it higher. Values live in [`server/README.md`](../server/README.md).
- Per-game lifetime is the `retentionDays` argument to `add-app` (and `--max-retention`). Per-request override: the client sends `retentionDays` (Unity `RetentionDaysOverride`, GameMaker `FASTLOGS_RETENTION_DAYS`).
- Expired rows are dropped by the sweeper: in-process every `SWEEP_INTERVAL_SEC`, plus `npm run sweep` from cron or the `fastlogs-sweeper.timer` unit.
- Pin: `POST /api/logs/<id>/pin` with `{ "pin": true }` sets `expiresAt=null`, so the log never expires. Unpin (`pin:false`) is open by link too, mirroring pin, and recomputes a finite expiry from `created_at`; `UNPIN_REQUIRES_ADMIN=1` puts it behind the admin token.
- There is no HTTP delete. Records leave only via retention (the sweeper) - keep that in mind when someone asks you to "just remove that log".

**Disk monitor.** A watchdog checks how full the `BLOB_DIR` filesystem is and how big `blobs/` has grown, then warns in the server log and optionally POSTs to `DISK_ALERT_WEBHOOK` (one URL works for Slack, Discord, or anything generic). It is on by default: `DISK_MONITOR_INTERVAL_SEC` inherits the sweep cadence, `DISK_ALERT_PCT` is 85, `DISK_ALERT_BYTES` is 0 (the absolute-size check is off). Alerts are edge-triggered, so a full disk will not flood the channel. Interval `0` turns the whole thing off.

---

## 6. Backup

State lives in two places, both outside the webroot:

- `DATA_DIR` (defaults to `./data`, i.e. `server/data`; the systemd walkthrough above points it at `/var/lib/fastlogs/data`) - the SQLite DB: apps, log metadata, token hashes, pin flags.
- `BLOB_DIR` (defaults to `./blobs`, i.e. `server/blobs`; the systemd walkthrough points it at `/var/lib/fastlogs/blobs`) - log bodies (gzip), PNG screenshots, uploaded files.

A backup is a snapshot of both. You cannot just copy the DB file while the server runs: under WAL that gets you a torn snapshot. Use the online backup.

**The script is `scripts/backup.js`** (`npm run backup`): an online SQLite `.backup` from a readonly connection (WAL-safe, no downtime) plus a `tar -czf` of the blob directory. Both land in `<DATA_DIR>/backups` as `fastlogs-<stamp>.db` and `blobs-<stamp>.tar.gz`. It keeps the newest `BACKUP_KEEP` of each (default 7) and prunes the rest, prints one JSON line, and exits 0 on success / 1 on failure.

```bash
# systemd deploy (as the fastlogs user):
sudo -u fastlogs bash -c 'set -a; . /etc/fastlogs/fastlogs.env; set +a; exec node /var/lib/fastlogs/app/server/scripts/backup.js'
# or via npm in the server directory:
npm run backup
# Docker:
docker compose exec -T fastlogs node scripts/backup.js
```

**Schedule it with `fastlogs-backup.timer`** (units in `deploy/fastlogs-backup.{service,timer}`): `daemon-reload`, then `enable --now fastlogs-backup.timer`. It runs daily with a randomized delay and catches up after downtime (`Persistent=true`). Snapshots stay on the same box, so copy `<DATA_DIR>/backups` offsite (rsync / rclone) unless losing the disk is an acceptable outcome.

**Restore (sketch):** stop the service (`docker compose down` or `systemctl stop fastlogsd`), put the chosen `fastlogs-<stamp>.db` in place of `data/fastlogs.db`, untar the matching `blobs-<stamp>.tar.gz` over `blobs/`, fix ownership (`chown -R fastlogs:fastlogs`), start it back up (`docker compose up -d` / `systemctl start fastlogsd`). Take the DB and the blobs from the **same** stamp: a newer DB against older blobs points at bodies that are not there.

---

## 7. Security

Secrets (`ADMIN_TOKEN`, `VIEWER_TOKEN`, `COMMAND_TOKEN`, `IP_SALT`, ingest tokens, deploy keys) belong in `/etc/fastlogs/fastlogs.env` and a password manager, never in the repo (see `.gitignore`). Game tokens are stored as sha256 hashes and handed to clients through a CI secret or 1Password. Rotation is a repeat `add-app` (new token, old one dies), then update the client config. `IP_SALT` must be random, otherwise the IP hashes stop being one-way.

Hand automation the scoped `COMMAND_TOKEN` rather than `ADMIN_TOKEN` (see the tier table in section 3). The agent then cannot unpin, triage, or read the catalog.

The catalog `/browse` sits behind the viewer token and fails closed when `VIEWER_TOKEN` is empty. Logs and screenshots can carry personal data, so short links are unlisted rather than public, an invalid id always gets the same `404` (no enumeration), and retention bounds how long anything lives.

If a deploy key or a token ever leaked, rotate it: a fresh ed25519 key with a passphrase, an unprivileged deploy user with scoped sudo, `PermitRootLogin prohibit-password`. Keep private deploy notes **out** of the repository. Deploy to production only after the service owner signs off.

---

## 8. Troubleshooting

| Symptom | Likely cause | Check |
|---------|--------------|-------|
| Client gets `401 unauthorized` on ingest | the app has a token and the client sent none (or a malformed header) | `Authorization: Bearer <token>`; `node scripts/list-apps.js` to see whether the app is tokenless |
| Client gets `403 forbidden` on ingest | wrong token, disabled app, or an unknown `appId` without auto-register | re-run `add-app` (mints a new token), check `enabled`, or set `TEAM_INGEST_TOKEN` + `ALLOW_AUTO_REGISTER=1` |
| `/browse` returns 401 for the whole team | `VIEWER_TOKEN` is empty (fail-closed) or wrong | set `VIEWER_TOKEN`, restart, re-share the link |
| Short links point at `localhost` | `BASE_URL` was never set | set `BASE_URL=https://<your-domain>` and restart (existing rows are unaffected, links are built at read time) |
| Reports arrive, nothing lands in Slack/Discord | `config/sinks.json` missing, `enabled:false`, or a `filter` eating them | check the server log for sink errors (they never fail the ingest) and the `filter` block |
| Commands sit queued and never ack | nothing is polling: the build is not running or has no ingest token | `GET /api/commands/<appId>/health` - `pollingNow:false` means no build is there, so it is not the handler hanging |
| Command result comes back as `{"__fastlogsResultRef":...}` | the result did not fit inline, so the client uploaded it as a file and acked a link instead | fetch the URL (the MCP server dereferences it for you). Raising `COMMAND_MAX_RESULT_BYTES` will **not** stop it: the Unity client decides locally, at its own fixed 60000-byte threshold, and never reads the server's cap |
| Report rejected or a field silently missing | a size cap: `MAX_PAYLOAD_BYTES` (8 MB body), `MAX_LOG_BYTES`, `MAX_SCREENSHOT_BYTES`, `MAX_SCENE_CONTEXT_BYTES` (oversized scene context is dropped, the report still lands), `MAX_SCREENSHOTS` | server log + the caps table in `server/README.md`; also `client_max_body_size` in nginx |
| File upload fails but logs work | `/api/files` has its own budget (`MAX_FILE_BYTES`, `MAX_FILE_BODY_BYTES`) and nginx caps the body | raise both, remembering base64 inflates the body by ~4/3 |
| Disk filling up | pinned logs never expire; the sweeper only touches expired, unpinned rows | catalog size dashboard, pinned counts, `DISK_ALERT_PCT` warnings in the log |
| `npm test` explodes with `MODULE_NOT_FOUND` | you ran `node --test test/` | run `npm test` (see `server/README.md`) |

For anything else: `journalctl -u fastlogsd -f` on systemd, `docker compose logs -f fastlogs` on Docker, and `GET /api/health` to tell "the server is down" from "the proxy is down".

```

`docs/GAMEMAKER.md`:

```md
# FastLogs - GameMaker integrator guide

Everything you need to wire FastLogs into a GameMaker game. The client is a set of `scr_fastlogs_*` scripts plus one object, `obj_fastlogs_controller`. On top sits a flat API prefixed `fastlogs_*`, with a short alias `flog`.

Want the project overview first? Start with the [README](../README.md). Exact signatures live in [PUBLIC-API.md](../gamemaker/PUBLIC-API.md), GM project-format notes in [GM-NOTES.md](../gamemaker/GM-NOTES.md), the request body in [CONTRACT.md](../CONTRACT.md). The Unity counterpart is [UNITY.md](./UNITY.md).

---

## What you get

From a running game the tester fires one gesture or hotkey and ships the log ring, a device slice, context and breadcrumbs, optionally a screenshot. Back comes a short link to the browser viewer. Crashes send themselves: an unhandled exception is written to disk first, then uploaded, so if the game dies before the request completes the report still arrives on the next launch.

One server serves both the Unity and GameMaker clients. The contract is shared.

One caveat before you start: the GameMaker client is less proven than the Unity one. It is written in full and reviewed against the contract, but no GameMaker game has reported to a production server yet, so nothing in this guide has been confirmed by a real run. The Unity client has. Expect rough edges on first import: the client still carries `// TODO verify` notes on points nobody could check on a live runtime (gamepad constants on consoles, `http_set_connect_timeout` behaviour, `keyboard_string` on consoles, HTML5 `surface_save`). Server-side crash grouping for GameMaker logs was broken until v0.5.3; it is fixed, but no GameMaker crash has been through it. Treat your first integration as a shakedown and report what breaks.

---

## 1. Install

### 1.1. Copy the resources

There is no `.yymps` yet, so resources move by hand. Copy into your project:

- all fourteen `scr_fastlogs_*` scripts: `core`, `recorder`, `device`, `payload`, `http`, `overlay`, `input`, `clipboard`, `screenshot`, `snapshot`, `context`, `files`, `util`, `config`;
- the object `obj_fastlogs_controller` with **all seven** of its events.

Miss an event and you lose a feature silently. The full list:

| Event | Type/Num | What it does |
|-------|----------|--------------|
| `Create` | 0/0 | Boots state, ring, persistence, exception handler. |
| `Step` | 3/0 | Polls input (hotkeys, gesture, overlay text fields). |
| `Alarm 0` | 2/0 | One-second tick for the deferred retry. |
| `Draw GUI` | 8/64 | Draws the overlay and the toast. |
| `Draw GUI End` | 8/65 | **Captures the screenshot frame.** Skip it and reports ship without a PNG, with no error. |
| `Async HTTP` | 7/62 | Parses ingest responses, drives retry and toasts. |
| `Async Save/Load` | 7/63 | Nothing today. No FastLogs path raises it: the screenshot and the recorder both write synchronously. Kept as a stub for future async file IO, so add it to match the reference object. |

The skeleton project `gamemaker/FastLogsGM.yyp` is a reference layout. Once a `.yymps` exists, import will go through `Tools > Import Local Package`.

### 1.2. The `debug` build config

Create a build config named `debug`. The name must match the config name in your `.yyp` exactly, because gating hangs off it (section 12). The `FastLogsGM` skeleton already has it.

---

## 2. Configuration

Most settings are macros in `scr_fastlogs_config`. Defaults are neutral and empty, no secrets inside. Open the script and set the server at minimum.

Some macros live next to their feature instead of in `config` - see 2.7 for the full map.

### 2.1. Minimum to start

```gml
#macro FASTLOGS_ENDPOINT      "https://logs.example.com/api/logs"  // <BASE_URL>/api/logs
#macro FASTLOGS_APP_ID        "mygame"    // [a-z0-9_-]{2,32}, = Project in the catalog
#macro FASTLOGS_TOKEN         ""          // ingest token, or "" for no header
#macro FASTLOGS_APP_VERSION   ""          // "" -> GM_version is used, or set it yourself
```

These four are macros only. There is no runtime override for them (see section 3). If `FASTLOGS_ENDPOINT` is empty, `fastlogs_send` does nothing and returns `false`: it prints `[FastLogs] send skipped: FASTLOGS_ENDPOINT is empty` and raises an "endpoint not set" error toast on every call, not just the first one.

| Macro | Default | Why |
|-------|---------|-----|
| `FASTLOGS_ENDPOINT` | `""` | Full ingest URL, `<BASE_URL>/api/logs`. Required. |
| `FASTLOGS_APP_ID` | `""` | Game id in the catalog, `[a-z0-9_-]{2,32}`. |
| `FASTLOGS_TOKEN` | `""` | Ingest token, sent as `Authorization: Bearer`. Empty means no header. |
| `FASTLOGS_APP_VERSION` | `""` | Build version. Empty falls back to `GM_version`. |
| `FASTLOGS_TESTER` | `""` | Tester name, rides along in every report. Empty means the field is omitted. |
| `FASTLOGS_RETENTION_DAYS` | `-1` | Retention for the record, in days. `-1` omits the field and lets the server decide. |
| `FASTLOGS_FILES_ENDPOINT` | `""` | File upload URL. Empty derives it from the ingest URL (section 8). |

### 2.2. Log recording

| Macro | Default | Why |
|-------|---------|-----|
| `FASTLOGS_RING_SIZE` | `2000` | How many recent lines the in-memory ring holds. |
| `FASTLOGS_AUTO_START_RECORDING` | `false` | Start writing to disk at boot. |
| `FASTLOGS_PERSIST_ENABLED` | `true` | Keep the log across sessions (rolling file under `game_save_id`). |
| `FASTLOGS_PERSIST_DIR` | `"fastlogs"` | Folder for the log, ini, pending queue and temp PNG. |
| `FASTLOGS_PERSIST_MAX_BYTES` | `1048576` (1 MB) | Log file size before rotation. |
| `FASTLOGS_PERSIST_FLUSH_SECONDS` | `2` | Batch flush interval. `0` writes every line immediately. |
| `FASTLOGS_PERSIST_FLUSH_MAX_BYTES` | `65536` (64 KB) | Hard batch cap: flush early rather than grow past this. |
| `FASTLOGS_MAX_LOG_BYTES` | `2000000` (~2 MB) | Truncation threshold for the log text in a report (marked when cut). |
| `FASTLOGS_LOG_ENCODING` | `"plain"` | Log encoding in the payload. Kept `plain` everywhere. |

Disk writes are batched on a timer rather than done per line, which keeps heavy logging off the filesystem. The batch is force-flushed before a send and on a crash.

### 2.3. Input and gestures

| Macro | Default | Script | Why |
|-------|---------|--------|-----|
| `FASTLOGS_HOTKEY_TOGGLE` | `vk_f8` | config | Opens/closes the overlay. |
| `FASTLOGS_HOTKEY_QUICK_SEND` | `vk_f9` | config | Sends immediately, no overlay. |
| `FASTLOGS_GP_TOGGLE` | `gp_select` | config | Gamepad overlay toggle (consoles). |
| `FASTLOGS_GP_QUICK_SEND` | `gp_start` | config | Gamepad quick send. |
| `FASTLOGS_GESTURE_TOUCHES` | `3` | input | Simultaneous touches that open the overlay. |
| `FASTLOGS_TOUCH_SLOTS` | `5` | input | Touch slots polled per step. |
| `FASTLOGS_GP_SLOT` | `0` | input | Gamepad slot polled for the hotkeys. |

With no keyboard the overlay opens on a three-finger tap. Keep the quick-send key different from the toggle: if they match, the toggle wins and quick send is skipped.

### 2.4. Screenshot

| Macro | Default | Script | Why |
|-------|---------|--------|-----|
| `FASTLOGS_SCREENSHOT_DEFAULT` | `false` | config | Attach a screenshot by default. |
| `FASTLOGS_SCREENSHOT_TMP` | `"fastlogs_shot_tmp.png"` | config | Temp PNG name inside the persist folder. |
| `FASTLOGS_SHOT_MAX_SIDE` | `1280` | screenshot | Longest side after downscale, in pixels. |

The frame comes off `application_surface`: it gets drawn stretched into a temp surface capped at `FASTLOGS_SHOT_MAX_SIDE` on the long side (aspect preserved), then `surface_save` writes a PNG, `buffer_load` reads it back and `buffer_base64_encode` produces the base64 that rides in the payload. The temp PNG is deleted right after.

If the app surface is switched off, capture falls back to `screen_save` at full screen size, with no downscale. That is the only path that ignores `FASTLOGS_SHOT_MAX_SIDE`, so the PNG can get heavy - the contract caps screenshots at roughly 2 MB.

Capture happens in `Draw GUI End` and nowhere else. On HTML5 it does not run at all (section 13).

### 2.5. Sending and retry

| Macro | Default | Script | Why |
|-------|---------|--------|-----|
| `FASTLOGS_COPY_ON_SEND` | `true` | config | Copy the short link to the clipboard after a successful send. |
| `FASTLOGS_HTTP_TIMEOUT_MS` | `15000` | config | Connection timeout, ms. |
| `FASTLOGS_HTTP_MAX_RETRIES` | `2` | http | Immediate retries of the same body before the deferred retry takes over. |
| `FASTLOGS_RETRY_INTERVAL_SEC` | `30` | config | Deferred retry interval on a transient failure. `0` disables it. |
| `FASTLOGS_RETRY_MAX` | `0` | config | Cap on deferred retries. `0` means no cap. |

Failure escalates in two stages. First the uploader retries the already-built body immediately, up to `FASTLOGS_HTTP_MAX_RETRIES` times. That is why you see "Sending... (retry)" before any countdown appears. Only when those are exhausted does the report go onto the deferred retry, which ticks on the controller's `Alarm 0` once a second and re-posts every `FASTLOGS_RETRY_INTERVAL_SEC`.

A `4xx` from the server ends it: that is a final rejection (bad token, malformed appId, body too large) and no retry helps. Transient failures (no network, `5xx`) keep retrying and arrive on their own.

### 2.6. Crashes and privacy

| Macro | Default | Why |
|-------|---------|-----|
| `FASTLOGS_AUTOSEND_ON_EXCEPTION` | `true` | Send a report on an unhandled exception. |
| `FASTLOGS_AUTOSEND_PATTERNS` | `[]` | Log markers that trigger an auto-send (section 10). |
| `FASTLOGS_AUTOSEND_THROTTLE_SECONDS` | `30` | Minimum gap between auto-deliveries. |
| `FASTLOGS_AUTOSEND_SESSION_LIMIT` | `10` | Auto-deliveries allowed per run. |
| `FASTLOGS_AUTOSEND_DEDUP` | `true` | Suppress the same stack within `throttle * 2`. |
| `FASTLOGS_PENDING_DIR` | `"pending"` | Crash queue folder inside the persist dir. |
| `FASTLOGS_PENDING_MAX` | `5` | Crashes kept in the disk queue. |
| `FASTLOGS_PENDING_RESEND_PER_START` | `5` | Pending reports re-sent per launch. |
| `FASTLOGS_SCRUB_PII` | `true` | Scrub PII before sending (email, IP, tokens, long digit runs). |
| `FASTLOGS_REDACT_PLACEHOLDER` | `"[redacted]"` | Replacement for a scrubbed fragment. |
| `FASTLOGS_REDACT_MIN_DIGITS` | `9` | Digit-run length that counts as PII. |
| `FASTLOGS_INCLUDE_SENSITIVE` | `false` | Declared but **not read anywhere** - see section 11. |
| `FASTLOGS_MAX_FILE_BYTES` | `26214400` (25 MB) | Cap on one file/archive blob. |

More on crashes in section 10, on privacy in section 11.

### 2.7. Macros that live outside `config`

Everything feature-specific sits next to its feature. If a macro is not in `scr_fastlogs_config`, look here:

| Script | Macros |
|--------|--------|
| `scr_fastlogs_core` | `FASTLOGS_LEVEL_LOG`, `FASTLOGS_LEVEL_WARN`, `FASTLOGS_LEVEL_ERROR` |
| `scr_fastlogs_http` | `FASTLOGS_HTTP_MAX_RETRIES` |
| `scr_fastlogs_screenshot` | `FASTLOGS_SHOT_MAX_SIDE` |
| `scr_fastlogs_overlay` | `FASTLOGS_COMMENT_MAX`, `FASTLOGS_TESTER_MAX` |
| `scr_fastlogs_snapshot` | `FASTLOGS_SNAPSHOT_INCLUDE_PERSISTENT`, `FASTLOGS_MAX_SNAPSHOT_BYTES` |
| `scr_fastlogs_input` | `FASTLOGS_GESTURE_TOUCHES`, `FASTLOGS_TOUCH_SLOTS`, `FASTLOGS_GP_SLOT` |

---

## 3. Init

Drop an instance of `obj_fastlogs_controller` in the very first room (it is `persistent` and survives room changes), or call `fastlogs_init()` early in your bootstrap. The function creates the controller if it does not exist, brings up the ring, counters and persistence, and registers the exception handler. It is idempotent: calling it twice does not spawn a second controller. It returns the controller instance id, or `noone` when the client is disabled.

```gml
fastlogs_init();

// or with a runtime override:
fastlogs_init({
    tester:             "alex",
    autoStartRecording: false,
    screenshot:         true
});
```

**The override only covers keys the code actually reads:**

| Key | Overrides | Read in |
|-----|-----------|---------|
| `tester` | `FASTLOGS_TESTER` | payload, overlay |
| `scrubPii` | `FASTLOGS_SCRUB_PII` | util |
| `screenshot` | `FASTLOGS_SCREENSHOT_DEFAULT` | core |
| `autoStartRecording` | `FASTLOGS_AUTO_START_RECORDING` | core |
| `autosendPatterns` | `FASTLOGS_AUTOSEND_PATTERNS` | core |
| `sessionId` | generated GUID | core |
| `comment` | comment for the crash fallback body only, never for a normal send | core |
| `snapshotIncludePersistent` | `FASTLOGS_SNAPSHOT_INCLUDE_PERSISTENT` | snapshot |

`fastlogs_init` merges any struct you hand it, but **nothing reads `endpoint`, `appId`, `token`, `appVersion` or `retentionDays` from it.** Sending pulls those macros directly. Set the server in `scr_fastlogs_config` (section 2.1); passing `{ endpoint: ... }` to `fastlogs_init` silently does nothing and you end up staring at `send skipped: FASTLOGS_ENDPOINT is empty`.

Every function is safe to call before `fastlogs_init`. They no-op or lazily initialize, so call order will not bite you.

---

## 4. Logging

`flog(message, [level])` is the main logging entry point; `fastlogs_log` is a wrapper that pins the level. The fastest way to pour FastLogs into an existing project is one line in your `show_debug_message` wrapper:

```gml
function trace(_msg) {
    show_debug_message(_msg);
    flog(_msg);                 // your whole existing log now also goes to FastLogs
}
```

Levels:

```gml
flog("Checkpoint reached");                   // FASTLOGS_LEVEL_LOG (default)
flog("Low memory", FASTLOGS_LEVEL_WARN);
fastlogs_error("Spawner failed");             // wrapper with a fixed level
```

Level constants live in `scr_fastlogs_core`: `FASTLOGS_LEVEL_LOG` = 0, `FASTLOGS_LEVEL_WARN` = 1, `FASTLOGS_LEVEL_ERROR` = 2. The wrappers `fastlogs_log` / `fastlogs_warn` / `fastlogs_error` are the same `flog` with the level pinned. Each entry bumps its level's session counter (visible in the overlay and via `fastlogs_get_counts`).

Every `flog` goes into the in-memory ring as long as the client is enabled. Recording only controls the **disk** copy, never the ring, and never whether you can send.

`fastlogs_clear()` wipes the in-memory ring and the session counters. It does not touch the log file on disk - for that use `fastlogs_record_clear()` (section 5).

---

## 5. Recording and persistence

Recording is the cross-session history, not a precondition for sending. Off by default:

```gml
fastlogs_record_start();      // on: new flog lines also go to disk
fastlogs_record_stop();       // off: what is already on disk stays
fastlogs_record_set(true);    // explicit set
fastlogs_is_recording();      // -> bool
fastlogs_record_clear();      // wipe the persisted log (memory + rolling file)
```

The recording flag is persisted in an ini, so it survives a restart. To record from boot, turn on `FASTLOGS_AUTO_START_RECORDING`.

What ends up in the report: if the persisted text is non-empty, that is the log text (it carries previous sessions plus the current one). Otherwise the report falls back to a snapshot of the in-memory ring. Either way you get a log.

---

## 6. Sending a report

```gml
fastlogs_set_screenshot(true);   // attach a screenshot to the next send
var ok = fastlogs_send({ title: "Crash on load", comment: "after the boss fight" });
```

`fastlogs_send([opts])` builds the contract payload and `POST`s it to `FASTLOGS_ENDPOINT`. It returns `true` when the request is queued and `false` on a no-op (client disabled, no endpoint, a send already in flight, or a report still waiting on the deferred retry - with `FASTLOGS_RETRY_MAX` at its default `0` that wait has no cap, so a report that cannot reach the server blocks new sends until it lands). The send is async: the response is parsed in the Async HTTP event, and a `201` fills in the link.

`opts` fields, all optional:

| Field | Type | What it does |
|-------|------|--------------|
| `title` | string (<=120) | Report title. |
| `comment` | string (<=4000) | Free-form description from the tester. Empty is omitted. |
| `retentionDays` | int | Retention for this record. |
| `screenshot` | bool | Override the screenshot toggle for this call. |
| `extraDevice` | struct | Extra fields for the device section. |

The tester name does not go through `opts`. It comes from `FASTLOGS_TESTER`, or `fastlogs_init({ tester })`, or the overlay field, and rides along automatically.

Useful getters:

```gml
fastlogs_is_sending();          // -> bool, is a send in flight right now
fastlogs_retry_is_pending();    // -> bool, is a report waiting on the deferred retry
fastlogs_last_url();            // URL of the last successful log ("" if none)
fastlogs_get_counts();          // -> struct { error, warn, log } for the session
fastlogs_session_id();          // GUID of this run (groups reports in the viewer)
```

### Quick send

`fastlogs_quick_send([opts])` ships the current log immediately, no overlay. Handy on a hotkey (`FASTLOGS_HOTKEY_QUICK_SEND`, default F9) or from code. It tags the report `Quick send` unless you set your own title, and marks it as code-initiated. If not a single `flog` has happened this session, it refuses rather than send an empty report: you get a "nothing to send" toast and `false`.

### Overlay

```gml
fastlogs_open();
fastlogs_close();
fastlogs_toggle();
fastlogs_is_open();   // -> bool
```

The overlay is drawn with primitives in Draw GUI. It opens on `FASTLOGS_HOTKEY_TOGGLE` (default F8), the gamepad button, or the touch gesture.

The overlay ships hardcoded Russian labels and has no localization hook, so an English-speaking tester sees Russian buttons. The names below are what is actually on screen.

Main panel, top to bottom:

- a **Настройки** (Settings) button (top right, opens the settings panel);
- **E / W / L** counters, colored per level;
- a **Тестер** (Tester) field, editable and required, pre-seeded from `FASTLOGS_TESTER` or the init override;
- a **Комментарий** (Comment) field, editable and multiline;
- an **Отправить** (Send) button plus a **Скриншот** (Screenshot) toggle;
- the last **URL** plus a **Копировать** (Copy) button;
- a **REC** indicator with **Start Rec** / **Stop Rec**, and **Clear**.

There is no title field. The overlay only sends `comment`; set `title` from code if you want one.

Send is disabled while the tester name is blank. That gate applies to manual overlay sends only. Code sends, crash sends and auto-sends are never blocked on a name.

**Typing:** both fields share GameMaker's single `keyboard_string`, so focus is mutually exclusive - clicking one field drops the other. Enter in **Тестер** finishes the entry (single line). Enter in **Комментарий** inserts a newline. Esc drops focus from whichever field has it. While a field has focus, hotkeys and gestures are suppressed so typing an "f8" does not close the overlay.

There is no QR code in the GameMaker overlay. That one is Unity only.

### Settings panel

Behind the **Настройки** (Settings) button. Same as the main panel, the labels here are the ones on screen:

| Control | What it does |
|---------|--------------|
| Endpoint | Read-only display of `FASTLOGS_ENDPOINT`, or `(не задан)` when empty. |
| AppId | Read-only display of `FASTLOGS_APP_ID`, or `(не задан)` when empty. |
| Скриншот в payload (Screenshot in payload) | Same toggle as on the main panel. Persisted in the ini. |
| Autosend | **Dead control.** Toggles and persists a flag nothing reads. |
| Чистка PII (PII scrub) | Toggles `scrubPii`. Persisted in the ini. Real. |
| Recording: ON/OFF | Same as Start/Stop Rec. |
| Буфер -/+ (Buffer) | **Dead control.** Moves a displayed number in steps of 500; the ring size never changes. |
| Clear | Wipes the ring and the session counters. |

`Endpoint:`, `AppId:`, `Autosend`, `Recording: ON/OFF` and `Clear` are genuinely English on screen; the rest are Russian. The panel is titled `Настройки FastLogs` and closes on a `Закрыть` (Close) button.

The two dead controls are a known gap: their state is stored in the ini and shown back to you, but no consumer exists. `Autosend` does not gate crash auto-send (that is `FASTLOGS_AUTOSEND_ON_EXCEPTION`), and `Буфер` does not resize the ring (that is `FASTLOGS_RING_SIZE`). Do not trust them in a test session.

### Toast status

Even with the overlay closed the tester sees send status as a toast over the game. These toast strings are Russian, like most of the overlay:

"Отправка..." (Sending) holds while the request is in flight, and becomes "Отправка... (повтор)" (Sending, retry) while an automatic retry is in flight. On success the toast reads "Готово (ссылка скопирована)" (Done, link copied), or just "Готово" when the copy did not happen or the server returned no link. On failure it reads "Ошибка: <причина>" (Error: cause), where the cause is "сеть недоступна" (network unreachable), "HTTP <code>", or "неизвестно" (unknown).

A success toast that carries a link shows it on a second line with the hint "(нажмите - копировать)" (click to copy); clicking anywhere on the toast copies the link. A terminal error toast shows a second line reading "Нажмите, чтобы повторить отправку" (Click to retry the send); clicking anywhere on the toast resends. In both cases the whole toast is the click target, not a separate button.

| Macro | Default | Why |
|-------|---------|-----|
| `FASTLOGS_TOAST_ENABLED` | `true` | Show status toasts at all. |
| `FASTLOGS_TOAST_SECONDS` | `3` | Duration of short toasts (success/info). |
| `FASTLOGS_TOAST_ERROR_SECONDS` | `6` | Duration of an error toast (longer, to read the cause). |

---

## 7. Context and breadcrumbs

Context and breadcrumbs answer "what was the player doing, what state were they in, and how did they get to the bug". Both ride with every report and every crash, and show up as separate Context and Breadcrumbs sections in the viewer.

### Context

Context is a set of key -> value pairs, a snapshot of "right now". Put in what you want to search and group bugs by: player id, level, mode, feature flags.

```gml
fastlogs_set_context("playerId", global.player_id);
fastlogs_set_context("level", "3-2");
fastlogs_remove_context("level");   // drop one key
fastlogs_clear_context();           // drop everything
```

Context accumulates and overwrites per key: set it once on a level change and it rides along with any later report. Limits are clamped client-side: key <= 64 chars, value <= 512. Do not stuff essays in here, that is what breadcrumbs are for.

### Breadcrumbs

A breadcrumb is a short note about an event ("opened shop", "tapped buy"). They land in a rolling buffer of the last 100, carry their timestamp and level, and render as a timeline in the viewer - a reconstruction of the last steps before the fall.

```gml
fastlogs_breadcrumb("Opened shop");
fastlogs_breadcrumb("Purchase tapped", "warn");
fastlogs_breadcrumb("Save load failed", FASTLOGS_LEVEL_ERROR);
fastlogs_clear_breadcrumbs();   // rarely needed, the ring evicts on its own
```

`level` is optional (default `"info"`) and takes `"info"|"warn"|"error"` or `FASTLOGS_LEVEL_*`. Text is clamped to 512 chars. Record the key transitions (screen, stage, important action, external call), not every frame.

---

## 8. Files and folders

Separately from a log report you can ship an arbitrary file or folder and get a short download link. The typical case: pull a save off the tester's device. Transport is JSON plus base64 to a separate `POST /api/files`.

```gml
fastlogs_send_file(save_path, { title: "Save before the crash", kind: "save" });
fastlogs_send_folder(save_dir, { title: "All saves" });   // zipped into one .zip
fastlogs_send_files([path_a, path_b], { title: "Repro set" });
```

All three return a `bool` immediately: `true` means queued, `false` means no-op (client disabled, missing file/folder, empty blob, over the cap, no endpoint, another send in flight, or a log report still waiting on the deferred retry). The outcome arrives later, as a toast and in the `onDone` callback.

Shared `opts`, all optional: `title` (<=120), `logId` (bind to a log report so the upload lands in the viewer's Attachments panel), `groupId` (group label), `mime` (override the type), `kind` (attachment kind, defaults to `"file"` for a file and `"folder"` for a folder), `retentionDays`, `name` (name inside the archive/catalog), `onDone(result)`.

Valid `kind` values are fixed by the server - see [CONTRACT.md](../CONTRACT.md) and [PUBLIC-API.md](../gamemaker/PUBLIC-API.md) for the current set. An unknown kind is not an error; the server collapses it to null.

The `result` struct in `onDone`:

```gml
{
    success:     true,           // bool
    id:          "abc123",       // string
    url:         "https://.../files/abc123",           // short viewer link
    downloadUrl: "https://.../files/abc123/download",   // direct blob link
    statusCode:  201,            // HTTP code (0 if the request never landed)
    error:       ""              // "" on success, "network" or "http_<code>" on failure
}
```

A folder is zipped client-side with STORE (no compression) into one `.zip`, directory structure preserved. If packing fails, the send degrades to a group upload: each file goes as its own upload under a shared `groupId`, so the operation lands even without an archive. The size cap is `FASTLOGS_MAX_FILE_BYTES` (default 25 MB), measured on the resulting `.zip` for a folder.

PII scrubbing is not applied to file bytes or file names. The file goes as-is, deliberately. Only the size is checked.

The files endpoint is derived from the main one by swapping `/api/logs` for `/api/files`. If your base paths differ, set `FASTLOGS_FILES_ENDPOINT` explicitly.

---

## 9. Full game snapshot

`fastlogs_send_snapshot([opts])` does two things in one call: it sends a normal log report (logs, context, breadcrumbs, device slice, optional screenshot) and packs the game's saves into a `snapshot.zip` attached to that same record. Result: one record that is a readable report plus a Download snapshot.zip button in the viewer.

```gml
fastlogs_send_snapshot({ title: "Bug after load", comment: "progress reset" });
```

`opts` passes through to `fastlogs_send` (`title`, `comment`, `screenshot`, `retentionDays`, `extraDevice`), plus snapshot-specific fields: `includePersistent`, `snapshotName` (default `"snapshot.zip"`), `snapshotTitle`, `onSnapshotDone(result)`. Returns a `bool` like `fastlogs_send`. The archive is built and sent only after the log report succeeds, because the server assigns the `logId` it binds to. If the report never lands, the attachment is not sent - there is nothing to bind it to.

The archive carries saves and registered data, not the FastLogs logs themselves (those are already the report body). The FastLogs folder (log, ini, crash queue, temp PNG) is excluded.

The default source is the whole save folder (`game_save_id`), recursively. Turn that off globally with `fastlogs_init({ snapshotIncludePersistent: false })`, or per call with `opts.includePersistent`. Add your own sources:

```gml
fastlogs_add_snapshot_source(path);              // extra source folder
fastlogs_add_snapshot_data(name, buffer_or_b64); // in-memory data as an archive file
fastlogs_remove_snapshot_source(path);
fastlogs_clear_snapshot_sources();
```

On platforms where `game_save_id` does not enumerate (consoles, HTML5) the default source comes out empty - register sources by hand. Saves can hold personal data; like files, the blob is not scrubbed, only size-capped (`FASTLOGS_MAX_SNAPSHOT_BYTES`, which tracks `FASTLOGS_MAX_FILE_BYTES`).

---

## 10. Crashes: capture and delivery

A hard crash kills the process before an HTTP send can finish. The model is simple: always capture, deliver at the first opportunity.

Every unhandled exception is written synchronously to a disk queue (the `pending` folder inside `game_save_id`) and only then does a send get attempted. A successful send deletes the file. If the process dies before the HTTP completes, the file stays, and on the next launch `fastlogs_init` scans the queue and ships the unsent crashes from previous sessions.

A crash loop does not breed files: capture is deduped by stack signature, and the `FASTLOGS_PENDING_MAX` cap (default 5) bounds the queue. The safety valves limit delivery rate, not capture: the throttle (`FASTLOGS_AUTOSEND_THROTTLE_SECONDS`, default 30 s) and the session limit (`FASTLOGS_AUTOSEND_SESSION_LIMIT`, default 10) only decide whether to send right now. A report skipped by them goes out on the next launch. If the server rejects a report for good (`4xx`), the file is deleted - resending is pointless. Transient failures (no network, `5xx`) stay on disk and retry later.

Crash auto-send is on by default (`FASTLOGS_AUTOSEND_ON_EXCEPTION = true`), goes without a screenshot, and carries context plus breadcrumbs. In retail and on consoles the tool is gated off and crashes are not sent, by design (section 12).

Short version: on a crash you do nothing. The report is already on the server or will arrive next launch. Open your game's `/browse` catalog.

### Auto-send by pattern

`FASTLOGS_AUTOSEND_PATTERNS` (default `[]`, off) is a list of marker strings. If a `flog` matches one of them, a report goes out automatically under the same throttle and session limit as crashes. Handy for catching markers like `[CRITICAL]` without a manual send.

GML has no regex, so matching is a case-insensitive substring plus a minimal glob via `*` at the edges: `"foo"` contains foo, `"*foo"` ends with foo, `"foo*"` starts with foo. A `*` in the middle is literal. You can set it at runtime: `fastlogs_init({ autosendPatterns: ["[CRITICAL]", "*FATAL"] })`.

---

## 11. Privacy

Out of the box the minimum goes out, and potential PII is cut before sending. Turning the scrub off is your deliberate call.

The scrub (`FASTLOGS_SCRUB_PII = true`) runs redaction over log text, context values and breadcrumb texts, both when building a report and when writing a crash: email, IPv4, IPv6, Bearer tokens and long digit runs (9+ digits, `FASTLOGS_REDACT_MIN_DIGITS`) become `[redacted]`.

The client never sends the tester's raw IP. The server takes the IP from the connection and stores it as salt plus `sha256`. The GM device script does not collect known-sensitive identifiers in the first place. Screenshots are off by default and only taken on explicit request.

```gml
fastlogs_init({ scrubPii: false });          // turn the scrub off
fastlogs_redact(text);                       // apply redaction to a string
fastlogs_redact_rules_set([...]);            // replace the rule set
fastlogs_redact_default_rules();             // the default set [{name, matcher}]
```

**`FASTLOGS_INCLUDE_SENSITIVE` does nothing today.** It is declared in `scr_fastlogs_config` for parity with the Unity client, but no script reads it, and there is no `includeSensitive` init key either. Flipping it changes nothing: the device slice is assembled the same way regardless. Treat it as a placeholder until a gate exists in device/payload.

The scrub toggle is also in the overlay settings panel (persisted in the ini). GML has no regex, so the rules are hand-written string scanners. The scrub is best-effort, not a guarantee: it can over-redact (a long number goes to `[redacted]`) or miss a non-standard secret format. Do not log secrets on purpose, and keep context to identifiers rather than private details.

---

## 12. Gating for consoles and release

In retail and on consoles FastLogs must stay inert, so it does not trip certification or fire off stray network calls. Gating hangs off `FASTLOGS_ENABLED`, set per build config:

```gml
#macro Default:FASTLOGS_ENABLED false   // release: client OFF, nothing runs
#macro debug:FASTLOGS_ENABLED true      // debug: client ON
```

Config-scope syntax is picky: no space after the colon (`Default:FASTLOGS_ENABLED`, not `Default: FASTLOGS_ENABLED`). The name `debug` must match the build config name in your `.yyp`.

With `!FASTLOGS_ENABLED` every public function takes a safe early exit (no-op), `fastlogs_init` returns `noone` without creating the controller, and a room-placed controller destroys itself in its Create event. Nothing in FastLogs then calls `http_request` or `screen_save` at runtime. Be precise about what that buys you: the scripts are still compiled into the build, so a static scan of the binary can still find those symbols - the gate guarantees they never execute, not that they are absent. Embedding `flog`, `fastlogs_set_context` and `fastlogs_breadcrumb` in game code is safe: in production they do nothing.

---

## 13. HTML5 limits

- **Screenshot.** Capture bails out immediately on a browser target (`os_browser` check) and returns an empty base64. The report goes without a PNG.
- **Files and snapshot.** There is no path-based filesystem access on HTML5, same as Unity WebGL. `game_save_id` does not enumerate, so the default snapshot source is empty - register data via `fastlogs_add_snapshot_data`.
- **Copying the link.** On the web, clipboard access needs a user gesture and may fail. The client does not crash; the overlay keeps the **Копировать** (Copy) button as the fallback.
- **Log encoding.** The body ships `plain` (no gzip) to avoid preflight and CORS.

---

## 14. What the GameMaker client does not have

The GameMaker client is the reporting half of FastLogs. **None of the agent-facing features exist here.** They are Unity only:

| Feature | Where it exists | GM |
|---------|-----------------|-----|
| Command channel (agent queues a command, game polls and acks) | server + Unity client | no |
| Scene-context snapshot (`fastlogs.scenecontext`) | Unity client | no |
| Component capture (`fastlogs.capture`) | Unity client | no |
| Component inspection (`fastlogs.inspect`) | Unity client | no |
| Command -> report correlation | server + Unity client | no |
| Headless send (`FastLogs.send` / `SendHeadlessAsync`) | Unity client | no |
| MCP tools | server + Unity client | no |

There are no command-registration functions in the GM API and nothing in `gamemaker/**` polls the command channel. If you came here from the README or [UNITY.md](./UNITY.md) and expected `scenecontext` or `inspect`, they are not in the GameMaker client at all. Plans are in [TODO.md](../TODO.md).

The overlay also has no QR code (Unity only), and two settings-panel controls are inert (section 6).

---

## 15. Public function reference

Exact signatures and edge cases are in [PUBLIC-API.md](../gamemaker/PUBLIC-API.md).

| Function | Short |
|----------|-------|
| `fastlogs_init([cfg])` | Bring the client up, return instance id or `noone`. |
| `flog(msg, [level])` / `fastlogs_log` | Write a log line. |
| `fastlogs_warn(msg)` / `fastlogs_error(msg)` | Write at a fixed level. |
| `fastlogs_clear()` | Clear the ring and the session counters. |
| `fastlogs_record_start/stop/set(en)` | Control writing to disk. |
| `fastlogs_is_recording()` | Is recording on. |
| `fastlogs_record_clear()` | Wipe the persisted log (memory + rolling file). |
| `fastlogs_set_screenshot(en)` | Attach a screenshot to the next send. |
| `fastlogs_send([opts])` | Send a log report. |
| `fastlogs_quick_send([opts])` | Send immediately, no overlay. |
| `fastlogs_is_sending()` / `fastlogs_retry_is_pending()` | Send / retry state. |
| `fastlogs_last_url()` | Link of the last successful log. |
| `fastlogs_get_counts()` | `{ error, warn, log }` for the session. |
| `fastlogs_session_id()` | GUID of this run. |
| `fastlogs_open/close/toggle()` | Overlay. |
| `fastlogs_is_open()` | Is the overlay open. |
| `fastlogs_comment_get()` / `fastlogs_comment_clear()` | Read / reset the overlay comment field. |
| `fastlogs_copy_url()` / `fastlogs_copy_text(text, toast)` / `fastlogs_clipboard_available()` | Clipboard. |
| `fastlogs_set_context(k, v)` / `fastlogs_remove_context(k)` / `fastlogs_clear_context()` | Context. |
| `fastlogs_breadcrumb(msg, [level])` / `fastlogs_clear_breadcrumbs()` | Breadcrumbs. |
| `fastlogs_send_file/folder/files(...)` | Send a file / folder / list. |
| `fastlogs_send_snapshot([opts])` | Report plus a `snapshot.zip` of saves. |
| `fastlogs_add_snapshot_source/data`, `fastlogs_remove_snapshot_source`, `fastlogs_clear_snapshot_sources` | Snapshot sources. |
| `fastlogs_redact(text)` / `fastlogs_redact_rules_set(rules)` / `fastlogs_redact_default_rules()` | PII scrub. |

---

## 16. When something does not work

**No link came back.** Check: is `FASTLOGS_ENDPOINT` set (and set as a *macro*, not passed to `fastlogs_init`); was `fastlogs_init` called; is the client enabled in this config (`Default` gates it off). If `fastlogs_last_url()` is empty, look at `http_status` in the Async HTTP event. Recording being off is *not* a cause - sending never needs it.

**The log is empty.** Nothing called `flog` this session, or the client is disabled in this config. Quick send says so honestly and refuses rather than ship an empty report. If you expected cross-session history, that is what recording is for (section 5).

**No screenshot in the report.** Check the `Draw GUI End` (8/65) event exists on `obj_fastlogs_controller` - capture happens only there. Then check the toggle (`FASTLOGS_SCREENSHOT_DEFAULT`, `fastlogs_set_screenshot`, or the overlay). On HTML5 it never works.

**401 / 403.** `401` means the game requires a token, fill in `FASTLOGS_TOKEN`. `403` means a wrong token or an unregistered `appId` - ask the admin to register the game.

**413 or a truncated log.** Limits exceeded. The client truncates the log itself with a marker; for files, `413` means `FASTLOGS_MAX_FILE_BYTES` (default 25 MB) was passed.

**CORS on the web.** The body ships `plain`, which the client handles. If CORS still bites, check `CORS_ALLOW_ORIGIN` on the server and that the endpoint is reachable from the game's domain.

More digging in the [README FAQ](../README.md), deployment and admin work in [ADMIN-GUIDE.md](./ADMIN-GUIDE.md) and [server/README.md](../server/README.md).

```

`docs/RELEASING.md`:

```md
# Releasing FastLogs

The maintainer's checklist for cutting a version and, when the server changed, getting it live. Day-to-day rules (branching, commit format) live in [`../CONTRIBUTING.md`](../CONTRIBUTING.md).

FastLogs ships as four things that move together: a Unity package installed by Git tag, a GameMaker client, the server, and the MCP subpackage. The tag is the only thing users pin, so the tag has to mean something exact.

## What a version means

Semantic versioning, judged from the **client and contract** side, because that is what other people depend on:

| Bump | When |
| --- | --- |
| **major** | A published contract in [`../CONTRACT.md`](../CONTRACT.md) changes so an old client breaks. |
| **minor** | New capability; existing clients keep working untouched. |
| **patch** | Fixes and internals only. |

A server-only change still gets a version: the server and the clients are one contract, and "which server is live" needs an answer.

## Cut the release

Steps 1 to 3 are yours. Step 4 refuses to run if any of them was skipped.

**1. Prove it green.** The server suite always. The other two whenever their side moved:

```bash
cd server && npm test                                    # always
# Unity EditMode (client or package touched):
Unity.exe -batchmode -projectPath <a project with the package> \
          -runTests -testPlatform EditMode -testResults <out>.xml
# e2e (command channel, capture, headless send or the ingest contract touched):
node test/e2e/run.mjs --player <path-to-a-built-player>
```

Two traps, both paid for already:

- `-runTests` with `-quit` exits before the runner starts. Leave `-quit` off.
- The verdict is in the NUnit XML, never the exit code. A batch run can exit 0 having run nothing.

**2. Write it down.** Add a `## [x.y.z] - YYYY-MM-DD` section to [`../CHANGELOG.md`](../CHANGELOG.md), grouped Added / Changed / Fixed. Mark every breaking change **BREAKING** and say what a user must do. These become the GitHub Release notes verbatim, so write them for a reader, not for yourself.

**3. Bump the pins together.** `unity/package.json` and every `#vX.Y.Z` install pin in the docs. CI (`version-pins`) fails if they disagree; so does the release script. Commit as `chore: x.y.z - version bump, release notes, install pins`.

**4. Run it.**

```bash
./scripts/release.sh 0.5.4 --dry-run   # look first
./scripts/release.sh 0.5.4
```

It verifies a clean tree on `main`, the tag is free, `unity/package.json` matches, the CHANGELOG section exists, every pin agrees, and the server suite is green. Then it tags, pushes, and publishes the GitHub Release from the CHANGELOG section, so notes and changelog cannot drift.

## Deploy the server

Only when `server/` changed. Tagging does not deploy: a shipped tag whose fix needs a deploy is a fix nobody has.

The reference deployment is Docker Compose. `data/` and `blobs/` are bind-mounted and `.env` holds the secrets, so **none of the three may be overwritten** by a deploy.

```bash
# 1. Package the code only.
tar -czf fastlogs-server.tgz -C server \
    --exclude=node_modules --exclude=data --exclude=blobs --exclude=.env .

# 2. On the host: stop (this checkpoints the SQLite WAL) and back up before migrating.
docker compose stop
cp -a data "data.bak.pre-<version>"

# 3. Unpack over the app dir, rebuild, start.
tar -xzf fastlogs-server.tgz
docker compose build && docker compose up -d
```

Back up before the container starts, not after: migrations run on boot.

**Verify, do not assume:**

```bash
curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' http://127.0.0.1:8787/api/health   # 200
docker ps --format '{{.Names}} {{.Status}}'                              # (healthy)
```

Roll back by restoring the backup: `docker compose stop && rm -rf data && mv data.bak.pre-<version> data && docker compose up -d`.

`server/deploy/deploy.sh` targets a different topology (systemd `fastlogsd`, a non-root deploy user). It is an alternative for that layout, not the Docker path above.

### Database migrations

Schema changes are additive `ALTER TABLE` guarded by `PRAGMA table_info`, so a re-run is a no-op. A **data** migration cannot be guarded that way, so it is versioned with `PRAGMA user_version` in `server/src/db.js`: bump the number, do the work once, stamp it. Without that stamp the work repeats on every boot.

After deploying a migration, read the version back rather than trusting the logs:

```bash
docker exec <container> node -e "const D=require('better-sqlite3');\
const db=new D('/app/data/fastlogs.db',{readonly:true});\
console.log(db.pragma('user_version',{simple:true}))"
```

## After a release

- Does the GitHub Release exist? Every version has one. A tag alone is not a release, and a missing one is invisible until someone goes looking.
- Does a fresh install of the new pin actually compile in a clean project? The package must declare every built-in module it uses; the host project masks a missing dependency, only an empty project catches it.
- If the release fixed something reported from production, confirm the fix in production, not in the test suite.

```

`docs/UNITY.md`:

```md
# FastLogs - Unity integrator guide

The full guide to the FastLogs Unity client. If you only need "install it and send the first log", the root [`README.md`](../README.md) has that. This page goes deep: config, lifecycle, sending, scene context, files, reliable delivery, privacy, release gating, and a big section on automation and agents.

Namespace is `PlayJoy.FastLogs`. The whole public API lives on one static facade, `FastLogs`.

## Contents

1. [Installation](#installation)
2. [Config asset](#config-asset)
3. [Init and lifecycle](#init-and-lifecycle)
4. [Sending a report](#sending-a-report)
5. [Context and breadcrumbs](#context-and-breadcrumbs)
6. [Screenshots](#screenshots)
7. [Files, folders, snapshots](#files-folders-snapshots)
8. [Scene context](#scene-context)
9. [Reliable delivery and the loop guard](#reliable-delivery-and-the-loop-guard)
10. [Crashes and the outbox](#crashes-and-the-outbox)
11. [Platforms, gating, release](#platforms-gating-release)
12. [Privacy](#privacy)
13. [Automation and agents](#automation-and-agents)
14. [Package tests](#package-tests)

---

## Installation

Unity versions: 6000.1 (Unity 6) and 2022.3 LTS.

### Via Git URL (recommended)

Add this to `Packages/manifest.json`:

```jsonc
{
  "dependencies": {
    "com.playjoy.fastlogs": "https://github.com/AitiX/Fastlogs.git?path=/unity#v0.5.3"
  }
}
```

`#v0.5.3` pins a release tag; the tag list lives in GitHub Releases and [`CHANGELOG.md`](../CHANGELOG.md). Swap the URL and tag for your own. The `?path=/unity` suffix tells Package Manager to treat the `unity/` subfolder as the package root instead of the whole repository.

### Embedded / local

Copy the `unity/` folder into your project's `Packages/` and rename it to `com.playjoy.fastlogs`. Unity picks it up on its own.

### asmdef and the CS0103 error

If your game code lives in its own asmdef rather than `Assembly-CSharp`, the package is invisible by default and the compiler says `CS0103: PlayJoy does not exist`. The reason is simple: an asmdef assembly does not inherit autoReference, so the reference has to be explicit.

Do not hand-edit the asmdef. Open `Tools > PlayJoy > FastLogs > Add Reference To Asmdef...`, pick the asmdef you call `FastLogs.Init()` from, and hit "Add reference to selected". If the project has a `FastLogsConfig` but no assembly references the package, the editor warns once per session in the console and points at that same menu item.

---

## Config asset

FastLogs reads its settings from a `FastLogsConfig` ScriptableObject placed in any `Resources/` folder under `Assets/`.

Create it with `Tools > PlayJoy > FastLogs > Create Config Asset`. The asset lands in `Assets/Resources/FastLogsConfig.asset` and gets selected in the Project window. If a config already exists somewhere, the menu item just pings it instead of making a duplicate. The other route is right-click in Project, `Create > PlayJoy > FastLogs > Config`.

Open the asset in the inspector and every section shows up as a foldout. The minimum for reports to leave: under **Server**, set `EndpointUrl` (the full ingest URL, e.g. `https://logs.example.com/api/logs`) and `AppId` (`[a-z0-9_-]{2,32}`). On iOS the endpoint must be `https://`; the inspector warns if you type `http://`.

### Config sections

What each section is for, briefly. **Full field tables with defaults** live in the package README: [`unity/README.md`](../unity/README.md#configuration). No point duplicating them here - defaults belong in one place.

| Section | What it drives |
| --- | --- |
| **Server** | Endpoint, appId, ingest token, retention override. |
| **Capture** | Log ring capacity, cap on log text, reading the SRDebugger console. |
| **Recording** | The persistent on-disk log store: master switch, autostart on Init, cross-session persistence. |
| **Auto-send** | Auto-send on unhandled exception plus its throttle; regexes that fire a report on a matching console line. |
| **Screenshot** | Whether to grab a screenshot by default, and the PNG's max edge. |
| **Scene Context** | Limits for the scene-hierarchy snapshot (objects, depth, fields, bytes). |
| **Component Capture** | Rule-driven component watching (see [Component capture](#component-capture)). |
| **Diagnostics** | Privacy: PII scrubbing and sensitive device fields. |
| **Trigger** | The overlay gesture (corner taps, key, shake) and the separate quick-send gesture. |
| **Files / Snapshot** | Caps and endpoint for file uploads and the save snapshot. |
| **Net / Retry / Loop Guard** | Timeout, retries, the outer retry loop, protection against send loops in code. |
| **UI** | Overlay, tester name, copying the link after a send. |
| **Commands** | The agent -> game command channel (see [Command channel](#command-channel-agent---game)). |
| **Enable** | Which builds FastLogs is active in (Editor / Development / Release). |

Several of these fields are mirrored at runtime on the overlay's **Settings** tab. Edits there go to `PlayerPrefs` and apply to the live config immediately:

- **Server**: appId, retention override.
- **Privacy**: sensitive device fields, PII scrubbing.
- **Recording**: buffer capacity, start / stop / clear recording, "open data folder".
- **Triggers and sending**: quick-send key, trigger choice, default screenshot, auto-send on exception, copy link on send.
- **Identity**: tester name.

---

## Init and lifecycle

Call `FastLogs.Init()` once, early in your bootstrap - for example in `Awake` on whichever manager loads first. `Init` is idempotent: a second call is ignored while FastLogs is already up.

```csharp
using PlayJoy.FastLogs;

// Simplest - loads Resources/FastLogsConfig automatically:
FastLogs.Init();

// Or hand it a specific asset (e.g. an inspector reference):
[SerializeField] private FastLogsConfig _config;
FastLogs.Init(_config);
```

`FastLogs.IsInitialized` tells you whether the client is up (always `false` in a stripped build). `FastLogs.Shutdown()` tears FastLogs down and destroys its host.

### The ring fills immediately; disk recording is a flag
