`scripts/release.sh`:

```sh
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Cut a FastLogs release: verify, tag, push, publish the GitHub Release.
#
#   ./scripts/release.sh 0.5.4          # do it
#   ./scripts/release.sh 0.5.4 --dry-run  # say what it would do
#
# This exists because the process used to live in someone's head, and a release
# shipped with its tag but no GitHub Release. Every check below is one that has
# actually been missed at least once.
#
# It does NOT deploy the server. That is a separate, explicit step (docs/RELEASING.md).

set -euo pipefail

VERSION="${1:-}"
DRY="${2:-}"
ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

die() { printf '\n  FAIL: %s\n\n' "$1" >&2; exit 1; }
ok()  { printf '  ok   %s\n' "$1"; }
run() { if [ "$DRY" = "--dry-run" ]; then printf '  (dry) %s\n' "$*"; else "$@"; fi; }

[ -n "$VERSION" ] || die "usage: ./scripts/release.sh <x.y.z> [--dry-run]"
printf '%s' "$VERSION" | grep -Eq '^[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+$' || die "version must be x.y.z, got '$VERSION'"

TAG="v$VERSION"
printf '\nReleasing %s\n\n' "$TAG"

# 1. The tree must be clean and on main: a release must be reproducible from the tag.
[ -z "$(git status --porcelain)" ] || die "working tree is dirty; commit or stash first"
branch=$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD)
[ "$branch" = "main" ] || die "on branch '$branch'; releases are cut from main"
ok "clean tree on main"

git tag -l "$TAG" | grep -q . && die "tag $TAG already exists"
ok "$TAG is free"

# 2. The version must already be bumped and written down. The script refuses to
#    invent either: a release with no changelog entry is invisible to users.
pkg=$(node -p "require('./unity/package.json').version")
[ "$pkg" = "$VERSION" ] || die "unity/package.json is $pkg, expected $VERSION (bump it and commit)"
ok "unity/package.json is $VERSION"

grep -q "^## \[$VERSION\]" CHANGELOG.md || die "CHANGELOG.md has no '## [$VERSION]' section"
ok "CHANGELOG.md has a $VERSION section"

# 3. Install pins in the docs must point at the tag being cut, or the docs will
#    tell every new user to install the previous release.
pinfail=0
while IFS= read -r hit; do
  [ -z "$hit" ] && continue
  file=${hit%%:*}
  pin=$(printf '%s' "$hit" | grep -oE '#v[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' | head -1)
  [ "$pin" = "#$TAG" ] || { printf '       %s pins %s\n' "$file" "$pin"; pinfail=1; }
done <<< "$(grep -rn 'Fastlogs.git?path=/unity#v' --include='*.md' . || true)"
[ "$pinfail" -eq 0 ] || die "install pins disagree with $TAG (see above)"
ok "every install pin is $TAG"

# 4. Tests. The server suite is the one gate that always applies and always runs
#    here; Unity EditMode and the e2e harness need an editor and a built player,
#    so they are the human's job (docs/RELEASING.md) and are only reminded about.
printf '  ...  server test suite\n'
if [ "$DRY" = "--dry-run" ]; then
  printf '  (dry) cd server && npm test\n'
else
  ( cd server && npm test >/dev/null 2>&1 ) || die "server tests are red; a release ships green or not at all"
fi
ok "server tests green"

# 5. Tag, push, publish. The GitHub Release notes come from the CHANGELOG section,
#    so the two can never disagree.
notes=$(mktemp)
awk -v v="$VERSION" '
  $0 ~ "^## \\[" v "\\]" {f=1; next}
  /^## \[/ {f=0}
  f
' CHANGELOG.md > "$notes"
[ -s "$notes" ] || die "extracted empty release notes for $VERSION"
ok "release notes extracted ($(wc -l < "$notes") lines)"

run git tag -a "$TAG" -m "$TAG"
run git push origin main
run git push origin "$TAG"
run gh release create "$TAG" --title "FastLogs $VERSION" --notes-file "$notes"

printf '\nDone: %s\n' "$TAG"
printf 'Server changed? Deploy is a separate step: docs/RELEASING.md\n\n'

```

`server/.env.example`:

```example
# FastLogs server - example environment.
# Copy to .env and adjust. The .env file is gitignored.

# HTTP port the server listens on (behind nginx).
PORT=8787

# Bind address. Default 127.0.0.1 (private, behind nginx on bare metal).
# In Docker set HOST=0.0.0.0 (the published port is mapped only to the host
# loopback via "127.0.0.1:8787:8787", so the service stays private).
HOST=127.0.0.1

# Data directory for the SQLite database (kept outside the future webroot).
DATA_DIR=./data

# Blob directory for gzipped log bodies and PNG screenshots (outside webroot).
BLOB_DIR=./blobs

# Public base URL used to build short links returned to clients.
# Neutral default; set this to your real public URL in production.
BASE_URL=http://localhost:8787

# Default retention in days for new logs when an app has no own setting.
DEFAULT_RETENTION_DAYS=30

# Hard upper bound for retention; per-request and per-app values are clamped to this.
MAX_RETENTION_DAYS=365

# Max size of the whole request body in bytes (application-level guard, ~8 MB).
MAX_PAYLOAD_BYTES=8388608

# Max size of a decoded PNG screenshot in bytes (~2 MB).
MAX_SCREENSHOT_BYTES=2097152

# Max size of the decompressed log text in bytes (~20 MB).
MAX_LOG_BYTES=20971520

# Max DECODED size of a standalone file upload (POST /api/files), in bytes (~25 MB).
MAX_FILE_BYTES=26214400

# Request-body read limit for POST /api/files (NOT the 8 MB MAX_PAYLOAD_BYTES):
# the blob arrives base64-encoded (+~33%) inside JSON. Defaults to
# MAX_FILE_BYTES*4/3 + 64 KB headroom when unset; override only if needed.
# MAX_FILE_BODY_BYTES=35717120

# Max screenshots accepted per report (a log can carry several). Extra are dropped.
MAX_SCREENSHOTS=8

# Admin token: unpin, delete, manage apps. Keep secret. Empty disables admin auth (dev only).
ADMIN_TOKEN=

# Scoped token for the agent command channel; admin also works. Grants ONLY the
# command endpoints (POST /api/commands/:appId and GET .../result/:cmdId), not the
# rest of the admin surface. Empty = no separate token, only ADMIN_TOKEN works.
COMMAND_TOKEN=

# Viewer token: team-wide read access to the catalog (/browse). Empty CLOSES the
# catalog (fail-closed): /browse returns 401 to everyone except the admin token.
# Set a value to give the team read access.
VIEWER_TOKEN=

# Shared "team" ingest token: a single master token valid for ANY app, so all
# games share one secret (e.g. from 1Password) instead of per-app tokens. Empty disables it.
TEAM_INGEST_TOKEN=

# Auto-register an unknown appId on first ingest when it presents the team token
# (tokenless), so new games self-onboard without running add-app.js. Requires
# TEAM_INGEST_TOKEN to be set. 1 to enable.
ALLOW_AUTO_REGISTER=0

# Unpinning policy. 0 (default) = unpin is OPEN: anyone with the log link can
# unpin (like pin). 1 = unpinning requires the admin token (only some can unpin).
# Pinning a log is always open.
UNPIN_REQUIRES_ADMIN=0

# Secret salt mixed into client IP hashing so stored ip_hash values are not reversible.
IP_SALT=change-me-please

# Trust X-Forwarded-For / X-Real-IP from the front proxy to determine the real
# client IP (used for per-IP rate limit and ip_hash). Keep 1 for the standard
# deploy where Node is bound to 127.0.0.1 behind nginx. Set 0 only if Node is
# exposed directly to clients, where these headers would be spoofable.
TRUST_PROXY=1

# CORS allowed origin. Use * for any origin (no credentials), or a concrete origin.
CORS_ALLOW_ORIGIN=*

# Retention sweep: the server deletes expired logs every SWEEP_INTERVAL_SEC
# seconds (0 disables the in-process sweep). SWEEP_BATCH bounds work per pass.
SWEEP_INTERVAL_SEC=3600
SWEEP_BATCH=500

# --- Backups (scripts/backup.js) ---
# How many timestamped backup snapshots to keep under <DATA_DIR>/backups; older
# ones are pruned after each run. Each snapshot is an online SQLite .backup (safe
# under WAL) plus a tar of the blobs.
BACKUP_KEEP=7

# --- Disk-usage monitor / alert ---
# An off-the-hot-path watchdog checks how full the BLOB_DIR volume is and how
# large blobs/ has grown, then warns (log + optional webhook) when a threshold
# is crossed. Alerts are edge-triggered with a recovery note, so a full disk
# does not flood the channel.
#
# Check cadence in seconds. Defaults to SWEEP_INTERVAL_SEC when unset; 0
# disables the monitor entirely.
# DISK_MONITOR_INTERVAL_SEC=3600
#
# Alert when the filesystem holding BLOB_DIR is at/above this used percentage.
# Default 85. Set 0 to disable the percentage check (e.g. fs.statfs unavailable
# on Node < 18.15, where this check is skipped automatically anyway).
DISK_ALERT_PCT=85
#
# Alert when blobs/ on-disk size reaches this many bytes. 0 (default) disables
# the absolute-size check; set e.g. 50000000000 (~50 GB) to also watch growth.
DISK_ALERT_BYTES=0
#
# Optional webhook the alert is POSTed to. Works with a Slack incoming webhook,
# a Discord channel webhook, or any generic receiver: the body carries both
# `text` (Slack) and `content` (Discord). Empty = log-only (no webhook).
# DISK_ALERT_WEBHOOK=

# --- Storage / size dashboard ---
# How many of the largest logs to list per app on the catalog dashboard.
STATS_TOP_N=5

# --- Log folders (manual catalog organisation) ---
# A log's folder is a "/"-separated path assigned by hand in the catalog
# (POST /api/folders/move); these bound a folder path so the column never holds
# abusive values. FOLDER_MAX_LEN = whole-path length; FOLDER_MAX_DEPTH = number
# of "/"-separated segments; FOLDER_SEGMENT_MAX_LEN = one segment's length.
FOLDER_MAX_LEN=200
FOLDER_MAX_DEPTH=8
FOLDER_SEGMENT_MAX_LEN=60
# Max log ids accepted in one POST /api/folders/move request.
FOLDER_MOVE_MAX_IDS=500

# --- Triage (log status + tags) ---
# Status/tags policy. 0 (default) = OPEN by link: anyone with the log link can
# set status/tags (like pin). 1 = requires the admin token.
TRIAGE_REQUIRES_ADMIN=0
# Max chars per tag (longer tags are truncated, not rejected).
TRIAGE_TAG_MAX_LEN=32
# Max tags kept per log (extra tags are dropped after dedupe).
TRIAGE_TAG_MAX_COUNT=20

# --- Crash grouping ---
# Number of top normalized stack frames folded into a crash signature.
CRASH_SIG_TOP_K=8
# Max crash_sig-NULL logs the /browse/:appId/crashes route backfills per request
# (bounds first-access latency on pre-feature logs). 0 disables the lazy backfill.
CRASH_RECOMPUTE_BATCH=200

# --- Full-text search (GET /api/search, SQLite FTS5) ---
# Max catalog rows returned by a single search query.
SEARCH_MAX_RESULTS=100
# Max snippet length per result, in whitespace tokens.
SEARCH_SNIPPET_TOKENS=12
# Max not-yet-indexed logs the search route lazily backfills into the FTS index
# per request (bounds first-query latency on pre-feature logs). 0 disables the
# lazy backfill and relies on `npm run backfill-fts` instead.
SEARCH_BACKFILL_BATCH=200

# --- Redmine "create issue from log" (optional) ---
# Base URL of the Redmine instance the SERVER calls for the API (no trailing
# /issues.json). Empty disables the integration (the viewer button is hidden and
# the endpoint returns 503). Use the address the server can actually reach: if
# Redmine is on the same host behind a proxy that the container cannot reach via
# the public IP (Docker hairpin NAT), use the bridge gateway, e.g.
# http://172.17.0.1:8282 .
REDMINE_URL=
# Browser-facing base URL used to build the clickable issue link. Optional;
# defaults to REDMINE_URL. Set this to the PUBLIC address when REDMINE_URL is a
# private/server-only address, e.g. REDMINE_PUBLIC_URL=http://your-host:8282 .
REDMINE_PUBLIC_URL=
# Redmine REST API key, sent as X-Redmine-API-Key. Server-side only. Empty also
# disables the integration.
REDMINE_API_KEY=
# Redmine project id or identifier that issues are created in. REQUIRED for issue
# creation: with URL + API key set but this empty, the endpoint returns 502.
REDMINE_PROJECT_ID=
# Optional numeric tracker id; empty uses the Redmine project default.
REDMINE_TRACKER_ID=
# Per-request timeout (ms) for the outbound Redmine call.
REDMINE_TIMEOUT_MS=10000
# When 1/true, create the issue AS the log's tester (issue Author = the QA),
# via Redmine X-Redmine-Switch-User. REQUIRES an ADMIN api key above, and the
# tester name must be a real Redmine login with permission in the project; if
# Redmine refuses, it falls back to the api-key user (the tester name is in the
# issue description regardless). Off by default.
REDMINE_IMPERSONATE_TESTER=0

```

`server/Dockerfile`:

```
# FastLogs server image.
#
# Debian-slim base (node:22-slim) is used instead of alpine so the native
# better-sqlite3 module installs cleanly: it has glibc prebuilt binaries, and if
# a build is needed the build-essential/python3 tools are present. node 22 is
# the current LTS and matches package.json (engines: node >= 18).

FROM node:22-slim

# Toolchain for compiling better-sqlite3 if no prebuilt binary matches.
RUN apt-get update \
 && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends python3 make g++ \
 && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /app

# Install production deps first for better layer caching. Only package manifests
# are copied here so a code change does not bust the dependency layer.
COPY package.json package-lock.json ./
RUN npm ci --omit=dev

# Copy the application source (node_modules excluded via .dockerignore).
COPY . .

# Persisted data lives outside the image; mount these as volumes.
ENV DATA_DIR=/app/data \
    BLOB_DIR=/app/blobs \
    PORT=8787
RUN mkdir -p /app/data /app/blobs

# The HTTP port the server listens on (nginx/compose maps it).
EXPOSE 8787

# Liveness probe (also honoured by `docker run` without compose). Uses node -
# always present, and the slim base ships no curl/wget - to GET /api/health on
# the in-container loopback; exits 0 only on HTTP 200.
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=5s --start-period=10s --retries=3 \
  CMD node -e "require('node:http').get('http://127.0.0.1:'+(process.env.PORT||8787)+'/api/health',r=>process.exit(r.statusCode===200?0:1)).on('error',()=>process.exit(1))"

# Drop privileges: the node:* images ship an unprivileged "node" user.
RUN chown -R node:node /app
USER node

CMD ["node", "src/server.js"]

```

`server/MODULES.md`:

```md
# FastLogs server - module contract

Exact export signatures of the core modules under `server/src`, for anyone
changing a route, sink, script or the sweeper. Source of truth for the API/data
shape is [../CONTRACT.md](../CONTRACT.md); the deployable surface (routes, env,
scripts) is [README.md](README.md). This file describes the internal module API
only.

Runtime: Node.js (CommonJS, `require`/`module.exports`), node >= 18. Storage:
SQLite via `better-sqlite3` (synchronous). Async modules (`storage`,
`util/http`) return Promises.

All paths in config are absolute. Time strings are ISO-8601 UTC.

---

## `src/config.js`

Frozen config object (singleton). `require('./config')` returns:

| Field | Type | Meaning |
|-------|------|---------|
| `port` | number | HTTP listen port (default 8787) |
| `serverRoot` | string | Absolute path to `server/` |
| `dataDir` | string | Absolute DB directory |
| `blobDir` | string | Absolute blob directory |
| `dbPath` | string | Absolute path to `fastlogs.db` inside `dataDir` |
| `baseUrl` | string | Public base URL, no trailing slash |
| `defaultRetentionDays` | number | Default retention (30) |
| `maxRetentionDays` | number | Hard retention ceiling (365) |
| `maxPayloadBytes` | number | Whole-body limit (~8 MB) |
| `maxScreenshotBytes` | number | PNG limit (~2 MB) |
| `maxLogBytes` | number | Decompressed log limit (~20 MB) |
| `maxFileBytes` / `maxFileBodyBytes` | number | Standalone upload: decoded blob cap (25 MB) / `POST /api/files` body cap, derived as `maxFileBytes * 4/3 + 64 KB` for the base64+JSON envelope |
| `maxSceneContextBytes` | number | `sceneContext` string cap (1 MB); a larger one is dropped at ingest, the report still lands |
| `maxScreenshots` | number | Screenshots kept per report (8); extras dropped |
| `adminToken` | string | Admin token; `''` => admin tier disabled |
| `viewerToken` | string | Viewer token; `''` => viewer tier disabled |
| `commandToken` | string | Scoped command-channel token; `''` => tier disabled (admin still works) |
| `commandTtlSec` | number | Default command lifetime (300; clamped 5..3600 per request) |
| `commandMaxArgsBytes` / `commandMaxResultBytes` | number | One command's `args` cap (8192) / inline `result` cap (65536; a larger result is offloaded to a file) |
| `commandsPollMax` | number | Max commands one poll may claim (16) |
| `commandsRetentionSec` | number | How long a terminal/expired command is kept before the sweeper drops it (86400) |
| `commandsLivenessSec` | number | How fresh a poll must be for the health beacon to report the channel live (10) |
| `diskMonitor` | object | Frozen `{ intervalSec, alertPct, alertBytes, webhook }`. `intervalSec` defaults to `sweepIntervalSec`; `0` disables the monitor. `alertPct` (85) / `alertBytes` (0) are the thresholds (`0` = check off); `webhook` empty = log-only |
| `ipSalt` | string | Salt for IP hashing |
| `corsAllowOrigin` | string | Allowed origin, `'*'` for any |
| `unpinRequiresAdmin` | boolean | Unpin requires the admin token when true |
| `triageRequiresAdmin` | boolean | Status/tags require the admin token when true (else open by link) |
| `triageTagMaxLen` / `triageTagMaxCount` | number | Per-tag length cap (32) / tags-per-log cap (20) |
| `statsTopN` | number | Largest-logs count for the dashboard (5) |
| `pinnedListLimit` | number | Max pinned logs returned by `GET /browse/:appId/pinned` (500) |
| `folderMaxLen` / `folderMaxDepth` / `folderSegmentMaxLen` | number | Folder path validation: whole-path length (200) / depth in `/`-segments (8) / one segment (60) |
| `folderMoveMaxIds` | number | Max log ids accepted in one `POST /api/folders/move` (500) |
| `crashSigTopK` / `crashRecomputeBatch` | number | Crash signature frame count (8) / lazy-backfill batch (200) |
| `searchMaxResults` / `searchSnippetTokens` / `searchBackfillBatch` | number | FTS search: result cap (100) / snippet tokens (12) / lazy-index batch (200) |
| `redmine` | object | Frozen `{ url, publicUrl, apiKey, projectId, trackerId, timeoutMs, impersonateTester, enabled }`. `enabled` = url && apiKey; `publicUrl` defaults to `url` (the browser-facing link when the server reaches Redmine privately); `impersonateTester` creates the issue as the log's tester |

(Other env-driven fields - `host`, `teamToken`, `allowAutoRegister`, `trustProxy`,
`sweepIntervalSec`, `sweepBatch`, `sweepMaxMs` - exist too. `src/config.js` is the full set:
`.env.example` documents the common ones, not all of them.)

Reads a `.env` file from `serverRoot` if present (real env vars win). No
external dotenv dependency.

---

## `src/db.js`

SQLite layer. Opens the DB (WAL, foreign_keys ON), runs idempotent migrations
on load. Exports:

- `db` - the raw `better-sqlite3` Database instance (escape hatch for tx/admin).
- `migrate(): void` - re-run idempotent migrations (also run automatically on import).

- `insertLog(row): RunResult` - insert a log. `row` keys (all required):
  `{ id, app_id, platform, app_version, device_json, title, comment, tester,
     context_json, breadcrumbs_json, scene_context, correlation_code, session_id,
     sent_via_code, caller_file, caller_line, crash_sig, engine, ts_utc,
     cnt_error, cnt_warn, cnt_log, log_bytes, has_shot, shot_count, created_at,
     expires_at, pinned, ip_hash }`.
  `device_json` is a JSON string (or null); `expires_at` is ISO string or null;
  `has_shot`/`pinned`/`sent_via_code` are 0|1; `caller_file` is a string or null
  and `caller_line` an integer or null (the game-code call site of a code send).
- `getLog(id): Row | undefined` - full log row by id.
- `deleteLog(id): RunResult` - delete a log row by id (`.changes` = rows deleted).
- `listExpired(now, limit): Array<{ id }>` - ids of non-pinned logs with
  `expires_at <= now` (ISO string), oldest first, capped at `limit`. Id only:
  the sweeper removes blobs and rows **by id**, so selecting the whole row would
  materialize `scene_context` (up to ~1 MB) per row for nothing. For the sweeper.
- `setPin(id, pinned, expiresAt): RunResult` - set `pinned` (0|1) and
  `expires_at` (ISO string or null). pin=true => pass `(id, 1, null)`.
- `listApps(): Row[]` - all apps, ordered by `app_id`.
- `getApp(appId): Row | undefined` - app row by id.
- `resolveAppId(idOrAlias): string | null` - resolve an appId-or-alias to the
  canonical `app_id` (a live app wins; else an alias from `app_aliases` is
  followed; else null). Used by ingest / browse / search / folders so a renamed
  project's **old** slug keeps working.
- `upsertApp(row): RunResult` - insert/update an app. `row` keys (all required):
  `{ app_id, name, token_hash, retention_days, max_retention_days,
     sinks_json, enabled, created_at }`.
  `token_hash` is a sha256 hex string or null; `enabled` is 0|1;
  `sinks_json` is a JSON string or null.
- `renameApp(oldAppId, newAppId, newName?): void` - rename a project in one
  transaction: re-key the app row + all its logs/files from `oldAppId` to
  `newAppId`, record `oldAppId` as an alias of `newAppId`, repoint any existing
  aliases onto `newAppId`, and (if `newName != null`) update the display name.
  Nothing is lost: log ids are unchanged (per-id links keep working) and blobs
  (keyed by log id) are untouched. Renaming to the same id only updates the name.
- `listVersions(appId): Array<{ version, count, last_at, totalBytes, pinnedCount }>` -
  distinct `app_version` values with per-version counts, latest `created_at`,
  total bytes and pinned count.
- `listLogs(appId, version): Row[]` - logs of one app+version, newest first.
  Columns: `id, title, ts_utc, platform, cnt_error, cnt_warn, cnt_log,
  log_bytes, has_shot, pinned, status, tags, crash_sig, engine, session_id, folder, created_at, expires_at`.
- `listLogsByFolder(appId, version, folder): Row[]` - same as listLogs but
  restricted to one folder. `folder` is the exact path string, or null for the
  root (folder NULL). Same row shape (incl. the `folder` column).
- `listLogsBySession(appId, sessionId, now): Row[]` - live logs of one session, newest first (same columns plus `app_version`).
- `listPinnedLogs(appId, now[, limit]): Row[]` - pinned (`pinned = 1`) logs of one
  app across **all** versions, newest first (same columns plus `app_version`). Backs
  the per-app "Pinned" view (`GET /browse/:appId/pinned`). `now` is accepted for
  signature parity but unused: a pinned row has `expires_at` NULL, so it is live
  by definition. `limit` caps the result (defaults to `config.pinnedListLimit`).
- `setLogFolder(id, appId, folder): RunResult` - assign a log to a folder (or
  clear it; `folder` null = root). Scoped by `app_id` (the move stays in the
  project); `.changes` is 1 when the log existed in that app.
- `listFolders(appId): string[]` - distinct non-empty folder paths of an app,
  alphabetically (the root is implicit and not listed).
- `statsByApp(): Array<{ app_id, logCount, totalBytes, pinnedCount }>` - storage rollup per app (all rows present).
- `statsForApp(appId): { logCount, totalBytes, pinnedCount }` - storage rollup for one app.
- `enginesByApp(): Array<{ app_id, engine, last_at }>` - engine of the latest log per app.
- `largestLogs(appId, limit): Row[]` - the `limit` largest logs of an app by `log_bytes`, largest first.
- `listCrashRows(appId, now): Row[]` - live crash rows (crash_sig set, not `''`) for grouping.
  Columns: `id, crash_sig, app_version, title, platform, tester, created_at, cnt_error`.
- `listLogsMissingSig(appId, limit): Array<{ id }>` - up to `limit` logs of an app with `crash_sig` NULL (lazy backfill).
- `getLatestByCode(appId, code, now): { id, created_at } | null` - most recent **live** log
  whose `correlation_code` equals `code`, or (fallback) whose comment contains it.
  LIKE wildcards in `code` are escaped. Backs `GET /api/await/:appId`.
- `updateCrashSig(id, sig): RunResult` - set `crash_sig` (`''` = computed, not a crash).
- `searchAvailable(): boolean` - whether the SQLite build has FTS5 (search live).
- `indexLog(metaRow, body): void` - index a log's catalog text + body into the FTS5 index and mark `fts_indexed=1` (no-op without FTS).
- `searchLogs(appId, match, limit, now, version?): Row[]` - app's live logs matching the FTS5 `match` string, relevance-ranked, catalog-row shape. `version` (optional) restricts to one exact `app_version` and is applied **during** the join, before the limit slice, so a version-scoped search never loses matches that ranked below an unfiltered top-`limit`.
- `listLogsMissingFts(appId, limit): Row[]` - up to `limit` of an app's not-yet-indexed logs (with text columns) for the search backfill.
- `setStatus(id, status): RunResult` - set the triage status enum.
- `setTags(id, tagsJson): RunResult` - set tags (JSON-array string, or null for none).
- `setRedmine(id, issueId, issueUrl): RunResult` - link a created Redmine issue (issueId coerced to string).
- `bumpRate(key, windowStart): number` - atomically increment the rate counter
  for `key` in window `windowStart` (integer window index); resets on window
  rollover. Returns the post-increment count in-window.

#### Files (standalone uploads)

- `insertFile(row): RunResult` - insert a file row; `row` carries every column of
  the `files` table (see Schema).
- `getFile(id): Row | undefined` - file row by id.
- `deleteFile(id): RunResult` - delete a file row (`.changes` = rows deleted).
- `listExpiredFiles(now, limit): Array<{ id }>` - ids of up to `limit` non-pinned
  files with `expires_at <= now` (ISO string), oldest first. Id only, like
  `listExpired`: the sweeper removes the blob and the row by id, so the rest of
  the row would be materialized and never read. For the sweeper.
- `listFilesByLog(logId, appId, now): Row[]` - live files attached to a log, oldest
  first. Scoped by `app_id`, so an attachment is only ever listed under its own project.
- `setFilePin(id, pinned, expiresAt): RunResult` - set `pinned` (0|1) and
  `expires_at` (ISO string or null => never expires).

#### Commands (agent -> game channel)

- `enqueueCommand(row): RunResult` - queue a command; `row` is
  `{ id, app_id, name, args_json, created_at, expires_at }`, status defaults to `'queued'`.
- `getCommand(id): Row | undefined` - command row by id.
- `claimCommands(appId, now, limit): Row[]` - atomically claim up to `limit`
  queued, unexpired commands of `appId` in **one** transaction (queued -> dispatched),
  so two concurrent polls never hand out the same command twice. Returns the
  claimed rows FIFO. Also stamps `apps.last_poll_at` on **every** poll, empty ones
  included: an idle poll is exactly what proves the build is alive.
- `getAppLastPoll(appId): string | null` - last poll timestamp (ISO) of an app's
  channel, or null if it never polled. Backs the health beacon.
- `ackCommand(id, appId, status, resultJson, error, completedAt): RunResult` -
  record a result. Allowed only from `'dispatched'` and only for the owning app
  (both enforced in SQL); `.changes === 0` means not dispatched (route answers 409).
- `sweepCommands(now, cutoff): RunResult` - delete terminal, abandoned-dispatched
  and expired-queued commands older than `cutoff` (ISO string).

### Schema
- `apps(app_id PK, name, token_hash, retention_days DEFAULT 30,
   max_retention_days DEFAULT 365, sinks_json, enabled DEFAULT 1, created_at)`
   plus `last_poll_at` (nullable TEXT, added by migration): the last time a build
   polled this app's command channel, stamped on every poll. Backs the health beacon.
- `logs(id PK, app_id, platform, app_version, device_json, title, ts_utc,
   cnt_error, cnt_warn, cnt_log, log_bytes, has_shot DEFAULT 0, created_at,
   expires_at NULL, pinned DEFAULT 0, ip_hash)` plus additive columns
   `comment, tester, context_json, breadcrumbs_json, crash_sig, tags,
   redmine_issue_id, redmine_issue_url, engine, scene_context, correlation_code,
   session_id, folder, caller_file` (all nullable TEXT), `caller_line INTEGER`
   (nullable; code-send call-site line), `status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'new'`,
   `shot_count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0`, `sent_via_code INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0`
   ("sent from code" provenance flag) and `fts_indexed INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0`.
- `app_aliases(alias PK, app_id)` - an **old** appId (`alias`) that resolves to the
   canonical `app_id` after a rename. `idx_app_aliases_app(app_id)`.
- `files(id PK, app_id, app_version, platform, log_id, group_id, name,
   mime, kind, size_bytes DEFAULT 0, sha256, title, tester, created_at,
   expires_at NULL, pinned DEFAULT 0, ip_hash)` - standalone uploads
   (`POST /api/files`). `log_id` attaches one to a log; `kind` is the validated
   hint. Indexes: `idx_files_expires(expires_at) WHERE pinned=0`,
   `idx_files_log(log_id) WHERE log_id IS NOT NULL`, `idx_files_app(app_id, created_at)`.
- `commands(id PK, app_id, name, args_json, status DEFAULT 'queued', result_json,
   error, created_at, dispatched_at, completed_at, expires_at NOT NULL)` - the
   agent -> game channel. `status` is `queued|dispatched|done|error`;
   `args_json`/`result_json` hold serialized JSON strings.
   `idx_commands_poll(app_id, status, created_at)` backs the FIFO claim scan.
- Indexes: `idx_logs_expires(expires_at) WHERE pinned=0`,
   `idx_logs_app(app_id, created_at)`,
   `idx_logs_crash(app_id, crash_sig) WHERE crash_sig IS NOT NULL`,
   `idx_logs_code(app_id, correlation_code) WHERE correlation_code IS NOT NULL` (await lookup),
   `idx_logs_session(app_id, session_id, created_at) WHERE session_id IS NOT NULL`,
   `idx_logs_folder(app_id, folder) WHERE folder IS NOT NULL` (catalog folder filter),
   `idx_logs_unindexed(app_id) WHERE fts_indexed=0` (FTS backfill scan).
- `logs_fts` - FTS5 virtual table (`content='', contentless_delete=1`) over
   `title, tester, comment, context, scene, body`; rowid derived from the log id
   (`ftsRowId`, FNV-1a folded to 52 bits). Created only when the SQLite build has
   FTS5; search degrades to disabled otherwise.
- `rate_counters(key PK, window_start, count)`.

---

## `src/id.js`

Short base62 ids.

- `newId(existsFn, startLength = 6): string` - unique id; `existsFn(id)` returns
  truthy if id taken. Grows length on repeated collisions. Default length 6.
- `randomBase62(length): string` - uniform random base62 string (rejection
  sampling, no modulo bias).
- `ALPHABET: string` - the 62-char alphabet (`0-9A-Za-z`).
- `DEFAULT_LENGTH: number` - 6.

---

## `src/storage.js`

Blob storage on disk, sharded by the first two chars of the id. Layout, all under
`<blobDir>/<shard>/`:

| File | What |
|------|------|
| `<id>.log.gz` | gzipped log body |
| `<id>.png` | first screenshot (index 0; the original single-shot layout) |
| `<id>.N.png` | screenshot at index N > 0 |
| `<id>.bin` | standalone upload, stored verbatim (never transformed) |

All async functions return Promises.

- `saveLogGz(id, data, alreadyGzipped = false): Promise<number>` - store a log
  body; gzips `data` (string/Buffer) unless `alreadyGzipped`. Returns bytes
  written (gzipped size).
- `readLogGz(id, opts?): Promise<string | Buffer | null>` - decompressed text by
  default; `{ raw: true }` returns the raw gzip Buffer (for serving `.log.gz`
  directly). `null` if missing.
- `saveShot(id, data, index = 0): Promise<number>` - store the PNG at `index`
  (`data` is Buffer or base64 string). Returns bytes written.
- `readShot(id, index = 0): Promise<Buffer | null>` - PNG bytes at `index`, or null if missing.
- `removeBlobs(id): Promise<number>` - delete the log body and **every** screenshot
  (`<id>.png`, `<id>.N.png`, matched by regex on the shard listing). Returns the
  count removed: up to 1 + the number of screenshots. Missing files ignored.
- `saveFile(id, buf): Promise<number>` - store a standalone upload verbatim (no
  gzip). Returns bytes written.
- `readFile(id): Promise<Buffer | null>` - the upload's bytes, or null if missing.
- `removeFileBlob(id): Promise<number>` - delete the upload; returns 0 or 1
  (idempotent).
- `logPath(id): string` / `shotPath(id, index = 0): string` / `filePath(id): string` -
  absolute blob paths.
- `shardDir(id): string` / `shardOf(id): string` - shard directory / shard key.
- `logExistsSync(id): boolean` - synchronous existence check for the log blob.

---

## `src/auth.js`

- `sha256(value): string` - lowercase hex sha256 of a string.
- `safeEqual(a, b): boolean` - constant-time string comparison.
- `parseBearer(headerValue): string | null` - extract token from an
  `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header value.
- `validateIngest(appId, bearer, options?): { ok, app, code }` - validate an ingest
  request. `bearer` is the raw Authorization header value. `code` is one of
  `'ok' | 'unauthorized' | 'forbidden' | 'auto_register'`; the first three map to
  HTTP 200/401/403, `'auto_register'` tells the caller to create the app. `app` is
  the app row (null on auto-register / unknown app). Rules: disabled app =>
  forbidden; the team token is a master ingest key for **any** app; app needs a token
  but none given => unauthorized; token mismatch => forbidden; app without a token
  => ok; unknown app + team token + `allowAutoRegister` => auto_register; unknown
  app otherwise => unauthorized (no token) / forbidden.
  `options.requireToken` (default false) refuses the "app has no token => open"
  branch, so a real token is needed even for an app whose log ingest is open. The
  command channel passes it: claiming an agent's commands is a higher tier than
  posting logs.
- `isAdmin(token): boolean` - true if raw `token` equals `config.adminToken`
  (false if admin token is empty/disabled).
- `isCommandIssuer(token): boolean` - true if raw `token` equals
  `config.commandToken`. This tier may **only** drive the command channel; an empty
  `COMMAND_TOKEN` disables it (returns false). Admin is a superset, composed in at
  the route (`routes/commands.js`), not here.
- `isViewer(token): boolean` - true if raw `token` matches the viewer token;
  the admin token also satisfies viewer access.

---

## `src/cors.js`

- `applyCors(req, res): void` - set CORS headers from `config.corsAllowOrigin`.
  With `'*'` sets `Allow-Origin: *` and never sets `Allow-Credentials`; with a
  concrete origin echoes it and sets `Vary: Origin`. Always sets
  `Allow-Methods: GET, POST, OPTIONS`, `Allow-Headers: Authorization,
  Content-Type`, `Max-Age: 86400`.
- `handlePreflight(req, res): boolean` - if `req.method === 'OPTIONS'`, write
  CORS headers, respond `204`, return `true` (handled). Otherwise return `false`.
- `ALLOW_METHODS`, `ALLOW_HEADERS`, `MAX_AGE_SECONDS` - the header constants.

---

## `src/ratelimit.js`

- `check(key, limit, windowSec): { allowed, retryAfter, count, limit }` -
  fixed-window check that consumes one unit for `key`. `allowed` is false once
  `count > limit`; `retryAfter` is seconds until the next window boundary (0
  when allowed). Suggested keys: `ingest:ip:<ip_hash>`, `ingest:app:<app_id>`.

---

## `src/util/http.js`

- `nowUtcIso(): string` - current time as ISO-8601 UTC.
- `sendJson(res, status, obj, extraHeaders?): void` - JSON response.
- `sendText(res, status, text, extraHeaders?): void` - text/buffer response.
- `sendError(res, status, code, message, extraHeaders?): void` - CONTRACT error
  body `{ error: code, message }`.
- `readBuffer(req, maxBytes): Promise<Buffer>` - read body with byte cap;
  rejects with `err.code === 'PAYLOAD_TOO_LARGE'` on overflow.
- `readJsonBody(req, maxBytes): Promise<object>` - read+parse JSON, honoring
  `Content-Encoding: gzip` (inflates with the same `maxBytes` ceiling). Throws
  errors with `.code`: `'PAYLOAD_TOO_LARGE'` (413), `'BAD_JSON'` (400),
  `'BAD_ENCODING'` (400).

---

## `src/util/ip.js`

Client-IP resolution shared by the ingest-family routes (logs, files, commands).

- `clientIp(req): string` - the real client IP. With `config.trustProxy`, reads the
  **last** hop of `X-Real-IP`, then of `X-Forwarded-For`; otherwise the socket address
  (`'0.0.0.0'` as a last resort). The last hop is the load-bearing part: nginx
  **appends** the peer to a client-supplied `X-Forwarded-For`, so every earlier hop is
  attacker-controlled and reading the first one would let a client rotate the
  header to dodge the per-IP rate limit. With `trustProxy` off, both headers are
  spoofable and are ignored entirely.
- `hashIp(ip): string` - anonymous, non-reversible rate-limit/storage key
  (`sha256(ipSalt + '|' + ip)`, first 16 hex chars).

---

## `src/crashsig.js`

Pure crash-signature module (only `node:crypto`).

- `computeSignature(logText, opts?): string | null` - 12-char sha1 of a grouping
  key plus the top K normalized stack frames of the representative error entry.
  The key is the exception type (`FooException: ...`) when the message carries
  one, else the normalized message text. Both line grammars are parsed: Unity's
  `[E] +12.345 message` (the frame token is optional) and GameMaker's
  `[10:00:12] ERROR: message`, whose spelled-out levels fold onto Unity's
  `L`/`W`/`E`, so one crash keeps one signature whichever engine reported it.
  The representative entry is the **last** error carrying a stack, falling back to
  the last error without one. `null` when the log has no error entry (not a
  crash) or the entry yields neither a key nor a frame. `opts.topK` overrides
  the default frame count.
- `parseEntries`, `pickError`, `exceptionType`, `normalizeMessage`,
  `normalizeFrame`, `isFrameLine` - the building blocks (unit-tested directly).
- `TOP_K` (8), `SIG_LEN` (12) - defaults.

---

## `src/util/version.js`

Total ordering for version strings (no deps, never throws).

- `compareVersions(a, b, createdAtA?, createdAtB?): -1 | 0 | 1` - semver ->
  loose numeric-dotted -> created_at -> lexicographic, with created_at as the
  tiebreaker for equal versions.
- `parseSemver(s): { major, minor, patch, pre } | null`.

---

## `src/redmine.js`

Redmine REST client + content builders (Node global `fetch` + `AbortController`,
no new deps).

- `buildIssueContent(row, publicUrl, overrides?): { subject, description }`.
- `createIssue({ url, apiKey, projectId, trackerId, subject, description, timeoutMs, switchUser? }): Promise<{ ok:true, issueId, impersonated, raw } | { ok:false, kind, status?, detail }>` -
  never throws; `kind` is `'validation' | 'auth' | 'http' | 'network'`.
  `switchUser` (a Redmine login) files the issue **as** that user via the
  `X-Redmine-Switch-User` header and needs an admin api key. When Redmine
  refuses the impersonation - 412 (unknown or inactive login) or 403 (that user
  lacks permission in the project) - it retries **once** as the api-key user so the
  issue is still filed, and `impersonated` in the result says which actually
  happened.

---

## Notes for downstream builders

- `package.json` declares scripts `start`/`test`/`sweep`/`backup`/`add-app`/`rename-app`/`list-apps`/`migrate`/`backfill-fts`.
  They live in `server/scripts/` (plus `src/server.js` and `test/run.js`) and are
  documented in [README.md](README.md).
- Error `code` strings in `auth.validateIngest` align with CONTRACT tokens; the
  route layer maps them to HTTP statuses and the `{ error, message }` body.
- Retention: route layer clamps `retentionDays` to `[1, app.max_retention_days]`
  and computes `expires_at = created_at + days` (ISO). Pinned logs use
  `expires_at = null`.
- `POST /api/files` (`src/routes/files.js`) classifies the optional `kind` hint
  against `VALID_KINDS = { file, folder, save, snapshot, screenshot, archive,
  other }`; any other value collapses to `null` so a bogus hint can never break
  the viewer. `kind: "snapshot"` is the full game-state archive (`snapshot.zip` =
  saves + registered data, **not** the log itself) attached to a log via `logId`,
  produced by the client's `SendSnapshot`. It is stored/served like any other
  attachment (same blob/row path, retention, `MAX_FILE_BYTES` cap); the viewer's
  Saves / Data panel labels it "Snapshot (saves + data)" and lists it first.

```

`server/README.md`:

```md
# FastLogs server

Node.js HTTP server behind nginx. Receives logs from game clients (Unity, GameMaker), stores them, and serves a web viewer with a short link.

Dependency-light (only `better-sqlite3`) and reusable: the defaults are neutral
(`BASE_URL=http://localhost:8787`), so any team can deploy it under their own
domain. The license is MIT (see the repo root `LICENSE`) - swap it freely.

Every `/api/...` endpoint is reachable under the canonical `/api/v1/...` prefix
and under the original unversioned `/api/...` alias; both are valid forever (see
[CONTRACT.md](../CONTRACT.md)). The browser-facing surface is not versioned: the
viewer (`/:id`), the catalog (`/browse`), the file pages (`/files/:id`) and the
static assets exist only at their unprefixed paths. Note the split around files:
the upload `POST /api/files` is an API endpoint and is mirrored, while the file
page `GET /files/:id` is not.

This file is the reference: routes, env, scripts. For running a deployment
end to end (VPS setup, tokens, backups, troubleshooting) read
[../docs/ADMIN-GUIDE.md](../docs/ADMIN-GUIDE.md).

## Quick start

```bash
cd server
cp .env.example .env        # Fill in ADMIN_TOKEN, VIEWER_TOKEN, IP_SALT, etc.
npm install
npm run add-app -- lfa "Looking For Aliens" 30
npm start
```

## Routes

The wiring lives in `src/index.js`; [CONTRACT.md](../CONTRACT.md) has the payloads. Every `/api/...` path below is mirrored under `/api/v1/...`.

| Route | Auth | Purpose |
|-------|------|---------|
| `POST /api/logs` | ingest | Upload a report |
| `GET /api/logs/:id` | open | Report metadata (the id is the capability) |
| `POST /api/logs/:id/pin` | open (unpin: admin when `UNPIN_REQUIRES_ADMIN`) | Pin/unpin a log against retention |
| `POST /api/logs/:id/status`, `.../tags` | open (admin when `TRIAGE_REQUIRES_ADMIN`) | Triage a log |
| `POST /api/logs/:id/redmine` | viewer | Create a Redmine issue from a log |
| `POST /api/files` | ingest | Standalone file upload (own body limit) |
| `GET /api/await/:appId?code=` | viewer | Latest live log carrying a correlation code |
| `POST /api/commands/:appId` | admin or command | Enqueue an agent -> game command |
| `GET /api/commands/:appId/poll` | ingest token (always required) | The build claims queued commands |
| `GET /api/commands/:appId/health` | admin or command | Liveness beacon: is a build polling? |
| `POST /api/commands/:appId/ack/:cmdId` | ingest token (always required) | The build returns a result |
| `GET /api/commands/:appId/result/:cmdId` | admin or command | Read a command's state/result |
| `GET /api/search?appId=&q=` | viewer | Full-text search over one app |
| `GET /api/folders`, `POST /api/folders/move` | viewer | List folders / move a selection of logs |
| `GET /api/health` | open | Health probe |
| `GET /browse`, `/browse/:appId`, then `/:version`, `/crashes` or `/pinned` | viewer | Catalog |
| `GET /:id`, `/:id/raw`, `/:id/screenshot[/:n]` | open | Viewer page, log text, screenshots |
| `GET /files/:id`, `/files/:id/download` | open | File viewer / download |

Two things worth knowing: there is no HTTP delete (logs and files leave only through retention, via the sweeper), and the command channel's poll/ack always demand a real token. An app with open log ingest still gets a closed command channel: a tokenless poll could claim the agent's commands away from the real build.

## Scripts

Every script in `package.json`, and what it is for:

| Command | What it does |
|---------|-------------|
| `npm start` | Start the server (`src/server.js`) |
| `npm test` | Run all tests (`test/run.js`) |
| `npm run sweep` | Delete expired logs and files (call from cron / systemd timer; the server also sweeps in-process) |
| `npm run add-app -- <appId> "<name>" [days]` | Register or update an app; mints and prints an ingest token unless `--no-token` |
| `npm run rename-app -- <oldAppId> <newAppId> ["New Name"]` | Re-key a project; the old slug keeps resolving through an alias |
| `npm run list-apps` | List registered apps (`--json` for machine output) |
| `npm run migrate` | Apply DB migrations (also done automatically on start) |
| `npm run backfill-fts` | Index pre-search logs into FTS5 (`--batch N`, `--app <appId>`) |
| `npm run backup` | Online SQLite `.backup` (safe under WAL) + a tar of the blobs into `<DATA_DIR>/backups`, keeping the newest `BACKUP_KEEP` of each |

### Agent CLI tools

Standalone helpers under `src/tools/` for driving a build from an automation agent (no deps beyond Node built-ins; each writes its state to a JSON file every poll and exits 0 on success / 2 on timeout / 1 on error):

- `fastlogs-await.js` - wait for the next log carrying a given debug/await code, then grab its link (`--app --code --token <viewer> --base`).
- `fastlogs-command.js` - enqueue an agent -> game command and poll its result until the game acks (`--app --name --token <admin|command> --base [--args <json>] [--ttl]`). Prefer the scoped `COMMAND_TOKEN` over admin. See the command channel in [CONTRACT.md](../CONTRACT.md) section 8.

An agent that speaks MCP should use [mcp/](mcp/) instead: the same channel as native tools, with the offloaded-result handling built in.

## Environment variables

See `.env.example` for inline docs. The ones you actually set on a deploy:

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| `PORT` | `8787` | HTTP listen port (nginx proxies here) |
| `HOST` | `127.0.0.1` | Bind address. Use `0.0.0.0` in Docker (the published port is mapped to host loopback only) |
| `BASE_URL` | `http://localhost:8787` | Public base URL for short links (set to your real URL in production) |
| `ADMIN_TOKEN` | _(none)_ | Superset of the viewer and command tiers; required for unpin/triage when `UNPIN_REQUIRES_ADMIN`/`TRIAGE_REQUIRES_ADMIN` are on. Empty disables admin auth (dev) |
| `COMMAND_TOKEN` | _(none)_ | Scoped token for the agent command channel; admin also works. Grants **only** `POST /api/commands/:appId`, `GET .../result/:cmdId` and `GET .../health`, nothing else on the admin surface. Empty = only the admin token works |
| `VIEWER_TOKEN` | _(none)_ | Team viewer token for the catalog `/browse`. Empty **closes** the catalog (fail-closed): `/browse` returns 401 for everyone except the admin token |
| `TEAM_INGEST_TOKEN` | _(none)_ | Shared master ingest token valid for **any** app (one secret instead of per-app tokens) |
| `IP_SALT` | _(none)_ | Salt for IP hashing (set a random value) |
| `DATA_DIR` | `./data` | SQLite DB directory |
| `BLOB_DIR` | `./blobs` | Log body (gzip) + screenshot storage |
| `DEFAULT_RETENTION_DAYS` | `30` | Default log lifetime (days) when an app has no own setting |
| `MAX_RETENTION_DAYS` | `365` | Retention ceiling a **new** app inherits when `--max-retention` is not given. Not an instance-wide cap: `add-app --max-retention N` writes a per-app ceiling verbatim and may exceed this. Per-request values are clamped to the app's own ceiling. |
| `ALLOW_AUTO_REGISTER` | `0` | With the team token, an unknown `appId` self-registers (tokenless) on first ingest. Requires `TEAM_INGEST_TOKEN` |
| `UNPIN_REQUIRES_ADMIN` | `0` | `0` = anyone with the link can unpin (like pin); `1` = unpinning requires the admin token. Pinning is always open |
| `TRIAGE_REQUIRES_ADMIN` | `0` | `0` = setting log status/tags is open by link (like pin); `1` = requires the admin token |
| `TRUST_PROXY` | `1` | Trust `X-Forwarded-For` / `X-Real-IP` from nginx for the real client IP (rate limit + `ip_hash`). Set `0` only if Node is exposed directly |
| `CORS_ALLOW_ORIGIN` | `*` | Allowed CORS origin (`*` for any) |
| `SWEEP_INTERVAL_SEC` | `3600` | How often expired (non-pinned) logs and files are purged. `0` disables the in-process sweep |
| `SWEEP_BATCH` | `500` | Rows per chunk, bounding **one** transaction. A pass keeps draining chunks until the backlog is gone or `SWEEP_MAX_MS` is spent, so this does not cap what a pass deletes |
| `SWEEP_MAX_MS` | `5000` | Time budget for a whole sweep pass. Checked between chunks (the first always runs); a spent budget defers the rest to the next pass |
| `MAX_PAYLOAD_BYTES` | `8388608` | Max whole request body (~8 MB) |
| `MAX_SCREENSHOT_BYTES` | `2097152` | Max decoded PNG screenshot (~2 MB) |
| `MAX_LOG_BYTES` | `20971520` | Max decompressed log text (~20 MB) |
| `MAX_FILE_BYTES` | `26214400` | Max decoded standalone upload (~25 MB) on `POST /api/files` |
| `MAX_SCENE_CONTEXT_BYTES` | `1048576` | Max `sceneContext` JSON string (~1 MB). A larger one is dropped at ingest; the report still lands |
| `MAX_SCREENSHOTS` | `8` | Max screenshots accepted per report (a log can carry several; extras dropped) |
| `COMMAND_TTL_SEC` | `300` | Default lifetime of an agent -> game command (clamped 5..3600 per request) |
| `COMMAND_MAX_ARGS_BYTES` | `8192` | Max size of one command's `args` payload |
| `COMMAND_MAX_RESULT_BYTES` | `65536` | Max size of the result the game returns for one command |
| `COMMANDS_POLL_MAX` | `16` | Max commands one poll may claim |
| `COMMANDS_RETENTION_SEC` | `86400` | How long a terminal (done/error) or expired command is kept before the sweeper drops it |
| `COMMANDS_LIVENESS_SEC` | `10` | How fresh a poll must be (seconds) for the liveness beacon to call the channel live |

### Advanced tuning

Rarely touched; the defaults are fine for most deployments.

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| `STATS_TOP_N` | `5` | How many largest logs to list per app on the catalog dashboard |
| `BACKUP_KEEP` | `7` | How many timestamped backup snapshots `scripts/backup.js` keeps under `<DATA_DIR>/backups` (older ones pruned) |
| `DISK_MONITOR_INTERVAL_SEC` | `SWEEP_INTERVAL_SEC` | Disk-usage check cadence. Defaults to the sweep cadence so both housekeeping passes line up; `0` disables the monitor |
| `DISK_ALERT_PCT` | `85` | Warn once the `BLOB_DIR` filesystem is at/above this used percentage. `0` disables the percentage check |
| `DISK_ALERT_BYTES` | `0` | Warn once `blobs/` reaches this on-disk size. `0` (default) disables the absolute check, leaving only the percentage one |
| `DISK_ALERT_WEBHOOK` | _(none)_ | Alert target. Empty = log-only. The body carries both `text` and `content`, so one URL works for Slack, Discord or a generic receiver |
| `MAX_FILE_BODY_BYTES` | `MAX_FILE_BYTES` x 4/3 + 64 KB | Request-body ceiling for `POST /api/files`, sized for the base64 + JSON envelope around the blob. Set it only if you change the envelope budget |
| `PINNED_LIST_LIMIT` | `500` | Max pinned logs returned by `GET /browse/:appId/pinned` |
| `FOLDER_MAX_LEN` | `200` | Max length of a whole normalized folder path |
| `FOLDER_MAX_DEPTH` | `8` | Max `/`-separated segments in a folder path |
| `FOLDER_SEGMENT_MAX_LEN` | `60` | Max length of one folder path segment |
| `FOLDER_MOVE_MAX_IDS` | `500` | Max log ids accepted in one `POST /api/folders/move` |
| `TRIAGE_TAG_MAX_LEN` | `32` | Max chars per tag (longer tags are truncated, not rejected) |
| `TRIAGE_TAG_MAX_COUNT` | `20` | Max tags kept per log (extras dropped after dedupe) |
| `CRASH_SIG_TOP_K` | `8` | Top normalized stack frames folded into a crash signature (higher = finer grouping) |
| `CRASH_RECOMPUTE_BATCH` | `200` | Max pre-feature logs the crashes view backfills with a signature per request. `0` disables the lazy backfill |
| `SEARCH_MAX_RESULTS` | `100` | Max catalog rows returned by a single full-text search query |
| `SEARCH_SNIPPET_TOKENS` | `12` | Max snippet length per search result, in whitespace tokens |
| `SEARCH_BACKFILL_BATCH` | `200` | Max not-yet-indexed logs the search route lazily indexes per request. `0` disables it (use `npm run backfill-fts`) |
| `SINKS_CONFIG` | `<server root>/config/sinks.json` | Path to the global sinks file. Point it outside the app tree when the config is a mounted volume or must survive a deploy that overwrites `config/` |
| `REDMINE_URL` | _(none)_ | Redmine base URL. Empty disables the "create issue from log" feature (button hidden, endpoint 503) |
| `REDMINE_API_KEY` | _(none)_ | Redmine REST API key (`X-Redmine-API-Key`). Server-side only. Empty also disables the feature |
| `REDMINE_PROJECT_ID` | _(none)_ | Redmine project id/identifier. **Required** for issue creation (with URL+key set but this empty, the endpoint returns 502) |
| `REDMINE_TRACKER_ID` | _(none)_ | Optional numeric tracker id; empty uses the project default |
| `REDMINE_PUBLIC_URL` | `REDMINE_URL` | Browser-facing Redmine URL for the issue link. Set it when the server reaches Redmine on a private address the browser cannot resolve: `REDMINE_URL` makes the API calls, this one is what teammates click |
| `REDMINE_IMPERSONATE_TESTER` | `0` | Create the issue **as** the log's tester (`X-Redmine-Switch-User`) so the Author is the QA who reported it. Needs an admin api key and a tester name that is a real Redmine login; otherwise it falls back to the api-key user |
| `REDMINE_TIMEOUT_MS` | `10000` | Per-request timeout (ms) for the outbound Redmine call |

## Adding an app

```bash
# With a token (minted and printed once on stdout) - the default:
npm run add-app -- mygame "My Game Title"

# Open ingest, no token:
npm run add-app -- mygame "My Game Title" --no-token

# 14-day retention, raised ceiling:
npm run add-app -- mygame "My Game Title" 14 --max-retention 90
```

The third positional is `retentionDays`, clamped to `[1, max-retention]`. Other flags: `--keep-token` (preserve the existing token on update), `--token V` (use a token you supply), `--disabled`.

The ingest token is shown **once** and never stored in plaintext (only its sha256). Distribute it to the game client via a secure channel (CI secret / 1Password). Re-running `add-app` on an existing app without `--keep-token` mints a **new** token and invalidates the old one.

## Sinks (forwarding)

On each successful ingest the server optionally forwards a payload (see CONTRACT.md section 5) to configured sinks. Copy `config/sinks.example.json` to `config/sinks.json` and fill in real webhook URLs. Set `SINKS_CONFIG` to read that file from somewhere else instead (handy when the config is a mounted volume rather than part of the image, and when it must stay outside a tree that a deploy overwrites); it defaults to `<server root>/config/sinks.json`. The effective sink list is the global file plus the app's own `sinks_json`, so a project can add a destination without touching the shared config.

Supported sink types: `slack`, `discord`, `webhook` (generic), `googlesheet`, `confluence`.

## Catalog features

The catalog (`/browse`, viewer-token gated) groups and triages logs:

- **Crashes** (`/browse/:appId/crashes`): logs are grouped by a normalized stack signature (computed at ingest from the error/exception line plus the top stack frames). Each group shows the count, distinct testers, versions, and a "NEW in `<version>`" / "REGRESSION in `<version>`" badge when a crash first appears in - or returns to - the latest version.
- **Full-text search** (`/api/search?appId=&q=`, search box on the versions page): SQLite FTS5 over each log's title, tester, comment, context values, scene snapshot and the log body. Results show a relevance-ranked list with a match snippet. Pre-feature logs are indexed lazily per query and/or via `npm run backfill-fts`.
- **Sessions** (`/browse/:appId?session=<id>`): when a client sends a per-launch `sessionId`, the catalog and viewer link "all logs of this session" so one app run's reports group together.
- **Size dashboard**: per-project and per-version storage totals, pinned counts, and the largest logs.
- **Status + tags**: each log has a triage status (new / triaged / in_progress / fixed / wontfix) and free-form tags, set from the viewer page and filtered in the catalog. Open by link by default (set `TRIAGE_REQUIRES_ADMIN=1` to restrict). Triage does not affect retention.
- **Folders** (`GET /api/folders`, `POST /api/folders/move`): manual organisation. A log belongs to at most one folder path (root = no folder); the catalog moves a selection at once, bounded by the `FOLDER_*` limits.
- **Engine**: the catalog shows each project's engine (Unity / GameMaker), detected from the log device info.

## Files

Separate from log ingest, a client can upload standalone files (`POST /api/files`, served at `/files/:id` and `/files/:id/download`): the Unity client's `SendFile`, `SendFolder` and `SendSnapshot`. They carry their own body ceiling (`MAX_FILE_BODY_BYTES`, not the 8 MB `MAX_PAYLOAD_BYTES`), their own retention, and their own sweeper pass.

An upload may attach itself to a log via `logId`, and hint what it is via `kind`: `file`, `folder`, `save`, `snapshot`, `screenshot`, `archive`, `other`. An unknown hint collapses to none rather than failing the upload. `kind: "snapshot"` is the game-state archive `SendSnapshot` produces (saves + registered data, **not** the log); the viewer's Saves / Data panel lists it first.

## Redmine (create issue from log)

Set `REDMINE_URL`, `REDMINE_API_KEY`, and `REDMINE_PROJECT_ID` to enable a "+ Redmine issue" button on the log viewer. It creates a Redmine issue (POST `/issues.json` with `X-Redmine-API-Key`) that links back to the log, and stores the issue id/url so a second click returns the existing issue (idempotent). The endpoint is viewer-token gated. Leaving `REDMINE_URL` or `REDMINE_API_KEY` empty disables the feature (the button is hidden).

## Deploying

### Docker (fastest)

```bash
cd server
cp .env.example .env        # set BASE_URL, tokens, IP_SALT, etc.
docker compose up -d        # builds the image, mounts ./data and ./blobs
# Register an app inside the container:
docker compose exec fastlogs node scripts/add-app.js mygame "My Game" 30
```

See `Dockerfile` and `docker-compose.yml`. The image runs `src/server.js` and
exposes port 8787.

### systemd / VPS

See `deploy/INSTALL.md` for a generic one-time setup (any VPS: systemd units,
`/var/lib/fastlogs`, nginx reverse proxy, certbot), and
[../docs/ADMIN-GUIDE.md](../docs/ADMIN-GUIDE.md) for the operational walkthrough.

For subsequent deploys:
```bash
./deploy/deploy.sh user@host
```

The deploy script rsyncs source files, runs `npm ci --omit=dev` on the server, applies migrations, and restarts `fastlogsd.service`.

## Tests

The canonical command is:

```bash
npm test
```

It runs `test/run.js`, which discovers every `*.test.js` file and hands the explicit list to the built-in `node:test` runner. Don't run `node --test test/` (bare dir -> `MODULE_NOT_FOUND`); call the runner directly only with a glob: `node --test "test/*.test.js"`.

Tests use `node:test` (built-in, no extra deps) and spin up a real server on a random port with an isolated SQLite DB in `os.tmpdir()`. No network calls to external services.

```

`server/config/sinks.example.json`:

```json
{
  "_comment": "Example global sinks config for FastLogs. Copy to config/sinks.json and edit. The file may be a bare array or this { sinks: [...] } wrapper. Each sink: { type, name?, enabled?, filter?, ...typeFields }. Per-app sinks (apps.sinks_json) are appended to this list. Replace all placeholder URLs/tokens. No real secrets in the repo.",

  "sinks": [
    {
      "type": "slack",
      "name": "qa-channel",
      "enabled": true,
      "filter": { "errorOnly": true },
      "url": "https://hooks.slack.com/services/REPLACE/WITH/REAL",
      "username": "FastLogs",
      "iconEmoji": ":rotating_light:",
      "mentionOnError": "<!here>"
    },

    {
      "type": "discord",
      "name": "dev-alerts",
      "enabled": false,
      "filter": { "minError": 1, "platforms": ["WebGL", "Android", "iOS"] },
      "url": "https://discord.com/api/webhooks/REPLACE/WITH-REAL-TOKEN",
      "username": "FastLogs",
      "mentionOnError": "@here"
    },

    {
      "type": "webhook",
      "name": "generic-json",
      "enabled": false,
      "filter": { "minWarn": 5, "excludePlatforms": ["Windows", "macOS", "Linux"] },
      "url": "https://example.com/ingest",
      "method": "POST",
      "headers": {
        "X-Api-Key": "REPLACE_ME",
        "X-Project": "{{project}}"
      },
      "bodyTemplate": {
        "text": "{{projectName}} {{version}} [{{platform}}] {{counts.summary}}",
        "link": "{{url}}",
        "errors": "{{counts.error}}",
        "raw": "{{json}}"
      },
      "retries": 3,
      "timeoutMs": 10000
    },

    {
      "type": "googlesheet",
      "name": "qa-spreadsheet",
      "enabled": false,
      "url": "https://script.google.com/macros/s/REPLACE_DEPLOY_ID/exec",
      "secret": "shared-secret-checked-by-apps-script"
    },

    {
      "type": "confluence",
      "name": "release-notes",
      "enabled": false,
      "mode": "webhook",
      "filter": { "apps": ["lfa"] },
      "url": "https://automation.atlassian.com/pro/hooks/REPLACE_WITH_WEBHOOK_ID"
    }
  ]
}

```

`server/deploy/INSTALL.md`:

```md
# FastLogs - generic server installation

This guide sets up FastLogs on any VPS with systemd, nginx, and Node. For a
container-based deploy see `server/Dockerfile` and `server/docker-compose.yml`
(run `docker compose up -d` after filling in `.env`).

Replace `your-domain.example` everywhere with your real domain.

## Prerequisites

- A Linux host with systemd (Ubuntu 22.04+ or similar)
- Node.js >= 18, installed system-wide at `/usr/bin/node`. The systemd units
  hardcode that path (`ExecStart=/usr/bin/node`), so an `nvm`-managed Node inside
  a user's home will not start the service - install it from your distro or from
  NodeSource. Check with `node -v` that you actually got >= 18: some LTS
  distributions still ship an older Node under the `nodejs` package.
- nginx
- certbot, plus `python3-certbot-nginx` (step 7 issues the certificate through
  the nginx plugin)
- A build toolchain for `better-sqlite3` (`apt install python3 make g++`). The
  module ships prebuilt binaries for common Node/OS combinations and falls back
  to compiling; without the toolchain that fallback breaks `npm ci`.

---

## 1. System user

```bash
useradd --system --create-home --home-dir /var/lib/fastlogs --shell /usr/sbin/nologin fastlogs
chmod 700 /var/lib/fastlogs
```

## 2. Deploy user (SSH-only, no root)

Create a deploy user that can rsync source files and restart only the FastLogs
service. The deploy user has **no** root access.

```bash
useradd --system --create-home --shell /bin/bash deploy
mkdir -p /home/deploy/.ssh
# Paste the deploy SSH public key:
nano /home/deploy/.ssh/authorized_keys
chmod 700 /home/deploy/.ssh && chmod 600 /home/deploy/.ssh/authorized_keys
chown -R deploy:deploy /home/deploy/.ssh
```

Scope the deploy user's sudo to exactly what a deploy needs:

```bash
# /etc/sudoers.d/deploy-fastlogs
deploy ALL=(fastlogs) NOPASSWD: /usr/bin/node, /usr/bin/npm
deploy ALL=(root)     NOPASSWD: /bin/systemctl restart fastlogsd
```

## 3. App and data directories

```bash
mkdir -p /var/lib/fastlogs/app
chown fastlogs:fastlogs /var/lib/fastlogs/app

mkdir -p /var/lib/fastlogs/data /var/lib/fastlogs/blobs
chown fastlogs:fastlogs /var/lib/fastlogs/data /var/lib/fastlogs/blobs
chmod 750 /var/lib/fastlogs/data /var/lib/fastlogs/blobs
```

Sync the repository's `server/` tree into `/var/lib/fastlogs/app/server`, then:

```bash
cd /var/lib/fastlogs/app/server && npm ci --omit=dev
```

For subsequent deploys `./deploy/deploy.sh` does the sync for you. Point it at the
`deploy` user from step 2 (`./deploy/deploy.sh deploy@your-domain.example`), never
at `fastlogs`: that account has a `nologin` shell and no `authorized_keys`, so SSH
to it can never work.

Expect to adapt the script before its first run. As shipped it assumes its SSH user
can write `/var/lib/fastlogs/app/server` and run `npm`, `node` and `systemctl`
directly, and the layout above grants `deploy` none of that: `/var/lib/fastlogs` is
`700 fastlogs`, `app/` belongs to `fastlogs`, and `deploy`'s sudo is scoped to
node/npm as `fastlogs` plus a single `systemctl restart`. Either open the path and
the app tree to `deploy` and prefix the script's remote commands to match those
sudoers rules, or run it as `root@your-domain.example` and accept that every deploy
then has full root.

Its migrate step needs one more thing. systemd hands the service
`/etc/fastlogs/fastlogs.env`, but a command over SSH gets no `EnvironmentFile`, so
`node scripts/migrate.js` falls back to `DATA_DIR=./data` and quietly builds an
empty database inside the app directory instead of migrating the real one. Load the
env file around it the same way step 9 does.

## 4. Config file

```bash
mkdir /etc/fastlogs
cp /var/lib/fastlogs/app/server/.env.example /etc/fastlogs/fastlogs.env
chmod 640 /etc/fastlogs/fastlogs.env
chown root:fastlogs /etc/fastlogs/fastlogs.env
# Fill in ADMIN_TOKEN, VIEWER_TOKEN, IP_SALT, BASE_URL (https://your-domain.example), etc.
# Point DATA_DIR and BLOB_DIR at /var/lib/fastlogs/data and /var/lib/fastlogs/blobs.
nano /etc/fastlogs/fastlogs.env
```

## 5. systemd units

```bash
cp /var/lib/fastlogs/app/server/deploy/fastlogsd.service          /etc/systemd/system/
cp /var/lib/fastlogs/app/server/deploy/fastlogs-backup.service    /etc/systemd/system/
cp /var/lib/fastlogs/app/server/deploy/fastlogs-backup.timer      /etc/systemd/system/

systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl enable --now fastlogsd
systemctl enable --now fastlogs-backup.timer
```

The backup timer runs `scripts/backup.js` once a day: an online SQLite `.backup`
(safe under WAL) plus a tar of the blobs into `<DATA_DIR>/backups`, keeping the
newest `BACKUP_KEEP` snapshots of each type. Snapshots stay on the same box, so see
[ADMIN-GUIDE.md](../../docs/ADMIN-GUIDE.md) section 6 for offsite copies and restore.

The server sweeps expired logs by itself, so there is no sweeper timer above. With
the shipped `SWEEP_INTERVAL_SEC=3600` it deletes expired, non-pinned logs once at
startup and then every hour, in-process. The `fastlogs-sweeper` units are an
**alternative** to that, not an addition: enabling both leaves two hourly sweepers
writing to the same SQLite file. Install them only if you first set
`SWEEP_INTERVAL_SEC=0` in `/etc/fastlogs/fastlogs.env`:

```bash
# Only with SWEEP_INTERVAL_SEC=0 in /etc/fastlogs/fastlogs.env:
cp /var/lib/fastlogs/app/server/deploy/fastlogs-sweeper.service   /etc/systemd/system/
cp /var/lib/fastlogs/app/server/deploy/fastlogs-sweeper.timer     /etc/systemd/system/

systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl enable --now fastlogs-sweeper.timer
```

## 6. DNS

Add an A record: `your-domain.example -> <server IP>` and wait until it resolves.
Certbot validates over HTTP, so this must be live before the next step.

## 7. TLS certificate

Get the certificate **before** enabling the site. The shipped vhost already points at
`/etc/letsencrypt/live/your-domain.example/fullchain.pem`,
`/etc/letsencrypt/options-ssl-nginx.conf` and `/etc/letsencrypt/ssl-dhparams.pem`,
so `nginx -t` in the next step fails with "cannot load certificate" until they exist.

```bash
certbot certonly --nginx -d your-domain.example
```

`certonly` issues the certificate without letting certbot rewrite the vhost, which
already carries its own TLS lines. Running it through the nginx plugin is also what
installs `options-ssl-nginx.conf` and `ssl-dhparams.pem` into `/etc/letsencrypt`,
and it leaves automatic renewal working with no extra hooks. Do not substitute
`certonly --standalone`: that never loads the nginx plugin, so those two files are
never created and step 8 fails on the `include` line.

## 8. nginx reverse proxy

```bash
cp /var/lib/fastlogs/app/server/deploy/nginx-fastlogs.conf \
   /etc/nginx/sites-available/your-domain.example
# Edit the file and replace your-domain.example with your domain.
ln -s /etc/nginx/sites-available/your-domain.example \
      /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/
nginx -t && systemctl reload nginx
```

## 9. Register the first app

```bash
# The scripts read DATA_DIR/BLOB_DIR from the environment; only systemd reads
# /etc/fastlogs/fastlogs.env. Run without it, add-app.js would create a second,
# empty database under app/server/data and mint the token into that one.
sudo -u fastlogs bash -c 'set -a; . /etc/fastlogs/fastlogs.env; set +a; \
  exec node /var/lib/fastlogs/app/server/scripts/add-app.js mygame "My Game" 30'
# Prints the ingest token once - store it in the game client config.
```

The same goes for every other script you run by hand (`sweep.js`, `backup.js`,
`list-apps.js`, `migrate.js`): load the env file first. The systemd units already
carry it via `EnvironmentFile=`.

## 10. Sinks (optional forwarding)

On each successful ingest the server can forward a small payload (see
`../../CONTRACT.md` section 5) to Slack / Discord / a generic webhook / etc. The
destinations live in a JSON config file, copied from `config/sinks.example.json`.

Keep that file **outside** the synced app tree: `deploy.sh` rsyncs `server/` with
`--delete` and does not exclude `config/`, so a sinks.json placed there is wiped by
the next deploy. Nothing in `config/` is gitignored either, so a copy made in a
checkout is one `git add .` away from leaking your webhook URLs. Point
`SINKS_CONFIG` at the secrets directory instead:

```bash
cp /var/lib/fastlogs/app/server/config/sinks.example.json /etc/fastlogs/sinks.json
chmod 640 /etc/fastlogs/sinks.json && chown root:fastlogs /etc/fastlogs/sinks.json
# Fill in real webhook URLs, then add this line to /etc/fastlogs/fastlogs.env:
#   SINKS_CONFIG=/etc/fastlogs/sinks.json
# Per-app additions live in apps.sinks_json.
```

---

## SSH key rotation (deploy key)

1. Generate a new ed25519 key pair on the dev machine (**with** a passphrase):
   ```bash
   ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -C "deploy@fastlogs" -f ~/.ssh/deploy_fastlogs_new
   ```

2. Add the new public key to the server:
   ```bash
   # On the server (as root or the deploy user):
   echo "<new pubkey>" >> /home/deploy/.ssh/authorized_keys
   ```

3. Verify the new key works:
   ```bash
   ssh -i ~/.ssh/deploy_fastlogs_new deploy@your-domain.example "echo ok"
   ```

4. Remove the old public key from `/home/deploy/.ssh/authorized_keys`.

5. Delete the old key pair from the dev machine.

**Rules:**
- The deploy user has **no** root access. sudo is scoped to only
  `systemctl restart fastlogsd` and running node/npm as the `fastlogs` user.
- Set `PermitRootLogin prohibit-password` in `/etc/ssh/sshd_config`.
- Secrets (ADMIN_TOKEN, VIEWER_TOKEN, IP_SALT) stay in
  `/etc/fastlogs/fastlogs.env`, never in the repo.

```

`server/deploy/deploy.sh`:

```sh
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# FastLogs deployment script.
#
# SECURITY NOTES:
#   - Rotate the deploy SSH key periodically (see INSTALL.md - SSH key rotation).
#   - NEVER commit secrets (tokens, passwords) to the repository.
#   - The deploy user has NO sudo/root privileges.
#
# Usage: ./deploy/deploy.sh user@host
# Example: ./deploy/deploy.sh fastlogs@your-domain.example

set -euo pipefail

REMOTE="${1:-fastlogs@your-domain.example}"
APP_DIR="/var/lib/fastlogs/app"
SERVER_DIR="${APP_DIR}/server"

echo "==> Deploying FastLogs to ${REMOTE}"

# 1. Sync source and public assets (exclude dev/test files and secrets).
echo "--> Syncing source files..."
rsync -az --delete \
  --exclude 'node_modules/' \
  --exclude 'data/' \
  --exclude 'blobs/' \
  --exclude '.env' \
  --exclude 'test/' \
  --exclude '*.test.js' \
  "$(dirname "$0")/../" \
  "${REMOTE}:${SERVER_DIR}/"

# 2. Install production dependencies on the server.
echo "--> Installing dependencies (npm ci --omit=dev)..."
ssh "${REMOTE}" "cd ${SERVER_DIR} && npm ci --omit=dev"

# 3. Run database migrations.
echo "--> Running migrations..."
ssh "${REMOTE}" "cd ${SERVER_DIR} && node scripts/migrate.js"

# 4. Restart the service.
echo "--> Restarting fastlogsd..."
ssh "${REMOTE}" "systemctl restart fastlogsd"

echo "==> Deploy complete. Verify the /api/health endpoint on your domain."

```

# Secrets and tunable config live in /etc/fastlogs/fastlogs.env (mode 640, root:fastlogs).
# Copy .env.example, fill in tokens, then: systemctl restart fastlogsd.

Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=5
StandardOutput=journal
StandardError=journal

# Hardening.

# Let fastlogs write only to its own data dirs.
