`server/deploy/nginx-fastlogs.conf`:

```conf
# FastLogs - nginx virtual host. Replace your-domain.example with your domain.
# Place in /etc/nginx/sites-available/your-domain.example
# and symlink to sites-enabled. Requires a certbot TLS certificate.
# CORS headers are set by the Node app; do NOT add them here.
#
# The viewer/catalog assets (/viewer.css, /viewer.js, /browse.css, /browse.js)
# are served by the Node app from an explicit allowlist, so there is no separate
# nginx static block: everything is proxied to Node. This keeps the asset paths
# in exactly one place and avoids a dead /assets/ alias.

# Redirect plain HTTP -> HTTPS.
server {
    listen 80;
    listen [::]:80;
    server_name your-domain.example;

    location / {
        return 301 https://$host$request_uri;
    }
}

server {
    listen 443 ssl;
    listen [::]:443 ssl;
    server_name your-domain.example;

    # TLS - managed by certbot.
    ssl_certificate     /etc/letsencrypt/live/your-domain.example/fullchain.pem;
    ssl_certificate_key /etc/letsencrypt/live/your-domain.example/privkey.pem;
    include             /etc/letsencrypt/options-ssl-nginx.conf;
    ssl_dhparam         /etc/letsencrypt/ssl-dhparams.pem;

    # Do not let logs end up in search engines.
    add_header X-Robots-Tag "noindex, nofollow" always;

    # Guard for oversized ingest payloads. Log ingest stays small (the Node app
    # caps the log body), but file uploads (/api/files, base64) need the larger
    # MAX_FILE_BODY_BYTES (~34 MB for a 25 MB decoded cap), so allow up to 35m here.
    client_max_body_size 35m;

    # Everything goes to the Node app (it serves viewer/catalog assets too).
    location / {
        proxy_pass         http://127.0.0.1:8787;
        proxy_http_version 1.1;
        proxy_set_header   Host              $host;
        proxy_set_header   X-Real-IP         $remote_addr;
        proxy_set_header   X-Forwarded-For   $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
        proxy_set_header   X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
        proxy_set_header   Connection        "";

        # Give ingest uploads a generous timeout.
        proxy_read_timeout 60s;
        proxy_send_timeout 60s;
    }
}

```

`server/docker-compose.yml`:

```yml
# FastLogs - one-command local/VPS deploy.
#
#   cp .env.example .env   # set BASE_URL, ADMIN_TOKEN, VIEWER_TOKEN, IP_SALT
#   docker compose up -d   # builds the image and starts the server on :8787
#
# Register an app inside the running container:
#   docker compose exec fastlogs node scripts/add-app.js mygame "My Game" 30

services:
  fastlogs:
    build: .
    image: fastlogs-server
    container_name: fastlogs
    restart: unless-stopped
    # Configuration comes from .env (gitignored). PORT/DATA_DIR/BLOB_DIR are set
    # in the image; override anything here via the env file.
    env_file:
      - .env
    ports:
      # host:container - put nginx in front of this in production.
      # Bound to host loopback ONLY (do NOT change to 0.0.0.0): the service must
      # stay private behind nginx; a previous 0.0.0.0 binding exposed it.
      - "127.0.0.1:8787:8787"
    # Liveness probe so Docker restarts the container if the HTTP server wedges.
    # Uses node (always present in the image; slim base has no curl/wget) to GET
    # /api/health on the in-container loopback. Exit 0 only on HTTP 200.
    healthcheck:
      test:
        - CMD
        - node
        - -e
        - "require('node:http').get('http://127.0.0.1:'+(process.env.PORT||8787)+'/api/health',r=>process.exit(r.statusCode===200?0:1)).on('error',()=>process.exit(1))"
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 10s
    volumes:
      # Persist the SQLite DB and blob storage across container restarts.
      - ./data:/app/data
      - ./blobs:/app/blobs

```

`server/mcp/README.md`:

```md
# FastLogs MCP server

The FastLogs API as native tools for an AI agent, over [MCP](https://modelcontextprotocol.io) (stdio transport).

An agent pointed at this server can drive a running build and read the reports back on its own: send a command into the build, wait for the result, check that the channel is alive, block until a report with a given code lands, search the logs. No file bridges, no CLI wrappers, no copy-pasting log links: the loop "do something in the build -> read what happened" closes inside the conversation.

This is an isolated subpackage. It has its **own** `package.json` and its own dependencies, and it is not part of the server runtime: the server core keeps its single dependency (`better-sqlite3`) and does not know this directory exists.

## Install

```bash
cd server/mcp
npm install
```

Requires Node >= 18 (the code uses the global `fetch`). The FastLogs server itself runs separately: this package only talks to its HTTP API.

## Environment

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| `FASTLOGS_BASE_URL` | `http://localhost:8787` | Base URL of the FastLogs server. Paths are built under the canonical `/api/v1` prefix |
| `FASTLOGS_COMMAND_TOKEN` | _(none)_ | Token for the command channel: the server's `COMMAND_TOKEN` (an admin token also works). Without it the command tools fail with a clear message |
| `FASTLOGS_VIEWER_TOKEN` | _(none)_ | Token for reading logs: the server's `VIEWER_TOKEN` (an admin token also works). Needed by `fastlogs_await_log` and `fastlogs_search_logs` |
| `FASTLOGS_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS` | `15000` | Per-request HTTP timeout (clamped to 1000..120000) |
| `FASTLOGS_POLL_INTERVAL_MS` | `1000` | How often the waiting tools re-ask the server; there is no long-poll (clamped to 100..30000) |

### About the tokens

Give the agent the scoped `COMMAND_TOKEN`, not the admin token. It grants **only** the three command endpoints (enqueue, read result, channel health) and nothing else, so a misfiring agent cannot unpin logs, retriage them, or reach the catalog. Admin is a superset and works too, but there is no reason to hand it over.

`FASTLOGS_VIEWER_TOKEN` is the team viewer token, the same one the catalog uses. `fastlogs_get_log` needs no token at all: a report id is its own capability.

## MCP client configuration

Claude Code, with absolute paths:

```bash
claude mcp add fastlogs \
  --env FASTLOGS_BASE_URL=https://logs.example.com \
  --env FASTLOGS_COMMAND_TOKEN=<command-token> \
  --env FASTLOGS_VIEWER_TOKEN=<viewer-token> \
  -- node /abs/path/to/fastlogs/server/mcp/index.js
```

Or as a JSON fragment, for any client that takes an `mcpServers` map:

```jsonc
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "fastlogs": {
      "command": "node",
      "args": ["/abs/path/to/fastlogs/server/mcp/index.js"],
      "env": {
        "FASTLOGS_BASE_URL": "https://logs.example.com",
        "FASTLOGS_COMMAND_TOKEN": "<command-token>",
        "FASTLOGS_VIEWER_TOKEN": "<viewer-token>"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## Tools

| Tool | Parameters | What it does |
|------|-----------|--------------|
| `fastlogs_run_command` | `appId`, `name`, `args?`, `timeoutSec=30` | Send a command to the build and wait for the result. Enqueues, then polls until the build acks (`done`/`error`), the command expires unclaimed, or the timeout hits. The main way to drive a build |
| `fastlogs_enqueue_command` | `appId`, `name`, `args?`, `ttlSec?` | Queue a command without waiting; returns its id. For long commands, or several in flight |
| `fastlogs_command_result` | `appId`, `id` | Read one command's state: `queued`, `dispatched`, `done`, `error` or `expired` |
| `fastlogs_channel_health` | `appId` | Is a build polling the channel right now (`pollingNow`, `lastPollAt`, `lastPollSecAgo`)? Ask this first when commands expire unclaimed |
| `fastlogs_await_log` | `appId`, `code`, `timeoutSec=60` | Block until a report carrying a correlation code is uploaded; returns its id and links |
| `fastlogs_search_logs` | `appId`, `q`, `version?` | Full-text search over one app's reports (title, tester, comment, context, scene snapshot, log body). Words are AND-ed; a trailing `*` is a prefix match |
| `fastlogs_get_log` | `id` | One report's metadata and links. The log text itself is at the returned `rawUrl` |

Notes:

- `args` is any JSON value (serialized cap: 8192 bytes). `result` comes back parsed when it is JSON, so a scene snapshot arrives as an object rather than one escaped string; `resultParsed` says which happened.
- `fastlogs_run_command` gives the command a TTL equal to `timeoutSec`, so a command the agent stopped waiting for is normally dead the moment the wait ends. The server floors any TTL at 5 seconds, so a `timeoutSec` under 5 leaves a short window in which a build can still claim it after the tool gave up.
- HTTP failures surface as readable tool errors (`HTTP 404 not_found: Unknown appId`), not stack traces.

## Big results arrive whole

A command result over the server's inline cap (`COMMAND_MAX_RESULT_BYTES`, 64 KB by default) is not truncated and does not fail: the build uploads it as a file and the ack carries a reference instead of the payload.

```jsonc
{ "__fastlogsResultRef": "https://logs.example.com/files/aB3xY9/download", "bytes": 150000 }
```

`fastlogs_run_command` and `fastlogs_command_result` follow that reference and download the body before answering, so a 2 MB scene dump reads exactly like a 200-byte one: `result` holds the real content, on the same terms as any other result. The reference rides along as `resultRef` / `resultBytes` when you want to know an offload happened.

Nothing to configure. Two cases worth recognising, both surfaced as `resultRefError` with `result: null`:

- **Refused.** An ack rides in on the ingest tier, which is open for tokenless apps, so the url is untrusted input. Only an absolute url on the configured `FASTLOGS_BASE_URL` under `/files/` is ever fetched; anything else is refused unfetched (a hostile ack must not turn the agent's machine into a read-SSRF against cloud metadata or an intranet). If you see this, `FASTLOGS_BASE_URL` probably disagrees with the server's `BASE_URL`.
- **Download failed.** The reference pointed at our server but the file was gone or unreachable (retention swept it, or the network dropped).

## Typical run

1. `fastlogs_channel_health(appId: "lfa")` -> `pollingNow: true` confirms a build is listening. If nothing ever polled: the game may never have switched the channel on (`Commands.Enabled`, see [unity/README.md](../../unity/README.md#commands-agent---game-channel)), the build is not running, it is on a different `appId`, or it has no FastLogs command client (the GameMaker client does not implement the channel).
2. `fastlogs_run_command(appId: "lfa", name: "fastlogs.scenecontext", args: { ... })` -> the build captures a scene snapshot and uploads a report; the result carries its id.
3. `fastlogs_get_log(id: "<id>")` -> metadata and links; fetch `rawUrl` for the log text.

When a command triggers a report indirectly rather than returning it, use `fastlogs_await_log` with the correlation code instead of step 3, then `fastlogs_get_log` on the id it returns.

Command names are whitelisted on the game side: the server is only a queue and executes nothing. See [CONTRACT.md](../../CONTRACT.md) section 8 for the channel, and [docs/UNITY.md](../../docs/UNITY.md#built-in-commands) for the built-in names a build answers and the arguments each one takes. A game adds its own with `FastLogs.RegisterCommand`.

## Command to report, in one loop

A command tags every report it produces with its own id as the correlation code, so
there is no timestamp matching: run the command, then await its id.

```
fastlogs_run_command(appId, "fastlogs.send", { title: "repro" })  -> { id: "SCveDL", ... }
fastlogs_await_log(appId, "SCveDL")                               -> { found: true, url: ... }
```

The same holds for a custom handler: anything it sends while the command runs carries
that command id. Turn it off game-side with `Commands.CorrelateReports` if the game
drives correlation codes itself.

```

`server/mcp/index.js`:

```js
#!/usr/bin/env node

// FastLogs MCP server: the REST API (CONTRACT.md) as native agent tools.
//
// An AI agent drives a running build through the command channel (CONTRACT
// section 8) and reads the reports back, without file bridges or CLI wrappers:
//
//   fastlogs_channel_health   -> is the build polling for commands right now?
//   fastlogs_run_command      -> enqueue + poll until done/error/expired
//   fastlogs_enqueue_command  -> enqueue only, returns the command id
//   fastlogs_command_result   -> read one command's state/result
//   fastlogs_await_log        -> block until a report with a correlation code lands
//   fastlogs_search_logs      -> full-text search over one app's logs
//   fastlogs_get_log          -> one report's metadata
//
// Transport is stdio; the config comes from the environment (see README.md).
// All paths use the canonical /api/v1 prefix.

import { McpServer } from '@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/mcp.js';
import { StdioServerTransport } from '@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/stdio.js';
import { z } from 'zod';
import { RESULT_REF_KEY, isOffloadedResultUrl } from './result-ref.js';

const DEFAULT_BASE_URL = 'http://localhost:8787';

const config = {
  baseUrl: (process.env.FASTLOGS_BASE_URL || DEFAULT_BASE_URL).replace(/\/+$/, ''),
  commandToken: process.env.FASTLOGS_COMMAND_TOKEN || '',
  viewerToken: process.env.FASTLOGS_VIEWER_TOKEN || '',
  // Per-request HTTP timeout; the poll loops have their own overall deadline.
  requestTimeoutMs: clampInt(process.env.FASTLOGS_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS, 15000, 1000, 120000),
  // The server has no long-poll: both poll loops re-ask on this interval.
  pollIntervalMs: clampInt(process.env.FASTLOGS_POLL_INTERVAL_MS, 1000, 100, 30000),
};

function clampInt(raw, fallback, min, max) {
  const n = Number.parseInt(raw, 10);
  if (!Number.isFinite(n)) return fallback;
  return Math.max(min, Math.min(max, n));
}

const sleep = (ms) => new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, ms));

// An HTTP error carrying the contract's { error, message } pair, so tools can
// report "404 not_found: ..." instead of a stack trace.
class ApiError extends Error {
  constructor(status, code, message, jsonBody = true) {
    super(message);
    this.status = status;
    this.code = code;
    // False when the body was not a contract error object: an unmatched route
    // answers with a plain-text 404, which tells a missing ENDPOINT apart from
    // a missing resource.
    this.jsonBody = jsonBody;
  }
  toString() {
    return `HTTP ${this.status} ${this.code}: ${this.message}`;
  }
}

// Build an /api/v1 URL with optional query parameters.
function apiUrl(path, query) {
  const url = new URL(config.baseUrl + '/api/v1' + path);
  for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(query || {})) {
    if (value !== undefined && value !== null && value !== '') url.searchParams.set(key, String(value));
  }
  return url;
}

// One JSON request. Resolves with the parsed body, or throws an ApiError on a
// non-2xx / transport / timeout / parse failure.
async function request(method, url, { token, body } = {}) {
  const headers = {};
  if (token) headers.authorization = `Bearer ${token}`;
  let payload;
  if (body !== undefined) {
    headers['content-type'] = 'application/json';
    payload = JSON.stringify(body);
  }

  // AbortController bounds a hung connection (fetch has no default timeout).
  const controller = new AbortController();
  const timer = setTimeout(() => controller.abort(), config.requestTimeoutMs);
  let res;
  try {
    res = await fetch(url, { method, headers, body: payload, signal: controller.signal });
  } catch (err) {
    if (err && err.name === 'AbortError') {
      throw new ApiError(0, 'timeout', `No response from ${config.baseUrl} within ${config.requestTimeoutMs}ms`);
    }
    throw new ApiError(0, 'network_error', `Cannot reach ${config.baseUrl}: ${(err && err.message) || err}`);
  } finally {
    clearTimeout(timer);
  }

  const raw = await res.text();
  if (!res.ok) {
    // Error bodies are { error, message }; fall back to the raw text when the
    // failure came from something that is not the FastLogs server (a proxy).
    let code = 'http_error';
    let message = raw.slice(0, 300);
    let jsonBody = false;
    try {
      const parsed = JSON.parse(raw);
      if (parsed && parsed.error) {
        code = parsed.error;
        jsonBody = true;
      }
      if (parsed && parsed.message) message = parsed.message;
    } catch {
      // keep the raw text
    }
    throw new ApiError(res.status, code, message, jsonBody);
  }
  if (!raw) return {};
  try {
    return JSON.parse(raw);
  } catch {
    throw new ApiError(res.status, 'bad_response', 'Server returned a non-JSON body');
  }
}

// Resolve the token for a tier, or throw a tool-level error naming the env var
// the operator has to set.
function requireToken(tier) {
  if (tier === 'command') {
    if (!config.commandToken) {
      throw new ApiError(0, 'no_token', 'FASTLOGS_COMMAND_TOKEN is not set (a command or admin token is required for the command channel)');
    }
    return config.commandToken;
  }
  if (!config.viewerToken) {
    throw new ApiError(0, 'no_token', 'FASTLOGS_VIEWER_TOKEN is not set (a viewer or admin token is required to read logs)');
  }
  return config.viewerToken;
}

// The contract carries args/result as serialized JSON STRINGS. Parse them for
// the agent when they parse, so a scene snapshot arrives as an object and not
// as one escaped blob.
function maybeJson(value) {
  if (typeof value !== 'string') return { value: value ?? null, parsed: false };
  try {
    return { value: JSON.parse(value), parsed: true };
  } catch {
    return { value, parsed: false };
  }
}

// Shape a command-state response (8d) into the tool's output object.
function shapeCommand(res) {
  const args = maybeJson(res.args);
  const result = maybeJson(res.result);
  return {
    id: res.id,
    name: res.name,
    status: res.status,
    args: args.value,
    result: result.value,
    resultParsed: result.parsed,
    error: res.error ?? null,
    createdAt: res.createdAt,
    dispatchedAt: res.dispatchedAt ?? null,
    completedAt: res.completedAt ?? null,
    expiresAt: res.expiresAt,
  };
}

// A result over the server's inline cap is uploaded as a file and the ack carries
// {"__fastlogsResultRef": url}. Download it so a caller never has to know that: a big
// scene dump reads exactly like a small one.
async function derefResult(shaped) {
  const v = shaped.result;
  if (!v || typeof v !== 'object' || typeof v[RESULT_REF_KEY] !== 'string') return shaped;

  const url = v[RESULT_REF_KEY];
  shaped.resultRef = url;
  shaped.resultBytes = typeof v.bytes === 'number' ? v.bytes : null;
  // The ref rides in on an ack, i.e. untrusted input: only our own /files/ is ever
  // a legitimate offload target (see result-ref.js).
  if (!isOffloadedResultUrl(url, config.baseUrl)) {
    shaped.result = null;
    shaped.resultParsed = false;
    shaped.resultRefError = `Refused to download the offloaded result from ${url}: an offloaded result must be an absolute url under ${config.baseUrl}/files/`;
    return shaped;
  }
  try {
    const r = await fetch(url, { signal: AbortSignal.timeout(config.requestTimeoutMs) });
    if (!r.ok) throw new Error(`HTTP ${r.status}`);
    const text = await r.text();
    const parsed = maybeJson(text);
    shaped.result = parsed.value;
    shaped.resultParsed = parsed.parsed;
  } catch (err) {
    shaped.result = null;
    shaped.resultParsed = false;
    shaped.resultRefError = `Could not download the offloaded result from ${url}: ${err.message}`;
  }
  return shaped;
}

const ok = (data) => ({ content: [{ type: 'text', text: JSON.stringify(data, null, 2) }] });
const fail = (text) => ({ content: [{ type: 'text', text }], isError: true });

// Run a tool body, mapping an ApiError to a readable tool error.
async function guard(fn) {
  try {
    return await fn();
  } catch (err) {
    if (err instanceof ApiError) return fail(err.toString());
    return fail(`Unexpected error: ${(err && err.message) || err}`);
  }
}

// --- API calls -------------------------------------------------------------

function enqueueCommand(appId, name, args, ttlSec) {
  const body = { name };
  if (args !== undefined) body.args = args;
  if (Number.isFinite(ttlSec)) body.ttlSec = ttlSec;
  return request('POST', apiUrl(`/commands/${encodeURIComponent(appId)}`), {
    token: requireToken('command'),
    body,
  });
}

function readCommand(appId, id) {
  const path = `/commands/${encodeURIComponent(appId)}/result/${encodeURIComponent(id)}`;
  return request('GET', apiUrl(path), { token: requireToken('command') });
}

function readChannelHealth(appId) {
  const path = `/commands/${encodeURIComponent(appId)}/health`;
  return request('GET', apiUrl(path), { token: requireToken('command') });
}

function awaitOnce(appId, code) {
  return request('GET', apiUrl(`/await/${encodeURIComponent(appId)}`, { code }), {
    token: requireToken('viewer'),
  });
}

// --- Tools -----------------------------------------------------------------

const server = new McpServer({ name: 'fastlogs', version: '0.1.0' });

const appIdSchema = z.string().regex(/^[a-z0-9_-]{2,32}$/, 'appId must be 2-32 chars [a-z0-9_-]');
const commandNameSchema = z.string().regex(/^[a-z0-9_.-]{1,64}$/, 'name must match ^[a-z0-9_.-]{1,64}$');

server.registerTool(
  'fastlogs_run_command',
  {
    title: 'Run a command in the build',
    description:
      'Send a command to a running build and wait for its result. Enqueues the command, then polls until the build acks it (done/error), it expires unclaimed, or timeoutSec elapses. This is the main way to drive a build (e.g. "fastlogs.scenecontext" for a scene snapshot, or "fastlogs.inspect" with args {type:"ParticleSystemRenderer"} to read the live state of one component). An "expired" status means no build was polling the channel: check fastlogs_channel_health. Any report the command produces is tagged with the command id, so fastlogs_await_log(appId, code=<that id>) finds it.',
    inputSchema: {
      appId: appIdSchema.describe('Target app id, as registered on the server.'),
      name: commandNameSchema.describe('Command name, whitelisted on the game side (e.g. "fastlogs.scenecontext").'),
      args: z.unknown().optional().describe('Any JSON value passed to the command handler (serialized cap: 8192 bytes).'),
      timeoutSec: z.number().int().min(1).max(600).default(30)
        .describe('How long to wait for the result. Also used as the command TTL, so an unclaimed command expires with the wait.'),
    },
  },
  ({ appId, name, args, timeoutSec = 30 }) => guard(async () => {
    // TTL tracks the wait so an abandoned command cannot be claimed after the
    // agent gave up. Both ends land together, so the timeout below normally
    // fires before the server reports "expired"; the expired branch stays for
    // the server-side clamp (ttl is floored at 5s for a shorter wait).
    const enq = await enqueueCommand(appId, name, args, timeoutSec);
    const id = enq && enq.id;
    if (!id) return fail('Enqueue returned no command id');

    const deadline = Date.now() + timeoutSec * 1000;
    for (;;) {
      const res = shapeCommand(await readCommand(appId, id));

      if (res.status === 'done') return ok(await derefResult(res));
      if (res.status === 'error') {
        return fail(`Command ${id} (${name}) failed in the build: ${res.error || 'no error text'}\n${JSON.stringify(res, null, 2)}`);
      }
      if (res.status === 'expired') {
        return fail(`Command ${id} (${name}) expired unclaimed after ${timeoutSec}s: no build polled the channel. Check fastlogs_channel_health for appId "${appId}".`);
      }

      if (Date.now() + config.pollIntervalMs >= deadline) {
        return fail(`Timed out after ${timeoutSec}s waiting for command ${id} (${name}); last status: ${res.status}.` +
          (res.status === 'dispatched'
            ? ' The build claimed the command but never acked it: the command name may not be registered in the build, or the handler hung or crashed.'
            : ` The command was never claimed: no build is polling the channel. Check fastlogs_channel_health for appId "${appId}".`));
      }
      await sleep(config.pollIntervalMs);
    }
  }),
);

server.registerTool(
  'fastlogs_enqueue_command',
  {
    title: 'Enqueue a command',
    description:
      'Queue a command for a build without waiting for the result; returns the command id. Use fastlogs_run_command unless you need to enqueue and poll separately (e.g. a long command, or several in flight).',
    inputSchema: {
      appId: appIdSchema.describe('Target app id, as registered on the server.'),
      name: commandNameSchema.describe('Command name, whitelisted on the game side.'),
      args: z.unknown().optional().describe('Any JSON value passed to the command handler (serialized cap: 8192 bytes).'),
      ttlSec: z.number().int().min(5).max(3600).optional()
        .describe('Command lifetime while queued (default: server COMMAND_TTL_SEC, 300).'),
    },
  },
  ({ appId, name, args, ttlSec }) => guard(async () => {
    const res = await enqueueCommand(appId, name, args, ttlSec);
    return ok({ id: res.id, status: res.status, expiresAt: res.expiresAt });
  }),
);

server.registerTool(
  'fastlogs_command_result',
  {
    title: 'Read a command result',
    description:
      'Read one command\'s current state: queued (waiting for the build to claim it), dispatched (claimed, no result yet), done, error, or expired (never claimed before its TTL ran out).',
    inputSchema: {
      appId: appIdSchema.describe('The app the command was sent to.'),
      id: z.string().min(1).max(64).describe('Command id returned by fastlogs_enqueue_command.'),
    },
  },
  ({ appId, id }) => guard(async () => ok(await derefResult(shapeCommand(await readCommand(appId, id))))),
);

server.registerTool(
  'fastlogs_channel_health',
  {
    title: 'Check the command channel',
    description:
      'Check whether a build is polling the command channel right now. Call this first when commands expire unclaimed: it tells you if anything is listening (pollingNow) and how long ago the last poll was (lastPollSecAgo). No poll ever seen means the build is not running, is on a different appId, or lacks the FastLogs command client.',
    inputSchema: {
      appId: appIdSchema.describe('App id whose command channel to check.'),
    },
  },
  ({ appId }) => guard(async () => {
    try {
      const res = await readChannelHealth(appId);
      return ok({
        appId: res.appId,
        pollingNow: res.pollingNow,
        lastPollAt: res.lastPollAt ?? null,
        lastPollSecAgo: res.lastPollSecAgo ?? null,
      });
    } catch (err) {
      // The endpoint is newer than the rest of the channel; say so rather than
      // letting a bare 404 read as "unknown app".
      if (err instanceof ApiError && err.status === 404) {
        // A plain-text 404 means the ROUTE is missing; a contract 404 means the
        // route ran and did not know the app.
        return fail(err.jsonBody
          ? `Unknown appId "${appId}" (HTTP 404 ${err.code}).`
          : 'This server has no /commands/:appId/health endpoint (HTTP 404, unmatched route): it predates channel health. Update the server, or infer liveness from whether commands get claimed.');
      }
      throw err;
    }
  }),
);

server.registerTool(
  'fastlogs_await_log',
  {
    title: 'Wait for a report by code',
    description:
      'Block until a report carrying a given correlation code is uploaded by the app, then return its id and links. Use it after triggering a report (by command, or by asking a tester to send one with that code). Returns the most recent match; polls until timeoutSec.',
    inputSchema: {
      appId: appIdSchema.describe('App expected to upload the report.'),
      code: z.string().min(1).max(64).describe('Correlation code carried by the report (its debug/await code).'),
      timeoutSec: z.number().int().min(1).max(600).default(60).describe('How long to wait for the report to land.'),
    },
  },
  ({ appId, code, timeoutSec = 60 }) => guard(async () => {
    const deadline = Date.now() + timeoutSec * 1000;
    for (;;) {
      const res = await awaitOnce(appId, code);
      if (res && res.found) {
        return ok({ found: true, id: res.id, url: res.url, rawUrl: res.rawUrl, createdAt: res.createdAt });
      }
      if (Date.now() + config.pollIntervalMs >= deadline) {
        return fail(`Timed out after ${timeoutSec}s: no log with code "${code}" for appId "${appId}".`);
      }
      await sleep(config.pollIntervalMs);
    }
  }),
);

server.registerTool(
  'fastlogs_search_logs',
  {
    title: 'Search logs',
    description:
      'Full-text search over one app\'s reports (title, tester, comment, context values, scene snapshot and the log body). Words are AND-ed; a trailing * is a prefix match (e.g. "Null*"). Returns catalog rows with a matching snippet.',
    inputSchema: {
      appId: appIdSchema.describe('App whose logs to search.'),
      q: z.string().min(1).max(256).describe('Free-text query, e.g. "null reference level load".'),
      version: z.string().max(128).optional().describe('Optional exact build version filter, e.g. "1.4.2 (build 318)".'),
    },
  },
  ({ appId, q, version }) => guard(async () => {
    const res = await request('GET', apiUrl('/search', { appId, q, version }), {
      token: requireToken('viewer'),
    });
    return ok(res);
  }),
);

server.registerTool(
  'fastlogs_get_log',
  {
    title: 'Get report metadata',
    description:
      'Read one report\'s metadata by id: title, time, platform, version, level counts and its links. Does not include the log text; fetch that from the returned rawUrl.',
    inputSchema: {
      id: z.string().min(1).max(64).describe('Report id, e.g. from fastlogs_await_log or fastlogs_search_logs.'),
    },
  },
  ({ id }) => guard(async () => {
    // Public endpoint: the id is the capability, no token needed.
    const res = await request('GET', apiUrl(`/logs/${encodeURIComponent(id)}`));
    return ok(res);
  }),
);

// --- Entry point -----------------------------------------------------------

async function main() {
  const transport = new StdioServerTransport();
  await server.connect(transport);
  // stdout is the JSON-RPC channel: diagnostics go to stderr only.
  console.error(`fastlogs-mcp ready (base ${config.baseUrl})`);
}

main().catch((err) => {
  console.error('fastlogs-mcp failed to start:', (err && err.message) || err);
  process.exit(1);
});

```

`server/mcp/result-ref.js`:

```js
// Guard for the offloaded-result reference (CONTRACT section 8).
//
// A result over the server's inline cap is uploaded as a file and the ack carries
// {"__fastlogsResultRef": url}. That ack is written through the INGEST tier, which
// is open for tokenless apps, so the url is UNTRUSTED input: without this check a
// hostile ack would make the MCP on a developer machine fetch any url it names
// (cloud metadata, intranet) and hand the body to the agent - a read-SSRF with
// exfiltration. By contract an offloaded result always lands on our OWN server
// under /files/<id>/download, so nothing else is ever worth fetching.
//
// Kept in its own dependency-free module so the rule is unit-testable without the
// MCP SDK (the server test suite installs only the server's own dependencies).

export const RESULT_REF_KEY = '__fastlogsResultRef';

// True only for an absolute url that is same-origin with baseUrl AND lives under
// /files/. A relative or malformed url is refused rather than resolved: a
// legitimate ref is always an absolute url on our own server.
export function isOffloadedResultUrl(rawUrl, baseUrl) {
  if (typeof rawUrl !== 'string' || rawUrl === '') return false;
  let target;
  let base;
  try {
    // No base argument on purpose: a relative ref must NOT inherit our origin.
    target = new URL(rawUrl);
    base = new URL(String(baseUrl));
  } catch {
    return false;
  }
  // Opaque origins serialize to "null" (file:, data:, unknown schemes); they must
  // never compare equal, or a broken baseUrl would let file:///files/x through.
  if (base.origin === 'null' || target.origin !== base.origin) return false;
  // URL parsing normalises the path, so "/files/../latest/meta-data" cannot pass.
  return target.pathname.startsWith('/files/');
}

```

`server/package.json`:

```json
{
  "name": "fastlogs-server",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "description": "FastLogs server: ingest, viewer, catalog, sinks, retention sweeper.",
  "license": "MIT",
  "private": true,
  "type": "commonjs",
  "main": "src/server.js",
  "engines": {
    "node": ">=18"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "start": "node src/server.js",
    "test": "node test/run.js",
    "sweep": "node scripts/sweep.js",
    "backup": "node scripts/backup.js",
    "add-app": "node scripts/add-app.js",
    "rename-app": "node scripts/rename-app.js",
    "list-apps": "node scripts/list-apps.js",
    "migrate": "node scripts/migrate.js",
    "backfill-fts": "node scripts/backfill-fts.js"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "better-sqlite3": "^11.8.1"
  }
}

`server/public/browse.css`:

```css
/* FastLogs - catalog (browse) styles. Dark theme. */

*, *::before, *::after { box-sizing: border-box; margin: 0; padding: 0; }

:root {
  --bg:        #0f1117;
  --bg2:       #181c25;
  --bg3:       #1e2330;
  --border:    #2a2f3e;
  --text:      #c9d1e0;
  --text-dim:  #6b7688;
  --accent:    #4a90e2;
  --accent-h:  #5ba3f5;
  --green:     #4caf6c;
  --yellow:    #e2b44a;
  --red:       #e25555;
  --radius:    6px;
  --mono:      'JetBrains Mono', 'Fira Code', 'Cascadia Code', 'Consolas', monospace;
}

html, body {
  height: 100%;
  background: var(--bg);
  color: var(--text);
  font-family: 'Atkinson Hyperlegible', -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif;
  font-size: 14px;
  line-height: 1.5;
}

a { color: var(--accent); text-decoration: none; }
a:hover { color: var(--accent-h); text-decoration: underline; }

.page { max-width: 1000px; margin: 0 auto; padding: 0 16px 40px; }

/* ---- Topbar ---- */

.topbar {
  display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 12px;
  padding: 12px 0; border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border);
  margin-bottom: 20px; flex-wrap: wrap;
}
.topbar-logo { font-weight: 700; font-size: 16px; color: var(--text); letter-spacing: -0.3px; }
.topbar-logo span { color: var(--accent); }
