```js
  backupsDir = path.join(dataDir, 'backups');
  fs.mkdirSync(dataDir, { recursive: true });
  fs.mkdirSync(blobDir, { recursive: true });

  // A real DB at the path config derives from DATA_DIR (join(dataDir,'fastlogs.db')).
  const db = new Database(path.join(dataDir, 'fastlogs.db'));
  db.pragma('journal_mode = WAL');
  db.exec('CREATE TABLE t (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, v TEXT)');
  db.prepare('INSERT INTO t (id, v) VALUES (?, ?)').run(1, 'alpha');
  db.prepare('INSERT INTO t (id, v) VALUES (?, ?)').run(2, 'beta');
  db.close();

  // A real blob file so the tarball has content.
  fs.writeFileSync(path.join(blobDir, 'sample.bin'), Buffer.from('blob-bytes'));
});

after(() => {
  try { fs.rmSync(tmpBase, { recursive: true, force: true }); } catch {}
});

test('backup produces a DB snapshot and a blob tarball', () => {
  const out = runBackup(7);
  assert.ok(fs.existsSync(out.db), 'db backup file exists');
  assert.ok(fs.existsSync(out.blobs), 'blobs tarball exists');
  assert.match(path.basename(out.db), /^fastlogs-.*\.db$/);
  assert.match(path.basename(out.blobs), /^blobs-.*\.tar\.gz$/);
  assert.ok(fs.statSync(out.blobs).size > 0, 'tarball is non-empty');
});

test('the DB snapshot restores and reads back the original rows', () => {
  const dbFile = listType('fastlogs-', '.db').sort().pop();
  const restored = new Database(path.join(backupsDir, dbFile), { readonly: true });
  try {
    const rows = restored.prepare('SELECT id, v FROM t ORDER BY id').all();
    assert.deepEqual(rows, [{ id: 1, v: 'alpha' }, { id: 2, v: 'beta' }]);
  } finally {
    restored.close();
  }
});

test('BACKUP_KEEP rotates each set down to the newest N', () => {
  // Two more runs (three total) with keep=2 must leave exactly 2 of each type.
  runBackup(2);
  runBackup(2);

  assert.equal(listType('fastlogs-', '.db').length, 2, 'db backups capped at 2');
  assert.equal(listType('blobs-', '.tar.gz').length, 2, 'blob tarballs capped at 2');
});

```

`server/test/browse-size.test.js`:

```js
'use strict';

// The catalog (/browse/:appId/:version) JSON exposes each log's byte size
// (logBytes), so the site can show the "weight" of every log.

const { test, before, after } = require('node:test');
const assert = require('node:assert/strict');
const { setup, makeIngestBody, req } = require('./helpers');

const ctx = setup();
const VIEWER = 'viewer-test-token'; // matches helpers.setup()

before(async () => {
  await ctx.ready;
  ctx.addApp({ appId: 'testapp', name: 'Test App' });
  const ing = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/logs', {
    method: 'POST',
    body: makeIngestBody({ appId: 'testapp', appVersion: '1.0.0', logText: 'hello world log line for size' }),
  });
  assert.equal(ing.status, 201, `ingest failed: ${ing.status} ${JSON.stringify(ing.body)}`);
});

after(() => ctx.close());

test('browse logs JSON includes per-log logBytes (size)', async () => {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/browse/testapp/1.0.0?format=json&token=' + VIEWER, {
    headers: { accept: 'application/json' },
  });
  assert.equal(r.status, 200, `browse failed: ${r.status} ${JSON.stringify(r.body)}`);
  assert.ok(Array.isArray(r.body.logs) && r.body.logs.length >= 1, 'expected at least one log');
  const log = r.body.logs[0];
  assert.equal(typeof log.logBytes, 'number', 'logBytes must be a number');
  assert.ok(log.logBytes > 0, 'logBytes should be > 0 for a non-empty log');
});
```

`server/test/browse-sort-filter.test.js`:

```js
'use strict';

// Contract for the catalog client's sort / errors-only / status controls
// (public/browse.js). Those controls are purely client-side, but they depend on
// fields the server must supply in GET /browse/:appId/:version JSON:
//   - counts.error  (number)  -> errors-only filter + "most errors" sort
//   - logBytes      (number)  -> "largest" sort
//   - time / createdAt        -> newest/oldest sort
// and on the default order being newest-first (created_at DESC). This test pins
// those guarantees so the UI does not silently break if the route changes.

const { test, before, after } = require('node:test');
const assert = require('node:assert/strict');
const { setup, makeIngestBody, req } = require('./helpers');

const ctx = setup();
const VIEWER = 'viewer-test-token'; // matches helpers.setup()

const APP = 'sortapp';
const VER = '2.0.0';

before(async () => {
  await ctx.ready;
  ctx.addApp({ appId: APP, name: 'Sort App' });

  // Three logs for one app/version with distinct error counts and clearly
  // distinct sizes (short / medium / long log text). Ingested sequentially so
  // their created_at timestamps are monotonically increasing.
  const ingests = [
    { counts: { error: 0, warn: 1, log: 2 }, logText: 'a' },
    { counts: { error: 2, warn: 0, log: 1 }, logText: 'b'.repeat(200) },
    { counts: { error: 5, warn: 3, log: 4 }, logText: 'c'.repeat(2000) },
  ];

  for (const o of ingests) {
    const ing = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/logs', {
      method: 'POST',
      body: makeIngestBody({ appId: APP, appVersion: VER, counts: o.counts, logText: o.logText }),
    });
    assert.equal(ing.status, 201, `ingest failed: ${ing.status} ${JSON.stringify(ing.body)}`);
  }
});

after(() => ctx.close());

test('browse logs JSON carries the fields the client sort/errors-only depend on', async () => {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, `/browse/${APP}/${VER}?format=json&token=` + VIEWER, {
    headers: { accept: 'application/json' },
  });
  assert.equal(r.status, 200, `browse failed: ${r.status} ${JSON.stringify(r.body)}`);
  assert.ok(Array.isArray(r.body.logs), 'logs must be an array');
  assert.equal(r.body.logs.length, 3, 'expected exactly the three ingested logs');

  for (const log of r.body.logs) {
    // counts.error drives errors-only filter and the "most errors" sort.
    assert.ok(log.counts && typeof log.counts.error === 'number',
      'each log must expose counts.error as a number');
    // logBytes drives the "largest" sort.
    assert.equal(typeof log.logBytes, 'number', 'logBytes must be a number');
    assert.ok(log.logBytes >= 0, 'logBytes must be >= 0');
    // A timestamp (time or createdAt) drives the newest/oldest sort.
    const ts = log.time || log.createdAt;
    assert.ok(typeof ts === 'string' && !Number.isNaN(Date.parse(ts)),
      'each log must expose a parseable time or createdAt');
  }
});

test('browse logs default order is newest-first (created_at DESC)', async () => {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, `/browse/${APP}/${VER}?format=json&token=` + VIEWER, {
    headers: { accept: 'application/json' },
  });
  assert.equal(r.status, 200, `browse failed: ${r.status} ${JSON.stringify(r.body)}`);
  const logs = r.body.logs;
  assert.equal(logs.length, 3, 'expected exactly the three ingested logs');

  // created_at must be non-increasing across the array (newest first). This is
  // the default order the client assumes before any "sort" control is touched.
  for (let i = 1; i < logs.length; i++) {
    const prev = Date.parse(logs[i - 1].createdAt);
    const cur = Date.parse(logs[i].createdAt);
    assert.ok(prev >= cur,
      `logs must be newest-first by createdAt: index ${i - 1} (${logs[i - 1].createdAt}) >= index ${i} (${logs[i].createdAt})`);
  }
});

```

`server/test/browse-stats.test.js`:

```js
'use strict';

// Storage / size dashboard: per-project + per-version totals and largest logs.

const { test, before, after } = require('node:test');
const assert = require('node:assert/strict');
const { setup, makeIngestBody, req } = require('./helpers');

const ctx = setup();
const VIEWER = 'viewer-test-token';

// Varied (low-compressibility) text of roughly `n` lines so gzipped sizes differ.
function body(n) {
  const parts = [];
  for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) parts.push('line ' + i + ' payload-' + (i * 7919 % 1000));
  return parts.join('\n');
}

async function ingest(overrides) {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/logs', { method: 'POST', body: makeIngestBody(overrides) });
  assert.equal(r.status, 201, `ingest failed: ${r.status} ${JSON.stringify(r.body)}`);
  return r.body.id;
}

let bigId;
before(async () => {
  await ctx.ready;
  ctx.addApp({ appId: 'appone', name: 'App One' });
  ctx.addApp({ appId: 'apptwo', name: 'App Two' });
  ctx.addApp({ appId: 'emptyapp', name: 'Empty App' });

  await ingest({ appId: 'appone', appVersion: '1.0.0', logText: body(50) });
  bigId = await ingest({ appId: 'appone', appVersion: '1.0.0', logText: body(2000) });
  await ingest({ appId: 'appone', appVersion: '1.1.0', logText: body(300) });
  await ingest({ appId: 'apptwo', appVersion: '1.0.0', logText: body(40) });

  // Pin the big log so pinnedCount is exercised.
  const p = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/logs/' + bigId + '/pin', { method: 'POST', body: { pin: true } });
  assert.equal(p.status, 200);
});
after(() => ctx.close());

test('GET /browse exposes per-project totals and a totals rollup', async () => {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/browse?format=json&token=' + VIEWER);
  assert.equal(r.status, 200);
  const byId = new Map(r.body.projects.map((p) => [p.appId, p]));

  assert.equal(byId.get('appone').logCount, 3);
  assert.ok(byId.get('appone').totalBytes > 0);
  assert.equal(byId.get('appone').pinnedCount, 1);

  // Registered but log-less app defaults to zeros (proves the listApps merge).
  assert.equal(byId.get('emptyapp').logCount, 0);
  assert.equal(byId.get('emptyapp').totalBytes, 0);
  assert.equal(byId.get('emptyapp').pinnedCount, 0);

  assert.equal(r.body.totals.logCount, 4);
  assert.equal(r.body.totals.pinnedCount, 1);
  assert.ok(r.body.totals.totalBytes > 0);
});

test('GET /browse/:appId exposes version sizes, app totals and largest logs', async () => {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/browse/appone?format=json&token=' + VIEWER);
  assert.equal(r.status, 200);

  assert.equal(r.body.totals.logCount, 3);
  assert.ok(r.body.totals.totalBytes > 0);

  const v100 = r.body.versions.find((v) => v.version === '1.0.0');
  assert.equal(v100.count, 2);
  assert.equal(v100.logCount, 2);
  assert.ok(v100.totalBytes > 0);
  assert.equal(v100.pinnedCount, 1);

  assert.ok(Array.isArray(r.body.largestLogs));
  assert.ok(r.body.largestLogs.length <= 5 && r.body.largestLogs.length >= 1);
  // Descending by size, and the big log is on top.
  for (let i = 1; i < r.body.largestLogs.length; i++) {
    assert.ok(r.body.largestLogs[i - 1].logBytes >= r.body.largestLogs[i].logBytes);
  }
  assert.equal(r.body.largestLogs[0].id, bigId);
});

test('unknown app -> 404', async () => {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/browse/nope?format=json&token=' + VIEWER);
  assert.equal(r.status, 404);
});

```

`server/test/command-health.test.js`:

```js
'use strict';

// Liveness beacon for the command channel (CONTRACT section 8e):
//   GET /api/commands/:appId/health -> {appId, pollingNow, lastPollAt, lastPollSecAgo}
//
// The beacon exists so an agent can tell "no build is running" from "the handler
// hung": both look like a command that never gets acked. apps.last_poll_at is
// stamped on every poll, including an empty one (an idle poll still proves life).

process.env.COMMAND_TOKEN = 'cmd-secret-token';
// poll always requires an ingest-tier token, so the beacon needs one to be stamped.
process.env.TEAM_INGEST_TOKEN = 'team-secret-token';

const { test, before, after } = require('node:test');
const assert = require('node:assert/strict');
const { setup, req } = require('./helpers');

const ctx = setup();
const ADMIN = 'admin-test-token';
const VIEWER = 'viewer-test-token';
const CMD = 'cmd-secret-token';
const GAME = { authorization: 'Bearer team-secret-token' };

before(async () => {
  await ctx.ready;
  ctx.addApp({ appId: 'liveapp', name: 'Live App' });
  ctx.addApp({ appId: 'quietapp', name: 'Quiet App' });
  ctx.addApp({ appId: 'staleapp', name: 'Stale App' });
  ctx.addApp({ appId: 'renamedapp', name: 'Renamed App' });
});

after(() => ctx.close());

test('health without a token -> 401', async () => {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/liveapp/health');
  assert.equal(r.status, 401);
});

test('health with a viewer token -> 401 (not a command tier)', async () => {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/liveapp/health', {
    headers: { authorization: `Bearer ${VIEWER}` },
  });
  assert.equal(r.status, 401);
});

test('health accepts the scoped command token and the admin token', async () => {
  const withCmd = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/liveapp/health', {
    headers: { authorization: `Bearer ${CMD}` },
  });
  assert.equal(withCmd.status, 200);

  const withAdmin = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/liveapp/health', {
    headers: { authorization: `Bearer ${ADMIN}` },
  });
  assert.equal(withAdmin.status, 200);

  const withQuery = await req(ctx.baseUrl, `/api/commands/liveapp/health?token=${CMD}`);
  assert.equal(withQuery.status, 200);
});

test('an app that never polled reads as not polling', async () => {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/quietapp/health', {
    headers: { authorization: `Bearer ${CMD}` },
  });
  assert.equal(r.status, 200);
  assert.equal(r.body.appId, 'quietapp');
  assert.equal(r.body.pollingNow, false);
  assert.equal(r.body.lastPollAt, null);
  assert.equal(r.body.lastPollSecAgo, null);
});

test('a poll stamps the beacon: pollingNow flips true with a fresh timestamp', async () => {
  const poll = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/liveapp/poll', { headers: GAME });
  assert.equal(poll.status, 200, 'an authorized poll goes through');
  assert.deepEqual(poll.body.commands, [], 'an EMPTY poll must still stamp the beacon');

  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/liveapp/health', {
    headers: { authorization: `Bearer ${CMD}` },
  });
  assert.equal(r.status, 200);
  assert.equal(r.body.pollingNow, true);
  assert.ok(r.body.lastPollAt, 'lastPollAt is set');
  assert.ok(r.body.lastPollSecAgo >= 0 && r.body.lastPollSecAgo <= 2, `fresh poll, got ${r.body.lastPollSecAgo}s`);
});

test('a stale poll reads as not polling but keeps reporting how long ago', async () => {
  // Backdate the beacon well past COMMANDS_LIVENESS_SEC (default 10).
  const old = new Date(Date.now() - 120 * 1000).toISOString();
  ctx.db.db.prepare('UPDATE apps SET last_poll_at = ? WHERE app_id = ?').run(old, 'staleapp');

  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/staleapp/health', {
    headers: { authorization: `Bearer ${CMD}` },
  });
  assert.equal(r.status, 200);
  assert.equal(r.body.pollingNow, false, 'two minutes silent is not live');
  assert.equal(r.body.lastPollAt, old);
  assert.ok(r.body.lastPollSecAgo >= 118 && r.body.lastPollSecAgo <= 125, `got ${r.body.lastPollSecAgo}s`);
});

test('a future timestamp (clock skew) never yields a negative age', async () => {
  const future = new Date(Date.now() + 60 * 1000).toISOString();
  ctx.db.db.prepare('UPDATE apps SET last_poll_at = ? WHERE app_id = ?').run(future, 'staleapp');

  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/staleapp/health', {
    headers: { authorization: `Bearer ${CMD}` },
  });
  assert.equal(r.status, 200);
  assert.equal(r.body.lastPollSecAgo, 0);
  assert.equal(r.body.pollingNow, true);
});

test('unknown app -> 404, invalid appId shape -> 400', async () => {
  const unknown = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/nosuchapp/health', {
    headers: { authorization: `Bearer ${CMD}` },
  });
  assert.equal(unknown.status, 404);

  const bad = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/A/health', {
    headers: { authorization: `Bearer ${CMD}` },
  });
  assert.equal(bad.status, 400);
});

test('health resolves an appId alias to the canonical app', async () => {
  await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/renamedapp/poll', { headers: GAME });
  ctx.db.renameApp('renamedapp', 'renamedapp2');

  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/renamedapp/health', {
    headers: { authorization: `Bearer ${CMD}` },
  });
  assert.equal(r.status, 200);
  assert.equal(r.body.appId, 'renamedapp2', 'canonical id is reported');
  assert.equal(r.body.pollingNow, true, 'the poll under the old slug counts');
});

```

`server/test/command-token.test.js`:

```js
'use strict';

// Tests for the scoped COMMAND_TOKEN (a token that may ONLY drive the command
// channel; admin remains a superset). Covers: the command token authorizes
// enqueue + result read; it is REJECTED on an admin-gated route (unpin under
// UNPIN_REQUIRES_ADMIN); it is NOT an ingest-tier token (poll on a token-
// protected app rejects it); and a wrong token is unauthorized.
//
// IMPORTANT: config.js reads env ONCE at first require, and helpers.setup()
// requires the server modules, so COMMAND_TOKEN (and the policy flags) MUST be
// set BEFORE setup(). This file runs in its own child process under
// `node --test`, so the env is isolated from the other test files. (Mirrors the
// TEAM_INGEST_TOKEN pattern in commands.test.js.)

process.env.COMMAND_TOKEN = 'command-secret-token';
process.env.UNPIN_REQUIRES_ADMIN = '1';

const { test, before, after } = require('node:test');
const assert = require('node:assert/strict');
const { setup, makeIngestBody, req } = require('./helpers');

const ctx = setup();
const ADMIN = 'admin-test-token';
const COMMAND = 'command-secret-token';
const APP_TOKEN = 'ingest-secret';

before(async () => {
  await ctx.ready;
  ctx.addApp({ appId: 'ctapp', name: 'CT App' });                      // tokenless (open ingest)
  ctx.addApp({ appId: 'cttok', name: 'CT Token App', token: APP_TOKEN }); // per-app token
});

after(() => ctx.close());

// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
// The command token drives the command channel: enqueue + result read.
// -------------------------------------------------------------------------

let cmdId;

test('enqueue with COMMAND_TOKEN -> 201', async () => {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/ctapp', {
    method: 'POST', body: { name: 'do.thing' },
    headers: { authorization: `Bearer ${COMMAND}` },
  });
  assert.equal(r.status, 201, JSON.stringify(r.body));
  assert.equal(r.body.status, 'queued');
  cmdId = r.body.id;
});

test('result read with COMMAND_TOKEN -> 200', async () => {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, `/api/commands/ctapp/result/${cmdId}`, {
    headers: { authorization: `Bearer ${COMMAND}` },
  });
  assert.equal(r.status, 200);
  assert.equal(r.body.id, cmdId);
});

test('result read with COMMAND_TOKEN via ?token= query -> 200', async () => {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, `/api/commands/ctapp/result/${cmdId}?token=${COMMAND}`);
  assert.equal(r.status, 200);
  assert.equal(r.body.id, cmdId);
});

// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
// The command token is NOT admin: it is rejected on an admin-gated route.
// With UNPIN_REQUIRES_ADMIN=1, unpin requires the admin token.
// -------------------------------------------------------------------------

test('COMMAND_TOKEN is rejected on an admin-gated route (unpin) -> 403', async () => {
  const ing = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/logs', {
    method: 'POST', body: makeIngestBody({ appId: 'ctapp' }),
  });
  assert.equal(ing.status, 201, JSON.stringify(ing.body));
  const id = ing.body.id;

  // Pin is open by link (no auth), so this succeeds regardless of the token tier.
  const pinned = await req(ctx.baseUrl, `/api/logs/${id}/pin`, {
    method: 'POST', body: { pin: true },
  });
  assert.equal(pinned.status, 200);

  // Unpin under UNPIN_REQUIRES_ADMIN needs admin; the command token must NOT pass.
  const denied = await req(ctx.baseUrl, `/api/logs/${id}/pin`, {
    method: 'POST', body: { pin: false },
    headers: { authorization: `Bearer ${COMMAND}` },
  });
  assert.equal(denied.status, 403);

  // Sanity: admin CAN unpin (proves the route is genuinely admin-gated).
  const ok = await req(ctx.baseUrl, `/api/logs/${id}/pin`, {
    method: 'POST', body: { pin: false },
    headers: { authorization: `Bearer ${ADMIN}` },
  });
  assert.equal(ok.status, 200);
});

// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
// The command token is NOT an ingest-tier token: poll rejects it.
// -------------------------------------------------------------------------

test('COMMAND_TOKEN on poll (ingest tier) is rejected -> 403', async () => {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/cttok/poll', {
    headers: { authorization: `Bearer ${COMMAND}` },
  });
  assert.equal(r.status, 403);
});

// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
// A wrong token is unauthorized on the command channel.
// -------------------------------------------------------------------------

test('enqueue with a wrong token -> 401', async () => {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/ctapp', {
    method: 'POST', body: { name: 'noop' },
    headers: { authorization: 'Bearer not-the-token' },
  });
  assert.equal(r.status, 401);
});

test('admin still enqueues (superset) -> 201', async () => {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/ctapp', {
    method: 'POST', body: { name: 'admin.ok' },
    headers: { authorization: `Bearer ${ADMIN}` },
  });
  assert.equal(r.status, 201);
});

```

`server/test/commands.test.js`:

```js
'use strict';

// Tests for the agent -> game command channel (CONTRACT section 8):
//   POST /api/commands/:appId              (admin)        - enqueue
//   GET  /api/commands/:appId/poll?max=N   (ingest token) - atomic claim
//   POST /api/commands/:appId/ack/:cmdId   (ingest token) - ack a result
//   GET  /api/commands/:appId/result/:cmdId(admin)        - read state
//
// Covers: auth matrix, happy path, single-claim + FIFO, expired command,
// repeat/errored ack, validation (name/args/result caps), ttl clamp, poll max
// cap, and appId alias resolution.
//
// poll/ack ALWAYS require a token, including for an app whose log ingest is open,
// so every game-side call below carries one (GAME = the team token).
//
// The shared team token is set BEFORE setup() so config reads it once (config.js
// caches env at first require).

process.env.TEAM_INGEST_TOKEN = 'team-secret-token';

const { test, before, after } = require('node:test');
const assert = require('node:assert/strict');
const { setup, makeIngestBody, req } = require('./helpers');

const ctx = setup();
const ADMIN = 'admin-test-token';
const TEAM = 'team-secret-token';
const APP_TOKEN = 'ingest-secret';
// What a real build sends on poll/ack; a tokenless app has no per-app token, so
// the team token is its only way onto the command channel.
const GAME = { authorization: `Bearer ${TEAM}` };

// A monotonic id/created_at source for db-level command inserts so FIFO order is
// deterministic (ascending created_at, unique ids).
let seq = 0;
function enqueueDirect(appId, name, args, opts = {}) {
  seq += 1;
  const id = opts.id || 'c' + seq.toString(36).padStart(5, '0');
  const createdAt = opts.createdAt || new Date(Date.now() - 60000 + seq * 100).toISOString();
  const ttlSec = opts.ttlSec != null ? opts.ttlSec : 300;
  const expiresAt = opts.expiresAt || new Date(Date.parse(createdAt) + ttlSec * 1000).toISOString();
  ctx.db.enqueueCommand({
    id,
    app_id: appId,
    name,
    args_json: args !== undefined ? JSON.stringify(args) : null,
    created_at: createdAt,
    expires_at: expiresAt,
  });
  return id;
}

before(async () => {
  await ctx.ready;
  ctx.addApp({ appId: 'cmdapp', name: 'Command App' });        // tokenless (open ingest)
  ctx.addApp({ appId: 'tokapp', name: 'Token App', token: APP_TOKEN });
  ctx.addApp({ appId: 'fifoapp', name: 'FIFO App' });
  ctx.addApp({ appId: 'expapp', name: 'Expire App' });
  ctx.addApp({ appId: 'ackapp', name: 'Ack App' });
  ctx.addApp({ appId: 'errapp', name: 'Err App' });
  ctx.addApp({ appId: 'capapp', name: 'Cap App' });
  ctx.addApp({ appId: 'maxapp', name: 'Max App' });
  ctx.addApp({ appId: 'aliasapp', name: 'Alias App' });
});

after(() => ctx.close());

// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Auth matrix.
// -------------------------------------------------------------------------

test('enqueue without an admin token -> 401', async () => {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/cmdapp', {
    method: 'POST', body: { name: 'noop' },
  });
  assert.equal(r.status, 401);
});

test('enqueue with a viewer token (not admin) -> 401', async () => {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/cmdapp', {
    method: 'POST', body: { name: 'noop' },
    headers: { authorization: 'Bearer viewer-test-token' },
  });
  assert.equal(r.status, 401);
});

test('poll on a token-protected app without a token -> 401', async () => {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/tokapp/poll');
  assert.equal(r.status, 401);
});

test('poll on a token-protected app with a wrong token -> 403', async () => {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/tokapp/poll', {
    headers: { authorization: 'Bearer wrong-token' },
  });
  assert.equal(r.status, 403);
});

test('poll on a token-protected app with the per-app token -> 200', async () => {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/tokapp/poll', {
    headers: { authorization: `Bearer ${APP_TOKEN}` },
  });
  assert.equal(r.status, 200);
  assert.deepEqual(r.body, { commands: [] });
});

test('poll on a token-protected app with the team token -> 200', async () => {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/tokapp/poll', {
    headers: { authorization: `Bearer ${TEAM}` },
  });
  assert.equal(r.status, 200);
});

// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Tier boundary: open LOG ingest must never mean an open COMMAND channel.
// Without this, anyone could claim the agent's commands away from the real
// build (queued -> dispatched) and ack a forged result the agent reasons on.
// -------------------------------------------------------------------------

test('poll on a tokenless app (open ingest) without a token -> 401', async () => {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/cmdapp/poll');
  assert.equal(r.status, 401);
  assert.equal(r.body.error, 'unauthorized');
  assert.match(String(r.body.message || ''), /token/i, 'the 401 says a token is required');
});

test('poll on a tokenless app with the team token -> 200', async () => {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/cmdapp/poll', { headers: GAME });
  assert.equal(r.status, 200);
  assert.deepEqual(r.body, { commands: [] });
});

test('poll on a tokenless app with a wrong token -> 403', async () => {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/cmdapp/poll', {
    headers: { authorization: 'Bearer wrong-token' },
  });
  assert.equal(r.status, 403);
});

test('ack on a tokenless app without a token -> 401, and the command stays claimable', async () => {
  const id = enqueueDirect('cmdapp', 'tier.check');
  const poll = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/cmdapp/poll', { headers: GAME });
  assert.equal(poll.body.commands[0].id, id, 'the authorized build claimed it');

  // A real dispatched command: auth must refuse BEFORE the state checks, so this
  // is a 401 and not a 409/404 (i.e. the forged result never lands).
  const forged = await req(ctx.baseUrl, `/api/commands/cmdapp/ack/${id}`, {
    method: 'POST', body: { status: 'done', result: '{"forged":1}' },
  });
  assert.equal(forged.status, 401);
  assert.equal(ctx.db.getCommand(id).status, 'dispatched', 'the forged ack did not complete it');
  assert.equal(ctx.db.getCommand(id).result_json, null, 'no forged result was stored');

  // The real build still acks it with a token.
  const real = await req(ctx.baseUrl, `/api/commands/cmdapp/ack/${id}`, {
    method: 'POST', body: { status: 'done', result: '{"real":1}' }, headers: GAME,
  });
  assert.equal(real.status, 200);
  assert.equal(ctx.db.getCommand(id).result_json, '{"real":1}');
});

test('log ingest stays OPEN for a tokenless app (the legacy path is untouched)', async () => {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/logs', {
    method: 'POST', body: makeIngestBody({ appId: 'cmdapp' }),
  });
  assert.equal(r.status, 201, JSON.stringify(r.body));
});

// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Happy path: enqueue -> poll (claims + dispatched) -> ack done -> result.
// -------------------------------------------------------------------------

let happyId;

test('enqueue returns 201 queued with an expiresAt', async () => {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/cmdapp', {
    method: 'POST', body: { name: 'do.thing', args: { level: 3 } },
    headers: { authorization: `Bearer ${ADMIN}` },
  });
  assert.equal(r.status, 201, JSON.stringify(r.body));
  assert.equal(r.body.status, 'queued');
  assert.ok(r.body.id, 'id present');
  assert.ok(r.body.expiresAt, 'expiresAt present');
  happyId = r.body.id;
});

test('poll claims the command (args as a string) and marks it dispatched', async () => {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/cmdapp/poll', { headers: GAME });
  assert.equal(r.status, 200);
  assert.equal(r.body.commands.length, 1);
  const cmd = r.body.commands[0];
  assert.equal(cmd.id, happyId);
  assert.equal(cmd.name, 'do.thing');
  assert.equal(cmd.args, '{"level":3}', 'args is the serialized JSON string');

  // result endpoint now reports dispatched, with dispatchedAt set.
  const rr = await req(ctx.baseUrl, `/api/commands/cmdapp/result/${happyId}?token=${ADMIN}`);
  assert.equal(rr.status, 200);
  assert.equal(rr.body.status, 'dispatched');
  assert.ok(rr.body.dispatchedAt, 'dispatchedAt set');
  assert.equal(rr.body.args, '{"level":3}');
});

test('a second poll does not re-hand-out the dispatched command', async () => {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/cmdapp/poll', { headers: GAME });
  assert.equal(r.status, 200);
  assert.deepEqual(r.body, { commands: [] });
});

test('ack done -> 200 {ok:true}, result reads done with the result string', async () => {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, `/api/commands/cmdapp/ack/${happyId}`, {
    method: 'POST', body: { status: 'done', result: '{"ok":1}' }, headers: GAME,
  });
  assert.equal(r.status, 200);
  assert.deepEqual(r.body, { ok: true });

  const rr = await req(ctx.baseUrl, `/api/commands/cmdapp/result/${happyId}?token=${ADMIN}`);
  assert.equal(rr.status, 200);
  assert.equal(rr.body.status, 'done');
  assert.equal(rr.body.result, '{"ok":1}');
  assert.ok(rr.body.completedAt, 'completedAt set');
});

test('a repeat ack on a done command -> 409', async () => {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, `/api/commands/cmdapp/ack/${happyId}`, {
    method: 'POST', body: { status: 'done' }, headers: GAME,
  });
  assert.equal(r.status, 409);
});

// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Single-claim + FIFO order across two polls.
// -------------------------------------------------------------------------

test('two polls claim each queued command once, in FIFO order', async () => {
  const a = enqueueDirect('fifoapp', 'first', { seq: 1 });
  const b = enqueueDirect('fifoapp', 'second', { seq: 2 });
  const c = enqueueDirect('fifoapp', 'third', { seq: 3 });

  const r1 = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/fifoapp/poll?max=2', { headers: GAME });
  assert.equal(r1.status, 200);
  assert.deepEqual(r1.body.commands.map((x) => x.id), [a, b], 'first poll returns oldest two');

  const r2 = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/fifoapp/poll', { headers: GAME });
  assert.deepEqual(r2.body.commands.map((x) => x.id), [c], 'second poll returns the last one');

  const r3 = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/fifoapp/poll', { headers: GAME });
  assert.deepEqual(r3.body.commands, [], 'nothing left to claim');
});

// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Expired command: not claimed, and result reports "expired" (read-computed).
// -------------------------------------------------------------------------

test('an expired queued command is not polled and reads as expired', async () => {
  const past = new Date(Date.now() - 5000).toISOString();
  const id = enqueueDirect('expapp', 'stale', null, { expiresAt: past });

  const poll = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/expapp/poll', { headers: GAME });
  assert.deepEqual(poll.body.commands, [], 'expired command is not handed out');

  const rr = await req(ctx.baseUrl, `/api/commands/expapp/result/${id}?token=${ADMIN}`);
  assert.equal(rr.status, 200);
  assert.equal(rr.body.status, 'expired');

  // The row is NOT mutated by the read: it is still stored as queued.
  assert.equal(ctx.db.getCommand(id).status, 'queued');
});

// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Ack error branch.
// -------------------------------------------------------------------------

test('ack error -> result reports status error with the error text', async () => {
  const id = enqueueDirect('errapp', 'boom');
  const poll = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/errapp/poll', { headers: GAME });
  assert.equal(poll.body.commands[0].id, id);

  const ack = await req(ctx.baseUrl, `/api/commands/errapp/ack/${id}`, {
    method: 'POST', body: { status: 'error', error: 'kaboom' }, headers: GAME,
  });
  assert.equal(ack.status, 200);

  const rr = await req(ctx.baseUrl, `/api/commands/errapp/result/${id}?token=${ADMIN}`);
  assert.equal(rr.body.status, 'error');
  assert.equal(rr.body.error, 'kaboom');
});

test('ack of an unknown / cross-app command -> 404', async () => {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/errapp/ack/does-not-exist', {
    method: 'POST', body: { status: 'done' }, headers: GAME,
  });
  assert.equal(r.status, 404);
});

test('ack with an invalid status -> 400', async () => {
  const id = enqueueDirect('errapp', 'badstatus');
  await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/errapp/poll', { headers: GAME }); // dispatch it
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, `/api/commands/errapp/ack/${id}`, {
    method: 'POST', body: { status: 'maybe' }, headers: GAME,
  });
  assert.equal(r.status, 400);
});

// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Validation + clamps.
// -------------------------------------------------------------------------

test('enqueue with an invalid name -> 400', async () => {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/cmdapp', {
    method: 'POST', body: { name: 'Bad Name!' },
    headers: { authorization: `Bearer ${ADMIN}` },
  });
  assert.equal(r.status, 400);
});

test('enqueue with args over the cap -> 400', async () => {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/cmdapp', {
    method: 'POST', body: { name: 'big', args: 'x'.repeat(9000) },
    headers: { authorization: `Bearer ${ADMIN}` },
  });
  assert.equal(r.status, 400);
});

test('ack with a result over the cap -> 400', async () => {
  const id = enqueueDirect('capapp', 'huge');
  await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/capapp/poll', { headers: GAME }); // dispatch it
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, `/api/commands/capapp/ack/${id}`, {
    method: 'POST', body: { status: 'done', result: 'x'.repeat(70000) }, headers: GAME,
  });
  assert.equal(r.status, 400);
});

test('ttlSec is clamped to [5, 3600]', async () => {
  const low = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/cmdapp', {
    method: 'POST', body: { name: 'lowttl', ttlSec: 1 },
    headers: { authorization: `Bearer ${ADMIN}` },
  });
  const lowDelta = Date.parse(low.body.expiresAt) - Date.now();
  assert.ok(lowDelta > 3000 && lowDelta < 8000, `low ttl clamped to ~5s, got ${lowDelta}ms`);

  const high = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/cmdapp', {
    method: 'POST', body: { name: 'highttl', ttlSec: 999999 },
    headers: { authorization: `Bearer ${ADMIN}` },
  });
  const highDelta = Date.parse(high.body.expiresAt) - Date.now();
  assert.ok(highDelta > 3500 * 1000 && highDelta < 3700 * 1000, `high ttl clamped to ~3600s, got ${highDelta}ms`);
});

// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Poll max cap (COMMANDS_POLL_MAX = 16).
// -------------------------------------------------------------------------

test('poll never returns more than COMMANDS_POLL_MAX commands', async () => {
  for (let i = 0; i < 18; i++) enqueueDirect('maxapp', 'flood_' + i);
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/maxapp/poll?max=100', { headers: GAME });
  assert.equal(r.status, 200);
  assert.equal(r.body.commands.length, 16, 'capped at 16 even with max=100');
});

// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
// appId alias resolution: enqueue under the OLD slug, poll under the new one.
// -------------------------------------------------------------------------

test('appId is resolved through aliases (old slug enqueues into the renamed app)', async () => {
  ctx.db.renameApp('aliasapp', 'aliasapp2'); // 'aliasapp' becomes an alias of 'aliasapp2'

  const enq = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/aliasapp', {
    method: 'POST', body: { name: 'via.alias' },
    headers: { authorization: `Bearer ${ADMIN}` },
  });
  assert.equal(enq.status, 201);

  // The command lands under the canonical id, so a poll of the new slug claims it.
  const poll = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/aliasapp2/poll', { headers: GAME });
  assert.equal(poll.status, 200);
  assert.equal(poll.body.commands.length, 1);
  assert.equal(poll.body.commands[0].id, enq.body.id);
  assert.equal(poll.body.commands[0].name, 'via.alias');
});

// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Sweep: abandoned dispatched commands (claimed, never acked) are reclaimed.
// -------------------------------------------------------------------------

test('sweep deletes abandoned dispatched commands older than the retention window', async () => {
  ctx.addApp({ appId: 'sweepapp', name: 'Sweep App' });

  // Claimed two days ago, never acked; expiry set in the future so poll CAN claim it.
  const abandoned = enqueueDirect('sweepapp', 'stale.dispatched', undefined, {
    createdAt: new Date(Date.now() - 2 * 86400 * 1000).toISOString(),
    expiresAt: new Date(Date.now() + 3600 * 1000).toISOString(),
  });
  const fresh = enqueueDirect('sweepapp', 'fresh.dispatched');
  const poll = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/commands/sweepapp/poll', { headers: GAME });
  assert.equal(poll.body.commands.length, 2, 'both commands claimed into dispatched');

  const cutoff = new Date(Date.now() - 86400 * 1000).toISOString();
  const changes = ctx.db.sweepCommands(new Date().toISOString(), cutoff).changes;
  assert.equal(changes, 1, 'only the abandoned dispatched row is swept');

  const gone = await req(ctx.baseUrl, `/api/commands/sweepapp/result/${abandoned}`, {
    headers: { authorization: `Bearer ${ADMIN}` },
  });
  assert.equal(gone.status, 404);

  const kept = await req(ctx.baseUrl, `/api/commands/sweepapp/result/${fresh}`, {
    headers: { authorization: `Bearer ${ADMIN}` },
  });
  assert.equal(kept.status, 200);
  assert.equal(kept.body.status, 'dispatched');
});

```

`server/test/comment-tester.test.js`:

```js
'use strict';

// Tests for the optional `comment` and `tester` ingest fields.
//
// Covers:
//   - round-trip: POST /api/logs with comment+tester -> 201, then
//     GET /api/logs/:id returns exactly the same values;
//   - missing/empty comment+tester do not break ingest and surface as null;
//   - overlong comment (>4000) and tester (>120) are accepted and truncated
//     server-side (the documented slice() behaviour) rather than rejected.

const { test, before, after } = require('node:test');
const assert = require('node:assert/strict');
const { setup, makeIngestBody, req } = require('./helpers');

const ctx = setup();

before(async () => {
  await ctx.ready;
  ctx.addApp({ appId: 'testapp', name: 'Test App' });
});

after(() => ctx.close());

// Ingest a body, assert 201, fetch its meta, and return the meta body.
async function ingestAndGetMeta(overrides) {
  const ingestRes = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/logs', {
    method: 'POST',
    body: makeIngestBody(overrides),
  });
  assert.equal(ingestRes.status, 201,
    `expected 201, got ${ingestRes.status}: ${JSON.stringify(ingestRes.body)}`);
  const { id } = ingestRes.body;

  const metaRes = await req(ctx.baseUrl, `/api/logs/${id}`);
  assert.equal(metaRes.status, 200,
    `meta expected 200, got ${metaRes.status}: ${JSON.stringify(metaRes.body)}`);
  return metaRes.body;
}

// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
// 1. Round-trip: comment + tester are stored and returned verbatim.
// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
test('POST /api/logs with comment and tester round-trips via GET /api/logs/:id', async () => {
  const comment = 'Boss fight crashes when picking up the second key.';
  const tester = 'Jane QA';

  const meta = await ingestAndGetMeta({ comment, tester });
  assert.equal(meta.comment, comment, 'comment must round-trip unchanged');
  assert.equal(meta.tester, tester, 'tester must round-trip unchanged');
});

// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
// 2. Missing comment/tester -> ingest still works, fields are null.
// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
test('POST /api/logs without comment/tester returns 201 and null fields', async () => {
  const meta = await ingestAndGetMeta({});
  assert.equal(meta.comment, null, 'absent comment must surface as null');
  assert.equal(meta.tester, null, 'absent tester must surface as null');
});

// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
// 3. Empty-string comment/tester -> not stored, surface as null.
// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
test('POST /api/logs with empty comment/tester returns 201 and null fields', async () => {
  // Empty strings are kept as '' by ingest (slice of ''), but publicLogObject
  // maps falsy values to null via `row.comment || null`. Pin this behaviour.
  const meta = await ingestAndGetMeta({ comment: '', tester: '' });
  assert.equal(meta.comment, null, 'empty comment must surface as null');
  assert.equal(meta.tester, null, 'empty tester must surface as null');
});

// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
// 4. Overlong comment/tester -> accepted (201) and truncated, not rejected.
// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
test('POST /api/logs truncates overlong comment (>4000) and tester (>120)', async () => {
  const longComment = 'c'.repeat(5000); // > 4000 limit
  const longTester = 't'.repeat(200);   // > 120 limit

  const meta = await ingestAndGetMeta({ comment: longComment, tester: longTester });

  // Server accepts and slices: comment to 4000, tester to 120.
  assert.equal(typeof meta.comment, 'string', 'comment must be present');
  assert.equal(meta.comment.length, 4000, 'comment must be truncated to 4000 chars');
  assert.equal(meta.comment, 'c'.repeat(4000), 'truncated comment must be the leading 4000 chars');

  assert.equal(typeof meta.tester, 'string', 'tester must be present');
  assert.equal(meta.tester.length, 120, 'tester must be truncated to 120 chars');
  assert.equal(meta.tester, 't'.repeat(120), 'truncated tester must be the leading 120 chars');
});

// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
// 5. Non-string comment/tester are coerced via String(), not rejected.
// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
test('POST /api/logs coerces non-string comment/tester to string', async () => {
  // ingest uses String(body.comment) / String(body.tester) for any non-null
  // value, so a number is stored as its string form rather than 400-ing.
  const meta = await ingestAndGetMeta({ comment: 12345, tester: 42 });
  assert.equal(meta.comment, '12345', 'numeric comment is stringified');
  assert.equal(meta.tester, '42', 'numeric tester is stringified');
});

```

`server/test/context-breadcrumbs.test.js`:

```js
'use strict';

// Tests for the optional `context` and `breadcrumbs` ingest fields.
//
// Covers:
//   - round-trip: POST /api/logs with context+breadcrumbs -> 201, then
//     GET /api/logs/:id returns them as { object } and [ array ];
//   - missing context/breadcrumbs surface as {} and [] (stable shape);
//   - invalid types (context as array, breadcrumbs as object) are ignored
//     (not 400) and surface as the empty defaults;
//   - oversize input is clamped server-side: breadcrumbs to <=100 items,
//     context entries dropped once the ~4KB budget is exceeded, per-field
//     key/value/text truncation, and lvl restricted to the enum.

const { test, before, after } = require('node:test');
const assert = require('node:assert/strict');
const { setup, makeIngestBody, req } = require('./helpers');

const ctx = setup();

before(async () => {
  await ctx.ready;
  ctx.addApp({ appId: 'testapp', name: 'Test App' });
});

after(() => ctx.close());

// Ingest a body, assert 201, fetch its meta, and return the meta body.
async function ingestAndGetMeta(overrides) {
  const ingestRes = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/logs', {
    method: 'POST',
    body: makeIngestBody(overrides),
  });
  assert.equal(ingestRes.status, 201,
    `expected 201, got ${ingestRes.status}: ${JSON.stringify(ingestRes.body)}`);
  const { id } = ingestRes.body;

  const metaRes = await req(ctx.baseUrl, `/api/logs/${id}`);
  assert.equal(metaRes.status, 200,
    `meta expected 200, got ${metaRes.status}: ${JSON.stringify(metaRes.body)}`);
  return metaRes.body;
}

// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
// 1. Round-trip: context + breadcrumbs are stored and returned.
// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
test('POST /api/logs with context and breadcrumbs round-trips via GET /api/logs/:id', async () => {
  const context = { level: '3', playerId: 'abc' };
  const breadcrumbs = [
    { t: '2026-06-12T09:29:58Z', m: 'opened shop', lvl: 'info' },
    { t: '2026-06-12T09:30:00Z', m: 'bought item', lvl: 'warn' },
  ];

  const meta = await ingestAndGetMeta({ context, breadcrumbs });

  assert.deepEqual(meta.context, context, 'context must round-trip as an object');
  assert.ok(Array.isArray(meta.breadcrumbs), 'breadcrumbs must be an array');
  assert.equal(meta.breadcrumbs.length, 2, 'both breadcrumbs must be present');
  assert.deepEqual(meta.breadcrumbs[0], breadcrumbs[0], 'first breadcrumb verbatim');
  assert.deepEqual(meta.breadcrumbs[1], breadcrumbs[1], 'second breadcrumb verbatim');
});

// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
// 2. Missing context/breadcrumbs -> stable empty defaults ({} and []).
// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
test('POST /api/logs without context/breadcrumbs returns 201 and empty defaults', async () => {
  const meta = await ingestAndGetMeta({});
  assert.deepEqual(meta.context, {}, 'absent context must surface as {}');
  assert.deepEqual(meta.breadcrumbs, [], 'absent breadcrumbs must surface as []');
});

// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
// 3. Invalid types are ignored, not rejected (fields are optional).
// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
test('POST /api/logs ignores invalid context/breadcrumbs types (no 400)', async () => {
  // context as array and breadcrumbs as object are the wrong shapes; the
  // server must coerce them to the empty defaults rather than returning 400.
  const meta = await ingestAndGetMeta({ context: [1, 2, 3], breadcrumbs: { not: 'an array' } });
  assert.deepEqual(meta.context, {}, 'array context must collapse to {}');
  assert.deepEqual(meta.breadcrumbs, [], 'object breadcrumbs must collapse to []');
});

// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
// 4. Breadcrumbs overflow -> clamped to <=100 items.
// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
test('POST /api/logs clamps breadcrumbs to at most 100 items', async () => {
  const many = [];
  for (let i = 0; i < 250; i++) many.push({ m: 'event ' + i, lvl: 'info' });

  const meta = await ingestAndGetMeta({ breadcrumbs: many });
  assert.ok(Array.isArray(meta.breadcrumbs), 'breadcrumbs must be an array');
  assert.ok(meta.breadcrumbs.length <= 100,
    `breadcrumbs must be clamped to <=100, got ${meta.breadcrumbs.length}`);
  assert.equal(meta.breadcrumbs.length, 100, 'exactly 100 kept (item cap, under byte cap)');
  // Order preserved from the start of the array.
  assert.equal(meta.breadcrumbs[0].m, 'event 0', 'first kept breadcrumb is the first sent');
});

// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
// 5. Context overflow -> entries dropped once the ~4KB budget is exceeded,
//    and per-field key/value lengths are truncated.
// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
test('POST /api/logs clamps oversize context (byte budget + key/value truncation)', async () => {
  const context = {};
  // 100 entries of ~600 bytes each (key ~ short, value 600) far exceeds 4KB.
  for (let i = 0; i < 100; i++) {
    context['k' + i] = 'v'.repeat(600); // value > 512 cap, will be truncated
  }
  // Add one deliberately overlong key (> 64 chars).
  const longKey = 'K'.repeat(200);
  context[longKey] = 'x';

  const meta = await ingestAndGetMeta({ context });

  assert.equal(typeof meta.context, 'object', 'context must be an object');
  assert.ok(!Array.isArray(meta.context), 'context must not be an array');

  const keys = Object.keys(meta.context);
  // Far fewer than 101 entries survive the 4KB budget.
  assert.ok(keys.length < 101, `context must be clamped under budget, got ${keys.length} keys`);
  assert.ok(keys.length >= 1, 'at least one context entry should survive');

  // Total serialized size of values+keys must respect the ~4KB cap.
  let total = 0;
  for (const k of keys) {
    assert.ok(Buffer.byteLength(k, 'utf8') <= 64, `key "${k}" must be <=64 bytes`);
    const v = meta.context[k];
    assert.ok(Buffer.byteLength(v, 'utf8') <= 512, 'each value must be <=512 bytes');
    total += Buffer.byteLength(k, 'utf8') + Buffer.byteLength(v, 'utf8');
  }
  assert.ok(total <= 4 * 1024, `summed context bytes must be <=4KB, got ${total}`);
});

// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
// 6. Breadcrumb field hygiene: text truncated, bad lvl dropped, no-m skipped.
// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
test('POST /api/logs sanitizes breadcrumb fields (truncate m, drop bad lvl, skip empty)', async () => {
  const breadcrumbs = [
    { m: 'a'.repeat(1000), lvl: 'bogus' }, // m truncated to 512, lvl dropped
    { m: 'ok', lvl: 'error' },             // valid lvl kept
    { lvl: 'info' },                       // no m -> skipped entirely
    { m: '' },                             // empty m -> skipped entirely
  ];

  const meta = await ingestAndGetMeta({ breadcrumbs });
  assert.equal(meta.breadcrumbs.length, 2, 'only the two breadcrumbs with usable m survive');

  const first = meta.breadcrumbs[0];
  assert.equal(first.m.length, 512, 'overlong m truncated to 512 chars');
  assert.equal(first.lvl, undefined, 'invalid lvl ("bogus") must be dropped, not stored');

  const second = meta.breadcrumbs[1];
  assert.equal(second.m, 'ok');
  assert.equal(second.lvl, 'error', 'valid lvl kept');
});

```

`server/test/crashes-detection.test.js`:

```js
'use strict';

// New / regression detection on the crashes endpoint.

const { test, before, after } = require('node:test');
const assert = require('node:assert/strict');
const { setup, makeIngestBody, req } = require('./helpers');

const ctx = setup();
const VIEWER = 'viewer-test-token';

// Distinct exception types -> distinct signatures. Same text reused across
// versions keeps a stable signature regardless of version.
const crash = (type) => [
  `[E] +1.000 ${type}: boom`,
  `Game.${type}Site:Run () [0x00001] in <a1>:0`,
].join('\n');

async function ingest(overrides) {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/logs', { method: 'POST', body: makeIngestBody(overrides) });
  assert.equal(r.status, 201, `ingest failed: ${r.status} ${JSON.stringify(r.body)}`);
  return r.body.id;
}

before(async () => {
  await ctx.ready;
  ctx.addApp({ appId: 'detectapp', name: 'Detect App' });
  // Z: present in every version (continuous).
  for (const v of ['1.0.0', '1.1.0', '1.2.0']) {
    await ingest({ appId: 'detectapp', appVersion: v, title: 'z', counts: { error: 1, warn: 0, log: 0 }, logText: crash('ZException') });
  }
  // Y: in 1.0.0, absent 1.1.0, back in 1.2.0 (regression).
  await ingest({ appId: 'detectapp', appVersion: '1.0.0', title: 'y', counts: { error: 1, warn: 0, log: 0 }, logText: crash('YException') });
  await ingest({ appId: 'detectapp', appVersion: '1.2.0', title: 'y', counts: { error: 1, warn: 0, log: 0 }, logText: crash('YException') });
  // X: only in latest 1.2.0 (new).
  await ingest({ appId: 'detectapp', appVersion: '1.2.0', title: 'x', counts: { error: 1, warn: 0, log: 0 }, logText: crash('XException') });
  // A non-crash log in the latest version (must list but never group).
  await ingest({ appId: 'detectapp', appVersion: '1.2.0', title: 'info', counts: { error: 0, warn: 0, log: 1 }, logText: 'just an info line' });
});
after(() => ctx.close());

test('latestVersion + per-group first/last seen', async () => {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/browse/detectapp/crashes?format=json&token=' + VIEWER);
  assert.equal(r.status, 200);
  assert.equal(r.body.latestVersion, '1.2.0');
  const z = r.body.crashes.find((c) => c.title === 'z');
  assert.equal(z.firstSeenVersion, '1.0.0');
  assert.equal(z.lastSeenVersion, '1.2.0');
  assert.equal(z.count, 3);
  assert.deepEqual(z.versions.slice().sort(), ['1.0.0', '1.1.0', '1.2.0']);
});

test('crash only in the latest version is kind=new', async () => {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/browse/detectapp/crashes?format=json&token=' + VIEWER);
  const x = r.body.crashes.find((c) => c.title === 'x');
  assert.equal(x.isNew, true);
  assert.equal(x.kind, 'new');
  assert.equal(x.firstSeenVersion, '1.2.0');
});

test('crash absent from preceding version but back in latest is kind=regression', async () => {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/browse/detectapp/crashes?format=json&token=' + VIEWER);
  const y = r.body.crashes.find((c) => c.title === 'y');
  assert.equal(y.isNew, true);
  assert.equal(y.kind, 'regression');
  assert.equal(y.firstSeenVersion, '1.0.0');
  assert.equal(y.lastSeenVersion, '1.2.0');
});

test('crash present in every version is not flagged', async () => {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/browse/detectapp/crashes?format=json&token=' + VIEWER);
  const z = r.body.crashes.find((c) => c.title === 'z');
  assert.equal(z.isNew, false);
  assert.equal(z.kind, null);
});

test('non-crash log lists in the version view but never appears in crashes', async () => {
  const crashes = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/browse/detectapp/crashes?format=json&token=' + VIEWER);
  assert.ok(!crashes.body.crashes.some((c) => c.title === 'info'));
  const logs = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/browse/detectapp/1.2.0?format=json&token=' + VIEWER);
  assert.ok(logs.body.logs.some((l) => l.title === 'info'), 'info log present in version listing');
});

test('non-semver versions: deterministic ordering, no 500', async () => {
  ctx.addApp({ appId: 'nightlyapp', name: 'Nightly App' });
  await ingest({ appId: 'nightlyapp', appVersion: 'nightly-2026-06-10', title: 'n', counts: { error: 1, warn: 0, log: 0 }, logText: crash('ZException') });
  await ingest({ appId: 'nightlyapp', appVersion: 'nightly-2026-06-13', title: 'n', counts: { error: 1, warn: 0, log: 0 }, logText: crash('ZException') });
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/browse/nightlyapp/crashes?format=json&token=' + VIEWER);
  assert.equal(r.status, 200);
  assert.equal(r.body.crashes.length, 1);
  assert.equal(r.body.crashes[0].firstSeenVersion, 'nightly-2026-06-10');
  assert.equal(r.body.crashes[0].lastSeenVersion, 'nightly-2026-06-13');
});

test('app with no crashes -> empty array', async () => {
  ctx.addApp({ appId: 'cleanapp', name: 'Clean App' });
  await ingest({ appId: 'cleanapp', appVersion: '1.0.0', counts: { error: 0, warn: 0, log: 1 }, logText: 'no errors at all' });
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/browse/cleanapp/crashes?format=json&token=' + VIEWER);
  assert.equal(r.status, 200);
  assert.deepEqual(r.body.crashes, []);
});

```

`server/test/crashes-route-order.test.js`:

```js
'use strict';

// Router-ordering invariant: /browse/:appId/crashes must be matched by the
// crashes handler even when an app has a real app_version literally named
// "crashes". This locks browseCrashes being registered before browseVersion.

const { test, before, after } = require('node:test');
const assert = require('node:assert/strict');
const { setup, makeIngestBody, req } = require('./helpers');

const ctx = setup();
const VIEWER = 'viewer-test-token';

async function ingest(overrides) {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/logs', { method: 'POST', body: makeIngestBody(overrides) });
  assert.equal(r.status, 201, `ingest failed: ${r.status} ${JSON.stringify(r.body)}`);
}

before(async () => {
  await ctx.ready;
  ctx.addApp({ appId: 'routeapp', name: 'Route App' });
  await ingest({ appId: 'routeapp', appVersion: '1.0.0', counts: { error: 0, warn: 0, log: 1 }, logText: 'normal log' });
  // A log whose app_version is literally "crashes".
  await ingest({ appId: 'routeapp', appVersion: 'crashes', counts: { error: 0, warn: 0, log: 1 }, logText: 'version named crashes' });
});
after(() => ctx.close());

test('GET /browse/:appId/crashes hits the crashes endpoint, not the version listing', async () => {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/browse/routeapp/crashes?format=json&token=' + VIEWER);
  assert.equal(r.status, 200);
  assert.ok('crashes' in r.body, 'response has crashes[]');
  assert.ok('latestVersion' in r.body, 'response has latestVersion');
  assert.ok(!('logs' in r.body), 'must NOT be the version listing');
});

test('GET /browse/:appId/:version still serves the version listing', async () => {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/browse/routeapp/1.0.0?format=json&token=' + VIEWER);
  assert.equal(r.status, 200);
  assert.ok(Array.isArray(r.body.logs), 'version listing has logs[]');
});

```

`server/test/crashes.test.js`:

```js
'use strict';

// End-to-end tests for crash grouping: GET /browse/:appId/crashes.

const { test, before, after } = require('node:test');
const assert = require('node:assert/strict');
const { setup, makeIngestBody, req } = require('./helpers');

const ctx = setup();
const VIEWER = 'viewer-test-token';
const ADMIN = 'admin-test-token';

// Same NullReferenceException crash; only the hex offsets and <assembly> token
// differ between runs (so it must collapse into one signature).
function nre(offset, asm) {
  return [
    '[L] +1.000 [Boot] starting up',
    'Terraf.App:Init()',
    '[E] +12.345 NullReferenceException: Object reference not set to an instance of an object',
    `Game.Player:TakeDamage (System.Int32 amount) [0x${offset}] in <${asm}>:0`,
    `Game.Combat:Resolve () [0x00aaa] in <${asm}>:0`,
  ].join('\n');
}
const IOOB = [
  '[E] +3.100 IndexOutOfRangeException: Index was outside the bounds of the array.',
  'Game.Inventory:Get (System.Int32 slot) [0x00001] in <a1>:0',
].join('\n');

async function ingest(overrides) {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/logs', { method: 'POST', body: makeIngestBody(overrides) });
  assert.equal(r.status, 201, `ingest failed: ${r.status} ${JSON.stringify(r.body)}`);
  return r.body.id;
}

let nreId1;
before(async () => {
  await ctx.ready;
  ctx.addApp({ appId: 'crashapp', name: 'Crash App' });
  nreId1 = await ingest({ appId: 'crashapp', appVersion: '1.0.0', tester: 'Jane', title: 'nre', counts: { error: 1, warn: 0, log: 2 }, logText: nre('00012', 'a1') });
  await ingest({ appId: 'crashapp', appVersion: '1.0.0', tester: 'Bob', title: 'nre', counts: { error: 1, warn: 0, log: 1 }, logText: nre('000ff', 'z9') });
  await ingest({ appId: 'crashapp', appVersion: '1.1.0', tester: 'Bob', title: 'ioob', counts: { error: 1, warn: 0, log: 0 }, logText: IOOB });
  await ingest({ appId: 'crashapp', appVersion: '1.0.0', tester: 'Jane', title: 'info', counts: { error: 0, warn: 0, log: 3 }, logText: 'plain info log, no error here' });
});
after(() => ctx.close());

test('identical crashes collapse into one group; non-crash excluded', async () => {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/browse/crashapp/crashes?format=json&token=' + VIEWER);
  assert.equal(r.status, 200, JSON.stringify(r.body));
  assert.ok(Array.isArray(r.body.crashes));
  assert.equal(r.body.crashes.length, 2, 'NRE group + IOOR group, info excluded');

  const nreG = r.body.crashes.find((c) => c.title === 'nre');
  assert.ok(nreG, 'NRE group present');
  assert.equal(nreG.count, 2);
  assert.match(nreG.signature, /^[0-9a-f]{12}$/);
  assert.equal(nreG.sig, nreG.signature);
  assert.ok(nreG.versions.includes('1.0.0'));
  assert.ok(nreG.testers >= 1);
  assert.ok(nreG.sampleLogIds.length >= 1 && nreG.sampleLogIds.length <= 5);
  assert.ok(nreG.firstSeenAt <= nreG.lastSeenAt);

  assert.ok(!r.body.crashes.some((c) => c.title === 'info'), 'non-crash not grouped');
});

test('different exception is a separate group', async () => {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/browse/crashapp/crashes?format=json&token=' + VIEWER);
  const a = r.body.crashes.find((c) => c.title === 'nre');
  const b = r.body.crashes.find((c) => c.title === 'ioob');
  assert.ok(a && b);
  assert.notEqual(a.signature, b.signature);
  assert.equal(b.count, 1);
});

test('auth: no token 401, viewer 200, admin 200', async () => {
  assert.equal((await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/browse/crashapp/crashes?format=json')).status, 401);
  assert.equal((await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/browse/crashapp/crashes?format=json&token=' + VIEWER)).status, 200);
  assert.equal((await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/browse/crashapp/crashes?format=json&token=' + ADMIN)).status, 200);
});

test('unknown app -> 404', async () => {
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/browse/nope/crashes?format=json&token=' + VIEWER);
  assert.equal(r.status, 404);
});

test('lazy recompute: a crash_sig NULL log is re-signed on access and rejoins its group', async () => {
  // Force a pre-feature state: clear the stored signature of one NRE log.
  ctx.db.db.prepare('UPDATE logs SET crash_sig = NULL WHERE id = ?').run(nreId1);
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/browse/crashapp/crashes?format=json&token=' + VIEWER);
  assert.equal(r.status, 200);
  const nreG = r.body.crashes.find((c) => c.title === 'nre');
  assert.ok(nreG, 'NRE group present after recompute');
  assert.equal(nreG.count, 2, 'recompute restored the cleared log into its group');
});

test('HTML negotiation: browser Accept gets the catalog shell (still token-gated)', async () => {
  const noTok = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/browse/crashapp/crashes', { headers: { accept: 'text/html' } });
  assert.equal(noTok.status, 401);
  const r = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/browse/crashapp/crashes?token=' + VIEWER, { headers: { accept: 'text/html' } });
  assert.equal(r.status, 200);
  assert.match(String(r.headers['content-type'] || ''), /text\/html/);
});

```

`server/test/crashsig-migration.test.js`:

```js
'use strict';

// Covers the user_version=1 data migration in src/db.js.
//
// Before the GameMaker line grammar landed, ingest stamped every GM log
// crash_sig='' ("computed, not a crash"). The lazy backfill only revisits
// crash_sig IS NULL, so those rows would stay out of crash grouping forever even
// after the parser learned GM. The migration clears the sentinel for GM rows only.
//
// db.js opens the database at require() time, so the pre-migration state is built
// on the file first and the module registry is then busted to re-run init against it.

const { test } = require('node:test');
const assert = require('node:assert/strict');
const fs = require('node:fs');
const os = require('node:os');
const path = require('node:path');
const crypto = require('node:crypto');
const Database = require('better-sqlite3');

function freshDbModule(dataDir) {
  process.env.DATA_DIR = dataDir;
  process.env.BLOB_DIR = path.join(dataDir, 'blobs');
  process.env.ADMIN_TOKEN = 'admin-test-token';
  process.env.IP_SALT = 'test-salt';
  process.env.DISK_MONITOR_INTERVAL_SEC = '0';
  delete require.cache[require.resolve('../src/db')];
  delete require.cache[require.resolve('../src/config')];
  return require('../src/db');
}

function insertLog(raw, id, engine, sig) {
  raw.prepare(
    `INSERT INTO logs (id, app_id, platform, app_version, ts_utc, created_at, engine, crash_sig)
     VALUES (?, 'app1', 'web', '1.0.0', '2026-07-16T10:00:00Z', '2026-07-16T10:00:00Z', ?, ?)`
  ).run(id, engine, sig);
}

test('user_version=1 clears the "" sentinel for GameMaker rows only, once', () => {
  const dataDir = path.join(os.tmpdir(), `fastlogs-mig-${crypto.randomBytes(6).toString('hex')}`);
  fs.mkdirSync(path.join(dataDir, 'blobs'), { recursive: true });

  try {
    // First require builds the schema on an empty file (migration is a no-op here).
    freshDbModule(dataDir);

    // Rewind to the pre-migration world and plant rows the way old ingest wrote them.
    const dbPath = path.join(dataDir, 'fastlogs.db');
    const raw = new Database(dbPath);
    raw.pragma('user_version = 0');
    insertLog(raw, 'gm-notcrash', 'GameMaker', '');
    insertLog(raw, 'gm-crash', 'GameMaker', '');
    insertLog(raw, 'unity-notcrash', 'Unity', '');
    insertLog(raw, 'unity-crash', 'Unity', 'a3e7fcb241a4');
    insertLog(raw, 'gm-already-done', 'GameMaker', 'ffffffffffff');
    raw.close();

    // Re-running init applies the migration.
    freshDbModule(dataDir);

    const after = new Database(dbPath);
    const sig = (id) => after.prepare('SELECT crash_sig FROM logs WHERE id = ?').get(id).crash_sig;

    assert.equal(sig('gm-notcrash'), null, 'GM sentinel is cleared for recompute');
    assert.equal(sig('gm-crash'), null, 'GM sentinel is cleared for recompute');
    assert.equal(sig('unity-notcrash'), '', 'Unity sentinel is left alone: its grammar never changed');
    assert.equal(sig('unity-crash'), 'a3e7fcb241a4', 'an existing Unity signature must not shift (groups would split)');
    assert.equal(sig('gm-already-done'), 'ffffffffffff', 'a real GM signature is not thrown away');
    assert.equal(after.pragma('user_version', { simple: true }), 1, 'version stamped so the pass does not repeat');

    // A second boot must not touch anything: re-null-ing non-crashes every restart
    // would make the backfill re-read and re-parse those blobs forever.
    after.prepare("UPDATE logs SET crash_sig = '' WHERE id = 'gm-notcrash'").run();
    after.close();

    freshDbModule(dataDir);

    const third = new Database(dbPath);
    assert.equal(
      third.prepare("SELECT crash_sig FROM logs WHERE id = 'gm-notcrash'").get().crash_sig,
      '',
      'migration is versioned, not repeated on every boot'
    );
    third.close();
  } finally {
    delete require.cache[require.resolve('../src/db')];
    delete require.cache[require.resolve('../src/config')];
    try { fs.rmSync(dataDir, { recursive: true, force: true }); } catch {}
  }
});

```

`server/test/crashsig.test.js`:

```js
'use strict';

// Unit tests for src/crashsig.js. Pure module, no server: require it directly.
// FastLogs log format is "[E] +time message" + raw stack frames (see
// unity Runtime/Capture/LogFormat.cs).

const { test } = require('node:test');
const assert = require('node:assert/strict');
const crashsig = require('../src/crashsig');

// A crash log: one [E] entry (NullReferenceException) with two IL2CPP frames,
// preceded by ordinary info logs that ALSO carry script stacks (the false-
// positive trap this module must avoid).
const CRASH_A = [
  '[L] +1.000 [Loading] Begin: PlayerProfile.Load',
  'Terraf.TerrafApp:LoadingStep(String)',
  'InitialLoadingScene:Update()',
  '[E] +12.345 NullReferenceException: Object reference not set to an instance of an object',
  'Game.Player:TakeDamage (System.Int32 amount) [0x00012] in <a1b2c3>:0',
  'Game.Combat:Resolve () [0x0007f] in <a1b2c3>:0',
].join('\n');

// The SAME crash from a different build/run: different hex offsets, different
// <assembly> token, different line numbers. Must hash identically to CRASH_A.
const CRASH_A_REBUILD = [
  '[L] +0.500 [Boot] starting',
  '[E] +88.001 NullReferenceException: Object reference not set to an instance of an object',
  'Game.Player:TakeDamage (System.Int32 amount) [0x000ff] in <z9y8x7>:42',
  'Game.Combat:Resolve () [0x00abc] in <z9y8x7>:88',
].join('\n');

// The SAME crash as CRASH_A, but the entry headers now carry a frame token
// ("[E] +12.345 f5821 ...") as emitted once the client captures Time.frameCount.
// crashsig must skip the optional f<frame> token so this hashes identically to
// CRASH_A (otherwise crash grouping would fragment across the format boundary).
const CRASH_A_WITHFRAME = [
  '[L] +1.000 f10 [Loading] Begin: PlayerProfile.Load',
  'Terraf.TerrafApp:LoadingStep(String)',
  'InitialLoadingScene:Update()',
  '[E] +12.345 f5821 NullReferenceException: Object reference not set to an instance of an object',
  'Game.Player:TakeDamage (System.Int32 amount) [0x00012] in <a1b2c3>:0',
  'Game.Combat:Resolve () [0x0007f] in <a1b2c3>:0',
].join('\n');

// A DIFFERENT crash: different exception type, different frames.
const CRASH_B = [
  '[E] +3.100 IndexOutOfRangeException: Index was outside the bounds of the array.',
  'Game.Inventory:Get (System.Int32 slot) [0x00001] in <a1b2c3>:0',
].join('\n');

// Not a crash: only info/warning entries (each with its own script stack).
const NON_CRASH = [
  '[L] +1.000 [Loading] Begin: PlayerProfile.Load',
  'Terraf.TerrafApp:LoadingStep(String)',
  'InitialLoadingScene:Update()',
  '[W] +2.000 Texture import settings overridden',
  'UnityEngine.Debug:LogWarning(Object)',
].join('\n');

test('crash log yields a 12-char lowercase hex signature', () => {
  const sig = crashsig.computeSignature(CRASH_A);
  assert.equal(typeof sig, 'string');
  assert.match(sig, /^[0-9a-f]{12}$/);
});

test('STABILITY: same crash across builds (offsets/lines/assembly differ) -> same signature', () => {
  assert.equal(crashsig.computeSignature(CRASH_A), crashsig.computeSignature(CRASH_A_REBUILD));
});

test('STABILITY: frame token in the header (new format) -> same signature as the frame-less format', () => {
  assert.equal(crashsig.computeSignature(CRASH_A_WITHFRAME), crashsig.computeSignature(CRASH_A));
});

test('DISTINCTNESS: different exception type / frames -> different signature', () => {
  assert.notEqual(crashsig.computeSignature(CRASH_A), crashsig.computeSignature(CRASH_B));
});

test('non-crash (no [E] entry) -> null even though info logs carry stacks', () => {
  assert.equal(crashsig.computeSignature(NON_CRASH), null);
});

test('empty / null / non-string input -> null', () => {
  assert.equal(crashsig.computeSignature(''), null);
  assert.equal(crashsig.computeSignature(null), null);
  assert.equal(crashsig.computeSignature(undefined), null);
  assert.equal(crashsig.computeSignature(12345), null);
});

test('exceptionType extracts the type, ignores plain "Error " words', () => {
  assert.equal(crashsig.exceptionType('NullReferenceException: foo'), 'NullReferenceException');
  assert.equal(crashsig.exceptionType('System.IO.IOException: disk full'), 'System.IO.IOException');
  assert.equal(crashsig.exceptionType('Error loading scene MainMenu'), '');
  assert.equal(crashsig.exceptionType('[Loading] Begin: PlayerProfile.Load'), '');
});

test('normalizeFrame strips offsets/lines/paths/generics, collapses args', () => {
  assert.equal(
    crashsig.normalizeFrame('Game.Player:TakeDamage (System.Int32 amount) [0x00012] in <a1b2c3>:0'),
    'Game.Player:TakeDamage()'
  );
  assert.equal(
    crashsig.normalizeFrame('  at Game.Combat:Resolve () [0x0007f] in <z9>:42'),
    'Game.Combat:Resolve()'
  );
  assert.equal(
    crashsig.normalizeFrame('Player.TakeDamage (Int32 amount) (at Assets/Scripts/Player.cs:42)'),
    'Player.TakeDamage()'
  );
  assert.equal(
    crashsig.normalizeFrame('System.Collections.Generic.List`1:Add (T item)'),
    'System.Collections.Generic.List:Add()'
  );
});

test('frame path/offset/line differences do not change the frame token', () => {
  const a = crashsig.normalizeFrame('Player.TakeDamage (Int32 a) (at C:/proj/Assets/Player.cs:42)');
  const b = crashsig.normalizeFrame('Player.TakeDamage (Int32 a) (at /home/ci/Assets/Player.cs:88)');
  assert.equal(a, b);
});

test('error without a type/stack groups by normalized message (volatile parts stripped)', () => {
  const m1 = '[E] +5.000 Failed to load asset bundle 12345 at 0xABCD';
  const m2 = '[E] +9.000 Failed to load asset bundle 67890 at 0x1234';
  const s1 = crashsig.computeSignature(m1);
  const s2 = crashsig.computeSignature(m2);
  assert.match(s1, /^[0-9a-f]{12}$/);
  assert.equal(s1, s2);
});

test('SIG_LEN / TOP_K exported constants', () => {
  assert.equal(crashsig.SIG_LEN, 12);
  assert.equal(crashsig.TOP_K, 8);
});

// --- GameMaker line grammar -------------------------------------------------
// GM writes "[hh:mm:ss] LEVEL: message" and a 5-"=" upper-case session marker,
// neither of which the Unity-only regexes matched: every GM log parsed to zero
// entries and every GM crash got a null signature, so the Crashes tab was
// silently empty for GM while the viewer rendered the same log fine.

const GM_CRASH = [
  '===== FASTLOGS SESSION 9f2a | 2026-07-16T10:00:00Z | dev =====',
  '[10:00:01] LOG: booting',
  '[10:00:12] ERROR: NullReferenceException: Object reference not set to an instance of an object',
  'Game.Player:TakeDamage (System.Int32 amount) [0x00012] in <a1b2c3>:0',
  'Game.Combat:Resolve () [0x0007f] in <a1b2c3>:0',
].join('\n');

test('GameMaker entries parse, with levels folded onto Unity letters', () => {
  const entries = crashsig.parseEntries(GM_CRASH);
  assert.deepEqual(entries.map((e) => e.level), ['L', 'E']);
  assert.equal(entries[1].message, 'NullReferenceException: Object reference not set to an instance of an object');
  assert.equal(entries[1].body.length, 2, 'the stack attaches to the error, not to the marker');
});

test('GameMaker crash produces a signature (was null: Crashes tab dead for GM)', () => {
  assert.match(crashsig.computeSignature(GM_CRASH), /^[0-9a-f]{12}$/);
});

test('same crash from Unity and GameMaker shares one signature', () => {
  const unity = [
    '==== FastLogs session 9f2a | 2026-07-16T10:00:00Z | dev ====',
    '[E] +12.345 NullReferenceException: Object reference not set to an instance of an object',
    'Game.Player:TakeDamage (System.Int32 amount) [0x00012] in <a1b2c3>:0',
    'Game.Combat:Resolve () [0x0007f] in <a1b2c3>:0',
  ].join('\n');
  assert.equal(crashsig.computeSignature(GM_CRASH), crashsig.computeSignature(unity));
});

test('GameMaker severities keep their own buckets (a warning is not a crash)', () => {
  assert.deepEqual(crashsig.parseEntries('[10:00:12] WARN: careful').map((e) => e.level), ['W']);
  assert.deepEqual(crashsig.parseEntries('[10:00:12] log: lower-case tag').map((e) => e.level), ['L']);
  assert.equal(crashsig.computeSignature('[10:00:12] WARN: careful'), null, 'warnings are not crashes');
});

test('session markers stay entry boundaries for both engines', () => {
  assert.equal(crashsig.parseEntries('===== FASTLOGS SESSION x | y | z =====').length, 0);
  assert.equal(crashsig.parseEntries('==== FastLogs session x | y | z ====').length, 0);
  // A marker must cut the previous entry's stack off, not extend it across runs.
  const twoRuns = [
    '[10:00:12] ERROR: Boom',
    'Game.A:Frame ()',
    '===== FASTLOGS SESSION new | t | d =====',
    'Game.B:NotMyFrame ()',
  ].join('\n');
  assert.deepEqual(crashsig.pickError(crashsig.parseEntries(twoRuns)).frames, ['Game.A:Frame ()']);
});

```

`server/test/engine-free.test.js`:

```js
'use strict';

// Discipline guard: the files below carry no DIRECT engine reference today, and a
// `using UnityEngine;` must not drift into them unnoticed. Nothing in the repo
// forbids it - there is no engine-free compilation unit - so the rule only exists
// if something checks it. This is that something.
//
// What this does NOT claim: that these files are portable or standalone-compilable.
// Several of them take FastLogsConfig, which is a ScriptableObject, so they depend
// on the engine THROUGH A TYPE without ever naming it. Interfaces.cs is the clearest
// case: every factory on it takes FastLogsConfig, so it only looks engine-free.
// Proving portability needs a compilation unit that forbids the engine; this only
// keeps a known-good list from quietly rotting.
//
// Lives in the node suite because it is a text check and the JS CI already runs it
// without a Unity licence.

const { test } = require('node:test');
const assert = require('node:assert/strict');
const fs = require('node:fs');
const path = require('node:path');

const UNITY = path.join(__dirname, '..', '..', 'unity');

// Declared engine-free (direct references). Grouped by why it is worth defending.
const ENGINE_FREE = [
  // Wire DTOs + serializer: the contract types. Engine-freedom here is what would
  // let another host reuse the payload shape.
  'Runtime/Model/CountsDto.cs',
  'Runtime/Model/DeviceInfoDto.cs',
  'Runtime/Model/FileUploadResultDto.cs',
  'Runtime/Model/LogReportDto.cs',
  'Runtime/Model/UploadResultDto.cs',
  'Runtime/Model/MiniJson.cs',
  // Pure algorithms and utilities.
  'Runtime/Net/PiiScrubber.cs',
  'Runtime/Net/GzipUtil.cs',
  'Runtime/Net/FolderZipUtil.cs',
  'Runtime/UI/QrCode.cs',
  // Capture core: the ring, the line format and the breadcrumb store.
  'Runtime/Capture/RingLogBuffer.cs',
  'Runtime/Capture/LogFormat.cs',
  'Runtime/Capture/FastLogsCrumbStore.cs',
  // Command core (the channel itself is NOT here: it is built on UnityWebRequest
  // and Unity coroutines, and does not belong on this list).
  'Runtime/Commands/CommandRegistry.cs',
  'Runtime/Commands/CommandResultRef.cs',
  'Runtime/Commands/CorrelationScope.cs',
  // Seams and gate.
  'Runtime/Core/FastLogsGate.cs',
  'Runtime/Core/Interfaces.cs',
  'Runtime/Core/FlogTask.cs',
  'Runtime/Capture/FastLogsCaptureServices.cs',
];

// Crude but adequate for a discipline check: drop block comments, whole-line
// comments and trailing comments, then look for the engine in what is left.
function stripComments(src) {
  return src
    .replace(/\/\*[\s\S]*?\*\//g, '')
    .replace(/^[ \t]*\/\/.*$/gm, '')
    .replace(/([^:"'])\/\/.*$/gm, '$1');
}

test('files declared engine-free carry no direct UnityEngine/UnityEditor reference', () => {
  const offenders = [];
  for (const rel of ENGINE_FREE) {
    const full = path.join(UNITY, rel);
    assert.ok(fs.existsSync(full), `${rel} is on the engine-free list but does not exist (renamed? update the list)`);
    const code = stripComments(fs.readFileSync(full, 'utf8'));
    const hit = /Unity(Engine|Editor)/.exec(code);
    if (hit) {
      const line = code.slice(0, hit.index).split('\n').length;
      offenders.push(`${rel}:${line} -> ${hit[0]}`);
    }
  }
  assert.deepEqual(offenders, [], 'either keep these files engine-free or drop them from the list deliberately');
});

```

`server/test/engine.test.js`:

```js
'use strict';

// Engine detection: device.application -> engine column, surfaced in the
// catalog (projects + per-log) and meta. Unity is inferred from an engine
// version; an explicit `engine` field (e.g. GameMaker) wins; key casing is
// ignored.
//
```
