`server/test/version-compare.test.js`:

```js
'use strict';

// Unit tests for src/util/version.js. Pure module: require directly.

const { test } = require('node:test');
const assert = require('node:assert/strict');
const { compareVersions, parseSemver } = require('../src/util/version');

const lt = (a, b, ca, cb) => assert.ok(compareVersions(a, b, ca, cb) < 0, `${a} < ${b}`);
const gt = (a, b, ca, cb) => assert.ok(compareVersions(a, b, ca, cb) > 0, `${a} > ${b}`);
const eq = (a, b, ca, cb) => assert.equal(compareVersions(a, b, ca, cb), 0, `${a} == ${b}`);

test('semver numeric ordering', () => {
  lt('1.0.0', '1.0.1');
  lt('1.0.1', '1.1.0');
  lt('1.1.0', '2.0.0');
  gt('2.0.0', '1.9.9');
});

test('prerelease sorts before release; identifiers compare', () => {
  lt('1.2.0-rc1', '1.2.0');
  lt('1.2.0-alpha', '1.2.0-beta');
  lt('1.2.0-beta', '1.2.0-rc1');
  lt('1.2.0-1', '1.2.0-2');
  lt('1.2.0-2', '1.2.0-10');
  lt('1.2.0-1', '1.2.0-alpha');
});

test('leading v tolerated; build metadata ignored', () => {
  eq('v1.2.3', '1.2.3');
  eq('1.2.3+abc', '1.2.3');
  eq('1.2.3+abc', '1.2.3+def');
});

test('loose numeric-dotted fallback', () => {
  lt('1.0', '1.0.1');
  lt('2024.3.10f1', '2024.3.11f1');
  gt('2025.1.0f1', '2024.3.11f1');
});

test('total order holds for a prerelease semver mixed with a 2-part loose version', () => {
  // Regression: '1.2.0-rc1' must not be misread as the loose triple [1,2,0].
  lt('1.2.0-rc1', '1.2.0');
  gt('1.2.0', '1.2');
  gt('1.2.0-rc1', '1.2');   // order is 1.2 < 1.2.0-rc1 < 1.2.0 (transitive, no ties)
});

test('opaque labels fall back to created_at then lexicographic', () => {
  lt('nightly', 'qa', '2026-01-01T00:00:00.000Z', '2026-01-02T00:00:00.000Z');
  gt('nightly', 'qa', '2026-01-03T00:00:00.000Z', '2026-01-02T00:00:00.000Z');
  lt('aaa', 'bbb');
  gt('zzz', 'aaa');
});

test('createdAt tiebreaks equal versions', () => {
  lt('1.2.3', '1.2.3', '2026-01-01T00:00:00.000Z', '2026-01-02T00:00:00.000Z');
  gt('1.2.3', '1.2.3', '2026-01-02T00:00:00.000Z', '2026-01-01T00:00:00.000Z');
  eq('1.2.3', '1.2.3');
});

test('mixed semver / non-semver is deterministic and never throws', () => {
  const r = compareVersions('1.2.3', 'nightly');
  assert.equal(typeof r, 'number');
  assert.equal(compareVersions('1.2.3', 'nightly'), compareVersions('1.2.3', 'nightly'));
});

test('total order on empty / null-ish input, no exceptions', () => {
  assert.equal(compareVersions('', ''), 0);
  assert.equal(typeof compareVersions(null, undefined), 'number');
  assert.doesNotThrow(() => compareVersions(null, '1.2.3'));
  assert.ok(compareVersions('', '1.2.3') < 0);
});

test('parseSemver', () => {
  assert.deepEqual(parseSemver('1.2.3'), { major: 1, minor: 2, patch: 3, pre: '' });
  assert.deepEqual(parseSemver('v1.2.3-rc1'), { major: 1, minor: 2, patch: 3, pre: 'rc1' });
  assert.equal(parseSemver('1.2'), null);
  assert.equal(parseSemver('nightly'), null);
  assert.equal(parseSemver(''), null);
  assert.equal(parseSemver(null), null);
});

```

`server/test/viacode.test.js`:

```js
'use strict';

// Tests for the optional "sent from code" provenance ingest fields:
// `sentViaCode`, `callerFile`, `callerLine`.
//
// Covers:
//   - round-trip: POST /api/logs with sentViaCode=true + callerFile/line -> 201,
//     then GET /api/logs/:id reflects them (sentViaCode true, caller file:line);
//   - absent fields: sentViaCode is false and the caller fields are null
//     (the "overlay send" default, stable shape);
//   - sentViaCode=false explicitly behaves like absent (false, no caller badge);
//   - callerFile is trimmed and clamped to 260 chars;
//   - a non-boolean sentViaCode collapses to false (no 400);
//   - a non-integer / negative callerLine collapses to null (no 400).

const { test, before, after } = require('node:test');
const assert = require('node:assert/strict');
const { setup, makeIngestBody, req } = require('./helpers');

const ctx = setup();

before(async () => {
  await ctx.ready;
  ctx.addApp({ appId: 'testapp', name: 'Test App' });
});

after(() => ctx.close());

// Ingest a body, assert 201, fetch its meta, and return the meta body.
async function ingestAndGetMeta(overrides) {
  const ingestRes = await req(ctx.baseUrl, '/api/logs', {
    method: 'POST',
    body: makeIngestBody(overrides),
  });
  assert.equal(ingestRes.status, 201,
    `expected 201, got ${ingestRes.status}: ${JSON.stringify(ingestRes.body)}`);
  const { id } = ingestRes.body;

  const metaRes = await req(ctx.baseUrl, `/api/logs/${id}`);
  assert.equal(metaRes.status, 200,
    `meta expected 200, got ${metaRes.status}: ${JSON.stringify(metaRes.body)}`);
  return metaRes.body;
}

// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
// 1. Round-trip: a code send with caller file:line surfaces in the meta.
// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
test('POST /api/logs round-trips sentViaCode=true and callerFile/callerLine', async () => {
  const meta = await ingestAndGetMeta({
    sentViaCode: true,
    callerFile: 'Assets/Scripts/Boss/BossFight.cs',
    callerLine: 142,
  });

  assert.equal(meta.sentViaCode, true, 'sentViaCode must be true for a code send');
  assert.equal(meta.callerFile, 'Assets/Scripts/Boss/BossFight.cs', 'callerFile round-trips');
  assert.equal(meta.callerLine, 142, 'callerLine round-trips');
});

// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
// 2. Absent fields -> overlay-send default (false + null caller fields).
// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
test('POST /api/logs without the via-code fields returns sentViaCode=false and null caller', async () => {
  const meta = await ingestAndGetMeta({});
  assert.equal(meta.sentViaCode, false, 'absent sentViaCode defaults to false');
  assert.equal(meta.callerFile, null, 'absent callerFile surfaces as null');
  assert.equal(meta.callerLine, null, 'absent callerLine surfaces as null');
});

// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
// 3. sentViaCode=false explicitly behaves like absent (no caller badge).
// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
test('POST /api/logs with sentViaCode=false is treated as an overlay send', async () => {
  const meta = await ingestAndGetMeta({ sentViaCode: false });
  assert.equal(meta.sentViaCode, false, 'explicit false stays false');
  assert.equal(meta.callerFile, null, 'no caller file for an overlay send');
  assert.equal(meta.callerLine, null, 'no caller line for an overlay send');
});

// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
// 4. callerFile is trimmed and clamped to 260 chars.
// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
test('POST /api/logs trims and clamps callerFile to 260 chars', async () => {
  const long = '  ' + 'f'.repeat(400) + '  ';
  const meta = await ingestAndGetMeta({ sentViaCode: true, callerFile: long });
  assert.equal(meta.callerFile.length, 260, 'callerFile clamped to 260 chars');
  assert.equal(meta.callerFile, 'f'.repeat(260), 'trimmed then clamped');
});

// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
// 5. A non-boolean sentViaCode collapses to false (no 400).
// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
test('POST /api/logs coerces a non-boolean sentViaCode to false', async () => {
  // Only a literal JSON true counts as a code send; a truthy non-boolean (e.g.
  // the string "true" or 1) must NOT flip the flag, so the column stays clean.
  const meta = await ingestAndGetMeta({ sentViaCode: 'true' });
  assert.equal(meta.sentViaCode, false, 'a non-boolean sentViaCode collapses to false');
});

// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
// 6. A non-integer / negative callerLine collapses to null (no 400).
// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
test('POST /api/logs drops a non-integer or negative callerLine to null', async () => {
  const bad = await ingestAndGetMeta({ sentViaCode: true, callerLine: 'NaN' });
  assert.equal(bad.callerLine, null, 'a non-numeric callerLine collapses to null');

  const negative = await ingestAndGetMeta({ sentViaCode: true, callerLine: -5 });
  assert.equal(negative.callerLine, null, 'a negative callerLine collapses to null');

  // A floating-point line number is floored to an integer.
  const floored = await ingestAndGetMeta({ sentViaCode: true, callerLine: 99.9 });
  assert.equal(floored.callerLine, 99, 'a fractional callerLine is floored');
});

```

`test/e2e/README.md`:

```md
# End-to-end harness

This harness exercises FastLogs as a whole: a **real server** and a **real built
player** talking over the live command channel. It exists to catch defects that
the unit and integration suites structurally cannot see, because those run the
server and the client in isolation and never build an actual player. Real defects
it has caught, each invisible to the unit tests:

- **A wrong built-in command name.** Only a real registry plus a real enqueue reveal it; a unit test asserting on the same constant just agrees with itself.
- **The component-capture session cap.** The auto cap must stop by itself while manual capture keeps working - behaviour that only exists once something has actually been capturing for a while.
- **Built-in module dependencies missing from the package manifest.** A host project masks this: its own manifest happens to pull the modules in. Only building a **clean** project against the package alone fails, which is exactly what this harness does.

`run.mjs` has no dependencies of its own (Node >= 18): it boots the server
from `../../server` against throwaway `DATA_DIR`/`BLOB_DIR` in the OS temp dir
with fixed test tokens, launches the player you point it at, runs the assertions,
tears everything down, and prints one JSON verdict. The server it boots is the
real one, so install the server's own dependency once before the first run:
`cd ../../server && npm install`. Without it the server process dies on startup
with `Cannot find module 'better-sqlite3'` and the harness reports
`server did not become healthy`.

## What it checks

Thirteen assertions, in the order `run.mjs` runs them:

| # | Assertion |
|---|-----------|
| 1 | `fastlogs.ping` round-trips as `pong` via the scoped command token over `/api/v1` |
| 2 | The liveness beacon agrees the build is polling (`pollingNow: true`, `lastPollSecAgo <= 10`). Checked after the ping, because the app only exists server-side once its first report auto-registers it |
| 3 | A custom `test.echo` handler (registered with `FastLogs.RegisterCommand`) uppercases its args |
| 4 | Manual `fastlogs.capture` still works **after** the tiny auto-cap burned out, and says so: `captured >= 2`, `autoStopped: true`, `sessionCount >= captured` |
| 5 | An oversized result (`test.big` returns 150 KB, over the 64 KB inline cap) is offloaded to a file, and the reference downloads whole with `bytes` matching |
| 6 | `fastlogs.inspect` returns **one** component's state: `SmokeMarker` with its `Ticks` field |
| 7 | `fastlogs.inspect` reports renderer state: `ParticleSystemRenderer` with material/shader/MPB probes |
| 8 | A command auto-correlates the report it produced: `await?code=<commandId>` finds it, no timestamp matching |
| 9 | `fastlogs.scenecontext` produces a report with an id/url |
| 10 | That report carries a non-empty `sceneContext` tree |
| 11 | The raw log ring contains `[flog.capture]` lines |
| 12 | Per-frame field changes are debounced (the flood is tamed: 1..15 changes) |
| 13 | An `ev:cap` marker is written when the auto cap stops |

Assertions 10-13 read the report that 9 produced, so they only run when it landed. Everything is asserted within the **last** session in the raw log: the recording is persistent and carries earlier runs.

The harness builds a standalone player (`unity-project/BuildSmoke.cs`), so every
assertion here drives the non-WebGL command transport: the coroutine poll loop in
`CommandChannel`. The client's other transport, a JS `setInterval` poll compiled
in only for WebGL builds, has no harness and is not covered by this suite.

## The server contract

`run.mjs` starts the server with fixed identities; the built player must carry the
matching ingest token. Keep these in sync:

| run.mjs constant | value | Used by |
|------------------|-------|---------|
| `TOKENS.TEAM_INGEST_TOKEN` | `e2e-team` | `TestBoot.cs` -> `FastLogsConfig.Server.Token` (auto-registers the app) |
| `TOKENS.COMMAND_TOKEN` | `e2e-cmd` | drives the command channel from the harness |
| `APP` | `upm-smoke` | `TestBoot.cs` -> `FastLogsConfig.Server.AppId` |
| `DEFAULT_PORT` | `8787` | `TestBoot.cs` `EndpointUrl` port |

`ADMIN_TOKEN`/`VIEWER_TOKEN` are also set (admin superset, viewer read) and
`ALLOW_AUTO_REGISTER=true` lets the unknown app self-onboard on its first report.
`DISK_MONITOR_INTERVAL_SEC=0` keeps the run hermetic (no statfs, no webhook).

The player must also answer the two commands the harness sends beyond the
built-ins. `TestBoot.cs` registers both, and assertions 3 and 5 fail without them:

| Command | `TestBoot.cs` registers | Drives |
|---------|------------------------|--------|
| `test.echo` | uppercases its args | assertion 3 (custom handler) |
| `test.big` | returns a 150 KB string | assertion 5 (result offload) |

`TestBoot.cs` also spawns a `SmokeMarker` component (a `Ticks` counter) and a
`ParticleSystem` as the targets assertions 6 and 7 inspect.

## Build the player (Unity CLI)

Requires Unity **2022.3 or newer** (the package's minimum; newer LTS lines also
work). Build a throwaway project that references this repo's package, then drop
in the two templates from `unity-project/`. From this directory:

```bash
UNITY=/path/to/Unity            # the Unity executable
PROJ=/tmp/fastlogs-e2e-proj     # throwaway project path

# 1. Create an empty project.
"$UNITY" -batchmode -quit -createProject "$PROJ" -logFile -

# 2. Reference the package (local file: path shown; a git URL works too).
#    From the repo root, unity/ holds the package.
cat > "$PROJ/Packages/manifest.json" <<'JSON'
{
  "dependencies": {
    "com.playjoy.fastlogs": "file:../../../path/to/repo/unity",
    "com.unity.modules.particlesystem": "1.0.0"
  }
}
JSON

# 3. Copy the two templates into the project.
mkdir -p "$PROJ/Assets/Editor"
cp unity-project/TestBoot.cs   "$PROJ/Assets/TestBoot.cs"
cp unity-project/BuildSmoke.cs "$PROJ/Assets/Editor/BuildSmoke.cs"

# 4. Build the standalone dev player (writes $PROJ/Build/Smoke.exe).
"$UNITY" -batchmode -quit -projectPath "$PROJ" -executeMethod BuildSmoke.Build -logFile -
```

The manifest stays minimal on purpose: the package declares its own built-in
module dependencies, and `com.unity.modules.particlesystem` is only here because
`TestBoot.cs` spawns a `ParticleSystem` as a capture target. Do not add
`testables` - the harness builds a player, not a test run.

The `file:` path in the manifest is relative to the project's `Packages/`
directory, so adjust it to point at this repo's `unity/` folder (or swap it for a
git URL such as `https://github.com/AitiX/Fastlogs.git?path=/unity`).

On macOS/Linux, change `BuildTarget`/`locationPathName` in `BuildSmoke.cs` and
point `--player` at the produced player.

## Run it

```bash
node run.mjs --player /tmp/fastlogs-e2e-proj/Build/Smoke.exe
# optional: --port 8787  (must match the port compiled into TestBoot.cs)
```

Exit code is `0` when every assertion passes, `1` otherwise. The full result set
is printed as one pretty-printed JSON object at the end of stdout, and the
per-assertion PASS/FAIL lines go to stderr. The server the harness boots inherits
both streams, so its own log is split the way it writes it: `[server] FastLogs
listening ...` lands on stdout ahead of the verdict, its warnings and errors on
stderr. Stdout is therefore not a clean JSON stream to pipe into a parser. Each
phase has a timeout so a stuck build or server can never hang the run forever.

```

`test/e2e/run.mjs`:

```mjs
#!/usr/bin/env node
// Self-contained end-to-end harness (Node >= 18, zero deps).
//
// It boots the REAL server from ../../server against isolated tmp dirs, launches
// a REAL built player, and drives the command channel end-to-end. This catches
// defects that unit/integration tests cannot see: a live build talking to a live
// server over the non-WebGL command transport (a bad builtin-command name and a
// wrong session-cap behaviour both slipped past the unit suite and were caught
// here). Usage:
//
//   node run.mjs --player <path-to-built-player> [--port 8787]
//
// The player must be built from the templates in ./unity-project (its endpoint,
// appId and ingest token are baked in at build time), so --port must match the
// port compiled into TestBoot.cs (default 8787). One JSON verdict is printed to
// stdout; exit code is 0 on all-pass, 1 otherwise.

import { spawn, spawnSync } from 'node:child_process';
import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url';
import fs from 'node:fs';
import os from 'node:os';
import path from 'node:path';
import crypto from 'node:crypto';

const HERE = path.dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url));
const SERVER_DIR = path.resolve(HERE, '..', '..', 'server');
const SERVER_ENTRY = path.join(SERVER_DIR, 'src', 'server.js');

// --- Fixed test configuration ------------------------------------------------
// The COMMAND token drives the channel from here. The TEAM token is what the
// built player carries (unity-project/TestBoot.cs -> FastLogsConfig.Server.Token)
// so its first boot report auto-registers the app under ALLOW_AUTO_REGISTER.
const TOKENS = {
  ADMIN_TOKEN: 'e2e-admin',
  VIEWER_TOKEN: 'e2e-viewer',
  TEAM_INGEST_TOKEN: 'e2e-team',
  COMMAND_TOKEN: 'e2e-cmd',
};
const APP = 'upm-smoke';   // must match TestBoot.cs FastLogsConfig.Server.AppId
const DEFAULT_PORT = 8787; // must match the port in TestBoot.cs EndpointUrl

// Per-phase timeouts (ms) so the harness never hangs forever.
const TIMEOUTS = {
  serverHealth: 20000,
  enqueue: 90000,   // player boot + auto-register can take a while
  command: 40000,
  autoCapDrain: 6000,
};

const sleep = (ms) => new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, ms));

function parseArgs(argv) {
  const out = { player: null, port: DEFAULT_PORT };
  for (let i = 0; i < argv.length; i++) {
    const a = argv[i];
    if (a === '--player') out.player = argv[++i];
    else if (a.startsWith('--player=')) out.player = a.slice('--player='.length);
    else if (a === '--port') out.port = Number(argv[++i]);
    else if (a.startsWith('--port=')) out.port = Number(a.slice('--port='.length));
  }
  return out;
}

// Kill a process and its children. Unity spawns helper processes, so a plain
// process.kill(pid) can orphan them; taskkill /T tears down the whole tree.
function killTree(pid) {
  if (!pid) return;
  if (process.platform === 'win32') {
    try { spawnSync('taskkill', ['/PID', String(pid), '/T', '/F'], { stdio: 'ignore' }); return; } catch {}
  }
  try { process.kill(pid); } catch {}
}

async function waitForHealth(base, timeoutMs) {
  const deadline = Date.now() + timeoutMs;
  while (Date.now() < deadline) {
    try {
      const r = await fetch(base + '/api/health');
      if (r.status === 200) { const j = await r.json().catch(() => null); if (j && j.ok) return true; }
    } catch {}
    await sleep(300);
  }
  return false;
}

async function main() {
  const args = parseArgs(process.argv.slice(2));
  if (!args.player) {
    console.log(JSON.stringify({ ok: false, error: 'missing --player <path-to-built-player>' }));
    process.exit(1);
  }
  if (!fs.existsSync(args.player)) {
    console.log(JSON.stringify({ ok: false, error: `player not found: ${args.player}` }));
    process.exit(1);
  }
  if (!fs.existsSync(SERVER_ENTRY)) {
    console.log(JSON.stringify({ ok: false, error: `server entry not found: ${SERVER_ENTRY}` }));
    process.exit(1);
  }

  const BASE = `http://127.0.0.1:${args.port}`;
  const CMD_TOKEN = TOKENS.COMMAND_TOKEN;

  // Isolated, disposable storage in the OS temp dir.
  const suffix = crypto.randomBytes(6).toString('hex');
  const tmpBase = path.join(os.tmpdir(), `fastlogs-e2e-${suffix}`);
  const dataDir = path.join(tmpBase, 'data');
  const blobDir = path.join(tmpBase, 'blobs');
  const playerLog = path.join(tmpBase, 'player.log');
  fs.mkdirSync(dataDir, { recursive: true });
  fs.mkdirSync(blobDir, { recursive: true });

  const results = [];
  const rec = (n, ok, d) => { results.push({ n, ok, d }); console.error(ok ? 'PASS' : 'FAIL', n, d || ''); };

  let server = null;
  let player = null;
  let jiraMock = null;

  // Mock Jira Cloud (create issue + add comment) for the ticket-from-report checks.
  // Counts calls so the dedup assertion can prove "one create, one comment".
  let jiraCreates = 0;
  let jiraComments = 0;
  const { createServer } = await import('node:http');
  jiraMock = createServer((req, res) => {
    let data = '';
    req.on('data', (c) => { data += c; });
    req.on('end', () => {
      if (/\/rest\/api\/3\/issue$/.test(req.url)) {
        jiraCreates += 1;
        res.writeHead(201, { 'content-type': 'application/json' });
        res.end(JSON.stringify({ id: String(9000 + jiraCreates), key: 'E2E-' + jiraCreates }));
        return;
      }
      if (/\/comment$/.test(req.url)) {
        jiraComments += 1;
        res.writeHead(201, { 'content-type': 'application/json' });
        res.end('{"id":"1"}');
        return;
      }
      res.writeHead(404); res.end('{}');
    });
  });
  await new Promise((r) => jiraMock.listen(0, '127.0.0.1', r));
  const jiraPort = jiraMock.address().port;

  const cleanup = () => {
    if (player) killTree(player.pid);
    if (server) killTree(server.pid);
    if (jiraMock) try { jiraMock.close(); } catch {}
    try { fs.rmSync(tmpBase, { recursive: true, force: true }); } catch {}
  };

  // --- HTTP + command helpers (ported from the field-proven drive.mjs) -------
  async function http(method, urlPath, body, bearer) {
    const r = await fetch(BASE + urlPath, {
      method,
      headers: {
        ...(body ? { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' } : {}),
        ...(bearer ? { Authorization: 'Bearer ' + bearer } : {}),
      },
      body: body ? JSON.stringify(body) : undefined,
    });
    let json = null, text = null;
    const ct = r.headers.get('content-type') || '';
    if (ct.includes('json')) { try { json = await r.json(); } catch {} }
    else { try { text = await r.text(); } catch {} }
    return { status: r.status, json, text };
  }

  async function runCommand(name, cmdArgs, timeoutSec = 30) {
    // The app auto-registers on the player's first boot report, so the enqueue
    // may 4xx for a while; retry until the app exists or the phase times out.
    const deadlineEnq = Date.now() + TIMEOUTS.enqueue;
    let enq;
    while (Date.now() < deadlineEnq) {
      enq = await http('POST', `/api/v1/commands/${APP}`, { name, ...(cmdArgs !== undefined ? { args: cmdArgs } : {}) }, CMD_TOKEN);
      if (enq.status === 201) break;
      await sleep(2000);
    }
    if (!enq || enq.status !== 201) return { error: `enqueue failed: ${enq && enq.status} ${JSON.stringify(enq && enq.json)}` };
    const id = enq.json.id;
    const deadline = Date.now() + timeoutSec * 1000;
    while (Date.now() < deadline) {
      await sleep(1000);
      const res = await http('GET', `/api/v1/commands/${APP}/result/${id}`, null, CMD_TOKEN);
      if (res.status !== 200) return { error: `result ${res.status}` };
      if (res.json.status === 'done' || res.json.status === 'error') return res.json;
    }
    return { error: 'ack timeout' };
  }

  try {
    // --- Phase 1: boot the server -------------------------------------------
    server = spawn(process.execPath, [SERVER_ENTRY], {
      cwd: SERVER_DIR,
      stdio: ['ignore', 'inherit', 'inherit'],
      env: {
        ...process.env,
        HOST: '127.0.0.1',
        PORT: String(args.port),
        DATA_DIR: dataDir,
        BLOB_DIR: blobDir,
        BASE_URL: BASE,
        ...TOKENS,
        ALLOW_AUTO_REGISTER: 'true',
        DISK_MONITOR_INTERVAL_SEC: '0', // keep the run hermetic (no statfs/webhook)
        IP_SALT: 'e2e-salt',
        // Ticket-from-report against the local mock Jira (never the real network).
        TRACKER: 'jira',
        JIRA_SITE: `http://127.0.0.1:${jiraPort}`,
        JIRA_EMAIL: 'e2e@example.com',
        JIRA_API_TOKEN: 'e2e-jira-token',
        JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: 'E2E',
        JIRA_TIMEOUT_MS: '2000',
      },
    });

    const healthy = await waitForHealth(BASE, TIMEOUTS.serverHealth);
    if (!healthy) throw new Error(`server did not become healthy at ${BASE}/api/health`);

    // --- Phase 2: launch the built player -----------------------------------
    player = spawn(args.player, ['-batchmode', '-nographics', '-logFile', playerLog], {
      stdio: 'ignore',
    });

    // --- Phase 3: assertions -------------------------------------------------
    // 1. Scoped-token round trip via a built-in command over the coroutine transport.
    const ping = await runCommand('fastlogs.ping', undefined, 40);
    rec('ping via COMMAND_TOKEN over /api/v1 (coroutine transport)',
      ping.status === 'done' && ping.result === 'pong', JSON.stringify(ping));

    // 2. Liveness beacon: the ping above proves a build is polling, so the beacon
    // must agree. Checked after it, because the app only exists on the server once
    // its first report auto-registers it.
    const health = await http('GET', `/api/v1/commands/${APP}/health`, null, TOKENS.COMMAND_TOKEN);
    rec('channel health reports the polling build as live',
      health.status === 200 && health.json && health.json.pollingNow === true && health.json.lastPollSecAgo <= 10,
      JSON.stringify(health.json));

    // 2. Custom game handler registered through FastLogs.RegisterCommand.
    const echo = await runCommand('test.echo', { a: 1 });
    rec('custom command test.echo uppercases its args',
      echo.status === 'done' && echo.result === '{"A":1}', JSON.stringify(echo));

    // 3. Manual component-capture AFTER the tiny auto-cap has burned out: the
    // fixed design must keep manual capture working and report the auto stop.
    await sleep(TIMEOUTS.autoCapDrain);
    const cap = await runCommand('fastlogs.capture');
    let capOk = false, capDetail = JSON.stringify(cap);
    if (cap.status === 'done' && cap.result) {
      try {
        const cr = JSON.parse(cap.result);
        capOk = cr.captured >= 2 && cr.autoStopped === true && cr.sessionCount >= cr.captured;
        capDetail = cap.result;
      } catch { capDetail = 'bad json: ' + cap.result; }
    }
    rec('manual capture works after the auto cap (structured result)', capOk, capDetail);

    // A result over the server's inline cap (64 KB) is uploaded as a file and the ack
    // carries a reference: without it the ack 400s and the agent only sees a timeout.
    const big = await runCommand('test.big', undefined, 40);
    let bigOk = false, bigDetail = JSON.stringify(big).slice(0, 140);
    if (big.status === 'done' && big.result) {
      try {
        const ref = JSON.parse(big.result);
        const url = ref['__fastlogsResultRef'];
        if (url) {
          const blob = await fetch(url);
          const text = await blob.text();
          bigOk = blob.status === 200 && text.length === 150000 && ref.bytes === 150000;
          bigDetail = `ref bytes=${ref.bytes} downloaded=${text.length}`;
        } else {
          bigDetail = 'no ref key in result: ' + big.result.slice(0, 80);
        }
      } catch (e) { bigDetail = 'bad ref json: ' + String(big.result).slice(0, 80); }
    }
    rec('an oversized command result is offloaded to a file and downloadable', bigOk, bigDetail);

    // 4. Point inspection: ask the live build for ONE component's state instead of a
    // whole scene dump. This is the original pain (what is in this particle's MPB?).
    const insp = await runCommand('fastlogs.inspect', { type: 'SmokeMarker' });
    let inspOk = false, inspDetail = JSON.stringify(insp).slice(0, 160);
    if (insp.status === 'done' && insp.result) {
      try {
        const r = JSON.parse(insp.result);
        const item = r.items && r.items[0];
        inspOk = r.n >= 1 && item && item.ev === 'inspect' && item.t === 'SmokeMarker' &&
                 item.f && Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(item.f, 'Ticks');
        inspDetail = JSON.stringify(item && item.f);
      } catch (e) { inspDetail = 'bad json: ' + String(insp.result).slice(0, 120); }
    }
    rec('fastlogs.inspect returns one component state (fields incl. Ticks)', inspOk, inspDetail);

    // The renderer path is what the feature was born for: probe a ParticleSystemRenderer.
    const inspR = await runCommand('fastlogs.inspect', { type: 'ParticleSystemRenderer', props: ['_MainTex', '_Color'] });
    let inspROk = false, inspRDetail = JSON.stringify(inspR).slice(0, 160);
    if (inspR.status === 'done' && inspR.result) {
      try {
        const r = JSON.parse(inspR.result);
        const item = r.items && r.items[0];
        inspROk = r.n >= 1 && item && item.r && Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(item.r, 'mat') &&
                  Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(item.r, 'mpb');
        inspRDetail = JSON.stringify(item && item.r);
      } catch (e) { inspRDetail = 'bad json'; }
    }
    rec('fastlogs.inspect reports renderer state (material/shader/MPB probes)', inspROk, inspRDetail);

    // 4. A command tags every report it produces with its own id as the correlation
    // code, so /api/await?code=<commandId> finds it without matching on timestamps.
    const corrEnq = await http('POST', `/api/v1/commands/${APP}`, { name: 'fastlogs.send', args: { title: 'e2e correlated report' } }, TOKENS.COMMAND_TOKEN);
    let corrOk = false, corrDetail = `enqueue ${corrEnq.status}`;
    if (corrEnq.status === 201) {
      const cmdId = corrEnq.json.id;
      for (let i = 0; i < 30; i++) {
        await sleep(1000);
        const res = await http('GET', `/api/v1/commands/${APP}/result/${cmdId}`, null, TOKENS.COMMAND_TOKEN);
        if (res.json && (res.json.status === 'done' || res.json.status === 'error')) break;
      }
      const found = await http('GET', `/api/v1/await/${APP}?code=${encodeURIComponent(cmdId)}&token=${TOKENS.VIEWER_TOKEN}`);
      corrOk = found.status === 200 && found.json && found.json.found === true && !!found.json.id;
      corrDetail = `cmd ${cmdId} -> ${JSON.stringify(found.json)}`;
    }
    rec('a command auto-correlates the report it produced (await by command id)', corrOk, corrDetail);

    // 4. Scene-context headless send; then check the report row and its payload.
    const sc = await runCommand('fastlogs.scenecontext');
    let scOk = false, scDetail = JSON.stringify(sc), reportId = null;
    if (sc.status === 'done' && sc.result) {
      try { const rr = JSON.parse(sc.result); if (rr.ok && rr.id) { reportId = rr.id; scOk = true; scDetail = rr.url; } } catch {}
    }
    rec('fastlogs.sceneContext produced a report', scOk, scDetail);

    if (reportId) {
      const meta = await http('GET', `/api/v1/logs/${reportId}`, null, null);
      const hasScene = meta.status === 200 && meta.json && typeof meta.json.sceneContext === 'string' && meta.json.sceneContext.length > 50;
      rec('report carries a non-empty sceneContext tree', hasScene, `len=${meta.json && meta.json.sceneContext ? meta.json.sceneContext.length : 0}`);

      const raw = await http('GET', `/${reportId}/raw`, null, null);
      // A persistent recording carries prior sessions; assert only within the LAST one.
      const full = raw.text || '';
      const lastSession = full.slice(full.lastIndexOf('==== FastLogs session'));
      const changeCount = (lastSession.match(/"ev":"change"/g) || []).length;
      const capMarks = (lastSession.match(/"ev":"cap"/g) || []).length;
      rec('log ring contains [flog.capture] lines', lastSession.includes('[flog.capture]'), `rawLen=${full.length}`);
      rec('change captures are debounced (flood tamed)', changeCount >= 1 && changeCount <= 15, `changes=${changeCount}`);
      rec('ev:cap marker written when the auto cap stops', capMarks >= 1, `capMarks=${capMarks}`);
    }

    // Ticket-from-report, end to end through the PLAYER: test.ticket fires two sends
    // of the same deterministic error with createIssue:true. The first must file ONE
    // ticket on the mock tracker; the second must dedup onto it (same URL, one create,
    // one comment). This exercises the full chain: facade -> DTO -> wire -> ingest
    // hook -> tracker adapter -> dedup table -> response -> client parse.
    const ticket = await runCommand('test.ticket', undefined, 40);
    const parts = String(ticket.result || '').split('|');
    const bothBrowse = parts.length === 2 && parts.every((p) => /\/browse\/E2E-\d+$/.test(p));
    rec('player-initiated send files a tracker ticket (browse URL returned)',
      ticket.status === 'done' && bothBrowse, JSON.stringify(ticket.result));
    rec('second report of the same crash dedups onto the SAME ticket',
      bothBrowse && parts[0] === parts[1], `urls=${ticket.result}`);
    rec('mock tracker saw exactly one create and one comment',
      jiraCreates === 1 && jiraComments === 1, `creates=${jiraCreates} comments=${jiraComments}`);
  } catch (err) {
    rec('harness ran without a fatal error', false, String(err && err.message || err));
  } finally {
    cleanup();
  }

  const ok = results.length > 0 && results.every((r) => r.ok);
  console.log(JSON.stringify({ ok, player: args.player, port: args.port, results }, null, 1));
  process.exit(ok ? 0 : 1);
}

main();

```

`test/e2e/unity-project/BuildSmoke.cs`:

```cs
using UnityEditor;
using UnityEditor.Build.Reporting;
using UnityEditor.SceneManagement;
using UnityEngine;

// Editor entry point invoked headless: -executeMethod BuildSmoke.Build.
// Builds a single-scene Windows standalone DEVELOPMENT player that run.mjs then
// drives. On macOS/Linux change target + locationPathName accordingly.
public static class BuildSmoke
{
    public static void Build()
    {
        var scene = EditorSceneManager.NewScene(NewSceneSetup.DefaultGameObjects, NewSceneMode.Single);
        EditorSceneManager.SaveScene(scene, "Assets/Smoke.unity");

        var report = BuildPipeline.BuildPlayer(new BuildPlayerOptions
        {
            scenes = new[] { "Assets/Smoke.unity" },
            locationPathName = "Build/Smoke.exe",
            target = BuildTarget.StandaloneWindows64,
            options = BuildOptions.Development,
        });

        Debug.Log("[BuildSmoke] result=" + report.summary.result + " size=" + report.summary.totalSize);
        EditorApplication.Exit(report.summary.result == BuildResult.Succeeded ? 0 : 1);
    }
}

```

`test/e2e/unity-project/TestBoot.cs`:

```cs
using UnityEngine;
using PlayJoy.FastLogs;

// Regression bootstrap for the capture-cadence fix: uncapped fps + a field that
// changes EVERY frame + a tiny session cap. The old design burned the cap in
// seconds and then killed manual capture too; the fixed design must debounce
// changes, stop only AUTOMATIC captures, and keep the manual pass working.
//
// Server contract (must match test/e2e/run.mjs):
//   Server.Token       = "e2e-team"  -> TOKENS.TEAM_INGEST_TOKEN (auto-registers the app)
//   Server.AppId       = "upm-smoke" -> APP
//   EndpointUrl port   = 8787        -> DEFAULT_PORT (pass --port to run.mjs if you change it)
public static class TestBoot
{
    [RuntimeInitializeOnLoadMethod(RuntimeInitializeLoadType.AfterSceneLoad)]
    private static void Boot()
    {
        var cfg = ScriptableObject.CreateInstance<FastLogsConfig>();
        cfg.Server.EndpointUrl = "http://127.0.0.1:8787/api/logs";
        cfg.Server.AppId = "upm-smoke";
        cfg.Server.Token = "e2e-team";
        cfg.Recording.Enabled = true;
        cfg.Recording.AutoStartRecording = true;
        cfg.Screenshot.CaptureByDefault = false;
        cfg.Commands.Enabled = true;
        cfg.Commands.PollIntervalSeconds = 0.5f;
        cfg.ComponentCapture.Enabled = true;
        cfg.ComponentCapture.ScanIntervalSeconds = 0.25f;
        cfg.ComponentCapture.MaxCapturesPerSession = 4;
        cfg.ComponentCapture.Rules = new[]
        {
            new FastLogsConfig.CaptureRule { TypeName = "ParticleSystemRenderer" },
            new FastLogsConfig.CaptureRule { TypeName = "SmokeMarker", CaptureOnChange = true },
        };

        FastLogs.Init(cfg);
        FastLogs.RegisterCommand("test.echo", args => (args ?? "null").ToUpperInvariant());
        // Result far past the server's inline cap: proves the offload-to-file path.
        FastLogs.RegisterCommand("test.big", args => new string('x', 150000));
        // Ticket-from-report, end to end THROUGH THE PLAYER: two sends of the same
        // deterministic error must yield ONE tracker ticket - the first send files it,
        // the second dedups onto it by crash signature. Resolves "url1|url2" so the
        // harness can assert both are present and EQUAL.
        FastLogs.RegisterCommandAsync("test.ticket", args =>
        {
            FastLogs.Error("E2ETicketProbe: deterministic error for dedup");
            var outTask = FlogTask.Create<string>();
            var first = FastLogs.SendHeadlessAsync(false, "ticket probe 1", null, createIssue: true, issueSubject: "e2e ticket");
            first.GetAwaiter().OnCompleted(() =>
            {
                var second = FastLogs.SendHeadlessAsync(false, "ticket probe 2", null, createIssue: true);
                second.GetAwaiter().OnCompleted(() =>
                {
                    outTask.SetResult((first.Result.IssueUrl ?? "none") + "|" + (second.Result.IssueUrl ?? "none"));
                });
            });
            return outTask;
        });
        FastLogs.SetContext("smoke", "cadence-regression");

        var go = new GameObject("SmokeParticles");
        go.AddComponent<ParticleSystem>();
        go.AddComponent<SmokeMarker>();

        // First report auto-registers the app so the command poll stops 404ing.
        FastLogs.SendHeadlessAsync(false, "smoke boot");
    }
}

public class SmokeMarker : MonoBehaviour
{
    [SerializeField] private string _label = "smoke-v1";
    public int Ticks;
    private void Update() { Ticks++; }
}

```

`unity/ARCHITECTURE.md`:

```md
# FastLogs (Unity) - internal architecture

Maintainer notes for the Unity client. This is not the integration guide (see
`../docs/UNITY.md` for that) and not the wire format (see `../CONTRACT.md`). It
describes how the package is put together so someone changing it knows where
things live and why they are shaped the way they are.

- Package: `com.playjoy.fastlogs`, `unity/package.json`. Targets Unity 2022.3+,
  runs on Unity 6. No third-party dependencies. SRDebugger is soft
  (`SRDEBUGGER` versionDefine), never a hard reference.
- Namespaces: `PlayJoy.FastLogs` (runtime), `PlayJoy.FastLogs.Editor`,
  `PlayJoy.FastLogs.Tests`, `PlayJoy.FastLogs.Samples`.
- No em dash anywhere in the code or docs. Use a hyphen.

## Layers

The package is a thin always-compiled facade over a gated runtime, plus a set of
swappable services behind interfaces.

- Facade: `FastLogs` (`Runtime/Core/FastLogs.cs`). Static, public, always
  compiles. Game code only ever touches this.
- Host: `FastLogsRuntime` (`Runtime/Core/FastLogsRuntime.cs`). One hidden
  `DontDestroyOnLoad` MonoBehaviour. Owns the services, pumps triggers, drives
  the send pipeline. The whole file is gated - it does not exist in a stripped
  build.
- Services: interfaces in `Runtime/Core/Interfaces.cs`, default implementations
  wired by `FastLogsDefaultServices` (`Runtime/Core/FastLogsDefaultServices.cs`),
  which extends `FastLogsCaptureServices` and inherits `CreateLogSource` from it.
  A project registers its own via `FastLogs.SetServicesProvider` before `Init`;
  most do not, and the defaults are the product. The seam is public on purpose:
  `FastLogsDefaultServices` is public and unsealed, and the concretes it hands
  back (`CapturingLogSource`, `UnityWebRequestUploader`, `ScreenshotCapturer`,
  `ClipboardService`) are public too, so a host can override one factory and keep
  the rest. Its two UI factories, `CreateTriggerSource` and `CreateOverlay`, both
  gate on `config.UI.EnableUI` through `UiEnabled`: the toggle gesture goes with
  the overlay, because opening it is the gesture's only job (the quick-send
  gesture is separate, owned by the runtime, and keeps working with no UI).
  `FastLogsHeadlessServices` (same file) is the shipped no-UI variant: it
  overrides those same two factories to return null outright, so it never
  consults `UiEnabled` and is independent of the config asset. Neither route
  worked before 0.5.3 (the types were internal sealed, and `EnableUI` was read
  only by the loop guard's confirm dialog, so the overlay was always built), so
  `ServicesUiSeamTests` now pins both the visibility and the withholding. Do not
  re-seal or re-internalize these types.
- Support: config (`Runtime/Core`), DTOs and the JSON serializer (`Runtime/Model`,
  including `MiniJson`), diagnostics (`Runtime/Device/DiagnosticsCollector.cs`),
  capture (`Runtime/Capture`), net (`Runtime/Net`).
- The default service implementations live outside `Core`: the IMGUI overlay and
  its settings panel (`Runtime/UI`), the gestures behind `ITriggerSource`
  (`Runtime/Trigger`: composite, key combo, multi-tap corner), screenshots
  (`Runtime/Screenshot`), clipboard (`Runtime/Clipboard`).
- SRDebugger is touched from two places, both soft (see the versionDefine note
  above), never a hard reference: `Runtime/Capture/SrDebuggerLogSource.cs`, the
  log source `CapturingLogSource` binds when `Capture.UseSrDebuggerConsoleIfPresent`
  is on and SRDebugger resolves (a direct path under the `SRDEBUGGER` define, a
  reflection fallback without it, and `UnityLogSource` when neither binds), and
  `Runtime/Bridge/SrDebuggerBridge.cs`, a public panel/action bridge that stays
  reflection-only even under the define and that nothing in the package currently
  calls.
- QR: `Runtime/UI/QrCode.cs` is our own dependency-free ISO/IEC 18004 encoder
  (byte mode, versions 1..10). The overlay calls it directly and rasterizes the
  module matrix itself. `Runtime/Qr/QrTextureRenderer.cs` wraps the same encoder
  as modules -> Texture2D and has no caller today.

## Build gating (read this before touching any gated file)

Two independent layers decide whether FastLogs does anything.

1. Compile-time. Every gated file starts with the exact same header:

   ```csharp
   #if UNITY_EDITOR || DEVELOPMENT_BUILD || (LOGSHARE_FORCE_ENABLED && !(UNITY_PS4 || UNITY_PS5 || UNITY_GAMECORE || UNITY_SWITCH))
   #define FASTLOGS_ENABLED
   #endif
   ```

   On retail and console targets `FASTLOGS_ENABLED` is undefined, so every file
   that does networking, overlay, screenshots or log hooks compiles to nothing.
   `FastLogsGate.IsCompiledIn` reflects this symbol. When you add a new gated
   file, copy that header byte for byte - the console exclusion list is load
   bearing and a paraphrased copy is a bug.

2. Run-time. `FastLogsConfig.Enable*` flags, checked by
   `FastLogsGate.IsEnabled(config)`: Editor uses `EnableInEditor`, a dev build
   uses `EnableInDevelopment`, a forced release build uses `EnableInRelease`. So
   a dev build can still keep FastLogs dormant per project.

The facade splits its methods to make the compile-time strip clean:

- Fire-and-forget void methods (`Init`, `Log`, `Send`, `ShowOverlay`, ...) carry
  `[Conditional("UNITY_EDITOR"), Conditional("DEVELOPMENT_BUILD"), Conditional("LOGSHARE_FORCE_ENABLED")]`.
  In a stripped build both the body and every call site are removed by the
  compiler, so game code that calls them costs nothing.
- Value-returning members (`IsInitialized`, `IsRecording`, `Counts`,
  `SendAsync`, `SendHeadlessAsync`, `RecordScope`, the file/snapshot awaitables)
  cannot be `[Conditional]` - the caller needs the return value. They always
  compile and return an inert default when stripped (`false`, `default`, or a
  completed `UploadResultDto.Disabled` task).

`FlogLog` (`Runtime/Core/FlogLog.cs`) is the package's internal logger. All its
methods are `[Conditional]`. Use `FlogLog`, never
`UnityEngine.Debug`, inside the package - and note it prefixes every line with
`FlogLog.Prefix` so the auto-send hook can recognise and ignore its own output.

## Init flow

`FastLogs.Init(config)` is idempotent (a second call while initialized returns).
It resolves the config (the passed one, else `FastLogsConfigLoader.LoadOrDefault`
which reads `Resources/FastLogsConfig` or an in-memory default), checks
`FastLogsGate.IsEnabled`, and if enabled calls
`FastLogsRuntime.Create(config, services)`.

`Create` builds the hidden GameObject (`HideAndDontSave`, `DontDestroyOnLoad`)
and runs `Initialize`, which:

- generates a per-run `_sessionId` (one GUID stamped on every report of this
  process run so the server can group a play session);
- creates each service through the provider (`CreateLogSource`,
  `CreateTriggerSource`, `CreateUploader`, `CreateFileUploader`,
  `CreateScreenshotCapturer`, `CreateClipboard`, `CreateOverlay`,
  `CreateWebDeviceInfoProvider`), each wrapped in `TryCreate` so one failing
  service does not sink the rest;
- starts the log source, configures the overlay trigger and the separate
  quick-send trigger, subscribes to overlay events;
- builds the breadcrumb/context store (`FastLogsCrumbStore`) and the crash
  outbox (`PendingCrashQueue`), then kicks off a resend of anything left pending
  from a previous run;
- compiles the auto-send regex patterns and hooks `Application.logMessageReceived`
  (only if auto-send-on-exception or at least one pattern is configured);
- seeds the snapshot exclude list with FastLogs's own data dir;
- on WebGL, registers `window.FastLogs` via `WebHeadlessBridge`;
- spins up `ComponentCaptureWatcher` only when that section is enabled and has
  rules.

Non-MonoBehaviour services (the uploaders, the screenshot capturer) need to run
coroutines. They use `FlogCoroutineHost`, a lazily created shared hidden
MonoBehaviour. `CapturingLogSource` has its own tiny pump (`CapturePump`) because
the off-thread Unity log hook must be drained onto the main thread each frame and
the `ILogSource` interface has no pump method. Both hosts are gated, so a
stripped build never spawns them.

## Config

`FastLogsConfig` (`Runtime/Core/FastLogsConfig.cs`) is one ScriptableObject
(`PlayJoy/FastLogs/Config`) split into serialized sections, each a nested
`[Serializable]` class exposed through a read-only property, in asset order:
Server, Capture, Recording, AutoSend, Screenshot, SceneContext, ComponentCapture,
Diagnostics, Trigger (`TriggerConfig`), Files, Snapshot, Net, Retry, LoopGuard,
UI, Commands, Enable. The defaults are neutral where it counts: no endpoint, no
app id, no token, so an unconfigured build never sends anything. The heavy
sections (Recording, ComponentCapture, Commands) are off; the cheap safety nets
(auto-send on exception, the loop guard, PII scrubbing, the overlay) are on. The
full field tables live in `README.md` (this package); do not duplicate the
numbers here, they drift. `CreateDefault()` returns an all-defaults in-memory
instance for the no-asset case and for tests.

## Send path (interactive)

The report send is a coroutine on the runtime MonoBehaviour. Entry points funnel
into `BeginSend`:

- overlay Send button -> `OnOverlaySendRequested` -> `BeginSend`;
- quick-send gesture / `FastLogs.Send()` -> `QuickSend` / `QuickSendFromCode`;
- `FastLogs.SendAsync(...)` -> `BeginSendFromCode`;
- auto-crash and auto-pattern -> `BeginSend` directly (deliberately not through
  the loop guard);
- toast Retry and the retry loop -> `BeginSend` with the remembered parameters.

`BeginSend` blocks a new external send while one is in flight or a retry is
pending (it surfaces the last result and a toast rather than starting a second
send), remembers the send parameters for a possible Retry, then starts
`SendRoutine`. `SendRoutine`:

1. optionally captures a screenshot (`IScreenshotCapturer.CaptureAsync`, polled
   through a `FlogTask`);
2. gathers any queued screenshots (`CaptureScreenshot`) and queued scene context
   (`CaptureSceneContext`);
3. builds the report with `BuildReport`;
4. uploads via `ILogUploader.UploadAsync`, polls the returned `FlogTask`, then
   calls `Complete`.

`BuildReport` collects the device snapshot (`DiagnosticsCollector.Collect`, plus
`IWebDeviceInfoProvider.Fill` on WebGL), the log text
(`ILogSource.BuildLogText`), the counts, a one-shot snapshot of context and
breadcrumbs from `FastLogsCrumbStore`, and stamps `_sessionId`,
`_correlationCode` (set either by `FastLogs.SetCorrelationCode` or, while a command
runs, automatically by `CorrelationScope` - see the command channel), and code-send
provenance (`SentViaCode` + caller file/line from `CallerInfo`, set only for code
sends). With `Diagnostics.ScrubPii` on
(default) it runs `PiiScrubber` over the log text, every context value and every
breadcrumb message. It leaves `LogEncoding = "plain"`; the uploader may switch it
to gzip off WebGL.

The report DTO field is `ScreenshotsPngBase64` - a `List<string>` of PNG base64
strings (no `data:` prefix), omitted when empty. It is a list, not a single
string, because `CaptureScreenshot` lets you stack several shots on one report.

### The synchronous first dispatch (recent fix)

`UnityWebRequestUploader.UploadRoutine` builds the `UnityWebRequest` and calls
`SendWebRequest()` before its first `yield`. That matters: `StartCoroutine` runs
the first `MoveNext` synchronously, so the POST leaves the moment `UploadAsync`
is called. The old code did `yield return SendOnce(...)` from a nested iterator,
which deferred the actual send by one frame. In a backgrounded WebGL tab
`requestAnimationFrame` (and therefore coroutines) is paused, so that one-frame
delay meant the request never went out at all. Now the request is issued
immediately; only the completion handling waits for the loop to tick again. Keep
the send call ahead of the first yield if you touch this routine.

The uploader never throws - every failure becomes a non-success
`UploadResultDto`. It retries `config.Net.MaxRetries` times on a connection error
or 5xx with exponential backoff (0.5s, 1s, 2s, capped at 5s); 4xx is never
retried. It sets `result.Retryable` so the core's outer retry loop can act
without re-deriving from the status code.

## Complete, retry loop, crash outbox

`Complete` records the result, best-effort copies the short link to the
clipboard, shows the outcome toast, raises `Uploaded` (which the facade forwards
to `FastLogs.OnUploaded`), and resolves the caller's task. Then it branches:

- Success: clear the queued shots / scene context, upload any files queued via
  `AttachFile` as attachments of this report (`UploadQueuedAttachments`, keyed by
  the report id), delete the owned crash file, cancel any pending retry, and
  drain the outbox if idle.
- Transient failure (`IsRetryable`): schedule one re-send of the same report
  after `Retry.RetryIntervalSeconds` (`TryScheduleRetry` / `RetryRoutine`). At
  most one retry is pending at a time; the crash file stays owned across
  attempts.
- Terminal failure: release the crash file without deleting it (the outbox drain
  or next start will retry it), drop the queued shots / scene context / blob
  attachments, show a Retry toast.

`PendingCrashQueue` (`Runtime/Net/PendingCrashQueue.cs`) is the durable outbox.
On an unhandled exception `HandleExceptionAutoSend` runs a two-phase path:

- Phase 1 (capture, always, before any guard): `CaptureCrashIfNew` writes a
  screenshot-less, PII-scrubbed, `plain`-encoded report to
  `persistentDataPath/FastLogs/pending/<id>.json`, synchronously, so a hard crash
  cannot lose it. If the full `BuildReport` throws or returns null it falls back
  to `BuildMinimalCrashReport`, which depends on nothing `BuildReport` depends on
  (just config ids, platform, version, the exception text). Capture dedup uses
  its own state so a crash loop does not flood the outbox; the file count is
  bounded by the outbox cap.
- Phase 2 (delivery, gated): only now do throttle, per-session cap, in-flight and
  dedup guards decide whether to attempt an immediate upload. Anything skipped
  here stays in the outbox for the idle drain or next start.

`ResendAll` (called at Init and on every idle `Complete`, guarded against
overlapping passes) drains the outbox oldest-first through the same uploader. A
permanent 4xx drops the file (sending it again is pointless); a transient failure
keeps it.

Auto-send on a matched log pattern (`AutoSend.AutoSendPatterns`) shares the same
throttle, cap and dedup as the crash path but does not persist to disk - a
matched info line is not a crash and is already in the captured log.

## Loop guard

Sends started from game code (`FastLogs.Send` / `SendAsync` / `SendSceneContext` /
`SendSnapshot`) go through `EvaluateLoopGuard` first, keyed on the caller
`file:line`. Under `LoopGuard.MaxCodeSendsPerSite` cumulative sends per site, the
send proceeds.
Over it, with UI, one confirm dialog is shown (Send resets the site counter and
tags the report with the confirmer; Cancel disables that site for the session).
Over it with no UI, the send is dropped and every Nth drop logs one warning. The
overlay Send and the auto-crash/pattern sends deliberately skip the guard - they
have their own gating. Deferred confirms are replayed exactly (including the
original awaited task) via `PendingLoopConfirm`.

## Headless send

`FastLogs.SendHeadlessAsync(sceneContext, title, comment)` ->
`FastLogsRuntime.BeginHeadlessSend`. This path is for automation and backgrounded
tabs. It skips the overlay, toasts, screenshot machinery and loop guard, builds
the report synchronously (with optional scene context captured into a local, not
the shared queue), and dispatches the upload. Its re-entrancy guard
(`_headlessBusy`) is separate from `_isBusy` and only brackets the synchronous
build+dispatch, so multiple headless uploads can be in flight and it never
touches the interactive path.

On WebGL it uses `WebFetchUploader` instead of `UnityWebRequestUploader`, because
fetch runs on the browser microtask queue and settles even when the player loop
is paused (see WebGL specifics). Everywhere else it reuses the normal uploader.

## Command channel (commands client)

An agent -> game control channel, defined in `../CONTRACT.md` section 8: the agent
queues, the game claims, runs and acks, the agent reads the result back. The agent
(admin or the scoped command token) queues a named command with
`POST /api/commands/<appId>`; the game (ingest token) polls `GET .../poll`, runs
it, and acks `POST .../ack/<id>` with `{status:"done"|"error", result?|error?}`;
the agent reads `GET .../result/<id>`. `args` and `result` are JSON strings the
client parses/serializes itself.

Four runtime files under `Runtime/Commands/`, plus the `FastLogsConfig.Commands`
section and the `Plugins/WebGL/FastLogsCommands.jslib` bridge:

- `CommandRegistry`: the static name -> handler table. Static on purpose, so game
  code can register through `FastLogs.RegisterCommand(name, Func<string,string>)`
  **before** `Init`; the channel's built-ins write to the same table. Also
  `RegisterAsync` (handler returns `FlogTask<string>`), `TryDispatch` (out
  `CommandOutcome { Ok, Result, Error, Pending }`), `Unregister` and `Clear`
  (test isolation). Names match `^[a-z0-9_.-]{1,64}$` (the server's grammar),
  are normalised trim + lower-invariant, last registration wins, and an invalid
  name is dropped with a warning. A sync handler that throws becomes an error
  outcome (message only; the full exception goes to `FlogLog`).
- `CommandChannel`: the transport, one instance per runtime, created and started
  from `FastLogsRuntime.Initialize` only when `Commands.Enabled`. It derives the
  endpoint from `Server.EndpointUrl` by swapping `/api/logs` for `/api/commands`
  (the same trick `FilesSection` uses for `/api/files`), and never throws out.
  Two transports:
  - off WebGL, a `PollLoop` coroutine on the runtime host (`WaitForSecondsRealtime`,
    a fresh instance per iteration - a reused one caches its absolute end time and
    would elapse instantly on the second wait);
  - on WebGL (`&& !UNITY_EDITOR`), a JS `setInterval` in `FastLogsCommands.jslib`,
    so the poll keeps running while the tab is hidden and the player loop is
    paused (same reason as the headless fetch path). Commands arrive at a static
    `[MonoPInvokeCallback]`.
- `CorrelationScope`: see below.
- `CommandResultRef`: see below.

Built-ins (registered when `Commands.RegisterBuiltins`, unregistered on `Stop`):
`fastlogs.ping` -> `"pong"`; `fastlogs.send {title?,comment?,sceneContext?}` ->
`{"ok":true,"id","url"}`; `fastlogs.scenecontext`; `fastlogs.capture` ->
`{captured, autoStopped, sessionCount}` (plus `disabled:true` when the
ComponentCapture section is off); `fastlogs.inspect {type,go?,props?,max?}` ->
`{"type","n","shown","items":[...]}` (errors come back as `{"error"}`).

`PollIntervalSeconds` is also the channel's **liveness** signal, which is easy to miss
because there is no health call anywhere in this package. The server stamps
`apps.last_poll_at` on every poll, including an empty one, and answers
`GET /api/commands/<appId>/health` with `pollingNow` only while that stamp is
within `COMMANDS_LIVENESS_SEC` (10s; `../CONTRACT.md` section 8e). Raise the poll
interval past that to "be nice to the server" and an agent reads a perfectly
healthy build as dead.

The agent-side CLI is `server/src/tools/fastlogs-command.js`, and there is an MCP
server under `server/mcp/`. There is no GameMaker client for this channel yet.

### Correlation (command -> the reports it produced)

`CorrelationScope` makes the command id the `correlationCode` of every report that
command produces, so an agent finds exactly what it triggered with
`GET /api/await/<appId>?code=<commandId>` instead of matching on timestamps. The
channel builds one when `Commands.CorrelateReports` (default on) and brackets each
dispatch with `Enter`/`Leave`; `BuildReport` then stamps the runtime's
`_correlationCode` as usual, which is where the value comes from.

The correlation code is a single runtime-wide value, so the scope has two rules
that look fussy and are not - both break under "simplification":

- **Nesting**: only the **last** `Leave` restores. A WebGL poll batch can settle async
  handlers out of order, and an inner command must not strip the code out from
  under an outer one that is still running.
- **A handler's own code wins**: on exit it restores only if the current value is
  still the one it set, so `FastLogs.SetCorrelationCode` inside a handler is never
  clobbered.

The scope is engine-light on purpose (delegates in, no MonoBehaviour), so it is
unit-testable.

### Oversized results (`__fastlogsResultRef`)

The server caps an inline result at `COMMAND_MAX_RESULT_BYTES` (64 KB) and rejects
a bigger ack with a 400 - which an agent only ever experiences as a timeout. So
`CommandChannel.PrepareAckBody` uploads a too-big result as a file and puts a
reference in the ack instead: `{"__fastlogsResultRef":"<downloadUrl>","bytes":N}`.
`result` is an opaque string by contract, so this is a convention between clients,
not a protocol field; the MCP server dereferences it transparently.

This is why the ack path is asynchronous (`PrepareAckBody` returns a `FlogTask`)
even though building a small ack body is trivially synchronous.

Two invariants worth keeping:

- `CommandResultRef.MaxInlineResultBytes` is 60000 against the server's 65536. The
  gap is deliberate headroom for the ack body's JSON escaping (every `"` or `\n`
  in the result grows on the wire). "Aligning it with the server" reintroduces the
  400 this code exists to avoid.
- A failed offload acks `status:"error"` with the size and reason. Never a success
  with a dead link: the agent cannot read a result it cannot download.

## Component capture

`ComponentCaptureWatcher` (`Runtime/Core/ComponentCaptureWatcher.cs`) drives the
config-driven capture (`ComponentCapture` section). It exists only when that
section is enabled and has rules, and is ticked from the runtime's `Update`. Its
reason to exist: debugging WebGL particles used to mean hand-adding
`renderer.GetPropertyBlock().GetTexture("_MainTex")` logs at the frame a particle
activated; this does the same from config.

Every `ScanIntervalSeconds` of real time (monotonic clock) it looks for components
matching each rule (type name, optional `NameContains` and `Tag`). Scanning is
time-based rather than frame-based because a high-FPS batchmode player scanned
hundreds of times a second and burned the per-session cap outright; the
`FramesAfterEnable` follow-up drain stays honestly frame-based. Both the clock and
the log sink are injected, so the watcher is unit-testable without a live runtime.

On enable (a newly active instance or an inactive->active flip), on change
(serialized fields differ between scans), on the next `FramesAfterEnable` frames,
or on demand, it writes one line to the log ring:

```
[flog.capture] {"ev":"enable|frame|change|manual","t","go","en","act","f":{fields},"r":{mat,sh,sl,so,mpb}}
```

plus a `{"ev":"cap","auto":N,"max":M}` marker when the cap halts the automatic
paths. The same object shape with `ev:"inspect"` is what `fastlogs.inspect`
returns - in the command result, not the ring.

`f` is serialized fields (via `SerializedFieldDumper`); `r` is present only for
`Renderer`-derived components and carries material, shader, sorting layer/order
and probed MaterialPropertyBlock properties (`RendererProperties`).

Manual passes come from `FastLogs.CaptureComponentsNow()` (void on the facade, as
every fire-and-forget facade method is; the runtime's own
`CaptureComponentsNow` / `CaptureComponentsWithResult` return the count and the
cap state for the command result) and the `fastlogs.capture` command.
`MaxCapturesPerSession` halts only the **automatic** paths (enable/frame/change) - a
manual pass always works, which is the point: an agent must still be able to
capture after a chatty scene burned the cap. Hitting it emits the `ev:cap` marker
into the ring plus one warning. Change captures are additionally debounced
per-instance by `MinSecondsBetweenChangeCaptures`; while debounced the baseline is
deliberately **not** advanced, so the change is caught by the first scan after the
window rather than lost. Type lookups and rule state are cached.

MPB limitation: a MaterialPropertyBlock has no presence flag for non-texture
properties. Textures read back null unless set, so they are reliable; a
float/color/vector reads its type's zero value when unset, so the watcher can
only report non-zero values and cannot distinguish an unset property from one
deliberately set to zero/black.

`ComponentJson` (`Runtime/Core/ComponentJson.cs`) owns the object shape - the
`{"ev","t","go","en","act","f",...,"r"}` writer - and is shared by the watcher and
the inspector so a snapshot reads identically no matter which path asked for it.
It also owns the component-type lookup (`Type.GetType`, then a scan of the loaded
assemblies by full name and then short name, cached including misses) that turns a
rule's or a request's type name into a `Type`. Main-thread only: the path and
MaterialPropertyBlock scratch buffers are shared statics.

`SerializedFieldDumper` (`Runtime/Core/SerializedFieldDumper.cs`) sits one level
down and emits the field format (public non-`[NonSerialized]` and `[SerializeField]`
instance fields; values formatted shallow and capped), shared with the
scene-context snapshot. The scene-context output is a byte-for-byte invariant - if
you change the dumper, you change both.

### On-demand inspection

`ComponentInspector` (`Runtime/Core/ComponentInspector.cs`) backs `fastlogs.inspect`:
name a component type, optionally narrow by GameObject, get the matching live
instances back in the ComponentJson shape. It answers "which texture is in that
particle's MaterialPropertyBlock right now" without pulling - and grepping - a
full scene-context dump.

- **Read-only by construction**: serialized fields plus the renderer extras, never
  a game property getter or method. Those can have side effects, and an inspection
  must not perturb the state it reports. Keep it that way.
- Deliberately independent of `ComponentCapture.Enabled` and of the configured
  Rules (it is a query, not the watcher's standing surveillance); it borrows only
  the field/string limits from that section.
- `max` defaults to 5, clamped to `HardMax` 20, so a type with hundreds of
  instances cannot blow up the command result. The `go` filter matches the object
  name **or** its full path. Never throws out: failures come back as `{"error":"..."}`.

## Scene context, snapshot, files

- `SceneContextCapturer` (`Runtime/Core/SceneContextCapturer.cs`) walks all
  loaded scenes plus DontDestroyOnLoad into a bounded JSON tree, rendered by the
  viewer. Manual/one-shot only, never per-frame. Queued via
  `CaptureSceneContext` or sent in one call via `SendSceneContext`.
- File uploads (`SendFileAsync`, `SendFolderAsync`, `AttachFile`, ...) go to a
  separate `POST /api/files` endpoint through `IFileUploader`
  (`UnityWebRequestUploader` implements it too). The blob is base64, never
  gzipped and never PII-scrubbed; only a size cap (`Files.MaxFileBytes`) applies.
  `FolderZipUtil` zips folders client-side. `WebFilePicker` is the WebGL
  `<input type=file>` bridge, created lazily.
- `SendSnapshot` builds a normal report and attaches a `snapshot.zip` of the
  game's saves to the same record (`kind="snapshot"`). The default source is the
  whole `persistentDataPath` minus FastLogs's own data dir (seeded into
  `Snapshot.ExcludePaths` at Init); `AddSnapshotSource` / `AddSnapshotData` add
  more. `_snapshotInFlight` closes a one-frame double-send window the report-level
  `_isBusy` guard cannot.

## WebGL specifics

WebGL is the only platform-specific seam. The `IWebDeviceInfoProvider` fills the
browser device group; everything else is platform-neutral. Three jslib bridges
under `Plugins/WebGL/`:

- `FastLogsWeb.jslib`: `FastLogsWeb_CopyToClipboard` and `FastLogsWeb_OpenUrl`,
  called synchronously from a user-gesture handler so the browser honours the
  clipboard write / popup, plus `FastLogsWeb_PickFile`, which opens the
  `<input type=file>` dialog (gesture-bound for the same reason). The C# sides are
  `ClipboardService` and `WebFilePicker`. `PickFile` is the one bridge that answers
  by `SendMessage` instead of a callback function pointer: its C# side is a
  MonoBehaviour host, so the extern is handed the GameObject name plus an ok and a
  cancel method name rather than a pointer, and the jslib resolves the target with
  `GameObject.Find`. On success the payload is `"<fileName>\n<base64>"` (base64 has
  no newline, so the first one splits name from data); on cancel or a read error it
  is a reason string. Because `Find` only sees active, named objects, the host is a
  non-hidden `DontDestroyOnLoad` object with a stable, unique name.
- `FastLogsFetch.jslib`: `FastLogsFetch_Send` (frame-independent POST via
  browser fetch, used by `WebFetchUploader`), and `FastLogsFetch_RegisterHeadless`
  / `FastLogsFetch_CompleteHeadless` which install and settle `window.FastLogs`.
  After Init, `window.FastLogs.send({title?,comment?,sceneContext?})` returns a
  `Promise<{ok,id,url,status,error}>` and `window.FastLogs.lastResult` holds the
  last one. The C# side is `WebHeadlessBridge` / `WebFetchUploader`.
- `FastLogsCommands.jslib`: the command-channel poll for WebGL.
  `FastLogsCmd_Start(pollUrl, token, intervalMs, cb)` polls from a `setInterval`
  (**not** `requestAnimationFrame`), so it keeps ticking in a hidden tab - the whole
  point of the channel; a single-flight guard skips a tick while the previous poll
  is still in flight, and each command's JSON is handed to `cb` as a UTF8 buffer
  freed right after the synchronous dynCall. `FastLogsCmd_Ack(ackUrl, token, body)`
  is a fire-and-forget fetch; `FastLogsCmd_Stop()` clears the interval. The C#
  side is `CommandChannel`'s `[DllImport("__Internal")]` block plus the static
  `[MonoPInvokeCallback]`. `stringToNewUTF8` is declared via `__deps` so the
  linker's DCE cannot drop it.

jslib rules to keep: decode string pointers with `UTF8ToString` /
`Marshal.PtrToStringUTF8` synchronously at entry, because IL2CPP frees the
pointer once the extern returns - never inside a deferred fetch/promise callback.
C# callbacks are function pointers invoked with `makeDynCall` (except
`FastLogsWeb_PickFile`, which answers by `SendMessage`); free any UTF8
buffer you allocate for a callback right after the synchronous dynCall. C#
callbacks are `[MonoPInvokeCallback]` held in a static field so their function
pointer stays rooted for the app lifetime.

## Editor tools

Under `Editor/` (`PlayJoy.FastLogs.Editor.asmdef`, Editor platform only):

- `FastLogsMenu`: `Tools/PlayJoy/FastLogs/...` - Create / Select Config Asset,
  Open README, Build Defines Helper.
- `FastLogsBuildDefines`: a window explaining the gating and adding/removing
  `LOGSHARE_FORCE_ENABLED` for Android/iOS/Standalone/WebGL (never consoles).
- `AsmdefReferenceValidator` + `AsmdefReferenceUtil`: when the game code lives in
  its own asmdef and does not reference the package, `FastLogs` is invisible
  (`CS0103`). `Tools/PlayJoy/FastLogs/Add Reference To Asmdef...` adds the
  reference; a startup check warns once per session if a likely-missing reference
  is detected.
- `FastLogsConfigEditor`: custom inspector for the config asset.

## Tests

`Tests/Editor` (`PlayJoy.FastLogs.Tests.Editor`) holds EditMode tests for the
pieces under the facade - 14 fixtures, 110 tests: MiniJson, PiiScrubber, the crumb
store, the log recorder, the serialized field dumper, the component-capture rules,
the component-capture watcher, the component inspector, the correlation scope, the
command registry, the command result ref, the config defaults, the services/UI seam
(`ServicesUiSeamTests`, which pins that the provider types stay public and that
"no UI" really withholds the overlay and its gesture) and the asmdef util.
Engine-light classes (the registry, the scope, the result ref) are unit-testable
precisely because they take delegates instead of touching MonoBehaviours - keep
new pieces that shape. Most fixtures are internal and reach internal types
through `[assembly: InternalsVisibleTo(...)]` declared in both
`Runtime/AssemblyInfo.cs` and `Editor/AssemblyInfo.cs` (the attribute must live
in the assembly that owns the internal type). A consuming project runs them by
adding `"testables": ["com.playjoy.fastlogs"]` to its `Packages/manifest.json`.

## Known limitations

- `FlogTask<T>` holds a single continuation slot (`_continuation`), so only one
  awaiter at a time is supported. Every internal consumer awaits once, which is
  fine; do not hand the same task to two `await`s.
- MaterialPropertyBlock zero-sentinel: non-texture MPB properties are only
  reported when non-zero (see component capture).
- The screenshot capture is a coroutine (end-of-frame grab). The headless path
  therefore does not take a screenshot - it must not depend on the player loop.
- Command handlers run on the main thread (the poll coroutine, or the JS command
  callback on WebGL), so Unity APIs are safe inside them - but a slow handler
  stalls the frame like any other main-thread work.
- A command result over ~60 KB costs a file upload before the ack lands (see
  `__fastlogsResultRef`), so a wide `fastlogs.inspect` is not free.
- There is no GameMaker client for the command channel.

`unity/Editor/AsmdefReferenceUtil.cs`:

```cs
// AsmdefReferenceUtil - pure, testable text manipulation of .asmdef JSON to add
// a reference to the FastLogs runtime assembly. Kept free of AssetDatabase so the
// core logic can be unit-tested with plain strings.

using System.Collections.Generic;
using UnityEditor;

namespace PlayJoy.FastLogs.Editor
{
    internal static class AsmdefReferenceUtil
    {
        // Assembly name and GUID of unity/Runtime/PlayJoy.FastLogs.asmdef.
        // GUID mirrors PlayJoy.FastLogs.asmdef.meta; a reference may use either form.
        internal const string AssemblyName = "PlayJoy.FastLogs";
        internal const string AssemblyGuid = "6da385d0f99bc394190636ff65c712d5";
        internal const string GuidReference = "GUID:" + AssemblyGuid;

        // Serializable mirror of the full .asmdef schema. Every field is declared so a
        // parse/serialize round-trip never drops data the file already carries.
        [System.Serializable]
        internal sealed class AsmdefSchema
        {
            public string name;
            public string rootNamespace;
            public List<string> references = new List<string>();
            public List<string> includePlatforms = new List<string>();
            public List<string> excludePlatforms = new List<string>();
            public bool allowUnsafeCode;
            public bool overrideReferences;
            public List<string> precompiledReferences = new List<string>();
            public bool autoReferenced = true;
            public List<string> defineConstraints = new List<string>();
            public List<VersionDefine> versionDefines = new List<VersionDefine>();
            public bool noEngineReferences;
        }

        [System.Serializable]
        internal sealed class VersionDefine
        {
            public string name;
            public string expression;
            public string define;
        }

        internal static bool HasFastLogsReference(string asmdefJson)
        {
            AsmdefSchema schema = Parse(asmdefJson);
            return HasFastLogsReference(schema);
        }

        // Returns the .asmdef JSON with a FastLogs reference added, matching the form the
        // file already uses (GUID: when any existing reference is GUID-based, else name).
        // If a reference is already present the input is returned unchanged.
        internal static string AddFastLogsReference(string asmdefJson)
        {
            AsmdefSchema schema = Parse(asmdefJson);
            if (schema.references == null) schema.references = new List<string>();
            if (HasFastLogsReference(schema)) return asmdefJson;

            schema.references.Add(UsesGuidReferences(schema.references) ? GuidReference : AssemblyName);
            return EditorJsonUtility.ToJson(schema, true);
        }

        // ---- Internals ------------------------------------------------------

        private static AsmdefSchema Parse(string asmdefJson)
        {
            var schema = new AsmdefSchema();
            if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(asmdefJson))
            {
                EditorJsonUtility.FromJsonOverwrite(asmdefJson, schema);
            }
            return schema;
        }

        private static bool HasFastLogsReference(AsmdefSchema schema)
        {
            if (schema == null || schema.references == null) return false;
            foreach (string r in schema.references)
            {
                if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(r)) continue;
                if (r == AssemblyName) return true;
                if (r == GuidReference) return true;
            }
            return false;
        }

        private static bool UsesGuidReferences(List<string> references)
        {
            if (references == null) return false;
            foreach (string r in references)
            {
                if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(r) &&
                    r.StartsWith("GUID:", System.StringComparison.Ordinal))
                {
                    return true;
                }
            }
            return false;
        }
    }
}

```
