`unity/Editor/AsmdefReferenceValidator.cs`:

```cs
// AsmdefReferenceValidator - one-shot editor check + helper window that reminds the
// developer to add a "PlayJoy.FastLogs" reference to their game asmdef. Without it the
// package types are invisible (CS0103) in projects whose code lives in its own assembly.

using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.IO;
using UnityEditor;
using UnityEngine;

namespace PlayJoy.FastLogs.Editor
{
    internal sealed class AsmdefReferenceValidator : EditorWindow
    {
        private const string MenuItemPath = "Tools/PlayJoy/FastLogs/Add Reference To Asmdef...";
        // Session-scoped so the warning fires at most once per editor session.
        private const string SessionKey = "PlayJoy.FastLogs.AsmdefWarningShown";

        [InitializeOnLoadMethod]
        private static void RegisterStartupCheck()
        {
            // Defer: AssetDatabase queries during static init are unreliable mid domain-reload.
            EditorApplication.delayCall += WarnOnceIfMissing;
        }

        private static void WarnOnceIfMissing()
        {
            if (SessionState.GetBool(SessionKey, false)) return;
            SessionState.SetBool(SessionKey, true);

            // Only nag when the project actually uses FastLogs (a config exists in Resources).
            var config = Resources.Load<FastLogsConfig>(FastLogsConfigLoader.ResourceName);
            if (config == null) return;

            List<AsmdefEntry> asmdefs = CollectGameAsmdefs();
            if (asmdefs.Count == 0) return;

            foreach (AsmdefEntry e in asmdefs)
            {
                if (e.HasReference) return; // At least one asmdef already links FastLogs.
            }

            Debug.LogWarning(
                "[FastLogs] FastLogsConfig is present but no assembly definition references " +
                "\"" + AsmdefReferenceUtil.AssemblyName + "\". FastLogs types will be invisible " +
                "(CS0103) to game code compiled into its own asmdef. " +
                "Fix it via menu: " + MenuItemPath);
        }

        // ---- Window ---------------------------------------------------------

        private Vector2 _scroll;
        private List<AsmdefEntry> _entries = new List<AsmdefEntry>();

        [MenuItem(MenuItemPath, false, 60)]
        private static void ShowWindow()
        {
            var win = GetWindow<AsmdefReferenceValidator>(
                utility: false,
                title:   "FastLogs - Asmdef Reference");
            win.minSize = new Vector2(460, 320);
            win.Refresh();
        }

        private void OnEnable()
        {
            Refresh();
        }

        private void Refresh()
        {
            _entries = CollectGameAsmdefs();
        }

        private void OnGUI()
        {
            EditorGUILayout.Space(6);
            GUILayout.Label("Add \"" + AsmdefReferenceUtil.AssemblyName + "\" reference",
                            EditorStyles.boldLabel);
            EditorGUILayout.HelpBox(
                "Game code compiled into its own assembly definition cannot see FastLogs " +
                "until that asmdef references \"" + AsmdefReferenceUtil.AssemblyName + "\". " +
                "Tick the asmdefs to update, then press Add.",
                MessageType.Info);

            EditorGUILayout.Space(4);
            EditorGUILayout.BeginHorizontal();
            GUILayout.Label(_entries.Count + " assembly definition(s) under Assets/",
                            EditorStyles.miniLabel);
            GUILayout.FlexibleSpace();
            if (GUILayout.Button("Refresh", GUILayout.Width(80)))
            {
                Refresh();
            }
            EditorGUILayout.EndHorizontal();

            EditorGUILayout.Space(2);
            _scroll = EditorGUILayout.BeginScrollView(_scroll);

            foreach (AsmdefEntry e in _entries)
            {
                EditorGUILayout.BeginHorizontal();
                if (e.HasReference)
                {
                    using (new EditorGUI.DisabledScope(true))
                    {
                        EditorGUILayout.Toggle(true, GUILayout.Width(18));
                        GUILayout.Label(e.Name + "  (already references FastLogs)");
                    }
                }
                else
                {
                    e.Selected = EditorGUILayout.Toggle(e.Selected, GUILayout.Width(18));
                    GUILayout.Label(e.Name);
                }
                EditorGUILayout.EndHorizontal();
            }

            EditorGUILayout.EndScrollView();

            EditorGUILayout.Space(6);
            using (new EditorGUI.DisabledScope(!AnySelected()))
            {
                if (GUILayout.Button("Add reference to selected"))
                {
                    ApplySelected();
                }
            }
        }

        private bool AnySelected()
        {
            foreach (AsmdefEntry e in _entries)
            {
                if (!e.HasReference && e.Selected) return true;
            }
            return false;
        }

        private void ApplySelected()
        {
            int changed = 0;
            foreach (AsmdefEntry e in _entries)
            {
                if (e.HasReference || !e.Selected) continue;
                try
                {
                    string json    = File.ReadAllText(e.Path);
                    string updated = AsmdefReferenceUtil.AddFastLogsReference(json);
                    if (updated != json)
                    {
                        File.WriteAllText(e.Path, updated);
                        AssetDatabase.ImportAsset(e.Path, ImportAssetOptions.ForceUpdate);
                        changed++;
                    }
                }
                catch (System.Exception ex)
                {
                    Debug.LogError("[FastLogs] Failed to update " + e.Path + ": " + ex.Message);
                }
            }

            if (changed > 0)
            {
                Debug.Log("[FastLogs] Added \"" + AsmdefReferenceUtil.AssemblyName +
                          "\" reference to " + changed + " asmdef(s).");
                AssetDatabase.Refresh();
            }
            Refresh();
        }

        // ---- Discovery ------------------------------------------------------

        private sealed class AsmdefEntry
        {
            public string Path;
            public string Name;
            public bool HasReference;
            public bool Selected;
        }

        private static List<AsmdefEntry> CollectGameAsmdefs()
        {
            var result = new List<AsmdefEntry>();
            string[] guids = AssetDatabase.FindAssets("t:AssemblyDefinitionAsset");
            if (guids == null) return result;

            foreach (string guid in guids)
            {
                string path = AssetDatabase.GUIDToAssetPath(guid);
                if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(path) ||
                    !path.StartsWith("Assets/", System.StringComparison.Ordinal))
                {
                    continue;
                }

                string name = System.IO.Path.GetFileNameWithoutExtension(path);
                // Skip FastLogs' own assemblies (runtime/editor/tests) - not user game code.
                if (name.StartsWith(AsmdefReferenceUtil.AssemblyName, System.StringComparison.Ordinal))
                {
                    continue;
                }

                bool hasRef = false;
                try
                {
                    hasRef = AsmdefReferenceUtil.HasFastLogsReference(File.ReadAllText(path));
                }
                catch (System.Exception ex)
                {
                    Debug.LogWarning("[FastLogs] Could not read " + path + ": " + ex.Message);
                }

                result.Add(new AsmdefEntry { Path = path, Name = name, HasReference = hasRef });
            }

            result.Sort((a, b) => string.CompareOrdinal(a.Name, b.Name));
            return result;
        }
    }
}

`unity/Editor/FastLogsBuildDefines.cs`:

```cs
// FastLogsBuildDefines - Editor window that explains the build-gating model and
// helps developers add or remove the LOGSHARE_FORCE_ENABLED scripting define
// for mobile / standalone targets (never for consoles).

using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Linq;
using UnityEditor;
using UnityEngine;

namespace PlayJoy.FastLogs.Editor
{
    internal sealed class FastLogsBuildDefines : EditorWindow
    {
        // Platforms on which LOGSHARE_FORCE_ENABLED is meaningful.
        // Consoles are deliberately excluded: the gate already hard-blocks them.
        private static readonly BuildTargetGroup[] AllowedGroups =
        {
            BuildTargetGroup.Android,
            BuildTargetGroup.iOS,
            BuildTargetGroup.Standalone,
            BuildTargetGroup.WebGL,
        };

        private const string Define = "LOGSHARE_FORCE_ENABLED";

        private Vector2 _scroll;

        internal static void ShowWindow()
        {
            var win = GetWindow<FastLogsBuildDefines>(
                utility: true,
                title:   "FastLogs - Build Defines");
            win.minSize = new Vector2(460, 400);
        }

        private void OnGUI()
        {
            _scroll = EditorGUILayout.BeginScrollView(_scroll);

            // ---- Title ----
            EditorGUILayout.Space(6);
            GUILayout.Label("FastLogs - Build Gating & " + Define, EditorStyles.boldLabel);
            EditorGUILayout.Space(4);

            // ---- Explanation ----
            EditorGUILayout.HelpBox(
                "FastLogs is active only in Editor and Development Builds by default.\n\n" +
                "To enable it in a non-console RELEASE build (Android, iOS, Standalone, WebGL), " +
                "add the scripting define \"" + Define + "\" to the desired platform group.\n\n" +
                "Consoles (PS4, PS5, GameCore, Switch) are always hard-blocked regardless " +
                "of this define - no HTTP, no screenshot, no overlay will be compiled in.",
                MessageType.Info);

            EditorGUILayout.Space(6);
            GUILayout.Label("Platform Status", EditorStyles.boldLabel);
            EditorGUILayout.Space(2);

            // ---- Per-platform status table ----
            foreach (BuildTargetGroup group in AllowedGroups)
            {
                bool hasDefine = HasDefine(group, Define);

                EditorGUILayout.BeginHorizontal();

                GUILayout.Label(group.ToString(), GUILayout.Width(120));

                GUIStyle tagStyle = new GUIStyle(EditorStyles.miniLabel)
                {
                    normal = { textColor = hasDefine ? new Color(0.2f, 0.7f, 0.2f) : new Color(0.5f, 0.5f, 0.5f) }
                };
                GUILayout.Label(hasDefine ? "FORCE ENABLED" : "default (Editor+Dev only)", tagStyle, GUILayout.Width(200));

                if (hasDefine)
                {
                    if (GUILayout.Button("Remove", GUILayout.Width(80)))
                    {
                        RemoveDefine(group, Define);
                    }
                }
                else
                {
                    if (GUILayout.Button("Add", GUILayout.Width(80)))
                    {
                        AddDefine(group, Define);
                    }
                }

                EditorGUILayout.EndHorizontal();
            }

            // ---- Console note ----
            EditorGUILayout.Space(8);
            var consoleStyle = new GUIStyle(EditorStyles.miniLabel)
            {
                wordWrap = true,
                normal   = { textColor = new Color(0.6f, 0.6f, 0.6f) }
            };
            GUILayout.Label(
                "PS4 / PS5 / GameCore / Switch: these are always excluded even when " +
                Define + " is defined. Editing their defines here is intentionally " +
                "not offered.",
                consoleStyle);

            // ---- Retail warning ----
            EditorGUILayout.Space(6);
            EditorGUILayout.HelpBox(
                "Warning: enabling FastLogs in a retail build means HTTP traffic " +
                "and (optional) screenshots will run in production. Only do this " +
                "if you have a deliberate reason and have scoped access via your " +
                "ingest token and endpoint.",
                MessageType.Warning);

            EditorGUILayout.EndScrollView();
        }

        // ---- Define helpers -------------------------------------------------

        private static bool HasDefine(BuildTargetGroup group, string define)
        {
#if UNITY_2023_1_OR_NEWER
            UnityEditor.Build.NamedBuildTarget nbt = UnityEditor.Build.NamedBuildTarget.FromBuildTargetGroup(group);
            PlayerSettings.GetScriptingDefineSymbols(nbt, out string[] symbols);
            return symbols != null && System.Array.IndexOf(symbols, define) >= 0;
#else
            string raw = PlayerSettings.GetScriptingDefineSymbolsForGroup(group);
            return raw != null && Array.Exists(raw.Split(';'), s => s.Trim() == define);
#endif
        }

        private static void AddDefine(BuildTargetGroup group, string define)
        {
            if (HasDefine(group, define)) return;

#if UNITY_2023_1_OR_NEWER
            UnityEditor.Build.NamedBuildTarget nbt = UnityEditor.Build.NamedBuildTarget.FromBuildTargetGroup(group);
            PlayerSettings.GetScriptingDefineSymbols(nbt, out string[] existing);
            var list = new List<string>(existing ?? Array.Empty<string>()) { define };
            PlayerSettings.SetScriptingDefineSymbols(nbt, list.ToArray());
#else
            string raw  = PlayerSettings.GetScriptingDefineSymbolsForGroup(group);
            string next = string.IsNullOrEmpty(raw) ? define : raw + ";" + define;
            PlayerSettings.SetScriptingDefineSymbolsForGroup(group, next);
#endif
            Debug.Log("[FastLogs] Added " + define + " to " + group);
        }

        private static void RemoveDefine(BuildTargetGroup group, string define)
        {
            if (!HasDefine(group, define)) return;

#if UNITY_2023_1_OR_NEWER
            UnityEditor.Build.NamedBuildTarget nbt = UnityEditor.Build.NamedBuildTarget.FromBuildTargetGroup(group);
            PlayerSettings.GetScriptingDefineSymbols(nbt, out string[] existing);
            string[] filtered = existing == null
                ? Array.Empty<string>()
                : existing.Where(s => s != define).ToArray();
            PlayerSettings.SetScriptingDefineSymbols(nbt, filtered);
#else
            string raw = PlayerSettings.GetScriptingDefineSymbolsForGroup(group);
            var parts = raw.Split(';').Select(s => s.Trim())
                           .Where(s => !string.IsNullOrEmpty(s) && s != define);
            PlayerSettings.SetScriptingDefineSymbolsForGroup(group, string.Join(";", parts));
#endif
            Debug.Log("[FastLogs] Removed " + define + " from " + group);
        }
    }
}

```

`unity/Editor/FastLogsConfigEditor.cs`:

```cs
// FastLogsConfigEditor - custom Inspector for FastLogsConfig.
// Shows contextual warnings (empty endpoint, iOS+http), and provides
// quick-action buttons (Ping config, Open README, add LOGSHARE_FORCE_ENABLED).

using UnityEditor;
using UnityEngine;

namespace PlayJoy.FastLogs.Editor
{
    [CustomEditor(typeof(FastLogsConfig))]
    internal sealed class FastLogsConfigEditor : UnityEditor.Editor
    {
        // Foldout state keyed by property path so new config sections appear automatically.
        private readonly System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary<string, bool> _foldouts =
            new System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary<string, bool>();

        public override void OnInspectorGUI()
        {
            serializedObject.Update();

            var cfg = (FastLogsConfig)target;

            DrawConfigHeader();
            DrawValidationMessages(cfg);
            EditorGUILayout.Space(4);

            // Generic pass over top-level serialized fields: every section (present or future)
            // is rendered as a foldout without editing this file when the config grows.
            SerializedProperty prop = serializedObject.GetIterator();
            bool enterChildren = true;
            while (prop.NextVisible(enterChildren))
            {
                enterChildren = false;
                if (prop.propertyPath == "m_Script") continue;
                DrawSection(prop);
            }

            EditorGUILayout.Space(6);
            DrawActions();

            serializedObject.ApplyModifiedProperties();
        }

        // ---- Helpers --------------------------------------------------------

        private static void DrawConfigHeader()
        {
            EditorGUILayout.Space(4);
            var style = new GUIStyle(EditorStyles.largeLabel)
            {
                fontStyle = FontStyle.Bold,
                fontSize  = 13
            };
            EditorGUILayout.LabelField("FastLogs Configuration", style);
            EditorGUILayout.Space(2);
        }

        private static void DrawValidationMessages(FastLogsConfig cfg)
        {
            string url = cfg.Server.EndpointUrl;

            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(url))
            {
                EditorGUILayout.HelpBox(
                    "Server.EndpointUrl is empty. FastLogs will not upload until it is set " +
                    "(e.g. https://logs.example.com/api/logs).",
                    MessageType.Warning);
            }
            else if (url.StartsWith("http://", System.StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
            {
#if UNITY_IOS
                EditorGUILayout.HelpBox(
                    "Endpoint is HTTP, not HTTPS. iOS App Transport Security (ATS) blocks " +
                    "plain HTTP requests at runtime unless an NSException is configured in " +
                    "your Info.plist. Prefer https:// for iOS builds.",
                    MessageType.Warning);
#else
                EditorGUILayout.HelpBox(
                    "Endpoint is HTTP, not HTTPS. On iOS, ATS will block this at runtime. " +
                    "Use https:// to be safe across all platforms.",
                    MessageType.Info);
#endif
            }

            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(cfg.Server.AppId))
            {
                EditorGUILayout.HelpBox(
                    "Server.AppId is empty. The server requires a valid appId " +
                    "([a-z0-9_-]{2,32}) to catalog logs.",
                    MessageType.Warning);
            }
        }

        private void DrawSection(SerializedProperty sectionProp)
        {
            // Leaf top-level fields (if any) render inline; section objects get a foldout.
            if (!sectionProp.hasVisibleChildren)
            {
                EditorGUILayout.PropertyField(sectionProp, true);
                return;
            }

            string key = sectionProp.propertyPath;
            if (!_foldouts.TryGetValue(key, out bool foldout)) foldout = true;

            foldout = EditorGUILayout.Foldout(foldout, sectionProp.displayName, true,
                                              EditorStyles.foldoutHeader);
            _foldouts[key] = foldout;
            if (!foldout) return;

            EditorGUI.indentLevel++;
            SerializedProperty child = sectionProp.Copy();
            SerializedProperty end   = sectionProp.GetEndProperty();
            bool enterChildren = true;
            while (child.NextVisible(enterChildren) && !SerializedProperty.EqualContents(child, end))
            {
                enterChildren = false;
                EditorGUILayout.PropertyField(child, true);
            }
            EditorGUI.indentLevel--;
            EditorGUILayout.Space(2);
        }

        private void DrawActions()
        {
            EditorGUILayout.LabelField("Actions", EditorStyles.boldLabel);

            EditorGUILayout.BeginHorizontal();

            if (GUILayout.Button("Ping this asset"))
            {
                EditorGUIUtility.PingObject(target);
            }

            if (GUILayout.Button("Open README"))
            {
                FastLogsMenu.OpenReadme();
            }

            EditorGUILayout.EndHorizontal();

            EditorGUILayout.BeginHorizontal();

            if (GUILayout.Button("Show Build Defines Helper"))
            {
                FastLogsBuildDefines.ShowWindow();
            }

            EditorGUILayout.EndHorizontal();
        }
    }
}

```

`unity/Editor/FastLogsMenu.cs`:

```cs
// FastLogsMenu - top-level Editor menu entries for FastLogs.
// Tools/PlayJoy/FastLogs/...

using System.IO;
using UnityEditor;
using UnityEngine;

namespace PlayJoy.FastLogs.Editor
{
    internal static class FastLogsMenu
    {
        private const string MenuRoot   = "Tools/PlayJoy/FastLogs/";
        private const string DefaultRes = "Assets/Resources";
        private const string AssetName  = "FastLogsConfig.asset";

        // ---- Create / select config -----------------------------------------

        [MenuItem(MenuRoot + "Create Config Asset", false, 0)]
        private static void CreateConfig()
        {
            string existingGuid = FindFirstConfigGuid();
            if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(existingGuid))
            {
                PingAsset(existingGuid);
                Debug.Log("[FastLogs] Config already exists at: " +
                          AssetDatabase.GUIDToAssetPath(existingGuid));
                return;
            }

            if (!AssetDatabase.IsValidFolder(DefaultRes))
            {
                Directory.CreateDirectory(DefaultRes);
                AssetDatabase.Refresh();
            }

            var config = ScriptableObject.CreateInstance<FastLogsConfig>();
            string path = AssetDatabase.GenerateUniqueAssetPath(DefaultRes + "/" + AssetName);
            AssetDatabase.CreateAsset(config, path);
            AssetDatabase.SaveAssets();

            Selection.activeObject = config;
            EditorGUIUtility.PingObject(config);
            Debug.Log("[FastLogs] Created config at: " + path);
        }

        [MenuItem(MenuRoot + "Select Config Asset", false, 1)]
        private static void SelectConfig()
        {
            string guid = FindFirstConfigGuid();
            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(guid))
            {
                Debug.LogWarning("[FastLogs] No FastLogsConfig asset found. " +
                                 "Use 'Create Config Asset' first.");
                return;
            }
            PingAsset(guid);
        }

        // ---- README ---------------------------------------------------------

        [MenuItem(MenuRoot + "Open README", false, 20)]
        public static void OpenReadme()
        {
            // Locate the README.md that ships with the package.
            // Works both from the Packages/ cache and when the package is
            // embedded / imported as a local folder.
            string[] guids = AssetDatabase.FindAssets("FastLogs README");
            foreach (string guid in guids)
            {
                string p = AssetDatabase.GUIDToAssetPath(guid);
                if (p.EndsWith("README.md", System.StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
                {
                    var asset = AssetDatabase.LoadAssetAtPath<TextAsset>(p);
                    if (asset != null)
                    {
                        Selection.activeObject = asset;
                        EditorGUIUtility.PingObject(asset);
                        return;
                    }
                }
            }

            // Fallback: open in the system text editor via the file system.
            string packageDir = FindPackageDirectory();
            if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(packageDir))
            {
                string readmePath = Path.Combine(packageDir, "README.md");
                if (File.Exists(readmePath))
                {
                    EditorUtility.OpenWithDefaultApp(readmePath);
                    return;
                }
            }

            Debug.LogWarning("[FastLogs] README.md not found. " +
                             "It should be at the root of the FastLogs package.");
        }

        // ---- Build defines helper -------------------------------------------

        [MenuItem(MenuRoot + "Build Defines Helper", false, 40)]
        private static void OpenBuildDefines()
        {
            FastLogsBuildDefines.ShowWindow();
        }

        // ---- Internals ------------------------------------------------------

        private static string FindFirstConfigGuid()
        {
            string[] guids = AssetDatabase.FindAssets("t:" + nameof(FastLogsConfig));
            return guids != null && guids.Length > 0 ? guids[0] : null;
        }

        private static void PingAsset(string guid)
        {
            string path  = AssetDatabase.GUIDToAssetPath(guid);
            var    asset = AssetDatabase.LoadAssetAtPath<FastLogsConfig>(path);
            Selection.activeObject = asset;
            EditorGUIUtility.PingObject(asset);
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Tries to locate the FastLogs package directory on disk.
        /// Works for git-URL packages (Library/PackageCache) and embedded packages.
        /// </summary>
        internal static string FindPackageDirectory()
        {
            // Attempt 1: find via package.json guid.
            string[] jsGuids = AssetDatabase.FindAssets("FastLogs package");
            foreach (string g in jsGuids)
            {
                string p = AssetDatabase.GUIDToAssetPath(g);
                if (p.EndsWith("package.json", System.StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
                {
                    string dir = Path.GetDirectoryName(p);
                    if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(dir)) return dir;
                }
            }

            // Attempt 2: walk up from this script's compiled location.
            // Editor assembly DLL is usually in Library/ScriptAssemblies or
            // Temp; not reliable. Just return null - callers fall back gracefully.
            return null;
        }
    }
}

`unity/Plugins/WebGL/FastLogsCommands.jslib`:

```jslib
// FastLogsCommands.jslib - frame-independent WebGL poll loop for the remote command channel.
//
// The command poll runs from setInterval (NOT requestAnimationFrame), so it keeps ticking
// while the tab is hidden and the Unity player loop is paused - which is the whole point of
// the channel (drive a backgrounded/headless build). Each claimed command is handed to C#
// via a synchronous makeDynCall; C# dispatches it and posts the ack back through
// FastLogsCmd_Ack (fire-and-forget fetch). String args arrive as heap pointers and are
// decoded with UTF8ToString SYNCHRONOUSLY at entry; a UTF8 buffer we allocate for a C#
// callback is freed with _free right after the synchronous dynCall returns.

mergeInto(LibraryManager.library, {

    // stringToNewUTF8 is a JS runtime helper subject to linker DCE; declare it as an
    // explicit dependency so the poll loop always links.
    FastLogsCmd_Start__deps: ['$stringToNewUTF8'],

    // FastLogsCmd_Start(pollUrl, token, intervalMs, cb): begin polling `pollUrl` every
    // intervalMs (Bearer `token` when non-empty). A single-flight guard skips a tick while a
    // previous poll is still in flight. For each command in the {commands:[...]} response,
    // cb(commandJsonPtr) is invoked with a UTF8 buffer holding that command's JSON
    // ({id,name,args}); the buffer is freed right after the synchronous dynCall. Any previous
    // interval is cleared first.
    FastLogsCmd_Start: function (pollUrlPtr, tokenPtr, intervalMs, cbPtr) {
        var pollUrl = pollUrlPtr ? UTF8ToString(pollUrlPtr) : '';
        var token = tokenPtr ? UTF8ToString(tokenPtr) : '';
        try {
            if (typeof globalThis === 'undefined') { return; }
            if (globalThis.__flogCmd && globalThis.__flogCmd.intervalId) {
                clearInterval(globalThis.__flogCmd.intervalId);
            }

            var inFlight = false;
            function poll() {
                if (inFlight) { return; }
                inFlight = true;
                var headers = { 'Accept': 'application/json' };
                if (token) { headers['Authorization'] = 'Bearer ' + token; }
                fetch(pollUrl, { method: 'GET', headers: headers })
                    .then(function (resp) { return resp.ok ? resp.json() : null; })
                    .then(function (data) {
                        inFlight = false;
                        if (!data || !data.commands || !data.commands.length) { return; }
                        for (var i = 0; i < data.commands.length; i++) {
                            var c = data.commands[i];
                            if (!c) { continue; }
                            var buf = stringToNewUTF8(JSON.stringify(c));
                            try {
                                {{{ makeDynCall('vi', 'cbPtr') }}}(buf);
                            } finally {
                                if (buf) { _free(buf); }
                            }
                        }
                    })
                    .catch(function () { inFlight = false; });
            }

            var id = setInterval(poll, (intervalMs | 0) > 0 ? (intervalMs | 0) : 2000);
            globalThis.__flogCmd = { intervalId: id };
        } catch (e) {
            try { console.warn('[FastLogsCommands] Start error', e); } catch (_) {}
        }
    },

    // FastLogsCmd_Ack(ackUrl, token, body): POST `body` (the ack JSON) to `ackUrl`
    // (Bearer `token` when non-empty). Fire-and-forget; errors are warned, not surfaced.
    FastLogsCmd_Ack: function (ackUrlPtr, tokenPtr, bodyPtr) {
        var ackUrl = ackUrlPtr ? UTF8ToString(ackUrlPtr) : '';
        var token = tokenPtr ? UTF8ToString(tokenPtr) : '';
        var body = bodyPtr ? UTF8ToString(bodyPtr) : '';
        try {
            var headers = { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', 'Accept': 'application/json' };
            if (token) { headers['Authorization'] = 'Bearer ' + token; }
            fetch(ackUrl, { method: 'POST', headers: headers, body: body })
                .catch(function (err) {
                    try { console.warn('[FastLogsCommands] Ack failed', err); } catch (_) {}
                });
        } catch (e) {
            try { console.warn('[FastLogsCommands] Ack error', e); } catch (_) {}
        }
    },

    // FastLogsCmd_Stop(): stop the poll interval.
    FastLogsCmd_Stop: function () {
        try {
            if (typeof globalThis !== 'undefined' && globalThis.__flogCmd && globalThis.__flogCmd.intervalId) {
                clearInterval(globalThis.__flogCmd.intervalId);
                globalThis.__flogCmd.intervalId = null;
            }
        } catch (e) {
            try { console.warn('[FastLogsCommands] Stop error', e); } catch (_) {}
        }
    }

});

```

`unity/Plugins/WebGL/FastLogsFetch.jslib`:

```jslib
// FastLogsFetch.jslib - frame-independent WebGL transport + headless JS API for FastLogs.
//
// Unlike UnityWebRequest coroutines (which only advance while requestAnimationFrame runs,
// so they stall in a backgrounded tab or a headless harness), browser fetch() runs on the
// microtask queue and always completes. These entry points let FastLogs POST a report and
// be driven from automation without depending on the Unity player loop.
//
// String args arrive as Emscripten heap pointers; UTF8ToString decodes them SYNCHRONOUSLY
// at entry (IL2CPP frees the pointer once the extern returns). C# callbacks are function
// pointers invoked via makeDynCall; a UTF8 buffer we allocate for a C# callback is freed
// with _free right after the synchronous dynCall returns.

mergeInto(LibraryManager.library, {

    // stringToNewUTF8 is a JS runtime helper subject to linker DCE; declare it as an
    // explicit dependency so the functions below always link.
    FastLogsFetch_Send__deps: ['$stringToNewUTF8'],
    FastLogsFetch_RegisterHeadless__deps: ['$stringToNewUTF8'],

    // FastLogsFetch_Send(endpoint, token, body, requestId, cb): POST `body` as
    // application/json to `endpoint` (Bearer `token` when non-empty). On completion or
    // error, calls back into C#: cb(requestId, statusCode, responsePtr) where responsePtr
    // is a freshly allocated UTF8 buffer holding the response body (or the error text),
    // freed here right after the synchronous dynCall. statusCode 0 = network error.
    FastLogsFetch_Send: function (endpointPtr, tokenPtr, bodyPtr, requestId, cbPtr) {
        var endpoint = endpointPtr ? UTF8ToString(endpointPtr) : '';
        var token = tokenPtr ? UTF8ToString(tokenPtr) : '';
        var body = bodyPtr ? UTF8ToString(bodyPtr) : '';

        function complete(status, text) {
            try {
                var buf = stringToNewUTF8(text || '');
                try {
                    {{{ makeDynCall('viii', 'cbPtr') }}}(requestId | 0, status | 0, buf);
                } finally {
                    if (buf) { _free(buf); }
                }
            } catch (e) {
                try { console.warn('[FastLogsFetch] Send complete error', e); } catch (_) {}
            }
        }

        try {
            var headers = { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', 'Accept': 'application/json' };
            if (token) { headers['Authorization'] = 'Bearer ' + token; }
            fetch(endpoint, { method: 'POST', headers: headers, body: body })
                .then(function (resp) {
                    var status = resp.status;
                    resp.text().then(function (t) {
                        complete(status, t);
                    }).catch(function () {
                        complete(status, '');
                    });
                })
                .catch(function (err) {
                    complete(0, (err && err.message) ? err.message : 'network error');
                });
        } catch (e) {
            complete(0, (e && e.message) ? e.message : 'fetch threw');
        }
    },

    // FastLogsFetch_RegisterHeadless(cb): install window.FastLogs = { send, lastResult }.
    // send(opts) serializes opts ({title,comment,sceneContext} | undefined) to JSON, stores
    // the Promise resolver under a jsRequestId, and calls cb(jsRequestId, jsonPtr) into C#.
    // C# later resolves via FastLogsFetch_CompleteHeadless. The json buffer is freed right
    // after the synchronous dynCall.
    FastLogsFetch_RegisterHeadless: function (cbPtr) {
        try {
            if (typeof window === 'undefined') { return; }

            var pending = {};   // jsRequestId -> resolve(fn)
            var nextId = 1;

            window.FastLogs = window.FastLogs || {};
            window.FastLogs.lastResult = window.FastLogs.lastResult || null;

            window.FastLogs.send = function (opts) {
                return new Promise(function (resolve) {
                    var id = nextId++;
                    pending[id] = resolve;
                    var json = '';
                    try { json = (opts === undefined || opts === null) ? '' : JSON.stringify(opts); }
                    catch (_) { json = ''; }
                    var buf = stringToNewUTF8(json);
                    try {
                        {{{ makeDynCall('vii', 'cbPtr') }}}(id | 0, buf);
                    } finally {
                        if (buf) { _free(buf); }
                    }
                });
            };

            // Called by FastLogsFetch_CompleteHeadless (below) to settle a pending send.
            window.FastLogs.__complete = function (id, result) {
                var resolve = pending[id];
                delete pending[id];
                window.FastLogs.lastResult = result;
                if (resolve) { resolve(result); }
            };
        } catch (e) {
            try { console.warn('[FastLogsFetch] RegisterHeadless error', e); } catch (_) {}
        }
    },

    // FastLogsFetch_CompleteHeadless(jsRequestId, okInt, resultJson): resolve the pending
    // send Promise with the parsed result object {ok,id,url,status,error} and store it in
    // window.FastLogs.lastResult.
    FastLogsFetch_CompleteHeadless: function (jsRequestId, okInt, resultJsonPtr) {
        try {
            var resultJson = resultJsonPtr ? UTF8ToString(resultJsonPtr) : '';
            var obj;
            try { obj = resultJson ? JSON.parse(resultJson) : {}; }
            catch (_) { obj = { ok: false, error: 'bad result json' }; }
            if (typeof obj.ok === 'undefined') { obj.ok = !!okInt; }
            if (typeof window !== 'undefined' && window.FastLogs && window.FastLogs.__complete) {
                window.FastLogs.__complete(jsRequestId | 0, obj);
            }
        } catch (e) {
            try { console.warn('[FastLogsFetch] CompleteHeadless error', e); } catch (_) {}
        }
    }

});

```

`unity/Plugins/WebGL/FastLogsWeb.jslib`:

```jslib
// FastLogsWeb.jslib - browser bridge for FastLogs on WebGL.
//
// Two entry points, called synchronously from a user-gesture handler (a click on
// the overlay) so the browser honours the clipboard write / window.open:
//   - FastLogsWeb_CopyToClipboard(textPtr): navigator.clipboard.writeText with a
//     textarea + document.execCommand('copy') fallback for older / insecure-context
//     browsers.
//   - FastLogsWeb_OpenUrl(urlPtr): window.open in a new tab (noopener), falling back
//     to location.href if the popup is blocked.
//
// Strings arrive as Emscripten heap pointers; UTF8ToString decodes them. Everything
// is wrapped in try/catch so a browser quirk never throws back into Unity.

mergeInto(LibraryManager.library, {

    FastLogsWeb_CopyToClipboard: function (textPtr) {
        try {
            var text = textPtr ? UTF8ToString(textPtr) : '';

            // Preferred path: async Clipboard API (secure contexts / user gesture).
            if (typeof navigator !== 'undefined' && navigator.clipboard && navigator.clipboard.writeText) {
                navigator.clipboard.writeText(text).catch(function (e) {
                    try { console.warn('[FastLogsWeb] clipboard.writeText failed, falling back', e); } catch (_) {}
                    try { FastLogsWeb_execCommandCopy(text); } catch (_) {}
                });
                return;
            }

            // Fallback: hidden textarea + execCommand('copy').
            FastLogsWeb_execCommandCopy(text);
        } catch (e) {
            try { console.warn('[FastLogsWeb] CopyToClipboard error', e); } catch (_) {}
        }

        function FastLogsWeb_execCommandCopy(value) {
            var ta = document.createElement('textarea');
            ta.value = value;
            ta.setAttribute('readonly', '');
            ta.style.position = 'fixed';
            ta.style.top = '-9999px';
            ta.style.left = '-9999px';
            document.body.appendChild(ta);
            ta.select();
            ta.setSelectionRange(0, ta.value.length);
            try { document.execCommand('copy'); } catch (_) {}
            document.body.removeChild(ta);
        }
    },

    FastLogsWeb_OpenUrl: function (urlPtr) {
        try {
            var url = urlPtr ? UTF8ToString(urlPtr) : '';
            if (!url) { return; }
            var win = null;
            try { win = window.open(url, '_blank', 'noopener'); } catch (_) {}
            // Popup blocked: navigate the current tab instead.
            if (!win) {
                try { window.location.href = url; } catch (_) {}
            }
        } catch (e) {
            try { console.warn('[FastLogsWeb] OpenUrl error', e); } catch (_) {}
        }
    },

    // FastLogsWeb_PickFile(title, goName, okMethod, cancelMethod): open a native file
    // dialog and read the chosen file, then call back into Unity via SendMessage. Must be
    // invoked from a user-gesture handler (a click) so the browser allows the dialog.
    //   - On success: SendMessage(goName, okMethod, "<fileName>\n<base64>"). The base64
    //     alphabet has no '\n', so the first newline cleanly splits name from data.
    //   - On cancel / read error: SendMessage(goName, cancelMethod, "<reason>").
    // The hidden <input> is appended to the DOM (some browsers ignore a detached input)
    // and removed after use. Everything is wrapped so a browser quirk never throws into
    // Unity. SendMessage is provided by the Unity WebGL loader (it is a jslib global).
    FastLogsWeb_PickFile: function (titlePtr, goNamePtr, okMethodPtr, cancelMethodPtr) {
        var goName = goNamePtr ? UTF8ToString(goNamePtr) : '';
        var okMethod = okMethodPtr ? UTF8ToString(okMethodPtr) : '';
        var cancelMethod = cancelMethodPtr ? UTF8ToString(cancelMethodPtr) : '';

        function cancel(reason) {
            try {
                if (goName && cancelMethod) { SendMessage(goName, cancelMethod, reason || ''); }
            } catch (_) {}
        }

        try {
            var input = document.createElement('input');
            input.type = 'file';
            input.style.position = 'fixed';
            input.style.top = '-9999px';
            input.style.left = '-9999px';

            // A focus event firing without a change shortly after means the dialog was
            // dismissed without a selection on most browsers; report it as a cancel.
            // `settled` flips only AFTER a file has been read; `changeFired` flips
            // SYNCHRONOUSLY the instant onchange runs (before the async FileReader), so the
            // focus->cancel timer can tell "a selection is being processed" from "dialog
            // dismissed". Without it, a slow FileReader on a large file could let the 500ms
            // timer fire a false cancel before onchange finishes reading.
            var settled = false;
            var changeFired = false;
            function cleanup() {
                try { if (input && input.parentNode) { input.parentNode.removeChild(input); } } catch (_) {}
            }

            input.onchange = function (ev) {
                changeFired = true;
                settled = true;
                try {
                    var files = (ev.target && ev.target.files) || input.files;
                    if (!files || files.length === 0) { cleanup(); cancel('cancelled'); return; }

                    var file = files[0];
                    var name = file.name || 'file.bin';
                    var reader = new FileReader();
                    reader.onload = function () {
                        cleanup();
                        try {
                            var result = reader.result || '';
                            // result is a data URL: "data:<mime>;base64,<data>". Strip the prefix.
                            var comma = result.indexOf(',');
                            var base64 = comma >= 0 ? result.substring(comma + 1) : result;
                            if (goName && okMethod) { SendMessage(goName, okMethod, name + '\n' + base64); }
                        } catch (e) {
                            try { console.warn('[FastLogsWeb] PickFile read parse error', e); } catch (_) {}
                            cancel('read error');
                        }
                    };
                    reader.onerror = function () {
                        cleanup();
                        cancel('read error');
                    };
                    reader.readAsDataURL(file);
                } catch (e) {
                    cleanup();
                    try { console.warn('[FastLogsWeb] PickFile onchange error', e); } catch (_) {}
                    cancel('read error');
                }
            };

            // Best-effort cancel detection: when the window regains focus after the dialog
            // closed without a change, report a cancel once (guarded by `settled`).
            window.addEventListener('focus', function onFocus() {
                window.removeEventListener('focus', onFocus);
                setTimeout(function () {
                    // Only treat as a cancel if onchange never fired at all. If it fired
                    // (changeFired) the FileReader is still working on a (possibly large)
                    // file, so leave the pick to resolve via onload / onerror.
                    if (!settled && !changeFired) { cleanup(); cancel('cancelled'); }
                }, 500);
            }, { once: true });

            document.body.appendChild(input);
            input.click();
        } catch (e) {
            try { console.warn('[FastLogsWeb] PickFile error', e); } catch (_) {}
            cancel('error');
        }
    }

});

```

`unity/README.md`:

```md
# FastLogs (Unity)

Lightweight in-game log capture and one-tap sharing for Unity. FastLogs records your
console into a ring buffer, gathers a device snapshot, optionally grabs a screenshot,
and uploads everything to a FastLogs server - returning a short, shareable link your
QA / team open in a browser (the overlay also shows a QR code of that link). It also
carries **context** (what the player was doing) and **breadcrumbs** (recent events),
and in dev it always captures unhandled crashes, persisting them to disk so they are
never lost.

- No hardcoded endpoints. Everything is configured per project.
- No third-party dependencies. JSON is self-contained.
- Private by default: outgoing text is PII-scrubbed and sensitive device fields are
  omitted unless you opt in.
- Console-safe: all capture, networking, overlay and screenshot code is gated to
  Editor and Development builds and stripped on retail / console. The public
  `FastLogs.*` API always compiles, so your game code builds everywhere.

**Supported Unity versions:** 6000.1 (Unity 6) and 2022.3 LTS. Namespace: `PlayJoy.FastLogs`.

The full integrator guide lives in [`docs/UNITY.md`](../docs/UNITY.md); this page is
the package overview with the complete config tables.

---

## Installation

### Via Git URL (recommended)

Add to `Packages/manifest.json`:

```jsonc
{
  "dependencies": {
    "com.playjoy.fastlogs": "https://github.com/AitiX/Fastlogs.git?path=/unity#v0.5.3"
  }
}
```

`#v0.5.3` pins to a release tag; the tag list is in GitHub Releases / [`../CHANGELOG.md`](../CHANGELOG.md). Replace the URL and branch/tag with your own. The `?path=/unity` suffix tells Package
Manager to use only the `unity/` subdirectory as the package root.

### Embedded / local

Copy the `unity/` folder into your project's `Packages/` directory and rename it to
`com.playjoy.fastlogs`. Unity detects it automatically.

### asmdef reference (CS0103)

If your game code lives in its own asmdef, the package types are invisible until that
asmdef references `PlayJoy.FastLogs` (`CS0103: PlayJoy does not exist`). Open
`Tools > PlayJoy > FastLogs > Add Reference To Asmdef...`, pick the asmdef that calls
`FastLogs.Init()`, and add the reference. If a config asset exists but nothing
references the package, the editor warns once per session and points you to that menu.

---

## Quick start

1. Create the config asset: `Tools > PlayJoy > FastLogs > Create Config Asset`. It
   lands at `Assets/Resources/FastLogsConfig.asset`. Set at least `Server.EndpointUrl`
   (the full ingest URL) and `Server.AppId` (`[a-z0-9_-]{2,32}`). On iOS the endpoint
   must be `https://`.
2. Call `Init` once early, then send:

```csharp
using PlayJoy.FastLogs;

FastLogs.Init();                                // loads Resources/FastLogsConfig; idempotent

FastLogs.SetContext("level", "3-2");            // rides with every report
FastLogs.Breadcrumb("Player opened shop");      // event in the timeline

FastLogs.Send();                                // quick send, no UI, result via toast
var r = await FastLogs.SendAsync(includeScreenshot: true, title: "Crash on load");
if (r.Success) GUIUtility.systemCopyBuffer = r.Url;
```

`SendAsync` is awaitable on every Unity version and on WebGL.

Logs land in the ring buffer from `Init` onwards, so a report carries your console out
of the box - nothing to switch on. **Recording is a separate feature**: a persistent
on-disk log that survives across play sessions and becomes the report's `logText` when
present. Turn it on with `Recording.Enabled = true`, then either
`Recording.AutoStartRecording = true` or `FastLogs.StartRecording()`. Without
`Enabled` both of those are no-ops.

Import the **Basic Usage** sample from the Package Manager (Samples tab) for a runnable
init / record / send example.

---

## Configuration

FastLogs reads a `FastLogsConfig` ScriptableObject from any `Resources/` folder. Open
it in the Inspector; every section is a foldout. Many of these knobs are also editable
at runtime on the overlay's **Settings** tab (persisted in `PlayerPrefs`).

### Server

| Field | Default | Description |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| `EndpointUrl` | "" | Full ingest URL, e.g. `https://logs.example.com/api/logs`. Required for uploads. |
| `AppId` | "" | Project identifier `[a-z0-9_-]{2,32}` - groups logs in the catalog. |
| `Token` | "" | Bearer token for the ingest endpoint. Empty for an app with open ingest. |
| `RetentionDaysOverride` | 0 | Per-request retention in days. 0 = server default. |

### Capture

| Field | Default | Description |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| `RingCapacity` | 1000 | Upper bound on log entries kept in the ring buffer. Oldest evicted. `MaxLogTextBytes` bounds the same buffer in bytes, so whichever limit binds first wins: raise both to keep more. |
| `MaxLogTextBytes` | 1 MB | Byte budget for the ring buffer **and** the client cap on the serialized log text. 0 = no cap on either (the server still clamps the upload). |
| `UseSrDebuggerConsoleIfPresent` | true | Read SRDebugger's console instead of installing our own hook. |

### Recording

| Field | Default | Description |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| `Enabled` | false | Master switch for the recording feature. |
| `AutoStartRecording` | false | Begin recording immediately on `Init`. |
| `PersistAcrossSessions` | true | Persist the ring buffer to disk across play sessions. |
| `MaxStoreBytes` | 2 MB | Max bytes of persisted log data. 0 = unlimited. |

### Auto-send (crash reports)

Every captured crash is written to a durable disk outbox before any upload, so it
survives a hard crash (re-sent next launch). `AutoSendOnException` is the master switch:
with it off, crashes are not captured at all. `AutoSendPatterns` does not change that -
a pattern match sends a report but never writes to the outbox. `MaxAutoSendsPerSession`
and `IncludeScreenshot` only shape the upload, never the capture.
`MinSecondsBetweenAutoSends` does both: it paces automatic uploads, and it is also the
window in which a crash whose stack is identical to the one captured last is not
re-written to the outbox (a different crash inside the window is still captured).

| Field | Default | Description |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| `AutoSendOnException` | true | Master switch for the crash path: capture an unhandled exception to the outbox, then auto-send it. Off = neither. On in dev/editor. |
| `MinSecondsBetweenAutoSends` | 30 | Minimum seconds between two automatic sends; looping exceptions are throttled. |
| `MaxAutoSendsPerSession` | 10 | Max automatic sends per play session. 0 = unlimited. |
| `IncludeScreenshot` | false | Capture a screenshot with auto-sent crash reports. |
| `AutoSendPatterns` | [] | Regexes matched against lines raised by Unity's console (`Debug.Log`/`LogWarning`/`LogError` and engine output); lines added via `FastLogs.Log`/`Warn`/`Error` bypass the console and never match. A match auto-sends a report (same throttle/cap). Read once at `Init`. |

### Screenshot

| Field | Default | Description |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| `CaptureByDefault` | false | Include a screenshot by default when a report is sent. |
| `MaxDimension` | 1280 | Longest edge of the PNG in pixels; larger captures are downscaled. |

### Scene Context

Bounds the runtime hierarchy snapshot attached via `CaptureSceneContext` / `SendSceneContext`.

| Field | Default | Description |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| `MaxObjects` | 5000 | Max GameObjects captured across all scenes; capture stops (truncated) beyond this. |
| `MaxDepth` | 12 | Max hierarchy depth to recurse (a root is depth 0). |
| `MaxComponentsPerObject` | 40 | Max components dumped per GameObject. |
| `MaxFieldsPerComponent` | 60 | Max fields dumped per component. |
| `MaxStringLength` | 200 | Max length of a single formatted field value; longer is truncated. |
| `MaxCollectionElements` | 20 | Max elements listed for a collection field. |
| `MaxBytes` | 1 MB | Hard cap on the serialized scene-context JSON. |

### Component Capture

Config-driven capture of component state (fields + Renderer/MaterialPropertyBlock) into
one `[flog.capture]` log line per matching component. When off, no watcher is created
(zero per-frame cost). See [`docs/UNITY.md`](../docs/UNITY.md#component-capture).

| Field | Default | Description |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| `Enabled` | false | Master switch. |
| `ScanIntervalSeconds` | 0.5 | Rescan the scene for matching components every N seconds of real time. Time-based, so a high-FPS batchmode player does not scan hundreds of times a second. Higher = cheaper. |
| `MaxCapturesPerSession` | 500 | Global cap on **automatic** captures per session; the auto paths stop (one warning + an `ev:cap` marker) beyond this. A manual pass keeps working. |
| `MaxFieldsPerComponent` | 40 | Max serialized fields dumped per captured component. |
| `MaxStringLength` | 200 | Max length of a single formatted field value. |
| `Rules` | [] | Rules deciding which components are watched (see `CaptureRule`). |

#### CaptureRule

| Field | Default | Description |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| `TypeName` | "" | Component type: short name (`ParticleSystemRenderer`) or namespace-qualified. |
| `NameContains` | "" | Only match objects whose name contains this substring (case-sensitive). Empty = no filter. |
| `Tag` | "" | Only match objects with this tag. Empty = no filter. |
| `CaptureOnEnable` | true | Capture when a matching component becomes active. |
| `CaptureOnChange` | false | Capture when an active component's serialized fields change between scans. |
| `MinSecondsBetweenChangeCaptures` | 1 | Per-instance floor between change captures; 0 = every scan. Debounces a fast-changing field so it cannot burn the cap; a change seen during the window is caught by the first scan after it (the baseline is not advanced while debounced). |
| `FramesAfterEnable` | 0 | After an enable, also capture on each of the next N frames. 0 = only the enable frame. |
| `RendererProperties` | `_MainTex`, `_Color`, `_BaseMap`, `_BaseColor` | For Renderer components: MaterialPropertyBlock properties probed and dumped. |

### Diagnostics (privacy)

| Field | Default | Description |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| `IncludeSensitive` | false | Include device model, app identifier, and (WebGL) page URL / referrer. Off = privacy by default. |
| `ScrubPii` | true | Scrub PII (emails, IPs, bearer tokens, 9+ digit runs) from log text, context and breadcrumbs before upload. Add your own patterns with `PiiScrubber.AddPattern(regex)` before the first send. |

### Trigger

Configures the gesture that toggles the overlay.

| Field | Default | Description |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| `EnableKeyboard` | true | Allow a keyboard shortcut to toggle the overlay. |
| `ToggleKey` | F8 | The keyboard key. |
| `Modifier` | None | Optional modifier: None, Ctrl, Alt, Shift, Cmd. |
| `MultiTouchFingerCount` | 0 | Despite the name, this is the number of **taps** the corner gesture needs to open the overlay (top-right corner, taps within 0.6s of each other). 0 = keep the default of 3 taps. This field cannot switch the gesture off; to remove it entirely clear `UI.EnableUI`. |
| `EnableShake` | false | Shake-to-open on mobile. |
| `ShakeThreshold` | 2.5 | Acceleration magnitude counted as a shake. |
| `ShakeCooldownSeconds` | 1.0 | Minimum seconds between shake triggers. |
| `EnableQuickSendKeyboard` | false | Shortcut that sends the current recording immediately, without opening the overlay. |
| `QuickSendKey` | F9 | Key for the quick-send shortcut (pick one different from ToggleKey). |
| `QuickSendModifier` | None | Modifier required alongside QuickSendKey (None = no modifier). |
| `QuickSendCornerTaps` | 0 | Taps on the **top-left** corner that fire a quick-send (the overlay gesture uses the top-right). Taps must land within 0.6s of each other. 0 = disabled. A mouse click counts as a tap, so it is testable in the Editor. |

Default gesture (no setup): tap the top-right corner 3 times quickly, or press F8.

### Files

| Field | Default | Description |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| `MaxFileBytes` | 25 MB | Cap on the decoded size of a single file/folder upload (server enforces its own too). |
| `FilesEndpointUrl` | "" | Files endpoint (`POST /api/files`). Empty = derived from `Server.EndpointUrl` by swapping `/api/logs` for `/api/files`. |
| `TimeoutSeconds` | 60 | Request timeout for file uploads (larger than the log timeout). |

### Snapshot

| Field | Default | Description |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| `IncludePersistentDataPath` | true | Include the whole saves folder in `snapshot.zip`, excluding FastLogs's own data dir. |
| `ExcludePaths` | [] | Extra absolute paths excluded from the default source (FastLogs's data dir is always excluded). |
| `MaxSnapshotBytes` | 25 MB | Cap on the decoded `snapshot.zip` size. 0 = no client cap. |

### Net

| Field | Default | Description |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| `TimeoutSeconds` | 20 | HTTP request timeout. |
| `MaxRetries` | 2 | Immediate retry attempts after the first failure. |
| `GzipBody` | true | Gzip the request body. Ignored on WebGL (always plain, to avoid CORS preflight). |

### Retry

| Field | Default | Description |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| `RetryIntervalSeconds` | 30 | Seconds before re-attempting a report that failed all immediate retries. 0 = outer retry loop disabled. |
| `MaxRetryAttempts` | 0 | Max outer retry attempts. 0 = unlimited while the app is alive. |

### Loop Guard

| Field | Default | Description |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| `Enabled` | true | Catch a code call site (`Send`/`SendAsync`/`SendSceneContext`/`SendSnapshot`) that keeps sending. |
| `MaxCodeSendsPerSite` | 10 | Code-sends allowed per call site per session before it is treated as a possible loop. |
| `NoUiWarnEvery` | 10 | With no UI, over-threshold sends are dropped; every Nth drop logs one warning. |

### UI

| Field | Default | Description |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| `EnableUI` | true | Enable the in-game share overlay. Off also removes the gesture that opens it (nothing left to toggle); capture, sending, screenshots, clipboard, the command channel and quick-send all keep working. To decide this in code instead of the asset, use `FastLogs.SetServicesProvider(new FastLogsHeadlessServices())` - see [`docs/UNITY.md`](../docs/UNITY.md#running-without-the-ui). |
| `TesterName` | "" | Tester name attached to every report's `tester` field. Empty is omitted. |
| `CopyLinkOnSend` | true | Copy the short link to the clipboard after a successful send (best-effort on WebGL). |
| `EnableTicketToggle` | false | Show a `[ ] Ticket` toggle on the overlay: the send also files a tracker issue (Redmine/Jira) server-side, deduped by crash signature. Needs a tracker configured on the server. |

### Commands (agent -> game channel)

Optional control channel: an agent enqueues named commands, the game polls, runs, and
acks. Off by default. See [`docs/UNITY.md`](../docs/UNITY.md#command-channel-agent---game)
and [`CONTRACT.md`](../CONTRACT.md) §8.

| Field | Default | Description |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| `Enabled` | false | Master switch for the command channel. |
| `PollIntervalSeconds` | 2 | How often the game polls for queued commands. Also the channel's liveness signal: the server stamps every poll and reports the build as live within `COMMANDS_LIVENESS_SEC` (10s), so a long interval reads as "no build running". |
| `MaxPerPoll` | 8 | Max commands claimed per poll (server ceiling is 16). |
| `RegisterBuiltins` | true | Register the built-in commands (`fastlogs.ping`, `fastlogs.send`, `fastlogs.scenecontext`, `fastlogs.capture`, `fastlogs.inspect`). |
| `CorrelateReports` | true | While a command runs, tag reports it produces with that command id as the correlation code, so `/api/await?code=<commandId>` finds them. A handler that sets its own code wins. |

### Enable

| Field | Default | Description |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| `EnableInEditor` | true | Active in the Unity Editor. |
| `EnableInDevelopment` | true | Active in Development Builds. |
| `EnableInRelease` | false | Active in release builds (requires `LOGSHARE_FORCE_ENABLED` and a non-console target). |

---

## Privacy

Private by default. `ScrubPii` (on) redacts emails, IPs, bearer tokens and long digit
runs from log text, context and breadcrumbs once per report. `IncludeSensitive` (off)
keeps device model, app id and page URL/referrer out of the snapshot. Both are also
toggles on the overlay's Settings tab. Details and how to extend the scrubber:
[`docs/UNITY.md`](../docs/UNITY.md#privacy).

## Gating and console safety

All capture, networking, overlay and screenshot code is gated to `UNITY_EDITOR ||
DEVELOPMENT_BUILD` (or `LOGSHARE_FORCE_ENABLED` on a non-console target). Void methods
carry `[Conditional]`, so both body and call sites are stripped in retail; value methods
compile everywhere and return no-op defaults. To enable in a release build use
`Tools > PlayJoy > FastLogs > Build Defines Helper` (adds `LOGSHARE_FORCE_ENABLED`),
then set `Enable.EnableInRelease = true`. Details (incl. passing the define through CI):
[`docs/UNITY.md`](../docs/UNITY.md#platforms-gating-release).

## Crashes

In dev/editor, unhandled exceptions are written to a durable disk outbox before any
upload, de-duplicated, drained when idle, and re-sent on the next launch. A permanent
4xx removes the report from the outbox (resending is pointless); transient failures are
kept and retried. Full model: [`docs/UNITY.md`](../docs/UNITY.md#crashes-and-the-outbox).

## Tests

The package ships EditMode tests under `Tests/Editor`. Unity does not run package tests
by default - add the package to your project's testables in `Packages/manifest.json`:

```jsonc
{ "testables": [ "com.playjoy.fastlogs" ] }
```

They then appear in the Test Runner (EditMode tab).

---

## Contract

The wire format shared with the server and all clients is defined in the repo-level
[`CONTRACT.md`](../CONTRACT.md). This package emits requests that conform to it exactly.

## License

MIT. See `LICENSE.md`.

`unity/Runtime/Bridge/SrDebuggerBridge.cs`:

```cs
// SrDebuggerBridge - OPTIONAL integration with StompyRobot SRDebugger.
//
// Goal: if SRDebugger is present in the project, expose FastLogs from its debug
// panel (open the panel, and register an "action"/option that triggers a send),
// WITHOUT a hard dependency. If SRDebugger is absent, this type is inert and the
// FastLogs overlay works fully on its own.
//
// How the soft dependency is kept soft:
//   - The asmdef declares a versionDefine SRDEBUGGER (com.stompyrobot.srdebugger).
//     We only touch SRDebugger under #if SRDEBUGGER.
//   - Even under #if SRDEBUGGER we go through REFLECTION rather than compiling
//     against SRDebugger types directly. SRDebugger's IDebugService surface has
//     varied across versions (ShowDebugPanel overloads, the Actions API, etc.);
//     reflection means a minor API change degrades to a no-op instead of breaking
//     compilation of the whole package. It also means the package still compiles
//     in a project that defines SRDEBUGGER through an unexpected path.
//
// Public surface is intentionally tiny: IsAvailable, ShowPanel(), and
// RegisterSendAction(label, action). The builder/overlay can call these; nothing
// here is required for FastLogs to function.
//
// Gated: the whole file compiles only where FastLogs is enabled.

#if UNITY_EDITOR || DEVELOPMENT_BUILD || (LOGSHARE_FORCE_ENABLED && !(UNITY_PS4 || UNITY_PS5 || UNITY_GAMECORE || UNITY_SWITCH))
#define FASTLOGS_ENABLED
#endif

#if FASTLOGS_ENABLED
using System;
using System.Reflection;

namespace PlayJoy.FastLogs
{
    /// <summary>
    /// Soft, reflection-based bridge to SRDebugger. All members are safe to call
    /// when SRDebugger is not installed (they become no-ops and IsAvailable is
    /// false).
    /// </summary>
    public sealed class SrDebuggerBridge
    {
        private readonly bool _available;

#if SRDEBUGGER
        // Cached reflection handles for the SRDebug.Instance service.
        private readonly object _service;
        private readonly MethodInfo _showPanel0;     // ShowDebugPanel()
        private readonly MethodInfo _showPanel1;     // ShowDebugPanel(bool)
        private readonly MethodInfo _hidePanel;      // HideDebugPanel()
        private readonly MethodInfo _addOption;      // Actions.Add(...) - resolved lazily
        private readonly object _actions;            // IDebugService.Actions, if present
#endif

        public SrDebuggerBridge()
        {
#if SRDEBUGGER
            try
            {
                // SRDebugger.SRDebug.Instance (static property) -> IDebugService.
                Type srDebugType = FindType("SRDebugger.SRDebug") ?? FindType("SRDebug");
                if (srDebugType != null)
                {
                    PropertyInfo instanceProp = srDebugType.GetProperty("Instance",
                        BindingFlags.Public | BindingFlags.Static);
                    _service = instanceProp != null ? instanceProp.GetValue(null, null) : null;
                }

                if (_service != null)
                {
                    Type svcType = _service.GetType();

                    _showPanel0 = svcType.GetMethod("ShowDebugPanel", new Type[0]);
                    _showPanel1 = svcType.GetMethod("ShowDebugPanel", new[] { typeof(bool) });
                    _hidePanel = svcType.GetMethod("HideDebugPanel", new Type[0]);

                    // Optional Actions service (newer SRDebugger versions).
                    PropertyInfo actionsProp = svcType.GetProperty("Actions");
                    _actions = actionsProp != null ? actionsProp.GetValue(_service, null) : null;
                    if (_actions != null)
                    {
                        // Add(string category, string name, Action action) (signature
                        // varies); resolved by name + parameter count at call time.
                        _addOption = FindAddMethod(_actions.GetType());
                    }

                    _available = _service != null;
                }
            }
            catch (Exception e)
            {
                FlogLog.Exception(e);
                _available = false;
            }
#else
            _available = false;
#endif
        }

        /// <summary>True only when SRDebugger is installed and its service resolved.</summary>
        public bool IsAvailable
        {
            get { return _available; }
        }

        /// <summary>Open the SRDebugger panel, if available. No-op otherwise.</summary>
        public void ShowPanel()
        {
#if SRDEBUGGER
            if (!_available)
            {
                return;
            }
            try
            {
                if (_showPanel1 != null)
                {
                    // ShowDebugPanel(false) -> do not require an entry code.
                    _showPanel1.Invoke(_service, new object[] { false });
                }
                else if (_showPanel0 != null)
                {
                    _showPanel0.Invoke(_service, null);
                }
            }
            catch (Exception e)
            {
                FlogLog.Exception(e);
            }
#endif
        }

        /// <summary>Hide the SRDebugger panel, if available. No-op otherwise.</summary>
        public void HidePanel()
        {
#if SRDEBUGGER
            if (!_available)
            {
                return;
            }
            try
            {
                if (_hidePanel != null)
                {
                    _hidePanel.Invoke(_service, null);
                }
            }
            catch (Exception e)
            {
                FlogLog.Exception(e);
            }
#endif
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Register an action button inside SRDebugger's Actions panel. When the
        /// tester clicks it, <paramref name="action"/> runs (e.g. a FastLogs send).
        /// No-op if SRDebugger or its Actions API is unavailable. Returns true if
        /// the action was registered.
        /// </summary>
        public bool RegisterSendAction(string category, string label, Action action)
        {
#if SRDEBUGGER
            if (!_available || _actions == null || _addOption == null || action == null)
            {
                return false;
            }

            try
            {
                ParameterInfo[] ps = _addOption.GetParameters();
                object[] args = BuildAddArgs(ps, category, label, action);
                if (args == null)
                {
                    return false;
                }
                _addOption.Invoke(_actions, args);
                return true;
            }
            catch (Exception e)
            {
                FlogLog.Exception(e);
                return false;
            }
#else
            return false;
#endif
        }

#if SRDEBUGGER
        // Finds a method named "Add" that takes an Action somewhere in its params.
        private static MethodInfo FindAddMethod(Type actionsType)
        {
            MethodInfo[] methods = actionsType.GetMethods(BindingFlags.Public | BindingFlags.Instance);
            for (int i = 0; i < methods.Length; i++)
            {
                if (methods[i].Name != "Add")
                {
                    continue;
                }
                ParameterInfo[] ps = methods[i].GetParameters();
                for (int p = 0; p < ps.Length; p++)
                {
                    if (ps[p].ParameterType == typeof(Action))
                    {
                        return methods[i];
                    }
                }
            }
            return null;
        }

        // Best-effort argument assembly for the resolved Add overload:
        // fills string parameters with category/label (in order) and the Action.
        private static object[] BuildAddArgs(ParameterInfo[] ps, string category, string label, Action action)
        {
            var args = new object[ps.Length];
            int stringSlot = 0;
            string[] strings = { category ?? "FastLogs", label ?? "Send Log" };

            for (int i = 0; i < ps.Length; i++)
            {
                Type t = ps[i].ParameterType;
                if (t == typeof(Action))
                {
                    args[i] = action;
                }
                else if (t == typeof(string))
                {
                    args[i] = stringSlot < strings.Length ? strings[stringSlot] : label;
                    stringSlot++;
                }
                else if (ps[i].HasDefaultValue)
                {
                    args[i] = ps[i].DefaultValue;
                }
                else if (t.IsValueType)
                {
                    args[i] = Activator.CreateInstance(t);
                }
                else
                {
                    args[i] = null;
                }
            }
            return args;
        }

        private static Type FindType(string fullName)
        {
            Type t = Type.GetType(fullName);
            if (t != null)
            {
                return t;
            }
            Assembly[] assemblies = AppDomain.CurrentDomain.GetAssemblies();
            for (int i = 0; i < assemblies.Length; i++)
            {
                try
                {
                    t = assemblies[i].GetType(fullName);
                    if (t != null)
                    {
                        return t;
                    }
                }
                catch (Exception)
                {
                    // Some dynamic assemblies throw on GetType; ignore.
                }
            }
            return null;
        }
#endif
    }
}
#endif

```
