`unity/Runtime/Clipboard/ClipboardService.cs`:

```cs
// ClipboardService - the default IClipboard, plus a synchronous OpenUrl helper.
//
// Copy / open must be SYNCHRONOUS on WebGL: browsers only honour clipboard writes
// and window.open from inside a user-gesture handler (the click that triggered the
// call). So these methods do the work inline - no coroutine, no FlogTask - and the
// overlay must call them directly from its button handler.
//
// Platform behaviour:
//   - Standalone / mobile / editor: GUIUtility.systemCopyBuffer for copy,
//     Application.OpenURL for open.
//   - WebGL: a jslib bridge (FastLogsWeb.jslib) - navigator.clipboard.writeText
//     (with a textarea/execCommand fallback) and window.open.
//
// Gated like the rest of the package.

#if UNITY_EDITOR || DEVELOPMENT_BUILD || (LOGSHARE_FORCE_ENABLED && !(UNITY_PS4 || UNITY_PS5 || UNITY_GAMECORE || UNITY_SWITCH))
#define FASTLOGS_ENABLED
#endif

#if FASTLOGS_ENABLED
using System;
using UnityEngine;

#if UNITY_WEBGL && !UNITY_EDITOR
using System.Runtime.InteropServices;
#endif

namespace PlayJoy.FastLogs
{
    /// <summary>
    /// Default clipboard + url-opener. All operations are synchronous so they work
    /// from a WebGL user-gesture handler. CopyToClipboard / OpenUrl return true when
    /// the action was issued (best-effort; the browser may still block it).
    /// </summary>
    public sealed class ClipboardService : IClipboard
    {
        public bool CopyToClipboard(string text)
        {
            if (text == null)
            {
                text = string.Empty;
            }

            try
            {
#if UNITY_WEBGL && !UNITY_EDITOR
                FastLogsWeb_CopyToClipboard(text);
                return true;
#else
                GUIUtility.systemCopyBuffer = text;
                return true;
#endif
            }
            catch (Exception e)
            {
                FlogLog.Exception(e);
                return false;
            }
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Open a url in the browser / system handler. Synchronous so it works from a
        /// WebGL user gesture. Returns true if the open was issued.
        /// </summary>
        public bool OpenUrl(string url)
        {
            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(url))
            {
                return false;
            }

            try
            {
#if UNITY_WEBGL && !UNITY_EDITOR
                FastLogsWeb_OpenUrl(url);
                return true;
#else
                Application.OpenURL(url);
                return true;
#endif
            }
            catch (Exception e)
            {
                FlogLog.Exception(e);
                return false;
            }
        }

#if UNITY_WEBGL && !UNITY_EDITOR
        [DllImport("__Internal")]
        private static extern void FastLogsWeb_CopyToClipboard(string text);

        [DllImport("__Internal")]
        private static extern void FastLogsWeb_OpenUrl(string url);
#endif
    }
}
#endif

```

`unity/Runtime/Commands/CommandChannel.cs`:

```cs
// CommandChannel - transport for the remote command channel on top of CommandRegistry.
//
// Off WebGL it drives a poll loop from a coroutine on the FastLogsRuntime host: every
// PollIntervalSeconds it GETs {base}/api/commands/{appId}/poll?max={n} (Bearer ingest
// token), dispatches each claimed command on the MAIN THREAD via CommandRegistry, then
// POSTs the ack. On WebGL (&& !UNITY_EDITOR) the poll loop lives in JS (setInterval in
// FastLogsCommands.jslib) so it keeps running even when the tab is hidden and the Unity
// player loop / requestAnimationFrame is paused; each command is handed to a static
// [MonoPInvokeCallback], dispatched, and acked through a JS fetch.
//
// The commands endpoint is derived from Server.EndpointUrl by swapping the '/api/logs'
// segment for '/api/commands' (the same derivation FilesSection uses for '/api/files').
//
// Built-in commands (fastlogs.ping / send / scenecontext / capture / inspect) are registered
// here when Commands.RegisterBuiltins is on, and unregistered on Stop. Never throws out.

#if UNITY_EDITOR || DEVELOPMENT_BUILD || (LOGSHARE_FORCE_ENABLED && !(UNITY_PS4 || UNITY_PS5 || UNITY_GAMECORE || UNITY_SWITCH))
#define FASTLOGS_ENABLED
#endif

#if FASTLOGS_ENABLED
using System;
using System.Collections;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Text;
using UnityEngine;
using UnityEngine.Networking;
#if UNITY_WEBGL && !UNITY_EDITOR
using System.Runtime.InteropServices;
using AOT;
#endif

namespace PlayJoy.FastLogs
{
    /// <summary>
    /// Remote command transport. One instance per runtime; created and started from
    /// <see cref="FastLogsRuntime.Initialize"/> only when Commands.Enabled is set.
    /// </summary>
    internal sealed class CommandChannel
    {
        private const string JsonContentType = "application/json";

        private readonly FastLogsRuntime _runtime;
        private readonly FastLogsConfig _config;

        private bool _running;
        private bool _builtinsRegistered;

        // Resolved once at Start.
        private string _pollUrl;   // {base}/api/commands/{appId}/poll?max={n}
        private string _ackBase;   // {base}/api/commands/{appId}/ack   (+ "/" + cmdId per ack)
        private string _token;     // ingest bearer (empty = no auth header)
        private int _timeoutSeconds;

        // Tags reports produced by a command with that command's id. Null when
        // Commands.CorrelateReports is off, so the game keeps its own code.
        private CorrelationScope _correlation;

#if !(UNITY_WEBGL && !UNITY_EDITOR)
        private Coroutine _pollCoroutine;
#endif

        public CommandChannel(FastLogsRuntime runtime, FastLogsConfig config)
        {
            _runtime = runtime;
            _config = config;
        }

        public void Start()
        {
            if (_running)
            {
                return;
            }

            FastLogsConfig.ServerSection server = _config != null ? _config.Server : null;
            string commandsBase = DeriveCommandsBase(server != null ? server.EndpointUrl : null);
            string appId = server != null ? server.AppId : null;
            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(commandsBase) || string.IsNullOrEmpty(appId))
            {
                FlogLog.Warn("Command channel not started: endpoint or appId is not configured.");
                return;
            }

            FastLogsConfig.CommandsSection cmd = _config.Commands;
            float interval = Mathf.Max(0.1f, cmd != null ? cmd.PollIntervalSeconds : 2f);
            int max = Mathf.Max(1, cmd != null ? cmd.MaxPerPoll : 8);

            _token = server.Token;
            _timeoutSeconds = _config.Net != null ? Mathf.Max(1, _config.Net.TimeoutSeconds) : 20;
            _pollUrl = commandsBase + "/" + appId + "/poll?max=" + max;
            _ackBase = commandsBase + "/" + appId + "/ack";

            if (cmd == null || cmd.CorrelateReports)
            {
                _correlation = new CorrelationScope(
                    () => _runtime != null ? _runtime.CorrelationCode : null,
                    (code) => { if (_runtime != null) _runtime.SetCorrelationCode(code); });
            }

            if (cmd == null || cmd.RegisterBuiltins)
            {
                RegisterBuiltins();
            }

            _running = true;

#if UNITY_WEBGL && !UNITY_EDITOR
            s_instance = this;
            try { FastLogsCmd_Start(_pollUrl, _token ?? string.Empty, Mathf.RoundToInt(interval * 1000f), s_callback); }
            catch (Exception e) { FlogLog.Exception(e); }
#else
            if (_runtime != null)
            {
                _pollCoroutine = _runtime.StartCoroutine(PollLoop(interval));
            }
#endif
            FlogLog.Info("Command channel started.");
        }

        public void Stop()
        {
            _running = false;

#if UNITY_WEBGL && !UNITY_EDITOR
            try { FastLogsCmd_Stop(); } catch (Exception e) { FlogLog.Exception(e); }
            if (s_instance == this) { s_instance = null; }
#else
            if (_pollCoroutine != null && _runtime != null)
            {
                try { _runtime.StopCoroutine(_pollCoroutine); } catch (Exception e) { FlogLog.Exception(e); }
                _pollCoroutine = null;
            }
#endif
            if (_builtinsRegistered)
            {
                UnregisterBuiltins();
            }
        }

        // ============================================================
        // Built-in commands
        // ============================================================

        private void RegisterBuiltins()
        {
            if (_builtinsRegistered)
            {
                return;
            }
            CommandRegistry.Register("fastlogs.ping", OnPing);
            CommandRegistry.RegisterAsync("fastlogs.send", OnSend);
            CommandRegistry.RegisterAsync("fastlogs.scenecontext", OnSendSceneContext);
            CommandRegistry.Register("fastlogs.capture", OnCapture);
            CommandRegistry.Register("fastlogs.inspect", OnInspect);
            _builtinsRegistered = true;
        }

        private void UnregisterBuiltins()
        {
            CommandRegistry.Unregister("fastlogs.ping");
            CommandRegistry.Unregister("fastlogs.send");
            CommandRegistry.Unregister("fastlogs.scenecontext");
            CommandRegistry.Unregister("fastlogs.capture");
            CommandRegistry.Unregister("fastlogs.inspect");
            _builtinsRegistered = false;
        }

        // No runtime-readable package version source, so a plain pong (package.json is an
        // editor-only asset, not shipped into the player).
        private static string OnPing(string args)
        {
            return "pong";
        }

        private FlogTask<string> OnSend(string args)
        {
            return SendCommand(args, false);
        }

        private FlogTask<string> OnSendSceneContext(string args)
        {
            return SendCommand(args, true);
        }

        // Config-driven component capture (TODO#1): run a manual pass over the ComponentCapture
        // rules and return a structured result {captured,autoStopped,sessionCount}. The manual
        // pass ignores the auto cap, so 0 means "nothing matched", not "the cap silenced it".
        // Runs on the main thread (poll coroutine / JS command callback), so Unity APIs are safe.
        private string OnCapture(string args)
        {
            if (_runtime == null)
            {
                return "{\"captured\":0,\"autoStopped\":false,\"sessionCount\":0,\"disabled\":true}";
            }

            FastLogsRuntime.ComponentCaptureResult r = _runtime.CaptureComponentsWithResult();
            if (r.Disabled)
            {
                return "{\"captured\":0,\"autoStopped\":false,\"sessionCount\":0,\"disabled\":true}";
            }

            var sb = new StringBuilder(64);
            sb.Append("{\"captured\":").Append(r.Captured)
              .Append(",\"autoStopped\":").Append(r.AutoStopped ? "true" : "false")
              .Append(",\"sessionCount\":").Append(r.SessionCount)
              .Append('}');
            return sb.ToString();
        }

        // On-demand component inspection: answers "what is in this component right now?"
        // without the cost of a full scene-context dump, and without the ComponentCapture
        // rules being configured. Read-only; never throws (errors come back as {"error"}).
        // Runs on the main thread (poll coroutine / JS command callback), so Unity APIs are safe.
        private string OnInspect(string args)
        {
            return _runtime != null ? _runtime.InspectComponents(args) : "{\"error\":\"no runtime\"}";
        }

        // Parse {title?,comment?,sceneContext?,createIssue?,issueSubject?}, run a headless
        // send, and resolve to the '{"ok":...}' result JSON when the upload completes.
        private FlogTask<string> SendCommand(string argsJson, bool forceSceneContext)
        {
            string title = null, comment = null, issueSubject = null;
            bool sceneContext = forceSceneContext;
            bool createIssue = false;
            try
            {
                if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(argsJson) && MiniJson.Parse(argsJson) is Dictionary<string, object> obj)
                {
                    title = GetString(obj, "title");
                    comment = GetString(obj, "comment");
                    if (!forceSceneContext)
                    {
                        sceneContext = GetBool(obj, "sceneContext");
                    }
                    // Agent-side ticket filing: the same opt-in the game clients have.
                    createIssue = GetBool(obj, "createIssue");
                    issueSubject = GetString(obj, "issueSubject");
                }
            }
            catch (Exception e) { FlogLog.Exception(e); }

            var outTask = FlogTask.Create<string>();

            FlogTask<UploadResultDto> uploadTask;
            try
            {
                uploadTask = _runtime.BeginHeadlessSend(sceneContext, title, comment, null, 0, createIssue, issueSubject);
            }
            catch (Exception e)
            {
                FlogLog.Exception(e);
                outTask.SetResult(SendResultJson(UploadResultDto.Fail(e.Message)));
                return outTask;
            }

            if (uploadTask == null)
            {
                outTask.SetResult(SendResultJson(UploadResultDto.Fail("no task")));
                return outTask;
            }

            var awaiter = uploadTask.GetAwaiter();
            awaiter.OnCompleted(() =>
            {
                UploadResultDto r;
                try { r = uploadTask.Result; }
                catch (Exception e) { r = UploadResultDto.Fail(e.Message); }
                outTask.SetResult(SendResultJson(r));
            });
            return outTask;
        }

        private static string SendResultJson(UploadResultDto r)
        {
            if (r.Success)
            {
                string json = "{\"ok\":true,\"id\":" + JsonString(r.Id) + ",\"url\":" + JsonString(r.Url);
                // Ticket outcome, present only when the send asked for one.
                if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(r.IssueUrl)) json += ",\"issueUrl\":" + JsonString(r.IssueUrl);
                if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(r.IssueError)) json += ",\"issueError\":" + JsonString(r.IssueError);
                return json + "}";
            }
            return "{\"ok\":false,\"error\":" + JsonString(r.Error) + "}";
        }

        // ============================================================
        // Ack body (with offload for a result too big to inline)
        // ============================================================

        // Resolves to the FINAL ack body. A result over the server's inline cap is
        // uploaded as a file and referenced instead, so a big inspect/dump is not lost
        // to a 400 the agent would only ever see as a timeout.
        private FlogTask<string> PrepareAckBody(string id, bool ok, string result, string error)
        {
            if (!ok || !CommandResultRef.IsTooBig(result))
            {
                return FlogTask.FromResult(BuildAckBody(ok, result, error));
            }

            byte[] bytes = Encoding.UTF8.GetBytes(result);
            var outTask = FlogTask.Create<string>();

            FlogTask<FileUploadResultDto> upload;
            try
            {
                upload = _runtime != null
                    ? _runtime.BeginSendFileBytes(bytes, CommandResultRef.FileName(id), "Command result " + id)
                    : null;
            }
            catch (Exception e)
            {
                FlogLog.Exception(e);
                outTask.SetResult(BuildAckBody(false, null, CommandResultRef.OffloadFailedError(bytes.Length, e.Message)));
                return outTask;
            }

            if (upload == null)
            {
                outTask.SetResult(BuildAckBody(false, null, CommandResultRef.OffloadFailedError(bytes.Length, "no uploader")));
                return outTask;
            }

            var awaiter = upload.GetAwaiter();
            awaiter.OnCompleted(() =>
            {
                FileUploadResultDto r;
                try { r = upload.Result; }
                catch (Exception e) { r = FileUploadResultDto.Fail(e.Message); }

                // The agent cannot read a result it cannot download, so a failed offload
                // is an error, not a success with a dead link.
                outTask.SetResult(r.Success
                    ? BuildAckBody(true, CommandResultRef.Build(r.DownloadUrl, bytes.Length), null)
                    : BuildAckBody(false, null, CommandResultRef.OffloadFailedError(bytes.Length, r.Error)));
            });
            return outTask;
        }

        // ============================================================
        // Correlation (command id -> the reports that command produced)
        // ============================================================

        private void EnterCorrelation(string commandId)
        {
            if (_correlation == null) return;
            try { _correlation.Enter(commandId); }
            catch (Exception e) { FlogLog.Exception(e); }
        }

        private void LeaveCorrelation(string commandId)
        {
            if (_correlation == null) return;
            try { _correlation.Leave(commandId); }
            catch (Exception e) { FlogLog.Exception(e); }
        }

        // ============================================================
        // Shared helpers (both transports)
        // ============================================================

        // Derive the commands endpoint from the log endpoint: .../api/logs -> .../api/commands.
        // Returns null when the marker segment is absent (caller then does not start).
        private static string DeriveCommandsBase(string endpointUrl)
        {
            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(endpointUrl))
            {
                return null;
            }
            int idx = endpointUrl.LastIndexOf("/api/logs", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
            if (idx < 0)
            {
                return null;
            }
            return endpointUrl.Substring(0, idx) + "/api/commands"
                + endpointUrl.Substring(idx + "/api/logs".Length);
        }

        // {status:"done",result?} | {status:"error",error?}
        private static string BuildAckBody(bool ok, string result, string error)
        {
            var sb = new StringBuilder(64);
            sb.Append('{');
            if (ok)
            {
                sb.Append("\"status\":\"done\"");
                if (result != null)
                {
                    sb.Append(",\"result\":").Append(JsonString(result));
                }
            }
            else
            {
                sb.Append("\"status\":\"error\"");
                sb.Append(",\"error\":").Append(JsonString(error ?? "error"));
            }
            sb.Append('}');
            return sb.ToString();
        }

        private static string GetString(Dictionary<string, object> obj, string key)
        {
            if (obj.TryGetValue(key, out var v) && v is string s)
            {
                return s;
            }
            return null;
        }

        private static bool GetBool(Dictionary<string, object> obj, string key)
        {
            if (!obj.TryGetValue(key, out var v) || v == null)
            {
                return false;
            }
            if (v is bool b) return b;
            if (v is string s) return string.Equals(s, "true", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
            if (v is double d) return d != 0d;
            return false;
        }

        // Minimal JSON string encoder (quoted, escaped literal, or "null").
        private static string JsonString(string s)
        {
            if (s == null)
            {
                return "null";
            }
            var sb = new StringBuilder(s.Length + 2);
            sb.Append('"');
            for (int i = 0; i < s.Length; i++)
            {
                char c = s[i];
                switch (c)
                {
                    case '"': sb.Append("\\\""); break;
                    case '\\': sb.Append("\\\\"); break;
                    case '\b': sb.Append("\\b"); break;
                    case '\f': sb.Append("\\f"); break;
                    case '\n': sb.Append("\\n"); break;
                    case '\r': sb.Append("\\r"); break;
                    case '\t': sb.Append("\\t"); break;
                    default:
                        if (c < ' ') sb.Append("\\u").Append(((int)c).ToString("x4"));
                        else sb.Append(c);
                        break;
                }
            }
            sb.Append('"');
            return sb.ToString();
        }

#if !(UNITY_WEBGL && !UNITY_EDITOR)
        // ============================================================
        // Non-WebGL transport: coroutine poll loop on the runtime host
        // ============================================================

        private IEnumerator PollLoop(float interval)
        {
            while (_running)
            {
                // A fresh instance each iteration: a reused WaitForSecondsRealtime caches its
                // absolute end time and would elapse instantly on the second wait.
                yield return new WaitForSecondsRealtime(interval);
                if (!_running)
                {
                    break;
                }
                yield return PollOnce();
            }
        }

        private IEnumerator PollOnce()
        {
            UnityWebRequest req = null;
            UnityWebRequestAsyncOperation op = null;
            try
            {
                req = UnityWebRequest.Get(_pollUrl);
                req.timeout = _timeoutSeconds;
                req.SetRequestHeader("Accept", JsonContentType);
                if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(_token))
                {
                    req.SetRequestHeader("Authorization", "Bearer " + _token);
                }
                op = req.SendWebRequest();
            }
            catch (Exception e)
            {
                FlogLog.Exception(e);
                if (req != null) { req.Dispose(); }
                yield break;
            }

            while (!op.isDone)
            {
                yield return null;
            }

            bool ok = !IsConnectionError(req) && req.responseCode >= 200 && req.responseCode < 300;
            string body = ok ? SafeDownloadText(req) : null;
            req.Dispose();

            if (!ok || string.IsNullOrEmpty(body))
            {
                yield break; // transient/empty; the next interval retries
            }

            List<Dictionary<string, object>> commands = ParseCommands(body);
            if (commands == null)
            {
                yield break;
            }

            for (int i = 0; i < commands.Count; i++)
            {
                if (!_running)
                {
                    break;
                }
                var c = commands[i];
                string id = GetString(c, "id");
                string name = GetString(c, "name");
                string args = GetString(c, "args");
                if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(id) || string.IsNullOrEmpty(name))
                {
                    continue;
                }
                yield return DispatchAndAck(id, name, args);
            }
        }

        private IEnumerator DispatchAndAck(string id, string name, string args)
        {
            string ackUrl = _ackBase + "/" + id;

            // Any report this command produces carries its id as the correlation code,
            // so the agent can find it with /api/await?code=<commandId>.
            EnterCorrelation(id);

            // CS1631 forbids yield inside a catch clause - capture the failure, ack after.
            CommandOutcome outcome = default;
            bool found = false;
            string dispatchError = null;
            try { found = CommandRegistry.TryDispatch(name, args, out outcome); }
            catch (Exception e)
            {
                FlogLog.Exception(e);
                dispatchError = e.Message;
            }

            if (dispatchError != null)
            {
                LeaveCorrelation(id);
                yield return AckOnce(ackUrl, BuildAckBody(false, null, dispatchError));
                yield break;
            }

            if (!found)
            {
                LeaveCorrelation(id);
                yield return AckOnce(ackUrl, BuildAckBody(false, null, "unknown command: " + name));
                yield break;
            }

            if (outcome.Pending != null)
            {
                FlogTask<string> t = outcome.Pending;
                while (!t.IsCompleted)
                {
                    yield return null;
                }
                bool ok; string result = null, error = null;
                try { result = t.Result; ok = true; }
                catch (Exception e) { ok = false; error = e.Message; }
                // The async handler is settled, so its reports are in: close the scope
                // before the ack (the ack itself must not be correlated).
                LeaveCorrelation(id);
                yield return AckPrepared(ackUrl, id, ok, result, error);
            }
            else
            {
                LeaveCorrelation(id);
                yield return AckPrepared(ackUrl, id, outcome.Ok, outcome.Result, outcome.Error);
            }
        }

        // Build the ack body (offloading an oversized result), then send it.
        private IEnumerator AckPrepared(string ackUrl, string id, bool ok, string result, string error)
        {
            FlogTask<string> bodyTask = PrepareAckBody(id, ok, result, error);
            while (!bodyTask.IsCompleted)
            {
                yield return null;
            }
            string body;
            try { body = bodyTask.Result; }
            catch (Exception e)
            {
                FlogLog.Exception(e);
                body = BuildAckBody(false, null, "ack build failed: " + e.Message);
            }
            yield return AckOnce(ackUrl, body);
        }

        private IEnumerator AckOnce(string ackUrl, string body)
        {
            UnityWebRequest req = null;
            UnityWebRequestAsyncOperation op = null;
            try
            {
                req = new UnityWebRequest(ackUrl, UnityWebRequest.kHttpVerbPOST)
                {
                    uploadHandler = new UploadHandlerRaw(Encoding.UTF8.GetBytes(body)) { contentType = JsonContentType },
                    downloadHandler = new DownloadHandlerBuffer(),
                    timeout = _timeoutSeconds
                };
                req.SetRequestHeader("Content-Type", JsonContentType);
                req.SetRequestHeader("Accept", JsonContentType);
                if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(_token))
                {
                    req.SetRequestHeader("Authorization", "Bearer " + _token);
                }
                op = req.SendWebRequest();
            }
            catch (Exception e)
            {
                FlogLog.Exception(e);
                if (req != null) { req.Dispose(); }
                yield break;
            }

            while (!op.isDone)
            {
                yield return null;
            }

            if (IsConnectionError(req) || req.responseCode < 200 || req.responseCode >= 300)
            {
                FlogLog.Warn("Command ack failed (" + req.responseCode + "): " + (req.error ?? "unknown"));
            }
            req.Dispose();
        }

        private static List<Dictionary<string, object>> ParseCommands(string json)
        {
            if (!(MiniJson.Parse(json) is Dictionary<string, object> root))
            {
                return null;
            }
            if (!root.TryGetValue("commands", out var raw) || !(raw is List<object> list))
            {
                return null;
            }
            var result = new List<Dictionary<string, object>>(list.Count);
            for (int i = 0; i < list.Count; i++)
            {
                if (list[i] is Dictionary<string, object> c)
                {
                    result.Add(c);
                }
            }
            return result;
        }

        private static string SafeDownloadText(UnityWebRequest request)
        {
            try
            {
                return request.downloadHandler != null ? request.downloadHandler.text : null;
            }
            catch (Exception e)
            {
                FlogLog.Exception(e);
                return null;
            }
        }

        private static bool IsConnectionError(UnityWebRequest request)
        {
#if UNITY_2020_2_OR_NEWER
            return request.result == UnityWebRequest.Result.ConnectionError
                || request.result == UnityWebRequest.Result.DataProcessingError;
#else
            return request.isNetworkError;
#endif
        }
#endif // non-WebGL transport

#if UNITY_WEBGL && !UNITY_EDITOR
        // ============================================================
        // WebGL transport: JS setInterval poll loop, runs even when hidden
        // ============================================================

        private static CommandChannel s_instance;

        // Rooted for the app lifetime (IL2CPP reverse P/Invoke via [MonoPInvokeCallback]).
        private delegate void CommandCallback(IntPtr commandJsonPtr);
        private static readonly CommandCallback s_callback = OnCommand;

        [MonoPInvokeCallback(typeof(CommandCallback))]
        private static void OnCommand(IntPtr commandJsonPtr)
        {
            CommandChannel self = s_instance;
            if (self == null)
            {
                return;
            }

            // Decode synchronously; JS frees the buffer once this returns.
            string json = commandJsonPtr != IntPtr.Zero ? Marshal.PtrToStringUTF8(commandJsonPtr) : null;
            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(json))
            {
                return;
            }

            string id = null, name = null, args = null;
            try
            {
                if (MiniJson.Parse(json) is Dictionary<string, object> obj)
                {
                    id = GetString(obj, "id");
                    name = GetString(obj, "name");
                    args = GetString(obj, "args");
                }
            }
            catch (Exception e) { FlogLog.Exception(e); return; }

            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(id) || string.IsNullOrEmpty(name))
            {
                return;
            }

            // Any report this command produces carries its id as the correlation code.
            // Async handlers keep the scope open until they settle, so a poll batch that
            // settles out of order nests instead of stripping an outer command's code.
            self.EnterCorrelation(id);

            CommandOutcome outcome;
            bool found;
            try { found = CommandRegistry.TryDispatch(name, args, out outcome); }
            catch (Exception e)
            {
                FlogLog.Exception(e);
                self.LeaveCorrelation(id);
                self.AckWebGL(id, BuildAckBody(false, null, e.Message));
                return;
            }

            if (!found)
            {
                self.LeaveCorrelation(id);
                self.AckWebGL(id, BuildAckBody(false, null, "unknown command: " + name));
                return;
            }

            if (outcome.Pending != null)
            {
                FlogTask<string> t = outcome.Pending;
                string cid = id;
                var awaiter = t.GetAwaiter();
                awaiter.OnCompleted(() =>
                {
                    bool ok; string result = null, error = null;
                    try { result = t.Result; ok = true; }
                    catch (Exception e) { ok = false; error = e.Message; }
                    self.LeaveCorrelation(cid);
                    self.AckWebGLPrepared(cid, ok, result, error);
                });
            }
            else
            {
                self.LeaveCorrelation(id);
                self.AckWebGLPrepared(id, outcome.Ok, outcome.Result, outcome.Error);
            }
        }

        // Build the ack body (offloading an oversized result), then fetch-ack it.
        private void AckWebGLPrepared(string cmdId, bool ok, string result, string error)
        {
            FlogTask<string> bodyTask = PrepareAckBody(cmdId, ok, result, error);
            if (bodyTask.IsCompleted)
            {
                AckWebGL(cmdId, SafeBody(bodyTask));
                return;
            }
            var awaiter = bodyTask.GetAwaiter();
            awaiter.OnCompleted(() => AckWebGL(cmdId, SafeBody(bodyTask)));
        }

        private static string SafeBody(FlogTask<string> bodyTask)
        {
            try { return bodyTask.Result; }
            catch (Exception e)
            {
                FlogLog.Exception(e);
                return BuildAckBody(false, null, "ack build failed: " + e.Message);
            }
        }

        private void AckWebGL(string cmdId, string body)
        {
            try { FastLogsCmd_Ack(_ackBase + "/" + cmdId, _token ?? string.Empty, body); }
            catch (Exception e) { FlogLog.Exception(e); }
        }

        [DllImport("__Internal")]
        private static extern void FastLogsCmd_Start(string pollUrl, string token, int intervalMs, CommandCallback callback);

        [DllImport("__Internal")]
        private static extern void FastLogsCmd_Ack(string ackUrl, string token, string body);

        [DllImport("__Internal")]
        private static extern void FastLogsCmd_Stop();
#endif // WebGL transport
    }
}
#endif

```

`unity/Runtime/Commands/CommandRegistry.cs`:

```cs
// CommandRegistry - the name -> handler table for the remote command channel.
//
// Plain, engine-light and unit-testable (no MonoBehaviour). Registrations live in
// STATIC storage so game code can register commands via the FastLogs facade BEFORE
// Init; the channel and the built-in commands write to the same table. A handler is
// either synchronous (Func<string,string>) or asynchronous (Func<string,FlogTask<string>>);
// TryDispatch runs it and reports the outcome. A synchronous handler that throws is
// caught and turned into an error outcome (its message, no stack trace); the full
// exception is logged through the package logger.

#if UNITY_EDITOR || DEVELOPMENT_BUILD || (LOGSHARE_FORCE_ENABLED && !(UNITY_PS4 || UNITY_PS5 || UNITY_GAMECORE || UNITY_SWITCH))
#define FASTLOGS_ENABLED
#endif

#if FASTLOGS_ENABLED
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Text.RegularExpressions;

namespace PlayJoy.FastLogs
{
    /// <summary>
    /// Outcome of a command dispatch. For a synchronous handler <see cref="Ok"/> +
    /// <see cref="Result"/>/<see cref="Error"/> are filled immediately. For an
    /// asynchronous handler <see cref="Pending"/> carries the task to settle later;
    /// the caller acks once it completes.
    /// </summary>
    internal struct CommandOutcome
    {
        public bool Ok;
        public string Result;
        public string Error;
        public FlogTask<string> Pending;
    }

    /// <summary>
    /// Static registry of remote commands (name -> handler). Thread-safe registration
    /// (a simple lock); dispatch runs on the caller's thread (the channel dispatches on
    /// the main thread).
    /// </summary>
    internal static class CommandRegistry
    {
        // Command name grammar, mirrored from the server (POST /api/commands validation).
        private static readonly Regex NamePattern = new Regex("^[a-z0-9_.-]{1,64}$", RegexOptions.CultureInvariant);

        private sealed class Handler
        {
            public Func<string, string> Sync;
            public Func<string, FlogTask<string>> Async;
        }

        private static readonly object s_lock = new object();
        private static readonly Dictionary<string, Handler> s_handlers = new Dictionary<string, Handler>(StringComparer.Ordinal);

        public static void Register(string name, Func<string, string> handler)
        {
            if (handler == null)
            {
                FlogLog.Warn("Command register ignored: null handler.");
                return;
            }
            string key = Normalize(name);
            if (key == null)
            {
                FlogLog.Warn("Command register ignored: invalid name '" + name + "'.");
                return;
            }
            lock (s_lock) { s_handlers[key] = new Handler { Sync = handler }; }
        }

        public static void RegisterAsync(string name, Func<string, FlogTask<string>> handler)
        {
            if (handler == null)
            {
                FlogLog.Warn("Command register ignored: null async handler.");
                return;
            }
            string key = Normalize(name);
            if (key == null)
            {
                FlogLog.Warn("Command register ignored: invalid name '" + name + "'.");
                return;
            }
            lock (s_lock) { s_handlers[key] = new Handler { Async = handler }; }
        }

        public static void Unregister(string name)
        {
            string key = Normalize(name);
            if (key == null)
            {
                return;
            }
            lock (s_lock) { s_handlers.Remove(key); }
        }

        /// <summary>Drop every registration (test isolation).</summary>
        public static void Clear()
        {
            lock (s_lock) { s_handlers.Clear(); }
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Look up and run the handler for <paramref name="name"/>. Returns false when no
        /// handler is registered (the caller acks an "unknown command"). Never throws:
        /// a synchronous handler's exception becomes an error outcome.
        /// </summary>
        public static bool TryDispatch(string name, string argsJson, out CommandOutcome outcome)
        {
            outcome = default;
            string key = Normalize(name);
            if (key == null)
            {
                return false;
            }

            Handler handler;
            lock (s_lock)
            {
                if (!s_handlers.TryGetValue(key, out handler))
                {
                    return false;
                }
            }

            try
            {
                if (handler.Async != null)
                {
                    // Pending settles asynchronously; a null task is treated as an empty result.
                    outcome.Pending = handler.Async(argsJson) ?? FlogTask.FromResult<string>(null);
                }
                else
                {
                    outcome.Ok = true;
                    outcome.Result = handler.Sync(argsJson);
                }
            }
            catch (Exception e)
            {
                // Full exception (with stack) to the package log; only the message to the ack.
                FlogLog.Exception(e);
                outcome.Ok = false;
                outcome.Result = null;
                outcome.Error = e.Message;
                outcome.Pending = null;
            }
            return true;
        }

        // Trim + lower-invariant so registration/dispatch are symmetric, then validate against
        // the same grammar the server enforces. Returns null when the name is unusable.
        private static string Normalize(string name)
        {
            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(name))
            {
                return null;
            }
            string key = name.Trim().ToLowerInvariant();
            return NamePattern.IsMatch(key) ? key : null;
        }
    }
}
#endif

```

`unity/Runtime/Commands/CommandResultRef.cs`:

```cs
// CommandResultRef - the "result was too big to inline" convention.
//
// The server caps an inline command result (COMMAND_MAX_RESULT_BYTES, 64 KB by
// default) and rejects a bigger ack with 400, which an agent only ever sees as a
// timeout. So a large result (a wide fastlogs.inspect, a game handler dumping
// state) is uploaded as a file instead and the ack carries a reference to it.
//
// The result field stays an opaque string per the contract, so the reference is
// itself JSON under a deliberately unlikely key: an agent that finds
// __fastlogsResultRef downloads that url to get the real payload.
//
// Pure string logic on purpose (no engine, no upload), so it is unit-testable.

#if UNITY_EDITOR || DEVELOPMENT_BUILD || (LOGSHARE_FORCE_ENABLED && !(UNITY_PS4 || UNITY_PS5 || UNITY_GAMECORE || UNITY_SWITCH))
#define FASTLOGS_ENABLED
#endif

#if FASTLOGS_ENABLED
using System.Globalization;
using System.Text;

namespace PlayJoy.FastLogs
{
    internal static class CommandResultRef
    {
        /// <summary>Key an agent looks for to tell a reference apart from a real result.</summary>
        public const string RefKey = "__fastlogsResultRef";

        // Under the server's 64 KB inline cap, with room for the ack body's JSON escaping.
        public const int MaxInlineResultBytes = 60000;

        /// <summary>Whether <paramref name="result"/> must be offloaded instead of inlined.</summary>
        public static bool IsTooBig(string result, int maxInlineBytes = MaxInlineResultBytes)
        {
            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(result)) return false;
            return Encoding.UTF8.GetByteCount(result) > maxInlineBytes;
        }

        /// <summary>File name for an offloaded result (one per command id).</summary>
        public static string FileName(string commandId)
        {
            return "command-" + (string.IsNullOrEmpty(commandId) ? "result" : commandId) + "-result.json";
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// The reference an ack carries instead of the payload:
        /// {"__fastlogsResultRef":"&lt;downloadUrl&gt;","bytes":N}.
        /// </summary>
        public static string Build(string downloadUrl, int bytes)
        {
            var sb = new StringBuilder(96);
            sb.Append("{\"").Append(RefKey).Append("\":");
            SerializedFieldDumper.AppendJsonString(sb, downloadUrl);
            sb.Append(",\"bytes\":").Append(bytes.ToString(CultureInfo.InvariantCulture));
            sb.Append('}');
            return sb.ToString();
        }

        /// <summary>Ack error text when the payload was too big AND the offload failed.</summary>
        public static string OffloadFailedError(int bytes, string error)
        {
            return "result too large to inline (" + bytes.ToString(CultureInfo.InvariantCulture)
                + " bytes) and the offload upload failed: " + (string.IsNullOrEmpty(error) ? "unknown error" : error);
        }
    }
}
#endif

```

`unity/Runtime/Commands/CorrelationScope.cs`:

```cs
// CorrelationScope - tags every report a command produces with that command's id.
//
// Without it, an agent that runs a command which logs or crashes has no automatic
// link between the command and the report it caused: it has to match by timestamp.
// With it, the command id IS the report's correlation code, so GET /api/await?code=
// <commandId> finds exactly what that command produced.
//
// The correlation code is a single runtime-wide value, so this wraps it in scope
// semantics: set on enter, restore the pre-command value on exit. Two rules keep
// that honest:
//   - overlapping commands (a WebGL poll batch can settle async handlers out of
//     order) nest: only the LAST one to leave restores, so an inner command cannot
//     strip the code out from under an outer one that is still running;
//   - a handler that sets its OWN code wins - on exit we only restore when the
//     current value is still the one we set, never clobbering a deliberate choice.
//
// Engine-light on purpose (delegates in, no MonoBehaviour), so it is unit-testable.

#if UNITY_EDITOR || DEVELOPMENT_BUILD || (LOGSHARE_FORCE_ENABLED && !(UNITY_PS4 || UNITY_PS5 || UNITY_GAMECORE || UNITY_SWITCH))
#define FASTLOGS_ENABLED
#endif

#if FASTLOGS_ENABLED
using System;

namespace PlayJoy.FastLogs
{
    internal sealed class CorrelationScope
    {
        private readonly Func<string> _get;
        private readonly Action<string> _set;

        private int _depth;
        private string _saved;      // value from before the outermost command
        private string _lastSet;    // what we last wrote, to detect a handler's own code

        public CorrelationScope(Func<string> get, Action<string> set)
        {
            _get = get;
            _set = set;
        }

        // Depth of currently correlated commands (tests / diagnostics).
        public int Depth
        {
            get { return _depth; }
        }

        /// <summary>Tag reports with <paramref name="commandId"/> until the matching Leave.</summary>
        public void Enter(string commandId)
        {
            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(commandId) || _get == null || _set == null)
            {
                return;
            }
            if (_depth == 0)
            {
                _saved = _get();
            }
            _depth++;
            _lastSet = commandId;
            _set(commandId);
        }

        /// <summary>End the scope opened by Enter(<paramref name="commandId"/>).</summary>
        public void Leave(string commandId)
        {
            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(commandId) || _depth == 0 || _get == null || _set == null)
            {
                return;
            }
            _depth--;
            if (_depth > 0)
            {
                // Another command is still running and owns the code: leave it alone.
                return;
            }
            if (string.Equals(_get(), _lastSet, StringComparison.Ordinal))
            {
                _set(_saved);
            }
            _saved = null;
            _lastSet = null;
        }
    }
}
#endif

```

`unity/Runtime/Core/ComponentCaptureWatcher.cs`:

```cs
// ComponentCaptureWatcher - config-driven capture of runtime component state.
//
// Motivation: debugging WebGL particles once meant hand-adding Debug.Log with
// renderer.GetPropertyBlock().GetTexture("_MainTex") / sharedMaterial / shader at the
// moment each particle activated. This watcher does that from CONFIG instead: given
// CaptureRules, every ScanIntervalSeconds of real time it looks for matching components
// and emits a compact "[flog.capture] {json}" line into the log ring (so it rides with the
// next report) when a component becomes active (enable), on the next N frames after that
// (frame), when its serialized fields change (change), or on demand (manual).
//
// Scanning is TIME-based (monotonic clock), not frame-based, so a high-FPS batchmode
// player does not scan hundreds of times a second and burn the per-session cap. The
// FramesAfterEnable follow-up drain stays honestly frame-based. The clock and the log
// emit are both injected, so the watcher is unit-testable without a live runtime.
//
// Cost is zero when the section is disabled (no watcher is created). While enabled the
// only per-frame work is a null-cheap follow-up drain plus one scene scan per interval.
// Type lookups and rule state are cached.
//
// Gated; removed in retail/console builds.

#if UNITY_EDITOR || DEVELOPMENT_BUILD || (LOGSHARE_FORCE_ENABLED && !(UNITY_PS4 || UNITY_PS5 || UNITY_GAMECORE || UNITY_SWITCH))
#define FASTLOGS_ENABLED
#endif

#if FASTLOGS_ENABLED
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Text;
using UnityEngine;

namespace PlayJoy.FastLogs
{
    /// <summary>
    /// Watches the scene for components matching configured rules and dumps their state
    /// as log lines. Created by <see cref="FastLogsRuntime"/> only when
    /// ComponentCapture.Enabled is set and there is at least one rule; ticked from the
    /// runtime's Update.
    /// </summary>
    internal sealed class ComponentCaptureWatcher
    {
        // No dedicated collection cap in ComponentCaptureSection; reuse a sane default for
        // the shared field dumper (mirrors the scene-context section default).
        private const int DefaultMaxCollectionElements = 20;

        // Injected log sink (the runtime passes its Append method group) and monotonic
        // clock, so the watcher has no hard dependency on FastLogsRuntime and is testable.
        private readonly Action<string, FastLogLevel> _emit;
        private readonly Func<double> _clock;
        private readonly FastLogsConfig.ComponentCaptureSection _section;
        private readonly RuleState[] _rules;

        private readonly List<Pending> _pending = new List<Pending>();
        private readonly StringBuilder _sb = new StringBuilder(512);
        private readonly StringBuilder _fieldSb = new StringBuilder(256);
        private readonly List<int> _pruneScratch = new List<int>();

        // Time of the next due scan on the injected monotonic clock (seconds).
        private double _nextScanAt;
        // Captures on the AUTOMATIC paths (enable/frame/change) only; the cap watches this.
        private int _autoCaptureCount;
        // Every emission (auto + manual), exposed as SessionCaptureCount for stats.
        private int _totalCaptureCount;
        // Set once the cap halts the AUTOMATIC paths; manual CaptureAll ignores it.
        private bool _autoStopped;

        /// <param name="section">Config section driving the rules and limits.</param>
        /// <param name="emit">Log sink (message, level); the runtime passes its Append.</param>
        /// <param name="clock">Monotonic seconds source; null = realtimeSinceStartupAsDouble.</param>
        internal ComponentCaptureWatcher(FastLogsConfig.ComponentCaptureSection section,
            Action<string, FastLogLevel> emit, Func<double> clock = null)
        {
            _section = section;
            _emit = emit ?? ((m, l) => { });
            _clock = clock ?? (() => Time.realtimeSinceStartupAsDouble);

            // Seed the first scan one interval out so a caller can observe "no scan before
            // the interval elapses" (and the test clock can drive it deterministically).
            _nextScanAt = _clock() + ScanInterval();

            FastLogsConfig.CaptureRule[] rules = section != null ? section.Rules : null;
            int n = rules != null ? rules.Length : 0;
            var list = new List<RuleState>(n);
            for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
            {
                if (rules[i] == null) continue;
                list.Add(new RuleState { Rule = rules[i] });
            }
            _rules = list.ToArray();
        }

        // Total emissions this session (auto + manual), for the structured command result.
        internal int SessionCaptureCount { get { return _totalCaptureCount; } }

        // True once the cap halted the automatic paths (manual capture still works).
        internal bool AutoStopped { get { return _autoStopped; } }

        // Effective scan interval, clamped to the [Min] floor for code-set section values.
        private float ScanInterval()
        {
            return Mathf.Max(0.05f, _section != null ? _section.ScanIntervalSeconds : 0.5f);
        }

        // ---- Per-frame pump (called from FastLogsRuntime.Update) ----

        public void Tick()
        {
            if (_autoStopped) return;

            // Follow-up captures scheduled by FramesAfterEnable: one per pending entry per
            // frame. Frame-based on purpose - independent of the time-based scan cadence.
            if (_pending.Count > 0)
            {
                DrainPending();
                if (_autoStopped) return;
            }

            double now = _clock();
            if (now < _nextScanAt) return;
            _nextScanAt = now + ScanInterval();
            Scan(now);
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Capture EVERY matching component across all rules right now with the given event
        /// tag (used for the manual/command path). Returns how many snapshots were emitted.
        /// </summary>
        public int CaptureAll(string ev)
        {
            // Manual pass is an explicit intent: it ignores _autoStopped and the cap and
            // always emits, so an agent can still capture after the auto path halted.
            int before = _totalCaptureCount;
            for (int r = 0; r < _rules.Length; r++)
            {
                CaptureRuleAll(_rules[r], ev);
            }
            return _totalCaptureCount - before;
        }

        // ---- Scanning ----

        private void Scan(double now)
        {
            for (int r = 0; r < _rules.Length; r++)
            {
                ScanRule(_rules[r], now);
                if (_autoStopped) return;
            }
        }

        private void ScanRule(RuleState rs, double now)
        {
            Type type = ResolveType(rs);
            if (type == null) return;

            UnityEngine.Object[] found;
            try { found = UnityEngine.Object.FindObjectsByType(type, FindObjectsInactive.Include, FindObjectsSortMode.None); }
            catch (Exception e) { FlogLog.Exception(e); return; }

            rs.SeenThisScan.Clear();

            for (int i = 0; i < found.Length; i++)
            {
                Component c = found[i] as Component;
                if (c == null) continue;
                GameObject go = c.gameObject;
                if (!MatchesFilter(go, rs.Rule)) continue;

                int id = c.GetInstanceID();
                rs.SeenThisScan.Add(id);

                bool active = go.activeInHierarchy && ComponentJson.GetEnabled(c);

                InstanceState st;
                bool known = rs.Known.TryGetValue(id, out st);
                if (!known)
                {
                    st = new InstanceState();
                    rs.Known[id] = st;
                }

                // Enable = a newly seen instance that is already active, or an inactive->active flip.
                bool enableEvent = known ? (active && !st.WasActive) : active;
                st.WasActive = active;

                if (enableEvent && rs.Rule.CaptureOnEnable)
                {
                    CaptureOne(c, rs.Rule, "enable", auto: true);
                    if (_autoStopped) return;
                    // Seed the change baseline so the enable snapshot is not re-reported as a change.
                    if (rs.Rule.CaptureOnChange) { st.FieldsHash = ComputeFieldsHash(c); st.HasHash = true; }
                    if (rs.Rule.FramesAfterEnable > 0) _pending.Add(new Pending { Comp = c, Rule = rs.Rule, FramesLeft = rs.Rule.FramesAfterEnable });
                }
                else if (rs.Rule.CaptureOnChange && active)
                {
                    int hash = ComputeFieldsHash(c);
                    if (st.HasHash && hash != st.FieldsHash)
                    {
                        // Debounce: emit a change capture only once per MinSecondsBetweenChangeCaptures
                        // per instance. While debounced we do NOT advance the stored baseline, so the
                        // change is caught by the first scan after the window rather than being lost.
                        float minGap = rs.Rule.MinSecondsBetweenChangeCaptures;
                        if (minGap <= 0f || now - st.LastChangeCaptureAt >= minGap)
                        {
                            CaptureOne(c, rs.Rule, "change", auto: true);
                            if (_autoStopped) return;
                            st.LastChangeCaptureAt = now;
                            st.FieldsHash = hash;
                            st.HasHash = true;
                        }
                    }
                    else
                    {
                        // No change (or first observation): keep the baseline current.
                        st.FieldsHash = hash;
                        st.HasHash = true;
                    }
                }
            }

            PruneKnown(rs);
        }

        private void CaptureRuleAll(RuleState rs, string ev)
        {
            Type type = ResolveType(rs);
            if (type == null) return;

            UnityEngine.Object[] found;
            try { found = UnityEngine.Object.FindObjectsByType(type, FindObjectsInactive.Include, FindObjectsSortMode.None); }
            catch (Exception e) { FlogLog.Exception(e); return; }

            for (int i = 0; i < found.Length; i++)
            {
                Component c = found[i] as Component;
                if (c == null) continue;
                if (!MatchesFilter(c.gameObject, rs.Rule)) continue;
                CaptureOne(c, rs.Rule, ev, auto: false);
            }
        }

        private void DrainPending()
        {
            for (int i = _pending.Count - 1; i >= 0; i--)
            {
                Pending p = _pending[i];
                if (p.Comp == null) { _pending.RemoveAt(i); continue; } // destroyed

                CaptureOne(p.Comp, p.Rule, "frame", auto: true);
                if (_autoStopped) return;

                p.FramesLeft--;
                if (p.FramesLeft <= 0) _pending.RemoveAt(i);
                else _pending[i] = p;
            }
        }

        private void PruneKnown(RuleState rs)
        {
            // Every seen id was inserted into Known, so Known is a superset of SeenThisScan;
            // equal counts mean nothing became stale this scan.
            if (rs.Known.Count == rs.SeenThisScan.Count) return;
            _pruneScratch.Clear();
            foreach (var kv in rs.Known)
            {
                if (!rs.SeenThisScan.Contains(kv.Key)) _pruneScratch.Add(kv.Key);
            }
            for (int i = 0; i < _pruneScratch.Count; i++) rs.Known.Remove(_pruneScratch[i]);
        }

        // ---- Capture / emit ----

        // auto = an automatic path (enable/frame/change): gated by _autoStopped + the cap.
        // Manual (auto = false) ignores both and always emits (explicit agent intent).
        private void CaptureOne(Component c, FastLogsConfig.CaptureRule rule, string ev, bool auto)
        {
            if (auto)
            {
                if (_autoStopped) return;
                if (_autoCaptureCount >= _section.MaxCapturesPerSession) { StopByCap(); return; }
            }

            string json;
            try { json = BuildJson(c, rule, ev); }
            catch (Exception e) { FlogLog.Exception(e); return; }

            _emit("[flog.capture] " + json, FastLogLevel.Log);
            _totalCaptureCount++;
            if (auto)
            {
                _autoCaptureCount++;
                if (_autoCaptureCount >= _section.MaxCapturesPerSession) StopByCap();
            }
        }

        private void StopByCap()
        {
            if (_autoStopped) return;
            _autoStopped = true;
            _pending.Clear();
            // Structural marker into the ring so an agent reading the report sees the cap
            // (distinct from a manual pass that found nothing). Not counted as a capture.
            _emit("[flog.capture] {\"ev\":\"cap\",\"auto\":" + _autoCaptureCount
                + ",\"max\":" + _section.MaxCapturesPerSession + "}", FastLogLevel.Log);
            FlogLog.Warn("Component capture: automatic captures stopped, reached MaxCapturesPerSession ("
                + _section.MaxCapturesPerSession + "). A manual CaptureComponentsNow pass still works.");
        }

        // Shape lives in ComponentJson so the watcher and the on-demand inspector emit the
        // same object; the builder is reused per capture (cleared, not reallocated).
        private string BuildJson(Component c, FastLogsConfig.CaptureRule rule, string ev)
        {
            StringBuilder sb = _sb;
            sb.Length = 0;
            ComponentJson.Write(sb, c, ev, _section.MaxFieldsPerComponent, _section.MaxStringLength,
                DefaultMaxCollectionElements, rule.RendererProperties);
            return sb.ToString();
        }

        // Hashed in place: the field dump can be kilobytes and is thrown away immediately, so
        // materializing it just to hash it allocated a full string per instance per scan. The
        // value only ever meets the same instance's previous hash in-session, so the algorithm
        // change is invisible outside.
        private int ComputeFieldsHash(Component c)
        {
            StringBuilder sb = _fieldSb;
            sb.Length = 0;
            SerializedFieldDumper.WriteFields(sb, c, c.GetType(),
                _section.MaxFieldsPerComponent, _section.MaxStringLength, DefaultMaxCollectionElements);
            return FlogHash.Fnv1a(sb);
        }

        // ---- Helpers ----

        private static bool MatchesFilter(GameObject go, FastLogsConfig.CaptureRule rule)
        {
            if (go == null) return false;
            if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(rule.NameContains) &&
                go.name.IndexOf(rule.NameContains, StringComparison.Ordinal) < 0)
            {
                return false;
            }
            if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(rule.Tag))
            {
                bool ok;
                try { ok = go.CompareTag(rule.Tag); } catch { ok = false; }
                if (!ok) return false;
            }
            return true;
        }

        private Type ResolveType(RuleState rs)
        {
            if (rs.TypeResolveAttempted) return rs.Type;
            rs.TypeResolveAttempted = true;

            string tn = rs.Rule.TypeName;
            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(tn)) { WarnMissing(rs, tn); return null; }
            tn = tn.Trim();

            Type t = ComponentJson.ResolveComponentType(tn);
            rs.Type = t;
            if (t == null) WarnMissing(rs, tn);
            return t;
        }

        private static void WarnMissing(RuleState rs, string tn)
        {
            if (rs.TypeMissingWarned) return;
            rs.TypeMissingWarned = true;
            FlogLog.Warn("Component capture: type not found or not a Component: '" + tn + "'.");
        }

        // ---- State types ----

        private sealed class RuleState
        {
            public FastLogsConfig.CaptureRule Rule;
            public Type Type;
            public bool TypeResolveAttempted;
            public bool TypeMissingWarned;
            public readonly Dictionary<int, InstanceState> Known = new Dictionary<int, InstanceState>();
            public readonly HashSet<int> SeenThisScan = new HashSet<int>();
        }

        private sealed class InstanceState
        {
            public bool WasActive;
            public int FieldsHash;
            public bool HasHash;
            // Clock time of this instance's last emitted change capture. NegativeInfinity
            // means "never": the FIRST change must emit immediately, not debounce against t=0.
            public double LastChangeCaptureAt = double.NegativeInfinity;
        }

        private struct Pending
        {
            public Component Comp;
            public FastLogsConfig.CaptureRule Rule;
            public int FramesLeft;
        }
    }
}
#endif

```

`unity/Runtime/Core/ComponentInspector.cs`:

```cs
// ComponentInspector - answers ONE question about live component state instead of dumping
// the whole scene.
//
// Motivation: finding out "which texture is in that particle's MaterialPropertyBlock" or
// "what is field X on component Y right now" used to mean pulling a FULL scene-context
// snapshot and grepping it - heavy, noisy, and often still missing the instance you meant.
// This is the pointed read: name a component type, optionally narrow by GameObject, get
// back the matching instances in the SAME shape the capture watcher emits (ComponentJson).
//
// Deliberately independent of ComponentCapture.Enabled and of the configured Rules: this
// is a query on demand, not the watcher's standing surveillance. Only the field/string
// limits are borrowed from that section.
//
// READ-ONLY by construction: serialized fields (SerializedFieldDumper) plus the renderer
// extras, never a game property getter or method - those can have side effects, and an
// inspection must not perturb the state it reports.
//
// Never throws out: every failure comes back as {"error":"..."} for the command channel.
//
// Gated; removed in retail/console builds.

#if UNITY_EDITOR || DEVELOPMENT_BUILD || (LOGSHARE_FORCE_ENABLED && !(UNITY_PS4 || UNITY_PS5 || UNITY_GAMECORE || UNITY_SWITCH))
#define FASTLOGS_ENABLED
#endif

#if FASTLOGS_ENABLED
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Globalization;
using System.Text;
using UnityEngine;

namespace PlayJoy.FastLogs
{
    /// <summary>
    /// On-demand inspection of live components, behind the "fastlogs.inspect" command.
    /// </summary>
    internal static class ComponentInspector
    {
        // Used when no config is supplied; mirror the ComponentCaptureSection defaults.
        private const int DefaultMaxFields = 40;
        private const int DefaultMaxStringLength = 200;

        // No collection cap in ComponentCaptureSection; same default the watcher uses.
        private const int DefaultMaxCollectionElements = 20;

        // Instances returned per request: enough to spot a pattern, capped so a type with
        // hundreds of instances cannot blow up the command result.
        private const int DefaultMax = 5;
        private const int HardMax = 20;

        private static readonly string[] DefaultRendererProperties =
            new string[] { "_MainTex", "_Color", "_BaseMap", "_BaseColor" };

        /// <summary>
        /// Inspect live components. Args: {"type":"SpriteRenderer","go":"Bee","props":["_MainTex"],"max":5}
        /// - only "type" is required. Returns {"type","n","shown","items":[...]} or {"error"}.
        /// </summary>
        internal static string Inspect(string argsJson, FastLogsConfig config)
        {
            try
            {
                return InspectCore(argsJson, config);
            }
            catch (Exception e)
            {
                FlogLog.Exception(e);
                return ErrorJson(e.Message);
            }
        }

        private static string InspectCore(string argsJson, FastLogsConfig config)
        {
            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(argsJson))
            {
                return ErrorJson("type is required");
            }
            if (!(MiniJson.Parse(argsJson) is Dictionary<string, object> args))
            {
                return ErrorJson("invalid args json: expected an object");
            }

            string typeName = GetString(args, "type");
            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(typeName) || string.IsNullOrEmpty(typeName.Trim()))
            {
                return ErrorJson("type is required");
            }
            typeName = typeName.Trim();

            Type type = ComponentJson.ResolveComponentType(typeName);
            if (type == null)
            {
                return ErrorJson("type not found or not a Component: " + typeName);
            }

            string goFilter = GetString(args, "go");
            string[] props = GetStringArray(args, "props") ?? DefaultRendererProperties;
            int max = Mathf.Clamp(GetInt(args, "max", DefaultMax), 1, HardMax);

            // Inactive included: an object that is off is usually exactly what is being asked about.
            UnityEngine.Object[] found;
            try { found = UnityEngine.Object.FindObjectsByType(type, FindObjectsInactive.Include, FindObjectsSortMode.None); }
            catch (Exception e) { FlogLog.Exception(e); return ErrorJson(e.Message); }

            var matches = new List<Component>();
            for (int i = 0; i < found.Length; i++)
            {
                Component c = found[i] as Component;
                if (c == null) continue;
                if (!MatchesGo(c.gameObject, goFilter)) continue;
                matches.Add(c);
            }

            // Limits ride on the capture section, but Enabled/Rules deliberately do not.
            FastLogsConfig.ComponentCaptureSection section = config != null ? config.ComponentCapture : null;
            int maxFields = section != null ? section.MaxFieldsPerComponent : DefaultMaxFields;
            int maxStringLength = section != null ? section.MaxStringLength : DefaultMaxStringLength;

            int shown = Mathf.Min(matches.Count, max);
            var sb = new StringBuilder(128 + shown * 256);
            sb.Append("{\"type\":");
            SerializedFieldDumper.AppendJsonString(sb, type.Name);
            sb.Append(",\"n\":").Append(matches.Count);
            sb.Append(",\"shown\":").Append(shown);
            sb.Append(",\"items\":[");
            for (int i = 0; i < shown; i++)
            {
                if (i > 0) sb.Append(',');
                ComponentJson.Write(sb, matches[i], "inspect",
                    maxFields, maxStringLength, DefaultMaxCollectionElements, props);
            }
            sb.Append("]}");
            return sb.ToString();
        }

        // Matches the GameObject name OR its full path, so both "Bee" and "/Level/Bees" narrow.
        private static bool MatchesGo(GameObject go, string filter)
        {
            if (go == null) return false;
            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(filter)) return true;
            if (go.name.IndexOf(filter, StringComparison.Ordinal) >= 0) return true;
            return ComponentJson.GetPath(go.transform).IndexOf(filter, StringComparison.Ordinal) >= 0;
        }

        private static string ErrorJson(string message)
        {
            var sb = new StringBuilder(64);
            sb.Append("{\"error\":");
            SerializedFieldDumper.AppendJsonString(sb, string.IsNullOrEmpty(message) ? "error" : message);
            sb.Append('}');
            return sb.ToString();
        }

        // ---- Args ----

        private static string GetString(Dictionary<string, object> obj, string key)
        {
            if (obj.TryGetValue(key, out var v) && v is string s)
            {
                return s;
            }
            return null;
        }

        private static int GetInt(Dictionary<string, object> obj, string key, int fallback)
        {
            if (!obj.TryGetValue(key, out var v) || v == null)
            {
                return fallback;
            }
            if (v is double d) return (int)d;
            if (v is string s && int.TryParse(s, NumberStyles.Integer, CultureInfo.InvariantCulture, out int parsed))
            {
                return parsed;
            }
            return fallback;
        }

        // Absent key -> null (caller substitutes defaults); an explicit [] means "probe nothing".
        private static string[] GetStringArray(Dictionary<string, object> obj, string key)
        {
            if (!obj.TryGetValue(key, out var v) || !(v is List<object> list))
            {
                return null;
            }
            var result = new List<string>(list.Count);
            for (int i = 0; i < list.Count; i++)
            {
                if (list[i] is string s && !string.IsNullOrEmpty(s)) result.Add(s);
            }
            return result.ToArray();
        }
    }
}
#endif
```
