`unity/Runtime/UI/SettingsPanel.cs`:

```cs
// SettingsPanel - an independent runtime settings UI for FastLogs.
//
// This is NOT tied to SRDebugger; it renders with IMGUI and is meant to be shown
// as a tab inside the ImguiOverlay (or standalone). It exposes the runtime-tunable
// knobs:
//   - endpoint URL (read-only) with a masked token preview,
//   - appId (editable),
//   - capture-screenshot-by-default toggle,
//   - include-sensitive-diagnostics toggle,
//   - trigger selection (which gesture opens the overlay),
//   - retention-days override,
//   - ring-buffer capacity,
//   - Start / Stop recording (reflecting FastLogs.IsRecording) and Clear,
//   - a button to open the persistent data directory (where recordings live).
//
// Runtime edits PERSIST to PlayerPrefs (namespaced keys) so they survive a
// restart without touching the asset. The panel applies values back onto the live
// FastLogsConfig instance so the rest of the system picks them up immediately.
//
// Available by default in Editor / Development (config.UI.EnableUI). The whole
// file is gated, so it is stripped together with the package in retail/console.

#if UNITY_EDITOR || DEVELOPMENT_BUILD || (LOGSHARE_FORCE_ENABLED && !(UNITY_PS4 || UNITY_PS5 || UNITY_GAMECORE || UNITY_SWITCH))
#define FASTLOGS_ENABLED
#endif

#if FASTLOGS_ENABLED
using System;
using UnityEngine;

namespace PlayJoy.FastLogs
{
    /// <summary>
    /// Trigger kinds the settings panel can select between. Maps onto TriggerConfig
    /// fields when applied.
    /// </summary>
    public enum TriggerKind
    {
        KeyCombo = 0,
        CornerMultiTap = 1,
        Both = 2
    }

    /// <summary>
    /// Runtime settings UI. Reads/writes a live FastLogsConfig and persists overrides
    /// in PlayerPrefs. Rendered via OnGUILayout from a host (the overlay) inside an
    /// already-open GUILayout area.
    /// </summary>
    public sealed class SettingsPanel
    {
        // PlayerPrefs key namespace so we never collide with the game's prefs.
        private const string Prefix = "FastLogs.";
        private const string KeyAppId = Prefix + "appId";
        private const string KeyCaptureShot = Prefix + "captureScreenshot";
        private const string KeyIncludeSensitive = Prefix + "includeSensitive";
        private const string KeyScrubPii = Prefix + "scrubPii";
        private const string KeyTriggerKind = Prefix + "triggerKind";
        private const string KeyRetention = Prefix + "retentionDays";
        private const string KeyRingCapacity = Prefix + "ringCapacity";
        private const string KeyTesterName = Prefix + "testerName";
        private const string KeyCopyLinkOnSend = Prefix + "copyLinkOnSend";
        private const string KeyAutoSendOnException = Prefix + "autoSendOnException";
        private const string KeyQuickSendKeyboard = Prefix + "quickSendKeyboard";
        private const string KeyQuickSendKey = Prefix + "quickSendKey";

        private readonly FastLogsConfig _config;

        private string _appId = string.Empty;
        private bool _captureShot;
        private bool _includeSensitive;
        private bool _scrubPii = true;
        private TriggerKind _triggerKind = TriggerKind.KeyCombo;
        private string _retentionText = "0";
        private string _ringCapacityText = "1000";
        private string _testerName = string.Empty;
        private bool _copyLinkOnSend = true;
        private bool _autoSendOnException = true;
        private bool _quickSendKeyboard;
        private KeyCode _quickSendKey = KeyCode.F9;

        // Built lazily inside OnGUILayout.
        private bool _stylesBuilt;
        private GUIStyle _label;
        private GUIStyle _field;
        private GUIStyle _button;
        private GUIStyle _section;

        public SettingsPanel(FastLogsConfig config)
        {
            _config = config;
            LoadFromPrefsAndConfig();
        }

        // ---- Persistence ----

        private void LoadFromPrefsAndConfig()
        {
            // Seed from the config, then let any saved PlayerPrefs override.
            if (_config != null)
            {
                _appId = _config.Server.AppId ?? string.Empty;
                _captureShot = _config.Screenshot.CaptureByDefault;
                _includeSensitive = _config.Diagnostics.IncludeSensitive;
                _scrubPii = _config.Diagnostics.ScrubPii;
                _retentionText = _config.Server.RetentionDaysOverride.ToString();
                _ringCapacityText = _config.Capture.RingCapacity.ToString();
                _testerName = _config.UI.TesterName ?? string.Empty;
                _copyLinkOnSend = _config.UI.CopyLinkOnSend;
                _autoSendOnException = _config.AutoSend.AutoSendOnException;
                _quickSendKeyboard = _config.Trigger.EnableQuickSendKeyboard;
                _quickSendKey = _config.Trigger.QuickSendKey;
            }

            _appId = PlayerPrefs.GetString(KeyAppId, _appId);
            _captureShot = PlayerPrefs.GetInt(KeyCaptureShot, _captureShot ? 1 : 0) != 0;
            _includeSensitive = PlayerPrefs.GetInt(KeyIncludeSensitive, _includeSensitive ? 1 : 0) != 0;
            _scrubPii = PlayerPrefs.GetInt(KeyScrubPii, _scrubPii ? 1 : 0) != 0;
            _triggerKind = (TriggerKind)PlayerPrefs.GetInt(KeyTriggerKind, (int)_triggerKind);
            _retentionText = PlayerPrefs.GetInt(KeyRetention, ParseIntOr(_retentionText, 0)).ToString();
            _ringCapacityText = PlayerPrefs.GetInt(KeyRingCapacity, ParseIntOr(_ringCapacityText, 1000)).ToString();
            _testerName = PlayerPrefs.GetString(KeyTesterName, _testerName);
            _copyLinkOnSend = PlayerPrefs.GetInt(KeyCopyLinkOnSend, _copyLinkOnSend ? 1 : 0) != 0;
            _autoSendOnException = PlayerPrefs.GetInt(KeyAutoSendOnException, _autoSendOnException ? 1 : 0) != 0;
            _quickSendKeyboard = PlayerPrefs.GetInt(KeyQuickSendKeyboard, _quickSendKeyboard ? 1 : 0) != 0;
            _quickSendKey = (KeyCode)PlayerPrefs.GetInt(KeyQuickSendKey, (int)_quickSendKey);

            ApplyToConfig();
        }

        // Push current panel values onto the live config so the rest of the system
        // (uploader, trigger, capture) picks them up immediately.
        private void ApplyToConfig()
        {
            if (_config == null)
            {
                return;
            }

            _config.Server.AppId = _appId ?? string.Empty;
            _config.Screenshot.CaptureByDefault = _captureShot;
            _config.Diagnostics.IncludeSensitive = _includeSensitive;
            _config.Diagnostics.ScrubPii = _scrubPii;
            _config.Server.RetentionDaysOverride = Mathf.Max(0, ParseIntOr(_retentionText, 0));
            _config.Capture.RingCapacity = Mathf.Max(1, ParseIntOr(_ringCapacityText, 1000));
            _config.UI.TesterName = _testerName ?? string.Empty;
            _config.UI.CopyLinkOnSend = _copyLinkOnSend;
            _config.AutoSend.AutoSendOnException = _autoSendOnException;
            _config.Trigger.EnableQuickSendKeyboard = _quickSendKeyboard;
            _config.Trigger.QuickSendKey = _quickSendKey;
            ApplyTriggerKind(_triggerKind, _config.Trigger);
        }

        private void SaveToPrefs()
        {
            PlayerPrefs.SetString(KeyAppId, _appId ?? string.Empty);
            PlayerPrefs.SetInt(KeyCaptureShot, _captureShot ? 1 : 0);
            PlayerPrefs.SetInt(KeyIncludeSensitive, _includeSensitive ? 1 : 0);
            PlayerPrefs.SetInt(KeyScrubPii, _scrubPii ? 1 : 0);
            PlayerPrefs.SetInt(KeyTriggerKind, (int)_triggerKind);
            PlayerPrefs.SetInt(KeyRetention, Mathf.Max(0, ParseIntOr(_retentionText, 0)));
            PlayerPrefs.SetInt(KeyRingCapacity, Mathf.Max(1, ParseIntOr(_ringCapacityText, 1000)));
            PlayerPrefs.SetString(KeyTesterName, _testerName ?? string.Empty);
            PlayerPrefs.SetInt(KeyCopyLinkOnSend, _copyLinkOnSend ? 1 : 0);
            PlayerPrefs.SetInt(KeyAutoSendOnException, _autoSendOnException ? 1 : 0);
            PlayerPrefs.SetInt(KeyQuickSendKeyboard, _quickSendKeyboard ? 1 : 0);
            PlayerPrefs.SetInt(KeyQuickSendKey, (int)_quickSendKey);
            PlayerPrefs.Save();
        }

        private static void ApplyTriggerKind(TriggerKind kind, TriggerConfig trigger)
        {
            if (trigger == null)
            {
                return;
            }
            // KeyCombo uses the keyboard path; CornerMultiTap uses a non-zero finger
            // count as the "corner multitap enabled" signal (the builder maps it).
            switch (kind)
            {
                case TriggerKind.KeyCombo:
                    trigger.EnableKeyboard = true;
                    if (trigger.MultiTouchFingerCount <= 0) trigger.MultiTouchFingerCount = 0;
                    break;
                case TriggerKind.CornerMultiTap:
                    trigger.EnableKeyboard = false;
                    if (trigger.MultiTouchFingerCount <= 0) trigger.MultiTouchFingerCount = 3;
                    break;
                default: // Both
                    trigger.EnableKeyboard = true;
                    if (trigger.MultiTouchFingerCount <= 0) trigger.MultiTouchFingerCount = 3;
                    break;
            }
        }

        // ---- Layout helpers used by the overlay ----

        /// <summary>Rough pixel height the panel needs (so the overlay can size itself).</summary>
        public float EstimateHeight(float scale)
        {
            // ~18 rows of controls at ~30pt each, plus section labels. Added the
            // auto-send toggle, scrub-PII toggle, quick-send toggle and (conditional)
            // key picker row.
            float rows = _quickSendKeyboard ? 595f : 560f;
            return rows * scale;
        }

        // ---- Rendering ----

        /// <summary>
        /// Draw the panel inside the caller's existing GUILayout context. The caller
        /// (overlay) supplies the active DPI scale so sizes match the rest of the UI.
        /// </summary>
        public void OnGUILayout(float scale)
        {
            EnsureStyles(scale);

            float lineH = Mathf.Max(44f, 30f * scale);
            bool changed = false;

            GUILayout.Label("Settings", _section);

            // Endpoint (read-only) + masked token.
            GUILayout.Label("Endpoint (read-only):", _label);
            GUILayout.Label(EndpointDisplay(), _field, GUILayout.Height(lineH));

            // App id (editable).
            GUILayout.Label("App Id:", _label);
            string newAppId = GUILayout.TextField(_appId, _field, GUILayout.Height(lineH));
            if (!string.Equals(newAppId, _appId, StringComparison.Ordinal))
            {
                _appId = newAppId;
                changed = true;
            }

            // Tester name (editable) - attached to every report's "tester" field.
            GUILayout.Label("Tester Name:", _label);
            string newTester = GUILayout.TextField(_testerName, _field, GUILayout.Height(lineH));
            if (!string.Equals(newTester, _testerName, StringComparison.Ordinal))
            {
                _testerName = newTester;
                changed = true;
            }

            // Toggles.
            bool newShot = GUILayout.Toggle(_captureShot, " Capture screenshot by default", GUILayout.Height(lineH));
            if (newShot != _captureShot) { _captureShot = newShot; changed = true; }

            bool newSensitive = GUILayout.Toggle(_includeSensitive, " Include sensitive device info", GUILayout.Height(lineH));
            if (newSensitive != _includeSensitive) { _includeSensitive = newSensitive; changed = true; }

            bool newScrub = GUILayout.Toggle(_scrubPii, " Scrub PII (emails, IPs, tokens)", GUILayout.Height(lineH));
            if (newScrub != _scrubPii) { _scrubPii = newScrub; changed = true; }

            bool newCopyOnSend = GUILayout.Toggle(_copyLinkOnSend, " Copy link on send", GUILayout.Height(lineH));
            if (newCopyOnSend != _copyLinkOnSend) { _copyLinkOnSend = newCopyOnSend; changed = true; }

            bool newAutoSend = GUILayout.Toggle(_autoSendOnException, " Auto-send on unhandled exception", GUILayout.Height(lineH));
            if (newAutoSend != _autoSendOnException) { _autoSendOnException = newAutoSend; changed = true; }

            // Quick-send (send without opening the overlay). Note: changing this takes
            // effect on the next FastLogs.Init; the live trigger is configured once.
            bool newQuickKb = GUILayout.Toggle(_quickSendKeyboard, " Quick-send keyboard shortcut", GUILayout.Height(lineH));
            if (newQuickKb != _quickSendKeyboard) { _quickSendKeyboard = newQuickKb; changed = true; }

            if (_quickSendKeyboard)
            {
                GUILayout.BeginHorizontal();
                GUILayout.Label("Quick-send key:", _label, GUILayout.Height(lineH));
                if (QuickSendKeyButton(KeyCode.F9, lineH)) { _quickSendKey = KeyCode.F9; changed = true; }
                if (QuickSendKeyButton(KeyCode.F10, lineH)) { _quickSendKey = KeyCode.F10; changed = true; }
                if (QuickSendKeyButton(KeyCode.F11, lineH)) { _quickSendKey = KeyCode.F11; changed = true; }
                GUILayout.EndHorizontal();
            }

            // Trigger selection.
            GUILayout.Label("Open overlay with:", _label);
            GUILayout.BeginHorizontal();
            if (TriggerButton("Key", TriggerKind.KeyCombo, lineH)) { _triggerKind = TriggerKind.KeyCombo; changed = true; }
            if (TriggerButton("Tap corner", TriggerKind.CornerMultiTap, lineH)) { _triggerKind = TriggerKind.CornerMultiTap; changed = true; }
            if (TriggerButton("Both", TriggerKind.Both, lineH)) { _triggerKind = TriggerKind.Both; changed = true; }
            GUILayout.EndHorizontal();

            // Retention override.
            GUILayout.BeginHorizontal();
            GUILayout.Label("Retention (days, 0=server):", _label, GUILayout.Height(lineH));
            string newRet = GUILayout.TextField(_retentionText, _field, GUILayout.Width(80f * scale), GUILayout.Height(lineH));
            if (!string.Equals(newRet, _retentionText, StringComparison.Ordinal)) { _retentionText = DigitsOnly(newRet); changed = true; }
            GUILayout.EndHorizontal();

            // Ring capacity.
            GUILayout.BeginHorizontal();
            GUILayout.Label("Buffer capacity (lines):", _label, GUILayout.Height(lineH));
            string newCap = GUILayout.TextField(_ringCapacityText, _field, GUILayout.Width(100f * scale), GUILayout.Height(lineH));
            if (!string.Equals(newCap, _ringCapacityText, StringComparison.Ordinal)) { _ringCapacityText = DigitsOnly(newCap); changed = true; }
            GUILayout.EndHorizontal();

            // Recording controls (via the facade, so no runtime reference needed).
            GUILayout.Label("Recording: " + (FastLogs.IsRecording ? "ON" : "off"), _label);
            GUILayout.BeginHorizontal();
            if (GUILayout.Button(FastLogs.IsRecording ? "Stop" : "Start", _button, GUILayout.Height(lineH)))
            {
                FastLogs.SetRecording(!FastLogs.IsRecording);
            }
            if (GUILayout.Button("Clear", _button, GUILayout.Height(lineH)))
            {
                FastLogs.ClearRecording();
            }
            GUILayout.EndHorizontal();

            // Open the persistent data directory (where recordings are stored).
            if (GUILayout.Button("Open data folder", _button, GUILayout.Height(lineH)))
            {
                OpenDataFolder();
            }

            if (changed)
            {
                ApplyToConfig();
                SaveToPrefs();
            }
        }

        private bool TriggerButton(string label, TriggerKind kind, float lineH)
        {
            bool selected = _triggerKind == kind;
            var prev = GUI.backgroundColor;
            if (selected)
            {
                GUI.backgroundColor = new Color(0.20f, 0.45f, 0.85f, 1f);
            }
            bool clicked = GUILayout.Button(label, _button, GUILayout.Height(lineH));
            GUI.backgroundColor = prev;
            return clicked && !selected;
        }

        private bool QuickSendKeyButton(KeyCode key, float lineH)
        {
            bool selected = _quickSendKey == key;
            var prev = GUI.backgroundColor;
            if (selected)
            {
                GUI.backgroundColor = new Color(0.20f, 0.45f, 0.85f, 1f);
            }
            bool clicked = GUILayout.Button(key.ToString(), _button, GUILayout.Height(lineH));
            GUI.backgroundColor = prev;
            return clicked && !selected;
        }

        private string EndpointDisplay()
        {
            if (_config == null)
            {
                return "(no config)";
            }
            string url = _config.Server.EndpointUrl;
            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(url))
            {
                url = "(not set)";
            }
            string token = _config.Server.Token;
            if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(token))
            {
                url += "  [token: " + MaskToken(token) + "]";
            }
            return url;
        }

        private static string MaskToken(string token)
        {
            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(token))
            {
                return string.Empty;
            }
            if (token.Length <= 4)
            {
                return new string('*', token.Length);
            }
            // Show first 2 and last 2, mask the middle.
            int hidden = token.Length - 4;
            return token.Substring(0, 2) + new string('*', Math.Min(hidden, 8)) + token.Substring(token.Length - 2);
        }

        private static void OpenDataFolder()
        {
            try
            {
                string path = Application.persistentDataPath;
#if UNITY_EDITOR || UNITY_STANDALONE
                Application.OpenURL("file://" + path);
#else
                FlogLog.Info("Data folder: " + path);
#endif
            }
            catch (Exception e)
            {
                FlogLog.Exception(e);
            }
        }

        // ---- Utils ----

        private static int ParseIntOr(string s, int fallback)
        {
            int v;
            return int.TryParse(s, out v) ? v : fallback;
        }

        private static string DigitsOnly(string s)
        {
            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(s))
            {
                return "0";
            }
            var sb = new System.Text.StringBuilder(s.Length);
            for (int i = 0; i < s.Length; i++)
            {
                if (char.IsDigit(s[i]))
                {
                    sb.Append(s[i]);
                }
            }
            return sb.Length == 0 ? "0" : sb.ToString();
        }

        private void EnsureStyles(float scale)
        {
            if (_stylesBuilt)
            {
                return;
            }
            _stylesBuilt = true;

            int fontSize = Mathf.RoundToInt(13f * scale);

            _label = new GUIStyle(GUI.skin.label) { fontSize = fontSize, wordWrap = true };
            _label.normal.textColor = Color.white;

            _section = new GUIStyle(_label) { fontStyle = FontStyle.Bold, fontSize = Mathf.RoundToInt(15f * scale) };

            _field = new GUIStyle(GUI.skin.textField) { fontSize = fontSize };
            _button = new GUIStyle(GUI.skin.button) { fontSize = fontSize };
        }
    }
}
#endif

```

`unity/Samples~/BasicUsage/FastLogsBasicUsage.cs`:

```cs
// FastLogs - Basic Usage sample.
//
// Drop this on a GameObject in a scene (or import the sample from the Package
// Manager). It initializes FastLogs, records logs, and sends a shareable report
// on a key press. Everything here compiles in retail/console too - the void
// FastLogs.* calls simply strip out.

using UnityEngine;
using PlayJoy.FastLogs;

namespace PlayJoy.FastLogs.Samples
{
    public sealed class FastLogsBasicUsage : MonoBehaviour
    {
        [Tooltip("Optional explicit config. Leave empty to load Resources/FastLogsConfig.")]
        [SerializeField] private FastLogsConfig _config;

        [Tooltip("Press to send a report.")]
        [SerializeField] private KeyCode _sendKey = KeyCode.F9;

        [Tooltip("Include a screenshot with the report.")]
        [SerializeField] private bool _includeScreenshot = true;

        private void Awake()
        {
            // Init is idempotent and safe to call early.
            FastLogs.Init(_config);

            // OnUploaded fires for both success and failure.
            FastLogs.OnUploaded += HandleUploaded;

            // Start capturing into the recording buffer.
            FastLogs.StartRecording();

            FastLogs.Log("FastLogs basic usage sample started.");

            // Context rides with every subsequent report (shown in the viewer's
            // Context section). Update it whenever your game state changes; pass a
            // null value to remove a key, or call ClearContext() to drop them all.
            FastLogs.SetContext("level", "1");
            FastLogs.SetContext("mode", "sample");

            // Breadcrumbs are a rolling trail of recent events (last ~100), shown as
            // a timeline next to the report. Drop one at meaningful moments.
            FastLogs.Breadcrumb("Sample initialized");
        }

        private void OnDestroy()
        {
            FastLogs.OnUploaded -= HandleUploaded;
        }

        private void Update()
        {
            if (Input.GetKeyDown(_sendKey))
            {
                Send();
            }
        }

        private async void Send()
        {
            // A breadcrumb just before the action it precedes - this shows up in the
            // report's Breadcrumbs timeline so you can see what led up to the send.
            FastLogs.Breadcrumb("Send key pressed", FastLogLevel.Warning);

            FastLogs.Warn("Manual report requested.");

            // Awaitable on every Unity version (FlogTask, coroutine-driven).
            // title is a short headline; comment is the tester's free-form problem
            // description (both optional). The tester name and copy-link-on-send
            // behaviour come from the config (Config > UI), not from this call.
            var result = await FastLogs.SendAsync(
                _includeScreenshot,
                "Manual report from sample",
                "Reproduced by pressing the send key in the Basic Usage sample.");

            if (result.Success)
            {
                // With Config > UI > CopyLinkOnSend enabled (default), the link is
                // already on the clipboard here; copying again is harmless.
                Debug.Log("[FastLogs sample] Uploaded: " + result.Url);
            }
            else
            {
                Debug.LogWarning("[FastLogs sample] Upload failed: " + result.Error);
            }
        }

        private void HandleUploaded(UploadResultDto result)
        {
            Debug.Log("[FastLogs sample] OnUploaded -> " + result);
        }
    }
}

```

`unity/Samples~/BasicUsage/README.md`:

```md
# FastLogs - Basic Usage Sample

This sample shows the minimal integration: init, record, send on a key press,
and handle the result. It covers everything a QA workflow needs.

## What the sample does

1. **Init** - calls `FastLogs.Init()` in `Awake`, picking up `Resources/FastLogsConfig`
   (or the in-memory default if none exists).
2. **Start recording** - immediately calls `FastLogs.StartRecording()` so all
   subsequent `Debug.Log` output is captured in the ring buffer.
3. **Log a message** - `FastLogs.Log("FastLogs basic usage sample started.")` adds
   a tagged entry to the buffer.
4. **Attach context and breadcrumbs** - in `Awake` the sample calls
   `FastLogs.SetContext("level", "1")` / `SetContext("mode", "sample")` and
   `FastLogs.Breadcrumb("Sample initialized")`, then drops another breadcrumb on each
   send. This enriches every report with what the player was doing (Context section in
   the viewer) and a trail of recent events (Breadcrumbs timeline). Context survives
   until changed/cleared; breadcrumbs roll over after the last ~100. Both are
   PII-scrubbed before upload by default.
5. **Send on key press** - press `F9` (configurable in the Inspector) to call
   `FastLogs.SendAsync(includeScreenshot: true, title: "Manual report from sample", comment: "...")`.
   `title` is a short headline; `comment` is the tester's free-form problem
   description (both optional). The tester name and "copy link on send" behaviour
   are read from the config (`Config > UI`), so they apply to every report.
   The coroutine-backed await works on every Unity version and on WebGL.
6. **Handle the result** - logs the shareable URL on success or the error on failure;
   also wires `FastLogs.OnUploaded` to demonstrate the event callback.
7. **Overlay** - once FastLogs is initialized, press `F8` (the default trigger key
   configured in `TriggerConfig`) to open or close the share overlay from anywhere.
   You can also call `FastLogs.ShowOverlay()` from code or a UI button.

## How to import

In the Unity Package Manager, select the FastLogs package, open the **Samples** tab,
and click **Import** next to "Basic Usage". Unity copies the sample into
`Assets/Samples/FastLogs/<version>/BasicUsage/`.

## Minimum setup before running

1. Create a `FastLogsConfig` asset:
   `Tools > PlayJoy > FastLogs > Create Config Asset`
2. In the Inspector, set `Server > EndpointUrl` to your FastLogs server address
   (e.g. `https://logs.example.com/api/logs`).
3. Set `Server > AppId` (e.g. `mygame`).
4. Optionally set `Server > Token` if your server requires one.
5. Add the `FastLogsBasicUsage` component to any GameObject in your scene and enter
   Play Mode (Editor) or build as a Development Build.

## Notes

- All `FastLogs.*` calls in this script compile in retail/console builds; the void
  calls are stripped by the compiler and the `SendAsync` return path returns a safe
  disabled result.
- `RecordScope` example: wrap code in `using (FastLogs.RecordScope()) { ... }` to
  capture only that section.
- Unhandled exceptions are captured and auto-sent in dev (on by default), persisted
  to a disk outbox first so a crash that kills the process is re-sent on the next
  launch. No sample wiring is needed - throw an uncaught exception to see it.
- On WebGL the `await` resolves via the same coroutine path - no threads are used.

```

`unity/Tests/Editor/AsmdefReferenceUtilTests.cs`:

```cs
using NUnit.Framework;
using PlayJoy.FastLogs.Editor;

namespace PlayJoy.FastLogs.Tests
{
    [TestFixture]
    internal sealed class AsmdefReferenceUtilTests
    {
        [Test]
        public void HasFastLogsReference_NameForm_IsTrue()
        {
            string json = "{\"name\":\"Game\",\"references\":[\"PlayJoy.FastLogs\"]}";
            Assert.IsTrue(AsmdefReferenceUtil.HasFastLogsReference(json));
        }

        [Test]
        public void HasFastLogsReference_GuidForm_IsTrue()
        {
            string json = "{\"name\":\"Game\",\"references\":[\"" + AsmdefReferenceUtil.GuidReference + "\"]}";
            Assert.IsTrue(AsmdefReferenceUtil.HasFastLogsReference(json));
        }

        [Test]
        public void HasFastLogsReference_Absent_IsFalse()
        {
            string json = "{\"name\":\"Game\",\"references\":[\"Some.Other.Asm\"]}";
            Assert.IsFalse(AsmdefReferenceUtil.HasFastLogsReference(json));
        }

        [Test]
        public void HasFastLogsReference_EmptyAndNull_IsFalse()
        {
            Assert.IsFalse(AsmdefReferenceUtil.HasFastLogsReference("{}"));
            Assert.IsFalse(AsmdefReferenceUtil.HasFastLogsReference(null));
        }

        [Test]
        public void AddFastLogsReference_NameOnlyFile_AddsNameForm()
        {
            string json = "{\"name\":\"Game\",\"references\":[\"Some.Other.Asm\"]}";
            string result = AsmdefReferenceUtil.AddFastLogsReference(json);

            Assert.IsTrue(AsmdefReferenceUtil.HasFastLogsReference(result));
            StringAssert.Contains(AsmdefReferenceUtil.AssemblyName, result);
            Assert.IsFalse(result.Contains("GUID:"), "a name-only file should get the name form");
        }

        [Test]
        public void AddFastLogsReference_GuidFile_AddsGuidForm()
        {
            string json = "{\"name\":\"Game\",\"references\":[\"GUID:1111222233334444aaaabbbbccccdddd\"]}";
            string result = AsmdefReferenceUtil.AddFastLogsReference(json);

            Assert.IsTrue(AsmdefReferenceUtil.HasFastLogsReference(result));
            StringAssert.Contains(AsmdefReferenceUtil.GuidReference, result);
        }

        [Test]
        public void AddFastLogsReference_AlreadyPresent_ReturnsUnchanged()
        {
            string json = "{\"name\":\"Game\",\"references\":[\"PlayJoy.FastLogs\"]}";
            string result = AsmdefReferenceUtil.AddFastLogsReference(json);
            Assert.AreEqual(json, result);
        }

        [Test]
        public void AddFastLogsReference_PreservesKnownFields()
        {
            string json = "{\"name\":\"MyGame\",\"references\":[\"Some.Other.Asm\"],"
                + "\"defineConstraints\":[\"UNITY_INCLUDE_TESTS\"],\"overrideReferences\":true}";
            string result = AsmdefReferenceUtil.AddFastLogsReference(json);

            Assert.IsTrue(AsmdefReferenceUtil.HasFastLogsReference(result));
            StringAssert.Contains("MyGame", result);
            StringAssert.Contains("UNITY_INCLUDE_TESTS", result);
            StringAssert.Contains("Some.Other.Asm", result);
        }
    }
}
```

`unity/Tests/Editor/CommandRegistryTests.cs`:

```cs
// NOTE: This fixture depends on stage U-B (unity/Runtime/Commands/CommandRegistry.cs +
// the FastLogsConfig.Commands section). It will not compile until U-B is applied.

using System.Text.RegularExpressions;
using NUnit.Framework;
using UnityEngine;
using UnityEngine.TestTools;

namespace PlayJoy.FastLogs.Tests
{
    [TestFixture]
    internal sealed class CommandRegistryTests
    {
        [SetUp]
        public void SetUp()
        {
            CommandRegistry.Clear();
        }

        [TearDown]
        public void TearDown()
        {
            CommandRegistry.Clear();
        }

        [Test]
        public void Dispatch_RegisteredSyncHandler_ReturnsResult()
        {
            CommandRegistry.Register("fastlogs.echo", args => "ok:" + args);

            bool found = CommandRegistry.TryDispatch("fastlogs.echo", "hi", out CommandOutcome outcome);

            Assert.IsTrue(found);
            Assert.IsTrue(outcome.Ok);
            Assert.AreEqual("ok:hi", outcome.Result);
            Assert.IsNull(outcome.Error);
            Assert.IsNull(outcome.Pending);
        }

        [Test]
        public void Dispatch_UnknownName_ReturnsFalse()
        {
            bool found = CommandRegistry.TryDispatch("fastlogs.nope", null, out CommandOutcome _);
            Assert.IsFalse(found);
        }

        [Test]
        public void Register_InvalidName_IsIgnored()
        {
            // A name with a space fails the ^[a-z0-9_.-]{1,64}$ grammar and is dropped.
            LogAssert.Expect(LogType.Warning, new Regex("invalid name"));
            CommandRegistry.Register("bad name", args => "x");

            bool found = CommandRegistry.TryDispatch("bad name", null, out CommandOutcome _);
            Assert.IsFalse(found);
        }

        [Test]
        public void Dispatch_ThrowingHandler_YieldsErrorAndKeepsRegistryAlive()
        {
            CommandRegistry.Register("fastlogs.boom", args => throw new System.InvalidOperationException("boom"));
            CommandRegistry.Register("fastlogs.ok", args => "fine");

            // The handler exception is logged in full via the package logger.
            LogAssert.Expect(LogType.Exception, new Regex("boom"));

            bool found = CommandRegistry.TryDispatch("fastlogs.boom", null, out CommandOutcome outcome);
            Assert.IsTrue(found);
            Assert.IsFalse(outcome.Ok);
            Assert.AreEqual("boom", outcome.Error);
            Assert.IsNull(outcome.Result);

            // The registry survives a failing handler: other commands still dispatch.
            bool found2 = CommandRegistry.TryDispatch("fastlogs.ok", null, out CommandOutcome outcome2);
            Assert.IsTrue(found2);
            Assert.AreEqual("fine", outcome2.Result);
        }

        [Test]
        public void Unregister_RemovesHandler()
        {
            CommandRegistry.Register("fastlogs.temp", args => "v");
            CommandRegistry.Unregister("fastlogs.temp");

            bool found = CommandRegistry.TryDispatch("fastlogs.temp", null, out CommandOutcome _);
            Assert.IsFalse(found);
        }

        [Test]
        public void Register_SameName_LastRegistrationWins()
        {
            CommandRegistry.Register("fastlogs.dup", args => "first");
            CommandRegistry.Register("fastlogs.dup", args => "second");

            CommandRegistry.TryDispatch("fastlogs.dup", null, out CommandOutcome outcome);
            Assert.AreEqual("second", outcome.Result);
        }

        [Test]
        public void Dispatch_NameIsNormalizedCaseInsensitively()
        {
            CommandRegistry.Register("Fastlogs.Mixed", args => "hit");

            bool found = CommandRegistry.TryDispatch("FASTLOGS.MIXED", null, out CommandOutcome outcome);
            Assert.IsTrue(found);
            Assert.AreEqual("hit", outcome.Result);
        }

        [Test]
        public void Dispatch_AsyncHandler_YieldsPendingTask()
        {
            CommandRegistry.RegisterAsync("fastlogs.async", args => FlogTask.FromResult("async:" + args));

            bool found = CommandRegistry.TryDispatch("fastlogs.async", "z", out CommandOutcome outcome);

            Assert.IsTrue(found);
            Assert.IsNotNull(outcome.Pending);
            Assert.IsTrue(outcome.Pending.IsCompleted);
            Assert.AreEqual("async:z", outcome.Pending.Result);
        }

        [Test]
        public void Config_CommandsSection_DefaultsToDisabled()
        {
            FastLogsConfig config = FastLogsConfig.CreateDefault();
            Assert.IsNotNull(config.Commands);
            Assert.IsFalse(config.Commands.Enabled);
        }
    }
}

```

`unity/Tests/Editor/CommandResultRefTests.cs`:

```cs
using System.Text;
using NUnit.Framework;
using PlayJoy.FastLogs;

namespace PlayJoy.FastLogs.Tests
{
    // The server rejects an oversized inline result with a 400 the agent only sees as a
    // timeout, so a big result is offloaded to a file and referenced. These pin the
    // decision and the wire shape of that reference.
    public class CommandResultRefTests
    {
        [Test]
        public void IsTooBig_MeasuresUtf8Bytes_NotCharacters()
        {
            Assert.IsFalse(CommandResultRef.IsTooBig(null), "no result is not too big");
            Assert.IsFalse(CommandResultRef.IsTooBig(string.Empty));
            Assert.IsFalse(CommandResultRef.IsTooBig(new string('a', 10), 10), "exactly at the cap stays inline");
            Assert.IsTrue(CommandResultRef.IsTooBig(new string('a', 11), 10), "one byte over is offloaded");

            // Cyrillic is 2 bytes per char in UTF-8: 6 chars = 12 bytes.
            Assert.IsTrue(CommandResultRef.IsTooBig("привет", 10), "byte count, not char count");
        }

        [Test]
        public void DefaultCap_StaysUnderTheServerInlineLimit()
        {
            // The server caps result at COMMAND_MAX_RESULT_BYTES (64 KB) and 400s beyond it.
            Assert.Less(CommandResultRef.MaxInlineResultBytes, 65536, "client threshold must leave headroom");
        }

        [Test]
        public void Build_EmitsTheReferenceEnvelope()
        {
            string json = CommandResultRef.Build("http://host/files/a7Bk9Q/download", 123456);

            Assert.AreEqual(
                "{\"__fastlogsResultRef\":\"http://host/files/a7Bk9Q/download\",\"bytes\":123456}",
                json);
            Assert.AreEqual("__fastlogsResultRef", CommandResultRef.RefKey, "agents match on this key");
        }

        [Test]
        public void Build_EscapesTheUrl_SoTheEnvelopeStaysValidJson()
        {
            string json = CommandResultRef.Build("http://host/a\"b", 1);
            Assert.IsTrue(json.Contains("a\\\"b"), "quote escaped: " + json);
        }

        [Test]
        public void FileName_IsPerCommand_AndSurvivesAMissingId()
        {
            Assert.AreEqual("command-SCveDL-result.json", CommandResultRef.FileName("SCveDL"));
            Assert.AreEqual("command-result-result.json", CommandResultRef.FileName(null));
        }

        [Test]
        public void OffloadFailedError_NamesTheSizeAndTheCause()
        {
            string err = CommandResultRef.OffloadFailedError(70000, "413 payload_too_large");
            StringAssert.Contains("70000", err);
            StringAssert.Contains("413 payload_too_large", err);

            StringAssert.Contains("unknown error", CommandResultRef.OffloadFailedError(1, null));
        }

        [Test]
        public void ARealisticInspectDump_CrossesTheDefaultCap()
        {
            var sb = new StringBuilder();
            while (Encoding.UTF8.GetByteCount(sb.ToString()) <= CommandResultRef.MaxInlineResultBytes)
            {
                sb.Append("{\"t\":\"SpriteRenderer\",\"go\":\"/Level/Deco/Item\",\"f\":{\"_color\":\"RGBA(1,1,1,1)\"}},");
            }
            Assert.IsTrue(CommandResultRef.IsTooBig(sb.ToString()), "a wide dump offloads");
        }
    }
}
```

`unity/Tests/Editor/ComponentCaptureRuleTests.cs`:

```cs
// MatchesFilter is private static inside ComponentCaptureWatcher and cannot be exercised
// through the public surface without a live FastLogsRuntime. It is a pure function, so we
// reach it by reflection to test the matching logic deterministically. (Brittle to renames
// - noted in the stage report.) Type resolution moved to ComponentJson.ResolveComponentType,
// which is internal and visible here, so those cases call it directly.

using System;
using System.Reflection;
using NUnit.Framework;
using UnityEngine;

namespace PlayJoy.FastLogs.Tests
{
    [TestFixture]
    internal sealed class ComponentCaptureRuleTests
    {
        private const BindingFlags PrivateStatic = BindingFlags.NonPublic | BindingFlags.Static;

        private static bool MatchesFilter(GameObject go, FastLogsConfig.CaptureRule rule)
        {
            MethodInfo m = typeof(ComponentCaptureWatcher).GetMethod("MatchesFilter", PrivateStatic);
            Assert.IsNotNull(m, "MatchesFilter helper not found (renamed?)");
            return (bool)m.Invoke(null, new object[] { go, rule });
        }

        private static Type ResolveTypeByName(string name)
        {
            return ComponentJson.ResolveComponentType(name);
        }

        [Test]
        public void Rule_Defaults_AreExpected()
        {
            var rule = new FastLogsConfig.CaptureRule();
            Assert.AreEqual(string.Empty, rule.TypeName);
            Assert.AreEqual(string.Empty, rule.NameContains);
            Assert.AreEqual(string.Empty, rule.Tag);
            Assert.IsTrue(rule.CaptureOnEnable);
            Assert.IsFalse(rule.CaptureOnChange);
            Assert.AreEqual(1f, rule.MinSecondsBetweenChangeCaptures);
            Assert.AreEqual(0, rule.FramesAfterEnable);
            Assert.IsNotNull(rule.RendererProperties);
            CollectionAssert.Contains(rule.RendererProperties, "_MainTex");
        }

        [Test]
        public void MatchesFilter_NameContains_IsCaseSensitiveSubstring()
        {
            var go = new GameObject("AlienBoss");
            try
            {
                Assert.IsTrue(MatchesFilter(go, new FastLogsConfig.CaptureRule { NameContains = "Boss" }));
                Assert.IsFalse(MatchesFilter(go, new FastLogsConfig.CaptureRule { NameContains = "boss" }));
                // Empty filter matches anything.
                Assert.IsTrue(MatchesFilter(go, new FastLogsConfig.CaptureRule { NameContains = string.Empty }));
            }
            finally
            {
                UnityEngine.Object.DestroyImmediate(go);
            }
        }

        [Test]
        public void MatchesFilter_Tag_MatchesOnlyExactTag()
        {
            var go = new GameObject("Tagged");
            try
            {
                go.tag = "Player"; // built-in tag, always defined
                Assert.IsTrue(MatchesFilter(go, new FastLogsConfig.CaptureRule { Tag = "Player" }));
                Assert.IsFalse(MatchesFilter(go, new FastLogsConfig.CaptureRule { Tag = "Respawn" }));
                // Empty tag filter matches anything.
                Assert.IsTrue(MatchesFilter(go, new FastLogsConfig.CaptureRule { Tag = string.Empty }));
            }
            finally
            {
                UnityEngine.Object.DestroyImmediate(go);
            }
        }

        [Test]
        public void ResolveTypeByName_ShortName_ResolvesComponentType()
        {
            // Short-name lookup returns the first assembly match; assert by name rather than
            // exact typeof identity to stay robust if another "Transform" exists in the domain.
            Type t = ResolveTypeByName("Transform");
            Assert.IsNotNull(t);
            Assert.AreEqual("Transform", t.Name);
        }

        [Test]
        public void ResolveTypeByName_FullName_ResolvesType()
        {
            Assert.AreEqual(typeof(Transform), ResolveTypeByName("UnityEngine.Transform"));
        }

        [Test]
        public void ResolveTypeByName_Unknown_ReturnsNull()
        {
            Assert.IsNull(ResolveTypeByName("NoSuchType_ZZZ_12345"));
        }
    }
}
```

`unity/Tests/Editor/ComponentCaptureWatcherTests.cs`:

```cs
// Drives ComponentCaptureWatcher through its injected log sink and clock (the internal
// ctor overload), so scan timing, change debounce and the auto-cap vs manual-pass split
// are exercised deterministically without a live FastLogsRuntime or real time passing.

using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using NUnit.Framework;
using UnityEngine;

namespace PlayJoy.FastLogs.Tests
{
    [TestFixture]
    internal sealed class ComponentCaptureWatcherTests
    {
        // A trivial component to match by short type name; Value drives the change hash.
        private sealed class ChangingProbe : MonoBehaviour
        {
            public int Value;
        }

        private static FastLogsConfig.ComponentCaptureSection Section(
            FastLogsConfig.CaptureRule rule, float scanSeconds, int maxCaptures)
        {
            return new FastLogsConfig.ComponentCaptureSection
            {
                Enabled = true,
                ScanIntervalSeconds = scanSeconds,
                MaxCapturesPerSession = maxCaptures,
                Rules = new[] { rule },
            };
        }

        private static bool AnyCapMarker(List<string> emitted)
        {
            for (int i = 0; i < emitted.Count; i++)
            {
                if (emitted[i] != null && emitted[i].Contains("\"ev\":\"cap\"")) return true;
            }
            return false;
        }

        // (a) A scan does not run until ScanIntervalSeconds has elapsed on the clock, then does.
        [Test]
        public void Scan_RunsOnlyAfterIntervalElapses()
        {
            var emitted = new List<string>();
            double t = 0d;
            var rule = new FastLogsConfig.CaptureRule { TypeName = "ChangingProbe", CaptureOnEnable = true };
            var watcher = new ComponentCaptureWatcher(Section(rule, 0.5f, 500),
                (m, l) => emitted.Add(m), () => t);

            var go = new GameObject("Probe_A");
            try
            {
                go.AddComponent<ChangingProbe>();

                t = 0d;
                watcher.Tick();
                Assert.AreEqual(0, emitted.Count, "no scan before the interval elapses");

                t = 0.5d;
                watcher.Tick();
                Assert.AreEqual(1, emitted.Count, "the enable scan runs once the interval elapses");
            }
            finally
            {
                UnityEngine.Object.DestroyImmediate(go);
            }
        }

        // (b) A change is debounced while inside MinSecondsBetweenChangeCaptures; the stored
        // baseline is NOT eaten, so the first scan past the window still emits exactly one.
        [Test]
        public void Change_IsDebounced_AndBaselineSurvives()
        {
            var emitted = new List<string>();
            double t = 0d;
            var rule = new FastLogsConfig.CaptureRule
            {
                TypeName = "ChangingProbe",
                CaptureOnEnable = false,
                CaptureOnChange = true,
                MinSecondsBetweenChangeCaptures = 1f,
            };
            var watcher = new ComponentCaptureWatcher(Section(rule, 0.05f, 500),
                (m, l) => emitted.Add(m), () => t);

            var go = new GameObject("Probe_B");
            try
            {
                var probe = go.AddComponent<ChangingProbe>();
                probe.Value = 0;

                // First scan seeds the change baseline (no enable capture, no change yet).
                t = 0.5d;
                watcher.Tick();
                Assert.AreEqual(0, emitted.Count, "baseline seed does not emit");

                // The FIRST change ever emits immediately: there is no prior capture to
                // debounce against (LastChangeCaptureAt starts at -infinity, not 0).
                probe.Value = 1;
                t = 0.6d;
                watcher.Tick();
                Assert.AreEqual(1, emitted.Count, "first change emits immediately");

                // A second change inside the window is debounced; the baseline is NOT eaten.
                probe.Value = 2;
                t = 1.0d;
                watcher.Tick();
                Assert.AreEqual(1, emitted.Count, "second change is debounced inside the window");

                // Past the window: the surviving baseline means the pending change emits now.
                t = 1.7d;
                watcher.Tick();
                Assert.AreEqual(2, emitted.Count, "one change emits after the debounce window");
            }
            finally
            {
                UnityEngine.Object.DestroyImmediate(go);
            }
        }

        // (c) The cap stops the AUTOMATIC paths (and drops an ev:cap marker), but a manual
        // CaptureAll pass afterwards still emits and returns the number of matches.
        [Test]
        public void Cap_StopsAuto_ButManualPassStillWorks()
        {
            var emitted = new List<string>();
            double t = 0d;
            var rule = new FastLogsConfig.CaptureRule { TypeName = "ChangingProbe", CaptureOnEnable = true };
            var watcher = new ComponentCaptureWatcher(Section(rule, 0.05f, 2),
                (m, l) => emitted.Add(m), () => t);

            var a = new GameObject("Probe_C1");
            var b = new GameObject("Probe_C2");
            var c = new GameObject("Probe_C3");
            try
            {
                a.AddComponent<ChangingProbe>();
                b.AddComponent<ChangingProbe>();
                c.AddComponent<ChangingProbe>();

                // One scan over 3 matches with a cap of 2: two captures, then auto-stop.
                t = 1.0d;
                watcher.Tick();

                Assert.IsTrue(watcher.AutoStopped, "auto path stops at the cap");
                Assert.IsTrue(AnyCapMarker(emitted), "an ev:cap marker is emitted at the cap");
                Assert.AreEqual(2, watcher.SessionCaptureCount, "two auto captures counted before the cap");

                // Manual pass ignores the cap: it captures every match (3) and returns that.
                int n = watcher.CaptureAll("manual");
                Assert.AreEqual(3, n, "manual pass captures all matches after the cap");
                Assert.AreEqual(5, watcher.SessionCaptureCount, "session total = 2 auto + 3 manual");
                Assert.IsTrue(watcher.AutoStopped, "manual pass does not clear the auto-stop");
            }
            finally
            {
                UnityEngine.Object.DestroyImmediate(a);
                UnityEngine.Object.DestroyImmediate(b);
                UnityEngine.Object.DestroyImmediate(c);
            }
        }

        // (e) The field signature (FNV-1a hashed straight off the dump buffer) still drives change
        // detection both ways: a real field change emits, an unchanged component stays silent.
        [Test]
        public void Change_DetectsRealChange_AndIgnoresNoChange()
        {
            var emitted = new List<string>();
            double t = 0d;
            var rule = new FastLogsConfig.CaptureRule
            {
                TypeName = "ChangingProbe",
                CaptureOnEnable = false,
                CaptureOnChange = true,
                MinSecondsBetweenChangeCaptures = 0f, // no debounce: isolate the hash's behaviour
            };
            var watcher = new ComponentCaptureWatcher(Section(rule, 0.05f, 500),
                (m, l) => emitted.Add(m), () => t);

            var go = new GameObject("Probe_E");
            try
            {
                var probe = go.AddComponent<ChangingProbe>();
                probe.Value = 0;

                t = 0.1d;
                watcher.Tick();
                Assert.AreEqual(0, emitted.Count, "the first scan only seeds the baseline");

                t = 0.2d;
                watcher.Tick();
                Assert.AreEqual(0, emitted.Count, "an unchanged component must not emit");

                probe.Value = 42;
                t = 0.3d;
                watcher.Tick();
                Assert.AreEqual(1, emitted.Count, "a changed field must emit exactly one change capture");

                t = 0.4d;
                watcher.Tick();
                Assert.AreEqual(1, emitted.Count, "no further emit once the baseline caught up");
            }
            finally
            {
                UnityEngine.Object.DestroyImmediate(go);
            }
        }

        // (d) AutoStopped / SessionCaptureCount start clean and track auto captures below the cap.
        [Test]
        public void State_TracksAutoCapturesBelowCap()
        {
            var emitted = new List<string>();
            double t = 0d;
            var rule = new FastLogsConfig.CaptureRule { TypeName = "ChangingProbe", CaptureOnEnable = true };
            var watcher = new ComponentCaptureWatcher(Section(rule, 0.05f, 500),
                (m, l) => emitted.Add(m), () => t);

            var go = new GameObject("Probe_D");
            try
            {
                go.AddComponent<ChangingProbe>();

                Assert.IsFalse(watcher.AutoStopped, "not stopped before any capture");
                Assert.AreEqual(0, watcher.SessionCaptureCount, "no captures before any scan");

                t = 1.0d;
                watcher.Tick();

                Assert.IsFalse(watcher.AutoStopped, "still running below the cap");
                Assert.AreEqual(1, watcher.SessionCaptureCount, "one enable capture counted");
            }
            finally
            {
                UnityEngine.Object.DestroyImmediate(go);
            }
        }
    }
}
```

`unity/Tests/Editor/ComponentInspectorTests.cs`:

```cs
// Drives ComponentInspector.Inspect over real GameObjects in the edit-mode scene, so the
// request parsing, the "go" narrowing, the instance cap and the error contract are all
// exercised without a live FastLogsRuntime or a command channel.
//
// Every test scopes its query with a unique "go" filter, so a probe left behind by another
// fixture cannot perturb the n/shown counts.

using NUnit.Framework;
using UnityEngine;

namespace PlayJoy.FastLogs.Tests
{
    [TestFixture]
    internal sealed class ComponentInspectorTests
    {
        // A trivial component to match by short type name; Hp is the serialized field asserted on.
        private sealed class InspectProbe : MonoBehaviour
        {
            public int Hp = 42;
        }

        private static FastLogsConfig ConfigWithCaptureDisabled()
        {
            FastLogsConfig c = FastLogsConfig.CreateDefault();
            // The headline case: inspection is a query, not the watcher's surveillance.
            c.ComponentCapture.Enabled = false;
            return c;
        }

        private static string Inspect(string args)
        {
            return ComponentInspector.Inspect(args, ConfigWithCaptureDisabled());
        }

        // (a) A type query finds the component and dumps it in the capture shape, including
        // the serialized field - and it does so with ComponentCapture.Enabled = false.
        [Test]
        public void Inspect_ByType_FindsComponentAndDumpsFields()
        {
            var go = new GameObject("Insp_Basic_1");
            try
            {
                go.AddComponent<InspectProbe>().Hp = 7;

                string json = Inspect("{\"type\":\"InspectProbe\",\"go\":\"Insp_Basic\"}");

                StringAssert.Contains("\"ev\":\"inspect\"", json);
                StringAssert.Contains("\"t\":\"InspectProbe\"", json);
                StringAssert.Contains("\"go\":\"/Insp_Basic_1\"", json);
                StringAssert.Contains("\"Hp\":\"7\"", json);
                StringAssert.Contains("\"n\":1", json);
                StringAssert.Contains("\"shown\":1", json);
                StringAssert.Contains("\"type\":\"InspectProbe\"", json);
            }
            finally
            {
                UnityEngine.Object.DestroyImmediate(go);
            }
        }

        // (b) The "go" filter narrows the selection to the matching GameObject only.
        [Test]
        public void Inspect_GoFilter_NarrowsSelection()
        {
            var a = new GameObject("Insp_Filter_Alien");
            var b = new GameObject("Insp_Filter_Robot");
            try
            {
                a.AddComponent<InspectProbe>().Hp = 1;
                b.AddComponent<InspectProbe>().Hp = 2;

                // Both objects share the "Insp_Filter" prefix: unfiltered within that scope = 2.
                string both = Inspect("{\"type\":\"InspectProbe\",\"go\":\"Insp_Filter\"}");
                StringAssert.Contains("\"n\":2", both);

                string onlyAlien = Inspect("{\"type\":\"InspectProbe\",\"go\":\"Insp_Filter_Alien\"}");
                StringAssert.Contains("\"n\":1", onlyAlien);
                StringAssert.Contains("\"go\":\"/Insp_Filter_Alien\"", onlyAlien);
                Assert.IsFalse(onlyAlien.Contains("Insp_Filter_Robot"), "the filtered-out object is not dumped");
            }
            finally
            {
                UnityEngine.Object.DestroyImmediate(a);
                UnityEngine.Object.DestroyImmediate(b);
            }
        }

        // (c) "max" limits the dumped items while n still reports the true match count.
        [Test]
        public void Inspect_Max_LimitsShownButNotMatchCount()
        {
            var a = new GameObject("Insp_Max_1");
            var b = new GameObject("Insp_Max_2");
            var c = new GameObject("Insp_Max_3");
            try
            {
                a.AddComponent<InspectProbe>();
                b.AddComponent<InspectProbe>();
                c.AddComponent<InspectProbe>();

                string json = Inspect("{\"type\":\"InspectProbe\",\"go\":\"Insp_Max\",\"max\":2}");

                StringAssert.Contains("\"n\":3", json);
                StringAssert.Contains("\"shown\":2", json);
            }
            finally
            {
                UnityEngine.Object.DestroyImmediate(a);
                UnityEngine.Object.DestroyImmediate(b);
                UnityEngine.Object.DestroyImmediate(c);
            }
        }

        // (d) "max" above the hard cap is clamped, not honoured.
        [Test]
        public void Inspect_Max_IsClampedToHardCap()
        {
            var go = new GameObject("Insp_Clamp_1");
            try
            {
                go.AddComponent<InspectProbe>();

                // 9999 must not throw or over-serve; with one match, shown stays 1.
                string json = Inspect("{\"type\":\"InspectProbe\",\"go\":\"Insp_Clamp\",\"max\":9999}");

                StringAssert.Contains("\"n\":1", json);
                StringAssert.Contains("\"shown\":1", json);
            }
            finally
            {
                UnityEngine.Object.DestroyImmediate(go);
            }
        }

        // (e) An inactive object is inspected too - its state is usually the point of the query.
        [Test]
        public void Inspect_InactiveObject_IsFound()
        {
            var go = new GameObject("Insp_Inactive_1");
            try
            {
                go.AddComponent<InspectProbe>().Hp = 3;
                go.SetActive(false);

                string json = Inspect("{\"type\":\"InspectProbe\",\"go\":\"Insp_Inactive\"}");

                StringAssert.Contains("\"n\":1", json);
                StringAssert.Contains("\"act\":false", json);
                StringAssert.Contains("\"Hp\":\"3\"", json);
            }
            finally
            {
                UnityEngine.Object.DestroyImmediate(go);
            }
        }

        // (f) Nothing matched is an empty result, not an error.
        [Test]
        public void Inspect_NoMatches_ReturnsEmptyItems()
        {
            string json = Inspect("{\"type\":\"InspectProbe\",\"go\":\"Insp_NoSuchObject_ZZZ\"}");

            StringAssert.Contains("\"n\":0", json);
            StringAssert.Contains("\"shown\":0", json);
            StringAssert.Contains("\"items\":[]", json);
            Assert.IsFalse(json.Contains("\"error\""), "an empty match set is not an error");
        }

        // (g) A type that does not exist (or is not a Component) is reported, not thrown.
        [Test]
        public void Inspect_UnknownType_ReturnsError()
        {
            string json = Inspect("{\"type\":\"NoSuchType_ZZZ_12345\"}");
            StringAssert.Contains("type not found", json);
        }

        [Test]
        public void Inspect_NonComponentType_ReturnsError()
        {
            // A real type, but not a Component: the inspector only dumps components.
            string json = Inspect("{\"type\":\"System.String\"}");
            StringAssert.Contains("type not found", json);
        }

        // (h) A missing / empty / whitespace "type" is an error, never an exception.
        [Test]
        public void Inspect_MissingType_ReturnsError()
        {
            StringAssert.Contains("type is required", Inspect("{}"));
            StringAssert.Contains("type is required", Inspect("{\"go\":\"Insp_Basic\"}"));
            StringAssert.Contains("type is required", Inspect("{\"type\":\"\"}"));
            StringAssert.Contains("type is required", Inspect("{\"type\":\"   \"}"));
        }

        [Test]
        public void Inspect_NullOrEmptyArgs_ReturnsError()
        {
            StringAssert.Contains("\"error\"", Inspect(null));
            StringAssert.Contains("\"error\"", Inspect(string.Empty));
        }

        // (i) Malformed JSON comes back as an error result, not a thrown exception.
        [Test]
        public void Inspect_MalformedJson_ReturnsError()
        {
            Assert.DoesNotThrow(() =>
            {
                StringAssert.Contains("\"error\"", Inspect("{\"type\": "));
                StringAssert.Contains("\"error\"", Inspect("not json at all"));
                StringAssert.Contains("\"error\"", Inspect("[1,2,3]"));
            });
        }

        // (j) A null config must not crash: the field/string limits fall back to defaults.
        [Test]
        public void Inspect_NullConfig_UsesDefaults()
        {
            var go = new GameObject("Insp_NullCfg_1");
            try
            {
                go.AddComponent<InspectProbe>().Hp = 9;

                string json = ComponentInspector.Inspect(
                    "{\"type\":\"InspectProbe\",\"go\":\"Insp_NullCfg\"}", null);

                StringAssert.Contains("\"n\":1", json);
                StringAssert.Contains("\"Hp\":\"9\"", json);
            }
            finally
            {
                UnityEngine.Object.DestroyImmediate(go);
            }
        }
    }
}
```

`unity/Tests/Editor/ConfigDefaultsTests.cs`:

```cs
using NUnit.Framework;

namespace PlayJoy.FastLogs.Tests
{
    [TestFixture]
    internal sealed class ConfigDefaultsTests
    {
        [Test]
        public void CreateDefault_IsNotNull()
        {
            Assert.IsNotNull(FastLogsConfig.CreateDefault());
        }

        [Test]
        public void CreateDefault_AllSectionAccessorsAreNonNull()
        {
            FastLogsConfig c = FastLogsConfig.CreateDefault();
            Assert.IsNotNull(c.Server);
            Assert.IsNotNull(c.Capture);
            Assert.IsNotNull(c.Recording);
            Assert.IsNotNull(c.AutoSend);
            Assert.IsNotNull(c.Screenshot);
            Assert.IsNotNull(c.SceneContext);
            Assert.IsNotNull(c.ComponentCapture);
            Assert.IsNotNull(c.Diagnostics);
            Assert.IsNotNull(c.Trigger);
            Assert.IsNotNull(c.Files);
            Assert.IsNotNull(c.Snapshot);
            Assert.IsNotNull(c.Net);
            Assert.IsNotNull(c.Retry);
            Assert.IsNotNull(c.LoopGuard);
            Assert.IsNotNull(c.UI);
            Assert.IsNotNull(c.Enable);
        }

        [Test]
        public void ComponentCapture_DefaultsToDisabled()
        {
            FastLogsConfig c = FastLogsConfig.CreateDefault();
            Assert.IsFalse(c.ComponentCapture.Enabled);
            Assert.IsNotNull(c.ComponentCapture.Rules);
            Assert.AreEqual(0, c.ComponentCapture.Rules.Length);
        }

        [Test]
        public void Diagnostics_ScrubPii_IsOnByDefault()
        {
            FastLogsConfig c = FastLogsConfig.CreateDefault();
            Assert.IsTrue(c.Diagnostics.ScrubPii, "privacy-by-default: PII scrubbing on");
        }

        [Test]
        public void NeutralDefaults_HaveNoEndpointOrAppId()
        {
            FastLogsConfig c = FastLogsConfig.CreateDefault();
            Assert.AreEqual(string.Empty, c.Server.EndpointUrl);
            Assert.AreEqual(string.Empty, c.Server.AppId);
            Assert.AreEqual(string.Empty, c.Server.Token);
        }

        [Test]
        public void CommonDefaults_MatchDeclaredValues()
        {
            FastLogsConfig c = FastLogsConfig.CreateDefault();
            Assert.AreEqual(1000, c.Capture.RingCapacity);
            Assert.AreEqual(20, c.Net.TimeoutSeconds);
            Assert.IsTrue(c.Enable.EnableInEditor);
            Assert.IsFalse(c.Recording.Enabled);
            Assert.IsTrue(c.AutoSend.AutoSendOnException);
        }
    }
}

```

`unity/Tests/Editor/CorrelationScopeTests.cs`:

```cs
using NUnit.Framework;
using PlayJoy.FastLogs;

namespace PlayJoy.FastLogs.Tests
{
    // The correlation code is one runtime-wide value, so the command channel wraps it
    // in scope semantics. These pin the three rules that keep that honest: restore on
    // exit, nesting for overlapping commands, and never clobbering a handler's own code.
    public class CorrelationScopeTests
    {
        private string _code;
        private CorrelationScope Scope() => new CorrelationScope(() => _code, (c) => _code = c);

        [SetUp]
        public void SetUp() => _code = null;

        [Test]
        public void Enter_TagsReports_AndLeaveRestoresTheEarlierValue()
        {
            var scope = Scope();

            scope.Enter("cmd1");
            Assert.AreEqual("cmd1", _code, "reports sent during the command carry its id");
            Assert.AreEqual(1, scope.Depth);

            scope.Leave("cmd1");
            Assert.IsNull(_code, "the pre-command value (none) is restored");
            Assert.AreEqual(0, scope.Depth);
        }

        [Test]
        public void Leave_RestoresTheGamesOwnCode_NotNull()
        {
            _code = "game-session-42";
            var scope = Scope();

            scope.Enter("cmd1");
            Assert.AreEqual("cmd1", _code);

            scope.Leave("cmd1");
            Assert.AreEqual("game-session-42", _code, "the game's code survives the command");
        }

        [Test]
        public void OverlappingCommands_Nest_SoAnInnerLeaveDoesNotStripTheOuterCode()
        {
            var scope = Scope();

            scope.Enter("cmdA");
            scope.Enter("cmdB");                 // a WebGL poll batch can overlap async handlers
            Assert.AreEqual("cmdB", _code);
            Assert.AreEqual(2, scope.Depth);

            scope.Leave("cmdA");                 // settles first, but B is still running
            Assert.AreEqual("cmdB", _code, "the still-running command keeps the code");
            Assert.AreEqual(1, scope.Depth);

            scope.Leave("cmdB");
            Assert.IsNull(_code, "the last one out restores the pre-command value");
            Assert.AreEqual(0, scope.Depth);
        }

        [Test]
        public void AHandlerThatSetsItsOwnCode_Wins()
        {
            var scope = Scope();

            scope.Enter("cmd1");
            _code = "handler-chose-this";        // the handler called SetCorrelationCode itself
            scope.Leave("cmd1");

            Assert.AreEqual("handler-chose-this", _code, "a deliberate choice is never clobbered");
        }

        [Test]
        public void UnbalancedOrEmptyCalls_AreIgnored()
        {
            var scope = Scope();

            scope.Leave("never-entered");        // no matching Enter
            Assert.IsNull(_code);
            Assert.AreEqual(0, scope.Depth);

            scope.Enter(null);                   // no id to correlate with
            scope.Enter("");
            Assert.IsNull(_code);
            Assert.AreEqual(0, scope.Depth);
        }
    }
}

```

`unity/Tests/Editor/CrumbStoreTests.cs`:

```cs
using System.Collections.Generic;
using NUnit.Framework;

namespace PlayJoy.FastLogs.Tests
{
    [TestFixture]
    internal sealed class CrumbStoreTests
    {
        // Mirrors FastLogsCrumbStore.MaxCrumbMessageLen (private const).
        private const int MaxCrumbMessageLen = 512;

        [Test]
        public void Breadcrumbs_RingCapsAndKeepsNewest()
        {
            var store = new FastLogsCrumbStore(3);
            for (int i = 0; i < 5; i++)
            {
                store.AddBreadcrumb("m" + i, FastLogLevel.Log, i);
            }

            Assert.AreEqual(3, store.BreadcrumbCount);

            List<BreadcrumbDto> snap = store.SnapshotBreadcrumbs();
            Assert.IsNotNull(snap);
            Assert.AreEqual(3, snap.Count);
            // Oldest two (m0, m1) were overwritten; newest three remain in order.
            Assert.AreEqual("m2", snap[0].Message);
            Assert.AreEqual("m3", snap[1].Message);
            Assert.AreEqual("m4", snap[2].Message);
        }

        [Test]
        public void Breadcrumbs_PreserveFifoOrder()
        {
            var store = new FastLogsCrumbStore(5);
            store.AddBreadcrumb("first", FastLogLevel.Log, 0);
            store.AddBreadcrumb("second", FastLogLevel.Log, 1);
            store.AddBreadcrumb("third", FastLogLevel.Log, 2);

            List<BreadcrumbDto> snap = store.SnapshotBreadcrumbs();
            Assert.IsNotNull(snap);
            Assert.AreEqual(3, snap.Count);
            Assert.AreEqual("first", snap[0].Message);
            Assert.AreEqual("second", snap[1].Message);
            Assert.AreEqual("third", snap[2].Message);
        }

        [Test]
        public void Breadcrumbs_LongMessageIsTruncated()
        {
            var store = new FastLogsCrumbStore(4);
            string longMsg = new string('x', MaxCrumbMessageLen + 100);
            store.AddBreadcrumb(longMsg, FastLogLevel.Log, 0);

            List<BreadcrumbDto> snap = store.SnapshotBreadcrumbs();
            Assert.IsNotNull(snap);
            Assert.AreEqual(1, snap.Count);
            Assert.AreEqual(MaxCrumbMessageLen, snap[0].Message.Length);
        }

        [Test]
        public void Breadcrumbs_LevelMapsToString()
        {
            var store = new FastLogsCrumbStore(4);
            store.AddBreadcrumb("i", FastLogLevel.Log, 0);
            store.AddBreadcrumb("w", FastLogLevel.Warning, 1);
            store.AddBreadcrumb("e", FastLogLevel.Error, 2);

            List<BreadcrumbDto> snap = store.SnapshotBreadcrumbs();
            Assert.AreEqual("info", snap[0].Level);
            Assert.AreEqual("warn", snap[1].Level);
            Assert.AreEqual("error", snap[2].Level);
        }

        [Test]
        public void Breadcrumbs_EmptySnapshotIsNull()
        {
            var store = new FastLogsCrumbStore(4);
            Assert.AreEqual(0, store.BreadcrumbCount);
            Assert.IsNull(store.SnapshotBreadcrumbs());
        }

        [Test]
        public void Context_SetAndSnapshot()
        {
            var store = new FastLogsCrumbStore(4);
            store.SetContext("level", "3");
            store.SetContext("player", "alex");

            Assert.AreEqual(2, store.ContextCount);
            Dictionary<string, string> snap = store.SnapshotContext();
            Assert.IsNotNull(snap);
            Assert.AreEqual("3", snap["level"]);
            Assert.AreEqual("alex", snap["player"]);
        }

        [Test]
        public void Context_NullValueRemovesKey()
        {
            var store = new FastLogsCrumbStore(4);
            store.SetContext("k", "v");
            Assert.AreEqual(1, store.ContextCount);
            store.SetContext("k", null);
            Assert.AreEqual(0, store.ContextCount);
        }

        [Test]
        public void Context_NullOrEmptyKeyIsIgnored()
        {
            var store = new FastLogsCrumbStore(4);
            store.SetContext(null, "v");
            store.SetContext(string.Empty, "v");
            Assert.AreEqual(0, store.ContextCount);
        }

        [Test]
        public void Context_ValueIsClampedToCap()
        {
            var store = new FastLogsCrumbStore(4);
            string longVal = new string('y', 1000);
            store.SetContext("big", longVal);

            Dictionary<string, string> snap = store.SnapshotContext();
            Assert.IsNotNull(snap);
            Assert.AreEqual(512, snap["big"].Length); // MaxValueLen
        }

        [Test]
        public void Context_EmptySnapshotIsNull()
        {
            var store = new FastLogsCrumbStore(4);
            Assert.IsNull(store.SnapshotContext());
        }

        [Test]
        public void Context_ClearRemovesAll()
        {
            var store = new FastLogsCrumbStore(4);
            store.SetContext("a", "1");
            store.SetContext("b", "2");
            store.ClearContext();
            Assert.AreEqual(0, store.ContextCount);
            Assert.IsNull(store.SnapshotContext());
        }
    }
}
```

`unity/Tests/Editor/LogRecorderTests.cs`:

```cs
// Drives LogRecorder against a temp store root (the ctor's rootOverride seam), so the byte-cap
// compaction is exercised without touching the real persistentDataPath store.
//
// The load-bearing assertion is the HYSTERESIS. EnforceCap runs on EVERY Append, and trimming
// exactly to the cap left only the trim marker's slack free: once the store reached the cap,
// every single further line paid a full file read + KeepTail + rewrite. Trimming down to a low
// watermark instead must amortize that - far fewer rewrites than appends - while keeping the
// cap, the retained tail and the trim marker intact.

using System;
using System.Globalization;
using System.IO;
using System.Text;
using NUnit.Framework;

namespace PlayJoy.FastLogs.Tests
{
    [TestFixture]
    internal sealed class LogRecorderTests
    {
        private const int Cap = 8192;
        private const long LowWatermark = Cap * 3 / 4; // the 3/4 target EnforceCap trims down to
        private const int LineBytes = 100;             // "[L] +1.000 " (11) + 88-char message + "\n"
        private const string TrimMarkerText = "[FastLogs] ...older recorded history trimmed...\n";

        private static readonly string Filler = new string('x', 82);

        private string _root;

        [SetUp]
        public void SetUp()
        {
            _root = Path.Combine(Path.GetTempPath(), "FastLogsRecTests_" + Guid.NewGuid().ToString("N"));
            Directory.CreateDirectory(_root);
        }

        [TearDown]
        public void TearDown()
        {
            // Best effort: a temp file still held by the OS must not turn into a red test.
            try { if (Directory.Exists(_root)) Directory.Delete(_root, true); }
            catch { }
        }

        private LogRecorder NewRecorder(int maxStoreBytes)
        {
            return new LogRecorder(false, maxStoreBytes, _root);
        }

        private string StorePath
        {
            get { return Path.Combine(_root, "FastLogs", "record.log"); }
        }

        // Fixed-width, so every formatted line is exactly LineBytes and the arithmetic below holds.
        private static LogEntry Entry(int i)
        {
            string msg = "L" + i.ToString("D4", CultureInfo.InvariantCulture) + "-" + Filler;
            return new LogEntry(msg, null, FastLogLevel.Log, 1.0d);
        }

        // Guards the fixed line width the other tests' expectations are built on.
        [Test]
        public void Fixture_LineWidthIsExactlyLineBytes()
        {
            var sb = new StringBuilder();
            LogFormat.AppendEntry(sb, Entry(0), 1);
            Assert.AreEqual(LineBytes, Encoding.UTF8.GetByteCount(sb.ToString()),
                "the fixture's expectations assume a " + LineBytes + "-byte line");
        }

        // THE regression guard: compaction must amortize, not run once per line past the cap.
        [Test]
        public void EnforceCap_CompactsWithHysteresis_NotOncePerAppend()
        {
            const int lines = 300; // ~30KB through an 8KB cap: several fill/trim cycles

            using (var rec = NewRecorder(Cap))
            {
                rec.StartRecording();
                for (int i = 0; i < lines; i++)
                {
                    rec.Append(Entry(i), 1);
                }

                Assert.Greater(rec.CompactionCount, 0, "writing ~30KB through an 8KB cap must compact");

                // Each compaction frees ~Cap/4, so it buys ~20 further appends. Trimming to the
                // cap instead would rewrite on nearly every line past the fill (~220 times here).
                Assert.LessOrEqual(rec.CompactionCount, lines / 10,
                    "compaction must be amortized by the low watermark, not run per Append");
            }
        }

        // A compaction lands at/below the low watermark (that gap is what amortizes it), and does
        // not over-trim the store to a stub.
        [Test]
        public void EnforceCap_TrimsDownToTheLowWatermark()
        {
            using (var rec = NewRecorder(Cap))
            {
                rec.StartRecording();

                int i = 0;
                while (rec.CompactionCount == 0 && i < 5000)
                {
                    rec.Append(Entry(i), 1);
                    i++;
                }

                Assert.AreEqual(1, rec.CompactionCount, "the loop must stop on the first compaction");

                // The writer is closed and reopened by the compaction, so nothing is buffered:
                // the on-disk length IS the compacted store size.
                long size = new FileInfo(StorePath).Length;
                Assert.LessOrEqual(size, LowWatermark,
                    "a compaction must trim to the low watermark, not to the cap");
                Assert.Greater(size, Cap / 2, "a compaction must not throw away more than the watermark asks");
            }
        }

        // Truncation behaviour is unchanged: newest content survives, oldest goes, marker is added.
        [Test]
        public void EnforceCap_KeepsRecentTail_DropsOldest_AndMarksTheTrim()
        {
            const int lines = 300;

            using (var rec = NewRecorder(Cap))
            {
                rec.StartRecording();
                for (int i = 0; i < lines; i++)
                {
                    rec.Append(Entry(i), 1);
                }
                Assert.Greater(rec.CompactionCount, 0, "the cap must have been enforced");

                string all = rec.ReadAll(0);

                StringAssert.StartsWith(TrimMarkerText, all, "a compacted store is prefixed with the trim marker");
                StringAssert.Contains("L0299-", all, "the newest line must survive the trim");
                Assert.IsFalse(all.Contains("L0000-"), "the oldest line must be trimmed away");
                Assert.IsFalse(all.Contains("==== FastLogs session"),
                    "the session marker at the very front is trimmed away with the oldest content");
            }
        }

        // The hysteresis must not weaken the cap itself.
        [Test]
        public void Append_KeepsStoreWithinTheCap()
        {
            using (var rec = NewRecorder(Cap))
            {
                rec.StartRecording();
                for (int i = 0; i < 300; i++)
                {
                    rec.Append(Entry(i), 1);
                }

                string all = rec.ReadAll(0);
                Assert.LessOrEqual(Encoding.UTF8.GetByteCount(all), Cap, "the store must stay within MaxStoreBytes");
            }
        }

        // maxStoreBytes <= 0 = unlimited: nothing is compacted or trimmed.
        [Test]
        public void EnforceCap_IsInertWhenUnlimited()
        {
            using (var rec = NewRecorder(0))
            {
                rec.StartRecording();
                for (int i = 0; i < 200; i++)
                {
                    rec.Append(Entry(i), 1);
                }

                Assert.AreEqual(0, rec.CompactionCount, "no cap = no compaction");

                string all = rec.ReadAll(0);
                StringAssert.Contains("L0000-", all, "nothing is trimmed without a cap");
                StringAssert.Contains("L0199-", all);
            }
        }

        // ReadAll's client-side clamp is independent of the store cap and still trims from the front.
        [Test]
        public void ReadAll_ClampsFromTheFront()
        {
            using (var rec = NewRecorder(0))
            {
                rec.StartRecording();
                for (int i = 0; i < 50; i++)
                {
                    rec.Append(Entry(i), 1);
                }

                string clamped = rec.ReadAll(1024);
                Assert.LessOrEqual(Encoding.UTF8.GetByteCount(clamped), 1024, "ReadAll honours its byte budget");
                StringAssert.Contains("L0049-", clamped, "the newest line survives a client clamp");
                Assert.IsFalse(clamped.Contains("L0000-"), "the oldest line is clamped away");
            }
        }
    }
}

```
