`unity/Tests/Editor/MiniJsonTests.cs`:

```cs
using System.Collections.Generic;
using NUnit.Framework;

namespace PlayJoy.FastLogs.Tests
{
    [TestFixture]
    internal sealed class MiniJsonTests
    {
        private static Dictionary<string, object> SerializeAndParse(LogReportDto report)
        {
            string json = MiniJson.SerializeReport(report);
            object parsed = MiniJson.Parse(json);
            var obj = parsed as Dictionary<string, object>;
            Assert.IsNotNull(obj, "report JSON must parse to an object");
            return obj;
        }

        [Test]
        public void SerializeReport_MinimalDto_HasRequiredKeys()
        {
            var report = new LogReportDto { AppId = "mygame", Platform = "WebGL" };

            Dictionary<string, object> obj = SerializeAndParse(report);

            Assert.AreEqual("mygame", obj["appId"]);
            Assert.AreEqual("WebGL", obj["platform"]);
            // Required-always keys are present even with an otherwise empty DTO.
            Assert.IsTrue(obj.ContainsKey("counts"), "counts is a required key");
            Assert.IsTrue(obj.ContainsKey("logText"), "logText is force-written");
            Assert.IsTrue(obj.ContainsKey("device"), "device is a required key");
        }

        [Test]
        public void SerializeReport_EmptyLogText_IsWrittenAsEmptyString()
        {
            var report = new LogReportDto { AppId = "g", Platform = "WebGL", LogText = null };
            Dictionary<string, object> obj = SerializeAndParse(report);
            Assert.AreEqual(string.Empty, obj["logText"]);
        }

        // The ticket-from-report request rides the same emit-only-when-set convention as
        // sentViaCode: a report that did not ask for a ticket must not carry the keys at
        // all (the server reads a missing createIssue as false).
        [Test]
        public void SerializeReport_CreateIssue_EmittedOnlyWhenRequested()
        {
            var plain = new LogReportDto { AppId = "g", Platform = "WebGL" };
            Dictionary<string, object> obj = SerializeAndParse(plain);
            Assert.IsFalse(obj.ContainsKey("createIssue"), "createIssue is omitted when false");
            Assert.IsFalse(obj.ContainsKey("issueSubject"), "issueSubject is omitted when empty");

            var asking = new LogReportDto { AppId = "g", Platform = "WebGL", CreateIssue = true, IssueSubject = "Boss crash" };
            obj = SerializeAndParse(asking);
            Assert.AreEqual(true, obj["createIssue"]);
            Assert.AreEqual("Boss crash", obj["issueSubject"]);
        }

        [Test]
        public void SerializeReport_StringEscaping_RoundTrips()
        {
            string tricky = "quote:\" backslash:\\ newline:\n tab:\t carriage:\r end";
            var report = new LogReportDto
            {
                AppId = "g",
                Platform = "WebGL",
                Title = tricky,
                Comment = tricky
            };

            Dictionary<string, object> obj = SerializeAndParse(report);
            Assert.AreEqual(tricky, obj["title"]);
            Assert.AreEqual(tricky, obj["comment"]);
        }

        [Test]
        public void SerializeReport_Unicode_RoundTrips()
        {
            string unicode = "cafe ✓ 日本語";
            var report = new LogReportDto { AppId = "g", Platform = "WebGL", Title = unicode };
            Dictionary<string, object> obj = SerializeAndParse(report);
            Assert.AreEqual(unicode, obj["title"]);
        }

        [Test]
        public void SerializeReport_Counts_AreSerialized()
        {
            var report = new LogReportDto
            {
                AppId = "g",
                Platform = "WebGL",
                Counts = new CountsDto(2, 3, 4)
            };

            Dictionary<string, object> obj = SerializeAndParse(report);
            var counts = obj["counts"] as Dictionary<string, object>;
            Assert.IsNotNull(counts);
            // The reader parses JSON numbers as double.
            Assert.AreEqual(2d, counts["error"]);
            Assert.AreEqual(3d, counts["warn"]);
            Assert.AreEqual(4d, counts["log"]);
        }

        [Test]
        public void SerializeReport_OptionalEmptyFields_AreOmitted()
        {
            var report = new LogReportDto { AppId = "g", Platform = "WebGL" };
            Dictionary<string, object> obj = SerializeAndParse(report);
            // Title/comment/tester are empty -> the writer omits them entirely.
            Assert.IsFalse(obj.ContainsKey("title"));
            Assert.IsFalse(obj.ContainsKey("comment"));
            Assert.IsFalse(obj.ContainsKey("tester"));
        }

        [Test]
        public void Parse_Malformed_ReturnsNull()
        {
            Assert.IsNull(MiniJson.Parse(null));
            Assert.IsNull(MiniJson.Parse(string.Empty));
        }

        [Test]
        public void TryParseUploadResponse_ExtractsFields()
        {
            string json = "{\"id\":\"abc123\",\"url\":\"https://logs.example.com/l/abc123\"}";
            bool ok = MiniJson.TryParseUploadResponse(
                json, out string id, out string url, out string rawUrl, out string expiresAt,
                out string error, out string message);

            Assert.IsTrue(ok);
            Assert.AreEqual("abc123", id);
            Assert.AreEqual("https://logs.example.com/l/abc123", url);
            Assert.IsNull(rawUrl);
            Assert.IsNull(error);
        }
    }
}
```

`unity/Tests/Editor/PiiScrubberTests.cs`:

```cs
using NUnit.Framework;

namespace PlayJoy.FastLogs.Tests
{
    [TestFixture]
    internal sealed class PiiScrubberTests
    {
        private const string Redacted = "[redacted]";

        [Test]
        public void Scrub_Email_IsRedacted()
        {
            string input = "contact john.doe@example.com for details";
            string output = PiiScrubber.Scrub(input);
            Assert.IsFalse(output.Contains("john.doe@example.com"), "email should be gone");
            StringAssert.Contains(Redacted, output);
        }

        [Test]
        public void Scrub_IPv4_IsRedacted()
        {
            string output = PiiScrubber.Scrub("server at 192.168.1.100 refused");
            Assert.IsFalse(output.Contains("192.168.1.100"), "IPv4 should be gone");
            StringAssert.Contains(Redacted, output);
        }

        [Test]
        public void Scrub_IPv6_IsRedacted()
        {
            string ip = "2001:0db8:85a3:0000:0000:8a2e:0370:7334";
            string output = PiiScrubber.Scrub("addr " + ip + " down");
            Assert.IsFalse(output.Contains(ip), "IPv6 should be gone");
            StringAssert.Contains(Redacted, output);
        }

        [Test]
        public void Scrub_BearerToken_IsRedacted()
        {
            string token = "s3cr3tTokenValue0";
            string output = PiiScrubber.Scrub("Authorization: Bearer " + token);
            Assert.IsFalse(output.Contains(token), "bearer token value should be gone");
            StringAssert.Contains(Redacted, output);
        }

        [Test]
        public void Scrub_LongDigitSequence_IsRedacted()
        {
            string digits = "1234567890"; // 10 digits, >= the 9-digit rule
            string output = PiiScrubber.Scrub("account " + digits + " closed");
            Assert.IsFalse(output.Contains(digits), "long digit run should be gone");
            StringAssert.Contains(Redacted, output);
        }

        [Test]
        public void Scrub_HarmlessText_IsUntouched()
        {
            string input = "hello world, level loaded with foo and bar items";
            Assert.AreEqual(input, PiiScrubber.Scrub(input));
        }

        [Test]
        public void Scrub_ShortDigitRun_IsUntouched()
        {
            // 8 digits is below the 9-digit threshold and must survive.
            string input = "code 12345678 ok";
            Assert.AreEqual(input, PiiScrubber.Scrub(input));
        }

        [Test]
        public void Scrub_Null_ReturnsNull()
        {
            Assert.IsNull(PiiScrubber.Scrub(null));
        }

        [Test]
        public void Scrub_Empty_ReturnsEmpty()
        {
            Assert.AreEqual(string.Empty, PiiScrubber.Scrub(string.Empty));
        }
    }
}

```

`unity/Tests/Editor/SerializedFieldDumperTests.cs`:

```cs
using System;
using System.Text;
using NUnit.Framework;
using UnityEngine;

namespace PlayJoy.FastLogs.Tests
{
    [TestFixture]
    internal sealed class SerializedFieldDumperTests
    {
        // Plain object (not a MonoBehaviour) is enough: the dumper reflects over any type.
        private sealed class Dummy
        {
#pragma warning disable 414 // read only via reflection; the compiler cannot see that
            [SerializeField] private int hidden = 7;
#pragma warning restore 414
            public string shown = "abc";
            [NonSerialized] public int ignored = 99;
            public int[] nums = { 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 };
            public string longStr = "abcdefghij"; // 10 chars
        }

        private static string Dump(object o, int maxFields, int maxStringLength, int maxCollectionElements)
        {
            var sb = new StringBuilder();
            SerializedFieldDumper.WriteFields(sb, o, o.GetType(), maxFields, maxStringLength, maxCollectionElements);
            return sb.ToString();
        }

        [Test]
        public void WriteFields_IncludesPrivateSerializeField()
        {
            string body = Dump(new Dummy(), 50, 200, 20);
            StringAssert.Contains("\"hidden\":\"7\"", body);
        }

        [Test]
        public void WriteFields_IncludesPublicField()
        {
            string body = Dump(new Dummy(), 50, 200, 20);
            StringAssert.Contains("\"shown\":\"abc\"", body);
        }

        [Test]
        public void WriteFields_ExcludesNonSerializedPublicField()
        {
            string body = Dump(new Dummy(), 50, 200, 20);
            Assert.IsFalse(body.Contains("ignored"), "[NonSerialized] public field must be skipped");
        }

        [Test]
        public void WriteFields_CapsStringLength()
        {
            string body = Dump(new Dummy(), 50, 4, 20);
            // longStr "abcdefghij" -> first 4 chars + "..."
            StringAssert.Contains("\"longStr\":\"abcd...\"", body);
        }

        [Test]
        public void WriteFields_CapsCollectionElements()
        {
            string body = Dump(new Dummy(), 50, 200, 2);
            // nums has 5 elements; only 2 shown, remainder summarized.
            StringAssert.Contains("+3 more", body);
        }

        // Comma-free scalar values so a single-pair body is unambiguous regardless of the
        // (unspecified) reflection field order.
        private sealed class TwoFields
        {
            public string a = "1";
            public string b = "2";
        }

        [Test]
        public void WriteFields_RespectsMaxFields()
        {
            // Only one field is written; the body is a single "name":"value" pair (no separator).
            string body = Dump(new TwoFields(), 1, 200, 20);
            Assert.IsFalse(string.IsNullOrEmpty(body));
            Assert.IsFalse(body.Contains(","), "with maxFields=1 there must be no field separator");
            Assert.IsTrue(body.Contains("\"a\"") || body.Contains("\"b\""), "exactly one field expected");
        }

        // The reflection cache must be invisible: a warm cache reproduces the cold output exactly
        // (the scene-context format is a byte-for-byte invariant).
        [Test]
        public void WriteFields_RepeatedCalls_ProduceIdenticalOutput()
        {
            var d = new Dummy();
            string cold = Dump(d, 50, 200, 20);
            string warm = Dump(d, 50, 200, 20);

            Assert.AreEqual(cold, warm, "a cached type must dump byte-for-byte identically");
            StringAssert.Contains("\"shown\":\"abc\"", warm);
            StringAssert.Contains("\"hidden\":\"7\"", warm);
            Assert.IsFalse(warm.Contains("ignored"), "the cache must not resurrect a [NonSerialized] field");
        }

        // Cache entries are per type: dumping another type must not bleed fields across.
        [Test]
        public void WriteFields_CacheDoesNotLeakBetweenTypes()
        {
            string dummy = Dump(new Dummy(), 50, 200, 20);
            string two = Dump(new TwoFields(), 50, 200, 20);

            Assert.IsFalse(two.Contains("shown"), "TwoFields must not carry Dummy's cached fields");
            Assert.IsTrue(two.Contains("\"a\"") && two.Contains("\"b\""), "TwoFields dumps its own fields");
            Assert.AreEqual(dummy, Dump(new Dummy(), 50, 200, 20),
                "Dummy's output is unchanged after another type was dumped");
        }

        // The cache holds fields, not limits: per-call caps still apply once a type is warm.
        [Test]
        public void WriteFields_PerCallLimitsStillApplyWhenCacheIsWarm()
        {
            Dump(new Dummy(), 50, 200, 20); // warm the cache with wide limits

            string capped = Dump(new Dummy(), 50, 4, 20);
            StringAssert.Contains("\"longStr\":\"abcd...\"", capped, "maxStringLength still applies to a cached type");

            string fewer = Dump(new TwoFields(), 50, 200, 20);
            Assert.IsTrue(fewer.Contains(","), "sanity: an uncapped TwoFields body has a separator");
            string one = Dump(new TwoFields(), 1, 200, 20);
            Assert.IsFalse(one.Contains(","), "maxFields still applies to a cached type");
        }

        [Test]
        public void IsSerializedField_MatchesUnitySerializationRules()
        {
            Type t = typeof(Dummy);
            var flags = System.Reflection.BindingFlags.Public | System.Reflection.BindingFlags.NonPublic
                | System.Reflection.BindingFlags.Instance;

            Assert.IsTrue(SerializedFieldDumper.IsSerializedField(t.GetField("hidden", flags)));
            Assert.IsTrue(SerializedFieldDumper.IsSerializedField(t.GetField("shown", flags)));
            Assert.IsFalse(SerializedFieldDumper.IsSerializedField(t.GetField("ignored", flags)));
        }
    }
}

```

`unity/Tests/Editor/ServicesUiSeamTests.cs`:

```cs
// Guards the "use FastLogs without its UI" seam: the config knob and the headless
// provider must both actually withhold the overlay. UI.EnableUI used to be read in
// exactly one place (the loop guard's confirm dialog), so clearing the knob still
// built the IMGUI overlay, its settings panel and a per-frame toggle gesture.

using NUnit.Framework;

namespace PlayJoy.FastLogs.Tests
{
    [TestFixture]
    internal sealed class ServicesUiSeamTests
    {
        private static FastLogsConfig Config(bool enableUi)
        {
            FastLogsConfig c = FastLogsConfig.CreateDefault();
            c.UI.EnableUI = enableUi;
            return c;
        }

        // NOTE: the UI-ON overlay itself cannot be built here - ImguiOverlay's ctor spawns
        // a DontDestroyOnLoad driver GameObject, which Unity forbids outside play mode.
        // Both factories consult the same UiEnabled(config), so the ON branch is pinned
        // through the trigger (a pure object) and the OFF branch through both.
        [Test]
        public void DefaultServices_UiEnabled_BuildsTrigger()
        {
            var s = new FastLogsDefaultServices();
            Assert.IsNotNull(s.CreateTriggerSource(Config(true)), "the toggle gesture comes with the overlay");
        }

        [Test]
        public void DefaultServices_UiDisabled_WithholdsOverlayAndTrigger()
        {
            var s = new FastLogsDefaultServices();
            FastLogsConfig c = Config(false);
            Assert.IsNull(s.CreateOverlay(c), "EnableUI=false must not construct the overlay");
            Assert.IsNull(s.CreateTriggerSource(c), "a gesture with no overlay to toggle is dead weight");
        }

        [Test]
        public void DefaultServices_UiDisabled_KeepsCaptureAndSend()
        {
            var s = new FastLogsDefaultServices();
            FastLogsConfig c = Config(false);
            Assert.IsNotNull(s.CreateLogSource(c), "no UI must still capture");
            Assert.IsNotNull(s.CreateUploader(c), "no UI must still send");
            Assert.IsNotNull(s.CreateFileUploader(c));
            Assert.IsNotNull(s.CreateScreenshotCapturer(c), "screenshots ride on reports, not on the overlay");
            Assert.IsNotNull(s.CreateClipboard(c), "auto-copy of the short link is not an overlay feature");
        }

        [Test]
        public void DefaultServices_NullConfig_KeepsDefaultOnBehaviour()
        {
            var s = new FastLogsDefaultServices();
            Assert.IsNotNull(s.CreateTriggerSource(null), "no config means defaults, and the UI defaults on");
        }

        [Test]
        public void HeadlessServices_WithholdUi_RegardlessOfConfig()
        {
            var s = new FastLogsHeadlessServices();
            Assert.IsNull(s.CreateOverlay(Config(true)), "headless ignores the asset: no UI means no UI");
            Assert.IsNull(s.CreateTriggerSource(Config(true)));
            Assert.IsNull(s.CreateOverlay(null));
            Assert.IsNull(s.CreateTriggerSource(null));
        }

        [Test]
        public void HeadlessServices_KeepEverythingElse()
        {
            var s = new FastLogsHeadlessServices();
            FastLogsConfig c = Config(true);
            Assert.IsNotNull(s.CreateLogSource(c));
            Assert.IsNotNull(s.CreateUploader(c));
            Assert.IsNotNull(s.CreateFileUploader(c));
            Assert.IsNotNull(s.CreateScreenshotCapturer(c));
            Assert.IsNotNull(s.CreateClipboard(c));
        }

        // The seam is only usable from outside if a host can actually subclass and
        // name these types; they were internal, which is what made "no UI" unreachable.
        [Test]
        public void ProviderTypesAreReachableFromOutsideTheAssembly()
        {
            Assert.IsTrue(typeof(FastLogsDefaultServices).IsPublic);
            Assert.IsTrue(typeof(FastLogsHeadlessServices).IsPublic);
            Assert.IsFalse(typeof(FastLogsDefaultServices).IsSealed, "a host must be able to swap one piece");
            Assert.IsTrue(typeof(CapturingLogSource).IsPublic);
            Assert.IsTrue(typeof(UnityWebRequestUploader).IsPublic);
            Assert.IsTrue(typeof(ScreenshotCapturer).IsPublic);
            Assert.IsTrue(typeof(ClipboardService).IsPublic);
        }
    }
}
```
